gmw_00001216.name: NGC 4569
gmw_00001216.overview: NGC 4569 is a barred spiral galaxy in the Virgo Cluster classified as a LINER or transition object [arXiv:1210.4171, arXiv:astro-ph/9912280]. It features a strongly truncated gas disk and a peculiar western arm shaped by a major ram-pressure stripping event [arXiv:1204.0430, arXiv:astro-ph/0401567].

sources.summary: The paper applies its age-dependent dust correction to NGC 4569/M90, an H I-deficient Virgo spiral with truncated gas and star formation, yielding lower FUV attenuation than standard methods.

quotes.quote: We test our method on the nearby spiral galaxy NGC 4569 (M90), the brightest spiral galaxy in the Virgo cluster recently studied by . NGC 4569 can be considered as the prototype of HI deficient galaxy having only about one-tenth of the atomic gas of a comparable field galaxy of similar type and size. This galaxy has a truncated H and HI radial profile (at a radius of 5 kpc, cayatte94,koop2,n4569 ) and shows significant colour gradients with star formation activity only in the central 5 kpc. Therefore, given its significant age gradients, the contribution of UV photons to the dust heating probably varies with the distance from the galaxy center, making this object ideal to compare with our recipes with age independent techniques.

As described in we determined the TIR/FUV ratio profile combining GALEX-FUV image and Spitzer 24, 70, and 160 m radial profiles. Then, we use the observed FUV-H colour profile to estimate at each radius, determining which TIR/FUV vs. A(FUV) relation to use at each radius. The A(FUV) profile so obtained is shown in Fig. n4569 (left panel, empty circles) and compared to the one estimated using the age independent TIR/FUV vs. A(FUV) conversion buat05 . As expected, our method gives a FUV attenuation 1.5 mag lower than the standard conversion implying a factor 4 difference in the SFR surface density profile obtained from the FUV profile (Fig. n4569 , right panel).

sources.summary: The paper gives NGC 4569 a foreground SKB distance but warns it may be underestimated because of dust and dark matter; its gas properties indicate recent ICM-ISM stripping.

quotes.quote: Following the use of the star formation classes introduced by Koopmann Kenney (2004), we can group our sample galaxies as follow Normal galaxies: NGC 4651. Truncated/Normal galaxies: NGC 4351, NGC 4569, NGC 4580, and NGC 4694. Truncated/Compact galaxies: NGC 4064, NGC 4424, and NGC 4606. Anemic or Truncated/Anemic galaxies: NGC 4293, NGC 4429, and NGC 4450.

NGC 4569 This galaxy has a SKB estimated distance of 9.9 0.2 Mpc, still in front of Virgo. However, the SKB distance for NGC 4569 may be an underestimate, because of its strong dust lanes and possibly large influence of its dark matter halo (Fig figdm ). If we include the dark matter halo in order to match the Rubin's H rotation curve we find a barycentric distance of 3.8 Mpc, locating this galaxy inside the maximum rebound radius. The SKB distance is slightly bigger than Solanes's HI-based distances, although there are different HI linewidths reported in the literature for NGC 4569, and one gets a different distance depending on which linewidths one uses.

Finally, the case of NGC 4569 is the most complicated. The SKB distance places this galaxy outside of the maximum rebound radius ( = 7.0 0.2 Mpc), and in front of the cluster. Its high HI deficiency, truncated HI and H distribution, and extraplanar gas arm (Vollmer etal 2004; Kenney etal 2008) are clear evidence for recent ICM--ISM stripping.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4569 as a Virgo spiral with an extended triangular X-ray halo, a modeled Mach cone, and hot gas linked to a galactic superwind.

quotes.quote: The diffuse X-ray emission associated to NGC 4569 is extended over an area of approximately 20' 20' ( 100 100 kpc; Fig. fig:n4569x ). The overall shape of the X-ray emission is that of a triangle with the tip pointing to the northeast. The galactic disk has a 5 times lower X-ray flux than the giant diffuse halo which encompasses also a dwarf satellite galaxy IC 3583. The large radio lobes observed at 20 cm (Chy z y et al. 2006) are located entirely within the diffuse X-ray halo.

For NGC 4569 it is hard to reconcile the derived Mach cone opening angle with a Mach number based on the sound speed alone. Instead, a Mach number involving the Alfv enic speed seems to be more appropriate, yielding a magnetic field strength of 3 6 G for a intracluster medium density of n 10^ 4 cm ^ 3 . Whereas the temperature of the hot component of NGC 4569's X-ray halo ( 0.5 keV) is at the high end but typical for a galactic outflow, the temperature of the hot gas tails of NGC 4388 and NGC 4501 are significantly hotter ( 0.7 0.9 keV). In NGC 4569 we find direct evidence for a Mach cone which is filled with hot gas from a galactic superwind.

sources.summary: The paper contrasts NGC 4569 with M89, arguing its weak X-ray emission and pericentric passage place its CGM in rapid bubble-drag stripping.

quotes.quote: On the other hand, the nearby galaxy NGC 4569 (a massive galaxy with D_ 25 of 47 kpc and M_ * =10^ 10 \ M_ ), lying north-east to M89, shows very limited X-ray emission (see Fig. 1 of Boselli2016A&A ).

Boselli2016A&A report the ambient ICM density of NGC 4569 to be 4 10^ 5 \ cm^ 3 , which implies at the present location, NGC 4569's CGM can survive ( >1 ) if it was in the first passage through the cluster. However, the X-ray emission from NGC 4569 is very small (observed X-ray mass 10^7 \ M_ ). NGC 4569 is moving at a line-of-sight velocity of 235 km \ s^ 1 with respect to the cluster mean, suggesting a highly eccentric orbit with a major velocity component in the plane of the sky. Boselli2016A&A use the narrow-band H + [ NII ] image of NGC 4569's tail with MegaCam and suggest that NGC 4569 is orbiting from west to east and the galaxy has encountered the maximal density of the ICM north to M87, at a radial distance of > 230 kpc.

At its pericentric passage, this suggests an ambient ICM density of 8 10^ 4 \ cm^ 3 (Fig 12. of Roediger2015a ) implying stripping in the `bubble drag' regime. NGC 4569 can therefore lose its CGM within t_ drag =C_ vam R_ 200 /v_ rel 100 Myr , assuming R_ 200 =200 \ kpc and v_ rel =1000 \ km \ s^ 1 , C_ vam =0.45 . Therefore, an orbit with pericentric distance close to the cluster core, strips the hot halo/CGM of NGC 4569, although it is presently at a location where its CGM could have survived.

sources.summary: The paper presents deep 21-cm observations of NGC 4569, finds a western low-surface-density H I arm, and argues simulations favor a major ram-pressure stripping event about 300 Myr ago.

quotes.quote: Deep 21-cm \ line observations of the Virgo cluster spiral galaxy NGC 4569 have been obtained with the VLA in its D configuration and with the Effelsberg 100-m telescope. A low surface density arm was discovered in the west of the galaxy, whose velocity field is distinct from that of the overall disk rotation. The observed gas distribution, velocity field, and velocity dispersion are compared to snapshots of dynamical simulations that include the effects of ram pressure. Two different scenarios were explored: (i) ongoing stripping and (ii) a major stripping event that took place about 300 Myr ago. It is concluded that only the post--stripping scenario can reproduce the main observed characteristics of NGC 4569.

The spiral galaxy NGC 4569 is a very peculiar member of the Virgo cluster. Its very large size ( D_ 25 = 9 5 , or 47 kpc at the assumed Virgo cluster distance of 17 Mpc) compared to other cluster members, and negative radial velocity ( v_ =-235 km s ^ 1 with respect to the cluster mean velocity of 1100 km s ^ 1 ) have lead to some doubt about its cluster membership, despite its close projected distance to the cluster center ( d=1.7^ o ). Van den Bergh (1976) classified this very red galaxy as anemic due to its low arm--interarm contrast on optical images.

sources.summary: The paper reports large polarized radio lobes around NGC 4569 and argues they are starburst-driven superwind outflows shaped by ram pressure rather than AGN jets.

quotes.quote: Using the Effelsberg radio telescope at 4.85 GHz and 8.35 GHz we discovered large symmetric lobes of polarized radio emission around the strongly H i deficient Virgo cluster spiral galaxy NGC 4569. These lobes extend up to 24 kpc from the galactic disk. Our observations were complemented by 1.4 GHz continuum emission from existing H i observations. This is the first time that such huge radio continuum lobes are observed in a cluster spiral galaxy. The eastern lobe seems detached and has a flat spectrum typical for in-situ cosmic ray electron acceleration. The western lobe is diffuse and possesses vertical magnetic fields over its whole volume. The lobes are not powered by an AGN, but probably by a nuclear starburst producing 10^5 supernovae which occurred 30 Myr ago.

The available data exclude present-day and past AGN activity in the core of NGC 4569. We exclude that the observed radio lobes are due to an extinct AGN, because NGC 4569 undergoes relatively strong ram pressure effects (Vollmer et al. ) which would lead to a head-tail galaxy. Instead, we propose that the observed radio lobes are due to a galactic superwind-like flows, induced by a starburst lasting several 10 Myr. Such an event, which requires 10^5 supernovae explosions and 10^ 56 erg total input energy, is consistent with a stellar population dominated by a large number of A supergiants in the central region of the galaxy (Keel 1996). This is supported by estimates of the combined magnetic and cosmic-ray pressure inside the lobes from our radio data.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4569 as a Virgo LINER/barred spiral with center, disk, and halo X-ray components, gas-deficient outer arms, and ambiguous compact nuclear emission.

quotes.quote: In both nearby galaxies The X-ray emission can be clearly distinguished between different galactic components: the center, the disk, and, in the case of NGC 4569, the halo. Both galaxies have been classified as LINERS from optical spectroscopy (, NGC 4569: Wil85 ; NGC 4303: Huc82 ). NGC 4569 is a bright early-type spiral in the Virgo Cluster, one of the few blue-shifted galaxies outside the local group. It is gas-deficient in the outer spiral arms, the neutral hydrogen content is strongly concentrated in the inner region .

NGC 4569 The global X-ray spectrum of NGC 4569 can be described by a two-component fit consisting of a power-law component (predominantly from the nucleus) and a thermal plasma component (hot gas from SNRs and superbubbles). The resulting value of hydrogen absorption is in agreement with the Galactic foreground, supporting the observed deficiency . The total luminosity in the ROSAT band for NGC 4569 amounts to 2 40 erg s ^ 1 . Two interpretations for the emission of the compact nucleus in the HRI image seem possible: either X-ray emission from an unresolved compact nuclear source, to be associated with the LINER-type nucleus of NGC 4569 in the optical, or a slightly extended nuclear starburst region, similar to the one found in NGC 1808 at a scale of 1 kpc.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4569 as having a strongly truncated gas disk and resettling western arm from ram pressure, with molecular gas asymmetry and normal arm star formation efficiency.

quotes.quote: NGC 4569 (Fig. fig:plot_n4569 ): has a strongly truncated gas disk with a one-sided arm structure to the west (Vollmer et al. 2004a, Chung et al. 2009). This arm structure consists of resettling ISM which has been pushed to larger galactic radii by a ram pressure stripping event 300 Myr ago (Vollmer et al. 2004a, Boselli et al. 2006). The Nobeyama CO(1--0) distribution is consistent with the IRAM 30m CO(2--1) distribution presented in Schruba et al. (2011). CO is detected at the northern tip of this arm. Within the disk the molecular fraction is higher on the southern side.
There, SFE_ tot is 2 times lower than north of the galaxy center. The star formation rate in the western arm is not significantly different from that of the disk.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4569 as a Virgo SABa galaxy with unusual radio lobes, a hot X-ray halo, and polarized features whose higher gas temperature indicates ram-pressure or wind compression.

quotes.quote: NGC 4569 is an SABa galaxy situated relatively close to the Virgo cluster centre, at a distance of 1 66 (500 kpc in the plane of the sky) from Virgo A. It is an anaemic galaxy showing extended radio lobes unusual for a normal spiral, which can be attributed to previous intense star-formation activity, that has now significantly diminished (Chy zy et al. ). At the southern edge of the western lobe, we can see a ridge of polarized emission that is parallel to the magnetic field (see Fig. 4569radio ).

For NGC 4569, a low resolution map of the X-ray emission, which is more sensitive to extended structures, reveals a large-scale hot gas halo around the galaxy, extending as far as 25 kpc from the disk plane (Fig. 4569xfig ). North of the galaxy, still within the X-ray halo, lies an irregular galaxy, IC 3583, with which NGC 4569 is suspected to interact (Tsch oke et al. and Chy zy et al. ). A slight X-ray extension (the third contour in Fig. 4569xfig at R.A. = 12 ^ h 36 ^ m 38 ^ s , Dec = +13 08 ) towards the region of the radio polarized ridge can be seen (marked in Fig. 4569xfig with a solid line).

The area of the polarized radio ridge in the western radio lobe of NGC 4569 shows a slight increase in the hot gas temperature, which is an evidence of a compression caused by the galactic wind hitting previously stripped material.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4569 as a bright barred Virgo galaxy with transition-type nuclear starburst, central star-formation concentration, bar-like molecular gas, possible bar-driven fueling, and short molecular depletion times.

quotes.quote: NGC 4569 (Messier 90) is a bright SAB(rs)ab galaxy at a distance of 17 Mpc in the Virgo cluster. A large scale bar is seen in NIR images laurikainen02 and is almost aligned with the major axis of the galaxy PA = 15 deg according to jogee05 . The galaxy harbors a nucleus of the transition type type T2 in ho97 , which exhibits a pronounced nuclear starburst activity.

Similar to NGC 3627, NGC 4569 exhibits a centrally concentrated morphology in the \ images and a bar-like distribution in the _ gas maps. This could mean that the action of the bar has transported the gas to the nuclear regions to fuel a mini-starburst episode as found in many nearby spiral galaxies sakamoto99 .
As in NGC 3627, NGC 4569 has a short molecular \ of 1 Gyr, suggesting that the gas is efficiently converted in stars. Within a radius of 0.5 kpc, \ is even smaller assuming values of 0.7--0.9 Gyr.

sources.summary: The paper describes M90 as a very blue Sab LINER with controversial distance, anomalous phase-space position, high inferred luminosity at Virgo, and evidence for bouncing out of the cluster.

quotes.quote: M90 Among the 13 galaxies listed in Table 7, this very blue Sab LINER (e.g. Keel96 ) deviates the most significantly from both the Tolman-Bondi locus (Fig. proj ) and from the color-luminosity diagram (Fig. BmH ). Its distance has been controversial. SKY86 suggest that the Tully-Fisher estimate is an underestimate because the optical rotation curve is still rising to the limit of its measurement (see RWK99 ) and that similarly low distances derived by CCM82 using their line index / bulge luminosity relation may be erroneous because the weakness of the nuclear lines is in contrast with the high metallicity expected from the stellar population of the bulge. M90 is extremely isolated in projected phase space.

Moreover, at the distance of Virgo, M90 would be extremely blue for its luminosity (Fig. BmH ). Finally, the contours of continuum radio emission seen from the NVSS survey taken at the VLA are compressed in the East-Northeast direction, which is opposite to the direction towards the center of the Virgo cluster. This suggests that M90 may be bouncing out of the cluster, which would also explain the galaxy's negative radial velocity.

sources.summary: The paper compares bar-shoulder simulations with NGC 4569, noting its buckling-bar isophotes, major-axis shoulder profile, star-formation contamination, and matched projected morphology.

quotes.quote: Buckling bars and shoulders erwin_debattista16 presented the first direct detection of buckling bars, in NGC 4569 and NGC 3227 through examination of their isophotes. fig:n4569 shows the surface brightness profile along the bar major axis (on the sky, not deprojected) of NGC 4569 ( i=69 , PA =26 ). It is a system with shoulders within the bar (albeit with significant star formation contaminating the x>0 profile).

fig:D8_t42_like_NGC4569_projection demonstrates that it is indeed possible: we show model 4 shortly after peak buckling, at t=4.2 , rotated to the same orientation as NGC 4569. The model presents the same isophotal morphology as NGC 4569, and the SRA recognizes shoulders in this projected profile. Thus, the models are consistent with those of NGC 4569, and the coexistence of shoulders and buckling does not necessarily imply a second buckling.

sources.summary: The paper detects polarized emission in NGC 4569's central disk, polarized double-lobe extension and bridge to IC 3583, and high polarized fractions in the bridge and lobes.

quotes.quote: NGC 4569 (M90) In this moderately inclined spiral galaxy, polarized emission is detected in the central disk region on either side of the minor axis. Polarized intensity declines more slowly to the southwest and more rapidly to the northeast (where the receding major axis is located (PA = 23 ^ , as tabulated in Table table:summary ). Moreover, the spectacular double lobe extension which is oriented roughly along the minor axis is also detected in polarization, particularly along its edges. Even more interesting is the continuum bridge connecting the galaxy to its small companion, IC 3583 (located about 6 arcminutes to the northwest, and visible in both Figures figure:images:a and figure:opt ), which also has a polarized counterpart. The large-scale structures in our map have already been observed at lower spatial resolution and analyzed by chyzy_etal_2006 , who observed this system with the Effelsberg telescope.

sources.summary: The paper presents 4569 as an anemic Virgo spiral with transition/LINER activity, nuclear starburst evidence, strong gas deficiency, and compact bar-like CO emission.

quotes.quote: 4569 (M90) is a member of the Virgo cluster classified as a SAB(rs)ab galaxy. Its nucleus was classified as a `transition object' or weak- [ O i ] LINER by , although the mechanism producing the nuclear activity is still unclear, with several lines of evidence indicating a strong contribution from a nuclear starburst e.g., Keel1996, Ho1997, Maoz1998, Barth2000, Alonso-Herrero2000, Ho2001, Gabel2002 . This galaxy has a bright, point-like nucleus at optical and UV bands that dominates the emission. The HST V-I map show that while the circumnuclear region is dusty, the nucleus itself is apparently unobscured by dust Pogge2000 . 4569 is considered an `anemic spiral' because of its \ deficiency (it has 1/10 of the atomic gas that similar field galaxies have) and small extent compared to its optical size e.g., vandenBergh1976, Cayatte1994, Koopmann2004 .

sources.summary: The paper describes 4569 as a barred Virgo spiral with strong ram-pressure stripping, possible nuclear outflow, a past stripping event, and complex inner molecular-gas kinematics.

quotes.quote: 4569 4569 is a large barred spiral in the Virgo Cluster, notable for exhibiting very strong effects of ram-pressure stripping, with the H i in the disc confined to within 1/3 of the optical radius e.g. Vollmer2004a,Chung2009 . There is also evidence for an extended bipolar outflow from the nuclear regions, possibly due to a recent starburst e.g. Chyzy2006 . Vollmer2009a suggested that the galaxy underwent a major stripping event 300 Myr ago, possibly when it passed closest to M87 in its orbit and experienced a strong degree of ram-pressure stripping. In Fig. f_n4569_im , we show log-spaced isophotes from a Spitzer IRAC1 image from SINGS Kennicutt2003a , along with a close-up of the central regions based on an HST NICMOS2 F160W image. The latter shows the inner boxy region of the bar and a more elliptical structure dominating the inner < 5 arcsec.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 4569 as an LLAGN with bright nuclear PAH emission, likely tied to massive young stars and recent nuclear star formation.

quotes.quote: We note that two LLAGN in our sample (NGC 4419 and NGC 4569) show bright PAH emission in their nuclear CanariCam spectra (see Fig. fig:Spectra and also Section sec:PAH ). Thus their 12 m luminosities might be dominated by emission related to star formation activity as found in a few LLAGN see e.g. Mason2012,Asmus2014,GonzalezMartin2015 .

Two of the LLAGN in our sample (NGC 4569 and NGC 4419) show bright nuclear PAH emission, whereas the other two (NGC 4579 and NGC 4258) do not and present the silicate feature in emission. At least for NGC 4569 the detection of bright 11.3 m PAH emission might be associated with the presence of massive young stars in the nuclear region Maoz1998 .

In any case, for the two LLAGN with PAH emission we can conclude that they have undergone star formation activity in the recent past and that the molecules responsible for the PAH features can survive at distances as close to the nucleus as 20 pc.

sources.summary: M 90 is presented as a bright Virgo spiral undergoing ram-pressure stripping, with LOFAR-detected radio lobes likely powered by a nuclear starburst wind.

quotes.quote: VCC 1690 (NGC 4569, M 90) M 90 is one of the brightest spiral galaxy in the Virgo cluster. As traced by tails of ionized gas to the east Boselli2016Spectacular4569 , it is currently undergoing a ram-pressure stripping event. Observations in the radio continuum revealed symmetric bubbles extending up to 24 kpc perpendicular to the disk ChyZy2006AstrophysicsWind . Our LOFAR map detects both radio lobes and the southwestern ridge of the emission connecting to the star-forming disk that likely resulting from gas and magnetic field compression by the ambient ICM. Since M 90 does not show signs of strong nuclear activity, it was concluded that the lobes are likely powered by a nuclear starburst event.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4569 as a non-starburst barred galaxy with extended molecular gas, disturbed CO kinematics, probable early bar- or tide-driven inflow, and shear that may inhibit star formation.

quotes.quote: The molecular gas shows a wide variety of morphologies (Fig. 2) ranging from relatively axisymmetric annuli or disks (starbursts NGC 4102, NGC 3504, NGC 4536, and non-starbursts NGC 4314), elongated double-peaked and spiral morphologies (starburst NGC 2782 and non-starbursts NGC 3351 and NGC 6951) to extended distributions elongated along the large-scale bar (non-starburst NGC 4569). In NGC 4569, the gas extends out to a large (2 kpc) radius, at a similar P.A. as the large-scale stellar bar (Fig. 2), and shows complex non-circular motions. The optical, NIR, and CO properties of NGC 4569 suggest it is in the early stages of bar-driven/tidally-driven inflow of gas towards the inner kpc.

In the non-starbursts, there are gas-rich regions with no appreciable star formation, for instance, inside the ring of HII regions in NGC 3351 and NGC 4314, at the CO peaks in NGC 6951, and in the extended gas in NGC 4569 (Fig. 2). The gas surface density, although high, is still sub-critical in regions of inhibited star formation, as illustrated for NGC 4314 in Fig. 5e--f. In NGC 4569, the large local shear in the extended gas with large non-circular kinematics along the large-scale stellar bar may inhibit star formation.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 4569 as a post-stripping comparison case, citing prior VLA and Effelsberg data, a western low-surface-density H i arm, and a 300 Myr post-peak passage.

quotes.quote: NGC 4569 Deep VLA and Effelsberg H i data together with a dynamical model of this galaxy are presented in Vollmer et al. (2004a). They discovered a low surface density H i arm in the west of the galaxy, whose velocity field is distinct from that of the overall disk rotation. No H i emission was detected in the Effelsberg H i off center observations. A post--stripping scenario is consistent with the main observed characteristics of NGC 4569. In this scenario the galaxy's closest approach to the cluster center, i.e. peak ram pressure, occured 300 Myr ago.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4569 as a weakly barred Virgo Cluster galaxy affected by ram pressure stripping, with a nuclear outflow, stripped tail, and poorly fitted superlinear star-formation-law indices.

quotes.quote: NGC 4569 NGC 4569 (Messier 90), shown in Figure fig:ngc4569 , is a weakly barred Virgo cluster galaxy which underwent a ram pressure stripping event 300 Myr ago, possibly due to motion relative to the intra-cluster medium vollmer2004 . The galaxy has a nuclear outflow and a stripped tail boselli2016 , which is not visible in the SCUBA-2 data. HARP ^ 12 CO J=3 2 observations of NGC 4569 were presented by wilson2009 and wilson2012 .

The exceptions are NGC 4569, in which the fitted indices are super-linear , but in which the correlation coefficients are relatively poor, and the well-correlated NGC 5055, in which the fitted indices are significantly sub-linear in both cases.

sources.summary: NGC 4569 is the one sample galaxy with no XRB-hosting clusters; because of its bright X-ray completeness limit, the authors exclude its clusters from later analysis.

quotes.quote: One of the galaxies in our sample, NGC 4569, has zero clusters that host XRBs. This may, in part, be due to the fact that NGC 4569 has the brightest X-ray completeness limit of all galaxies in our sample, at 37.8 log L _ X . Indeed, an inspection of X-ray sources with log L _ X 37.8 yields far fewer sources that are spatially coincident with a cluster: only 12 of the 33 XRB clusters have X-ray luminosities brighter than the completeness limit of NGC 4569, with NGC 4826 having none. For this reason, we exclude the clusters from NGC 4569 from the remainder of our analysis, particularly where we calculate statistics using the full star cluster population.

sources.summary: NGC 4569 is described as a Virgo late-type galaxy with the sample's most peculiar color-color distribution, where ram-pressure stripping quenched star formation and erased the YCL.

quotes.quote: Examples of this are NGC 4826, and NGC 4569, which has the most peculiar color-color distribution of the sample. In this context it is notable that NGC 4569 is the brightest late-type galaxy in the Virgo cluster. It experienced a ram pressure stripping event about 300 Myr ago vollmer_ngc_2004,crowl_stellar_2008,boselli_spectacular_2016 which drained the galaxy's gas reservoir and quenched its star formation. This event is reflected in the nearly complete absence of the YCL and unusual MAP in NGC 4569's cluster color-color diagram.

sources.summary: NGC 4569 is reported to have a bright, compact radio-continuum disk aligned with the stellar component, a minor-axis double lobe, and radio overluminosity relative to the radio-FIR correlation.

quotes.quote: A bright radio continuum disk with a small scale-length is detected co-aligned with the stellar component. The most salient feature is a double lobe extension along the minor axis at PA 100 . There are but very few spiral galaxies of this kind, namely 0421+0400 (Beichman et al. ), 0313-192 (Keel et al. ), NGC 2992 (Wehrle Morris ), NGC 3079 (s.a.), NGC 3367 (Garc ia-Barreto et al. ), and NGC 4258 (e.g. van Albada van der Hulst ). They all share the common property of being radio-overluminous in the radio-FIR correlation.

sources.summary: The paper links NGC 4569's north-south CO line-ratio gradient to intracluster-medium interaction and possible active ram pressure affecting molecular-gas density or temperature.

quotes.quote: NGC 4569 shows a large-scale gradient in the gas properties traced by the CO J =3-2/ J =1-0 line ratio, which suggests that its interaction with the intracluster medium is affecting the dense star-forming portion of the interstellar medium directly.
NGC 4569 shows a significant gradient from north to south in its line ratio, from 0.53 at the northern CO J =3-2 peak to just 0.06 at the southern peak.

In NGC 4569, the gradient in the CO J =3-2/ J =1-0 line ratio (and more weakly in the gas depletion time) from south to north suggests that active ram pressure in the northern half of the galaxy may be producing an increase in the average density or temperature of the molecular gas.

sources.summary: The paper places NGC 4569 among galaxies with radial cold-dust temperature dependence, nonzero residual behavior when emissivity is free, and stronger SFR-to-dust-mass proxy behavior.

quotes.quote: When both parameters are fitted simultaneously, the cold dust temperature maps for 10 galaxies (NGC 1097, NGC 1512, NGC 3351, NGC 4254, NGC 4321, NGC 4569, NGC 4579, NGC 4736, NGC 5055, and NGC 7331) exhibit a strong radial dependence.
When is a free parameter, for all galaxies except NGC 3621 and NGC 4569, the residuals as a function of radius are consistent with 0.
The remaining three galaxies, NGC 1512, NGC 4569, and NGC 5055, have 0.4 .

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4569 as a massive, late-type, gas-poor Virgo spiral with Balmer absorption and MIR PAH evidence for recent or ongoing star formation.

quotes.quote: NGC 4569 is the most massive, spiral, late-type, and gas-poor galaxy in the Virgo cluster Bergh76 . This source shows strong Balmer absorption lines which could be indicating SF in the last 1.5 Gyr Ho03 . Dale06 and Mason15 also suggested recent and/or ongoing SF activity based on the detection PAH emission at MIR.

sources.summary: NGC 4569 has a severely truncated H disk, smooth outer spiral arms, a ring of star formation, and a peculiar HII arm with HI counterpart that resembles simulated ICM-stripping morphologies.

quotes.quote: NGC 4569 has smooth, relatively featureless, outer spiral arms and a ring of star formation at 0.3 r_ 24 . A distinct peculiar arm of HII regions, which begins at the H truncation radius and has an HI counterpart (Vollmer et al. 2004), may be extraplanar (Hensler et al. 2003). This arm resembles the one-arm gas morphologies commonly seen in the ICM-ISM interaction simulations of Schulz Struck (2001) and Vollmer et al. (2001).

gmw_00001217.name: NGC 3310
gmw_00001217.overview: NGC 3310 is a distorted spiral galaxy [arXiv:0911.3778, arXiv:1409.7526] undergoing an intense starburst [arXiv:0805.1167] triggered by a recent minor merger with a metal-poor dwarf companion [arXiv:1406.6345, arXiv:1505.00789]. This interaction has produced a circumnuclear star-forming ring [arXiv:0805.1167, arXiv:1811.06335], an exceptionally high star formation efficiency [arXiv:astro-ph/0201478, arXiv:1901.11197], and an overabundance of ultraluminous X-ray sources [arXiv:1812.05958].

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 3310 as an outlier with unusually large outflow speeds, extended star formation within the aperture, high metallicity, and a mature starburst that may host an evolved wind.

quotes.quote: The case of NGC 3310 requires further discussion as it is a significant outlier in most of our plots, with an outflow speed that is larger than expected for its SFR or mass. We note that the very large outflow speeds we see have been confirmed with STIS far-UV spectra of three individual regions in NGC 3310 (Schwartz et al. 2006). Examining the UV morphology of NGC 3310 in Figure f:images1 , we see that NGC 3310 is the only galaxy with significant star formation spread throughout multiple locations in the aperture. Radio HI velocity maps kreg01 of the regions observed by display a spread of almost 200 which could increase the observed width of our absorption features, but should not produce highly blueshifted absorption.

Thus, this is unlikely to explain the large observed outflow speed in NGC 3310. NGC 3310 is included in the young stellar population study of 16 starburst galaxies (11 also in our sample) by pell07 . By fitting the FUV spectra with models of instantaneous bursts, they estimated an age of 18 2 Myr for the starburst in NGC 3310 which was 10 Myrs older than any of the other galaxies in their sample. As the Type II supernovae that drive the galactic wind persist for 40 Myr, the total amount of kinetic energy injected into the wind will increase as the starburst ages. NGC 3310 appears to be a more mature starburst and may therefore have a more evolved wind.

sources.summary: The paper studies NGC 3310 as a low-metallicity starburst spiral, measuring stellar and gas velocity dispersions in its circumnuclear regions and nucleus and finding a perturbed rotation curve with gas and stellar velocities in agreement.

quotes.quote: In this paper we present high-resolution far-red spectra and stellar velocity dispersion measurements ( _ ) along the line of sight for eight CNSFRs and the nucleus of the spiral galaxy NGC 3310. NGC 3310 (UGC 5786, Arp217) is a starburst galaxy classified as an SAB()bc by , with an inclination of the galactic disc of about i 40 2000MNRAS.312....2S . Its coordinates are _ 2000 =10 ^h 38 ^m 45 9, _ 2000 =+53 ^ 30 12 1991trcb.book.....D . These authors derived a distance to the galaxy equal to 15 Mpc, giving a linear scale of 73 pc arcsec ^ 1 . This galaxy is a good example of an overall low metallicity galaxy, with a high rate of star formation and very blue colours.

The rotation curve of NGC 3310 shows a typical S feature, with the presence of some perturbations, particularly near the location of the Jumbo region. The values derived from the gas emission lines and the stellar absorption features are in very good agreement. The position going through the nucleus shows maximum and minimum values more or less coincident with the location of the CNSFRs.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 3310 as a nearby starburst with disturbed merger morphology; its stellar populations vary across the nucleus, and low metallicity suggests metal-poor gas from an interacting companion.

quotes.quote: NGC 3310: it is a well known nearby SB (d=13.2 Mpc) which presents a disturbed morphology elmg02 , suggesting that it recently undergone at least one significant merger. balick81 were the first to propose these merging scenario, suggesting that star formation occurring in the past 10^8 yr has been triggered by a collision with a dwarf galaxy. These authors also states that the well known `arrow' morphology wal67 on the western side comprises the remnant of this dwarf galaxy.

NGC 3310 Our results point out to a predominant young/intermediate age SP southwards (see Fig. hist3310 a ). An increase in the older component can be seen in both flux and mass contribution towards the opposite direction.

R08 also studied the inner 56 pc of NGC 3310 in the NIR part of the spectrum, detecting a dominant 1 Gyr old SP. Similar to the scenario we propose for NGC 1614, the low metallicity SP derived for this source suggests that the gas present in the galaxy interacting with NGC 3310 balick81 should be metal poor, given rise to a metal poorer SP. We found a extinction of A _ v 1.7 mag in the nucleus, ranging from 0.27 mag southwards to 1.5 mag toward the north direction ( A _ v =1.43 0.06).

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 3310 as a distorted, moderately low-metallicity star-forming spiral and minor-merger remnant, mapping its WR regions and comparing integrated properties with GRB hosts.

quotes.quote: In , hereafter Paper I , we characterized the properties (i.e., age, mass, impact on gas metallicity) of the ionizing population in 99 identified regions in the stellar disc of NGC 3310 in order to study the impact of the minor merger on the star formation properties of the remnant. This moderately-low metal galaxy ( 12 + log(O/H) 8.2--8.4 ; Pastoriza93 ; Paper I ) is a very distorted spiral galaxy classified as an SAB()bc by , with strong star formation. With a broad range of stellar masses from about 10 ^4 to 6 10^ 6 and a narrow age range of about 2.5-5 Myr, WR signatures are expected to be observed at least for the most massive population. These features were early mentioned in the intense star-forming region known as Jumbo in , and in several regions in Paper I . In the present study, we have identified a sample of 18 regions with measurable WR features in their spectra distributed across the disc of NGC 3310.

We have thus derived the integrated luminosity, mass and metallicity of NGC 3310 and have compared these relations with those obtained in H10. First of all, the adopted oxygen abundance for NGC 3310 corresponds to 12 + log(O/H) = 8.25 0.10 since, according to Paper I , this is the typical abundance measured at one effective radius and in any case the metallicity gradient in NGC 3310 is rather flat.
There is clear evidence that NGC 3310 collided with a poor-metal dwarf galaxy Balick81,Schweizer88,Kregel01 , producing a minor merger event.

sources.summary: NGC 3310 is treated as a nearby starburst shaped by minor mergers; its spectrum is fit by diffuse plus broken power-law components, with excess ULX emission plausibly tied to low metallicity.

quotes.quote: NGC 3310 is a closer (19.8 Mpc) starburst system with a SFR that is a factor of 10 times lower than NGC 3256 ( L_ IR 4 10^ 10   L_ ; Sanders \ 2003); however, given its relatively low stellar mass ( M_ 10^ 10   ), this system has a comparable and perhaps larger sSFR than NGC 3256 (see Fig. 1). The system contains evidence for a minor-merger that has triggered a vigorous, young ( 2.5--5 Myr old) circumnuclear star-forming ring with a 20--30 arcsec ( 2 kpc) diameter (e.g., Balick Heckman 1981; D az \ 2000; Elmegreen \ 2002; Chandar \ 2005; Miralles-Caballero \ 2014). The most recent minor merger was inferred to occur in the last 30 Myr (Elmegreen \ 2002; de Grijs \ 2003a), and is likely to be one of several past interactions with small metal-poor dwarf galaxies (e.g., Wehner \ 2006), as evidence for starburst activity as old as 100 Myr is apparent in the star clusters (Meurer 2000).

For NGC 3310, we find that the diffuse component plus a broken power-law model provides the best fit to the data; no additional absorbed hotter component was required to fit the spectrum, as we do not see clear evidence for an Fe-line. The best-fitting slopes ( _1 2.1 and _2 2.7 ) and energy break ( E_ break 4.7  keV) are within the range of values found for ULXs (e.g., Gladstone \ 2009). In contrast to NGC 3256, which had strong emission features related to hot gas out to E 3  keV, we find that the overall spectrum of NGC 3310 above 1 keV is dominated by the point-source population.

The relatively low metallicity in NGC 3310 is the result of the recent cannibalization of a low-metallicity dwarf galaxy that triggered the current star-formation event. It is therefore plausible that the lower metallicity of NGC 3310 is the driving mechanism behind the excess of ULXs per unit SFR in this galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper identifies a central X-ray source in NGC 3310, detects a narrow 6.4 keV feature, and argues this may be the first X-ray evidence for AGN activity in the starburst galaxy.

quotes.quote: NGC 3310 More than ten discrete X-ray sources, embedded in diffuse emission, can be seen in the ACIS image of this galaxy. One of the sources corresponds extremely well with the optical centre, and has been isolated for further study here. As this isolated source shows 1538 net counts, a high-quality spectrum can be extracted. Up to 7 keV, the continuum is well defined, making a prominent emission feature clearly noticeable at 6.4 keV at a level of 2 above the local continuum. The spectrum ( fig:3310spec ) is well fitted with a power law, a thermal component and moderate intrinsic absorption.

This galaxy (Arp 217), is also an M 82-class starburst, of type SAB()bc pec 1991trcb.book.....D . Its peculiar optical morphology and tidal debris 2005ApJ...618L..21W suggest that the starburst has been triggered by a merger with either a gas-rich dwarf companion 1981A&A....96..271B,1996ApJ...473L..21S or a similar-size disk 2001A&A...376...59K . The nuclear spectrum has been explained by using only photoionisation by hot stars 1993RMxAA..26..124P . Until now there has been no indication of AGN activity in any wavelength band.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC3310 as a nearby Sbc peculiar galaxy with arc-and-bow morphology interpreted as merger debris, and HRI data showing extended X-ray emission about 3 kpc across.

quotes.quote: NGC3310 is a well studied nearby galaxy of Sbc()pec type (Mulder 1996).

The most prominent is an arc and bow structure which extends for 100'' to the North West of the centre of the galaxy. Bertolla and Sharp (1984) propose that the arc could be part of a spiral arm and the bow is the remnant of an old jet. Balick and Heckman (1985) suggest that the whole structure is the remnant of a collision between the galaxy and a dwarf companion. This is in agreement with the suggestion of Mulder (1985) who characterize the system as a young merger based on the anomalies found in its rotation curve.

This figure clearly shows that the X-ray source has a radius of 0.5 arcmin (at larger radii the emission from the galaxy begins to blend with the profile of AR-Lac, within the 2 error bars). Although this is a somewhat arbitrary point at which to define the extent of the emission, it gives a lower limit to the source size. At the distance of NGC3310 this corresponds to 3 kpc.

sources.summary: NGC3310 is described as a disturbed face-on spiral with merger evidence, a circumnuclear star-forming ring, high star formation, and an excess of ULXs.

quotes.quote: There is strong evidence that the face-on spiral galaxy NGC3310 has undergone some kind of close encounter or merger in the past. It displays significant evidence of past disturbance, a circum-nuclear ring of star formation and evidence of multiple minor merger events (see Figure fig:detection ). A number of compact radio sources are known within the central 15 arcsec of NGC 3310 including several historical supernova remnants. The galaxy exhibits a high rate of ongoing star formation ( >5M_ yr^ 1 ). Various scenarios to explain NGC3310's morphology have been suggested, including a major merger , minor merger , or multiple minor mergers , and it is possible that the cannibalised remains of any interloper may remain in the disk of NGC3310.

Recent simultaneous Chandra/NuSTAR observations have shown that NGC3310 has an overabundance of ULXs thought to be due to the relatively low metallicity of the young stellar population resulting in an excess of luminous X-ray binaries . Recent comparison of archival X-ray (XMM/Chandra) and multi-frequency radio (VLA, MERLIN, EVN) data has resulted in the localisation of a radio source with three ULXs in a spiral arm of NGC3310.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 3310 as a pilot starburst galaxy, emphasizing its HST data, high star-formation efficiency, disturbed morphology, recent interaction, and central star-cluster candidates.

quotes.quote: As a pilot case for our study of systematic effects introduced by the choice of any particular passband combination we decided to concentrate on NGC 3310. This choice was made because of (i) the large and homogenous set of observations of this galaxy available in the HST archive, and (ii) the large number of prior extragalactic star cluster studies available in the literature to which we can compare our results. NGC 3310 is a local ( D = 12.5 Mpc), very active starburst galaxy with high global star formation efficiency. A number of morphological and kinematical peculiarities suggest that NGC 3310 was affected by a major gravitational disturbance, which led to high, possibly sustained, star formation rates in the past 100 Myr (cf. Balick Heckman 1981).

The bar-driven star formation scenario suggested by Conselice et al. (2000), combined with the recent infall of a companion galaxy, is the currently most attractive scenario for this: it provides a natural explanation for the low metallicity observed in the star-forming knots near the centre, while it also explains why we observe concentrated star formation in star clusters or very luminous H ii regions in a tightly-wound ring-like structure surrounding the centre. All of the observational evidence points at very recent star formation in the star clusters and H ii regions, and a time since the interaction of 10^ 7-8 yr (e.g., Balick Heckman 1981, Smith et al. 1996). In the galaxy's central 22 55 22 65 region covered by all of the WFPC2 and NICMOS observations available in the HST archive, we detected 289 point-like (or marginally extended) star cluster candidates brighter than 4 the .m.s. noise of the background in both of the F606W and F814W source lists.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes NGC 3310 as a distorted starburst spiral affected by a minor merger, finding flat gas abundances and young ionizing populations across its disc.

quotes.quote: NGC 3310 is a very distorted spiral galaxy classified as an SAB()bc by , with strong star formation. The star formation activity, especially a vigorous circumnuclear star-forming ring, has been studied over a wide range of wavelengths from X-rays to radio (e.g. Balick81,Pastoriza93,Zezas98,Diaz00a,Elmegreen02,Hagele10b,Hagele13,Mineo12 ). Several studies support that this galaxy collided with a poor metal dwarf galaxy, which caused a burst of star formation Balick81,Schweizer88,Smith96 . It has also been suggested that NGC 3310 has actually experienced several interactions with small galaxies Wehner06 .

With a sample of over 100 regions we observe a rather flat abundance gradient in the disc of NGC 3310 out to about 10 kpc away from the nucleus. We can thus confirm the evidence that the minor merger event had a substantial impact on metal mixing in the remnant.

sources.summary: The paper presents Chandra observations of NGC 3310, finding 27 discrete X-ray sources, 14 ULXs concentrated in star-forming regions, and a disturbed merger-linked starburst morphology.

quotes.quote: firstpage - lastpage We present results from Chandra observations of the X-ray starburst galaxies NGC 3310 and NGC 2276. We detect 27 discrete sources in NGC 3310, and 19 discrete sources in NGC 2276 with luminosities above 1.0 10^ 38 \ erg \ s^ 1 . The majority of the sources have photon indices of 1.7 2.0 , typical for X-ray binaries. Both galaxies have large numbers of ultra-luminous X-ray sources (ULXs; sources with L(0.3-10.0 \ keV)>10^ 39 \ erg \ s^ 1 ), 14 for NGC 3310 concentrated on the circumnuclear star-forming ring and north spiral arm and 11 for NGC 2276 with the brighter ones on the west side of the galaxy which is compressed due to harassment by the intra-group medium it is moving into.

NGC 3310, which is among the most luminous star-forming galaxies in the local Universe, shows a disturbed morphology, which is possibly the result of a recent merger 30 Myr ago; elmegreen02,degrijs03a with a low metallicity dwarf galaxy which triggered a circumnuclear star forming ring of about 20 arcsec. The galaxy also shows two distinct spiral arms, one on the north and one on the south.

sources.summary: The paper uses HST photometry of NGC 3310's central star clusters to test SED-based age and mass estimates, describing its circumnuclear starburst ring and mostly subsolar cluster metallicities.

quotes.quote: Our basic cluster samples were taken from the following sources: NGC 3310, a nearby spiral galaxy exhibiting dominant star cluster formation in a circumnuclear starburst ring. This galaxy was covered by HST by the largest possible wavelenth range. The full set of eight broad-band stmag magnitudes for the 300 clusters in the galaxy's centre (located in the starburst ring and outside of it), from F300W (``mid-UV'') to F205W were published and analysed in de Grijs et al. (2003a,). We selected 17 of these clusters for the present analysis, all with well-determined, high-quality photometry in the entire set of available passbands (see Table ngc3310.tab ).

Based on the multi-dimensional analysis presented in Fig. n3310ages.fig a, most (70 per cent) of the NGC 3310 clusters in our current sample are characterised by metallicities Z 0.01 , with the remainder split evenly between solar metallicity ( Z = 0.02 ) and Z 0.05 , see Fig. metallicities.fig . Support for these metallicity estimates is provided by the unusually low (subsolar) metallicity found independently in star-forming regions surrounding the nucleus of NGC 3310, while the nucleus itself appears to have solar metallicity (e.g., Heckman Balick 1980, Puxley, Hawarden Mountain 1990, Pastoriza et al. 1993).

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 3310 as a nearby starburst disk with a circumnuclear star-forming ring, multiple recent bursts, and uncertain X-ray emission origins.

quotes.quote: NGC 3310 NGC 3310 (Arp 217) is a nearby disk galaxy undergoing intense starburst activity. It has been the subject of many studies across the electromagnetic spectrum see and references therein elmg02 . This galaxy has a circumnuclear ring of star formation with angular diameter of 713 to 770 pc assuming a diameter between 8 '' to 12 '' from and a distance of 13.24 M pc from NED elmg02 . NGC 3310 hosts a spectacular starburst, as seen in numerous H ii regions.
As can be observed in Fig. pop and in Tab. sps , NGC 3310 displays an intense starburst activity, with four bursts of star formation.

sources.summary: For NGC 3310, the paper finds stellar and H gas velocity dispersions in relatively good agreement and notes that its low-metallicity regions do not show the component ionization differences seen in the other galaxies.

quotes.quote: We have analysed the results derived by H07, H09 and H10 using high spectral resolution data of 17 CNSFRs belonging to three barred spiral galaxies: NGC 2903, NGC 3310 and NGC 3351. The stellar velocity dispersions were measured from the CaT lines at 8494, 8542, 8662 . The gas velocity dispersions have been measured by Gaussian fits to the H 4861 \ and the [ O iii ] 5007 \ emission lines. Stellar velocity dispersions range between 31 and 80 km s ^ 1 . In the case of NGC 2903 and NGC 3351 ( _ between 37 and 64, and between 39 and 66 km s ^ 1 , respectively) these values are about 25 km s ^ 1 larger than those measured for the gas from the H emission line using a single Gaussian fit. For NGC 3310 the stellar (between 31 and 80 km s ^ 1 ) and gas (H ) velocity dispersions are in relatively good agreement, the stellar ones being marginally larger.

The gas velocity dispersion derived from a single Gaussian fit for the [ O iii ] line in NGC 3310 is also comparable to the stellar velocity dispersion but, contrary to what was found in the other two galaxies, its value is very similar also to that derived using H . The best Gaussian fits for H , however, involved two different components for the gas: a ``broad component'' with a velocity dispersion similar to that measured for the stars for NGC 2903 and NGC 3351, and about 20 km s ^ 1 larger than the stellar one for NGC 3310, and a ``narrow component'' with a velocity dispersion lower than the stellar one by about 30 km s ^ 1 . This narrow component shows a relatively constant value for the two emission lines (H and [ ] ), close to 23 km s ^ 1 (with scatter errors of 1.1 and 1.4 , respectively) for all the studied CNSFRs. The [ O iii ] /H ratio distribution shows that the two systems are clearly segregated for the high-metallicity regions of NGC 2903 and NGC 3351, with the narrow component having the lowest excitation. In the regions of the low-metallicity galaxy, NGC 3310, these two components and those values derived using the single Gaussian fit are very similar.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC3310 as a local UV-bright starburst with exceptionally high star formation efficiency and a molecular gas cycling timescale below 0.1 Gyr.

quotes.quote: The shortest gas cycling timescale corresponds to galaxies with exceptional SFE like NGC3310 (see Fig. 3). Star formation rates in these galaxies are so high, compared to their gas supplies, that the gas cycling timescale is _ H_ 2 0.1 Gyr.

Finally, there are some galaxies for which the SFE is so high that the gas cycling timescale is _ H_ 2 0.1 Gyr, e.g. the starburst NGC3310. NGC3310 is one of the best examples of a local UV-bright starburst (see Conselice et al. 2000 and reference therein). The exceptional high SFE of this galaxy indicates that its interstellar medium is truly affected by an extraordinary star formation event.

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 3310 as an intensely star-forming local galaxy with tidal debris, a closed stellar loop, and photometric evidence that recent accretion drives its evolution.

quotes.quote: NGC 3310 is a local galaxy with an intense, ongoing starburst thought to result from a merger with a companion galaxy. It has several known tidal features in the northwest and southern regions around the main galactic disc, as well as a closed, tidal loop emerging from the eastern side of the disc and rejoining in the north. This loop appears to be distinct from the rest of the shells surrounding NGC 3310 and is the first of its kind to be detected in a starburst galaxy. In this work, we present UBVR photometry to faint surface brightness levels of this debris network, and we explore various strategies for modelling NGC 3310's disc and subtracting its contribution from each region of debris. We then compare these photometric results with the GALEV spectral synthesis models, and find possible material from the intruder galaxy, suggesting that the recent accretion of several small galaxies is driving the evolution of NGC 3310.

sources.summary: The paper flags NGC 3310 as an extreme high-SFE case, attributing the value to an interaction-driven starburst and low metallicity that may make molecular gas underestimated.

quotes.quote: In figure 4, NGC 3310 shows extremely high SFE ( > 10^ 8 yr ^ 1 ). This is partly because of an intense starburst likely caused by a recent minor interaction ( ), which yields an elevated SFR. In addition, NGC 3310 shows a moderately low metallicity (0.2 -- 0.4 Z_ ; ), which suggests higher X_ CO than the standard value and thus the underestimation of _ H_2 . For these reasons, such an extremely high SFE is observed in NGC 3310.

sources.summary: The paper compares NGC 3310 with VV 114, noting elevated soft and hard X-ray SED normalization and arguing this may reflect lower effective gas-phase metallicity.

quotes.quote: The SED of NGC 3310 likewise appears elevated in the soft band relative to the solar metallicity galaxies, and furthermore displays an even higher normalization than VV 114 at energies > 1 keV (enhancement by a factor of 8--25 relative to solar). This enhancement factor for NGC 3310 is more consistent with theoretical predictions for an XRB population at 0.1 Z_ , as the theoretical simulations suggest a nearly order of magnitude difference between the galaxy-integrated /SFR between XRB populations at solar and 0.1 Z_ Linden2010,Fragos2013 . However, the reported gas phase metallicity for NGC 3310 is closer to 0.3 Z_ dG2003,Eng2008 , which is only slightly lower than the half solar value reported for VV 114. It is possible that the simulated SEDs based on theoretical predictions presented in Figure  fig:sed_comp , although simplified, are actually constraining the metallicity of NGC 3310, indicating that the galaxy may have a gas phase metallicity closer to the low end ( 0.2 Z_ ) of the measured uncertainty range Eng2008 .

sources.summary: The paper describes UGC 5786 as an exceptionally strong emission-line galaxy, with Br-gamma detected across nearly the whole galaxy and high-extinction nuclear regions.

quotes.quote: \ detections Out of the 22 galaxies observed, only eight show any sign of \ detections after continuum subtraction. In the cases of six of these, there are 1-3 detections of small, isolated regions. The \ image of UGC 5786, however, contained a detection of nearly the whole galaxy, with eight strong regions that particularly stand out (Fig. fig:u5786_brg_ha , left image). UGC 5786 is a very strong \ emitter (Fig. fig:u5786_brg_ha , right image), and has the highest observed \ flux in the \ Galaxy Survey.

sources.summary: The atlas treats NGC 3310 as an individually observed active or starburst galaxy, summarizing its prior classification and near-infrared continuum, emission-line, and absorption properties.

quotes.quote: NGC 3310 . One of the four starburst galaxies of our sample, NGC 3310, is thought to have merged with a companion galaxy wg05 . It has been extensively studied in the UV and optical regions because of its peculiar properties. It is one of the bluest spiral galaxies in the vau91 catalog and its far-infrared luminosity (L _ ir = 1.1 10 ^ 10 L ) indicates that the starburst in this galaxy is comparable to that of the ``prototypical'' starburst galaxy M82 smi96 . Our JHK spectrum of this object (see Fig. indiv3 ) displays a continuum that decreases in flux with wavelength.

sources.summary: The paper cites prior IFS work locating NGC 3310 WR-rich regions, reporting WR features in 18 disk regions and WC-star limits of 5-20% of WN stars.

quotes.quote: In particular, (henceforth, MC14b) used data from the PPAK Integral Field Spectroscopy (IFS) Nearby Galaxies Survey PINGS Rosales-Ortega10 to locate the WR-rich regions in the nearby spiral galaxy NGC 3310.
Among the almost 100 regions identified throughout the disc of NGC 3310 by , MC14b reported the detection of WR features in 18 of them.
MC14b estimated an upper-limit to the number of WC stars in NGC 3310 to be in the range 5--20 of the number of WN stars.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 3310 as a starburst transition toward AGN activity whose enormous starburst followed accretion of a dwarf companion, with high-resolution radio observations of cosmic rays.

quotes.quote: NGC 3079 and NGC 3310 are starburst which can be considered as transition phases towards AGN (active galactic nucleus) activity. NGC 3079 exhibits a focused wind emerging from its centre and giving rise to a `figure-8' bow-shock structure seen in the nonthermal radio continuum 1983ApJ...273L..11D, 1988ApJ...326..574D . NGC 3310 has recently swallowed one of its dwarf companion galaxies see, e.g., 2001A&A...376...59K , this having led to the enormous starburst. High-resolution radio continuum observations were performed by 1986ApJ...304...82D , with the aim to study the distribution of cosmic rays in this galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 3310 through SDSS surface photometry, noting its visible disk structure and using its radial profile features to classify the galaxy structure.

quotes.quote: NGC 3310 : J103845.8+533012 The galaxy is clearly disturbed with a shell-like structure in the outer disk, which is probably the result of a recent merger with a smaller galaxy . The inner region is also slightly asymmetric, but still used for centering. The inner region, corresponding to a high surface brightness, tightly wound spiral, inside the break at 50 , is curved starting at 20 .

gmw_00001218.name: VCC128
gmw_00001218.overview: VCC 128 is a dark matter-dominated dwarf spheroidal [arXiv:0806.1951] or dwarf elliptical galaxy located in the Virgo cluster [arXiv:0806.4186]. The galaxy harbors a binary nucleus [arXiv:0806.1951, arXiv:0806.4186], though no radio emission has been detected from the nuclei themselves [arXiv:0806.4186].

sources.summary: The paper treats VCC128 as a Virgo dwarf elliptical with a binary nucleus and tests whether its putative central black hole produces detectable X-band radio emission.

quotes.quote: Using archival HST/WFPC2 data, we found the Virgo cluster dwarf elliptical galaxy VCC128 to harbor a binary nucleus, a feature that is usually interpreted as the observable signature of a stellar disk orbiting a central massive black hole. db06 estimated its mass M _ 6 10^6-5 10^7 M _ . One of the most robust means of verifying the existence of a BH is radio continuum and/or X-ray emission, however because of the deficiency of gas in dEs, radio continuum emission forms the best option here. We have tried to detect the X-band radio emission coming from the putative black hole in VCC128 when it accretes gas from the surrounding ISM. While we made a positive 4 detection of a point source 4.63 '' south-west of the binary nucleus, no statistically significant evidence for emission associated with the nuclei themselves was detected.

sources.summary: The paper uses VCC 128 as a binary-nucleus dwarf spheroidal test case, arguing its nuclei can stall if they create a dark-matter core from a moderately shallow cusp.

quotes.quote: We focus on one particularly interesting binary nucleus system, the dwarf spheroidal galaxy VCC 128 which is dark matter dominated at all radii. We show that its nuclei would rapidly coalesce within a few million years if it has a central dark matter cusp slope steeper than ^ 1 . However, if its initial dark matter cusp is slightly shallower than a log slope of 0.75 at 0.1 of the virial radius, then the sinking nuclei naturally create a core equal to their observed separation and stall.

gmw_00001219.name: IC 5249
gmw_00001219.overview: IC 5249 is an edge-on, superthin Sd spiral galaxy located approximately 30 to 32 Mpc away [arXiv:1003.3110, arXiv:1303.2463]. It features a differentially rotating disk with a steeply rising rotation curve [arXiv:1003.3110, arXiv:1303.2463] and exhibits a slightly asymmetric position-velocity structure [arXiv:1303.2463, arXiv:1608.05559].

sources.summary: The paper presents ESO109-G021 as a superthin Sd galaxy with differential rotation, improved ATCA imaging, radial resolution, marginal vertical flaring detection, and limited signal-to-noise.

quotes.quote: ESO109-G021 is a superthin Sd galaxy with B-band stellar scalelength-to-scaleheight ratio of 11 vdkjvkf2001 . The rotation curve was initially found to be in solid body rotation by abeetal1999 , however vdkjvkf2001 pointed out such a rotation curve was inconsistent with the double-horned \ spectrum (see Fig. fig:ch2-eso109-g021-combi-plot ). By modelling the XV diagram vdkjvkf2001 show that ESO109-G21 is differentially rotating. To improve signal-to-noise and recovery of image structure we obtained two additional 12 hour synthesis observations at the ATCA and added these to the earlier observation by Carignan which was used in the rotation curve analysis by abeetal1999 and vdkjvkf2001 .

The distance of ESO109-G21 is about 30 Mpc distant so the \ beam is relatively large, 1179 pc. The \ column density map in Fig. fig:ch2-eso109-g021-combi-plot shows the galaxy to be well-resolved in the radial direction, while perpendicular to the plane the resolution is just sufficient to detect flaring in the extreme velocity channel maps. Comparison of the XV map in Fig. fig:ch2-eso109-g021-combi-plot with that measured by vdkjvkf2001 shows significantly improved structure recovery. However the peak signal-to-noise of the \ channel maps is 10.1 , which will make measurement of the \ flaring quite difficult.

sources.summary: The paper presents IC 5249 as the most distant sample galaxy, still resolved in H I, with temperature and flux plateaus, asymmetric position-velocity structure, and a steeply rising rotation curve.

quotes.quote: IC 5249 is the most distant galaxy in our sample, at a distance of 32 Mpc. The galaxy is only 5 arcminutes. This is still enough to fully resolve the H I disk in both width and height.

The maximum temperatures exhibit a plateau between 80-100 K, over the radius -100 and 100 arcseconds, most of the galaxy (figure fig:IC5249-maxtemperature ). Another plateau is visible in integrated flux along the major axis ( fig:IC5249-PV ), wherein over that same region the intensity is seen to hover around 0.06 Jy km/s / arcsec. The PV-diagram itself is slightly asymmetric, with the higher velocity side having more mass than the low velocity side. vanderKruit2001A have previously analyzed the rotation curve of the galaxy, using H I and H . They find the galaxy to exhibit a steep rising rotation curve, where-after it flattens out to v_ max 100 km/s.

sources.summary: The paper confirms prior rotation-curve results for IC 5249, notes possible asymmetry, and finds that self-absorption raises the inferred H I mass by about a third.

quotes.quote: IC 5249 The structure and kinematics of galaxy IC 5249 have been previously analyzed by Abe1999 , vanderKruit2001A and OBrien2010C . Abe1999 report a linearly rising rotation curve to about 100 km/s at 17 kpc. The data was re-analyzed by vanderKruit2001A , who reports a steeper inner rotation curve that flattens out to 105 km/s at 10 kpc and remains flat throughout the remaining disc. This rotation curve was confirmed by OBrien2010C .

Our fits with both the optically thin model and the self-absorption model confirms their results (Figure fig:IC5249-velocitydispersion-thin ). We however note that rotation curves differs between both sides, so the galaxy might be asymmetric.

Comparing the total masses of both types of fits, we find that the optically thin model has a total mass of 4.8 0.2 10^9 M _ . The self-absorption model finds a total of 7.8 0.8 10^9 M _ . This would put the hidden mass fraction at about a third.

sources.summary: The paper describes IC 5249 as an edge-on superthin with high axial ratio, moderate rotation and dark halo, finding thin and thick stellar disc dispersions and nearly constant HI dispersion.

quotes.quote: IC 5249 IC 5249 is an edge-on galaxy observed at an inclination i=89^ abe1999observation with axial ratio b/a =10.2 . The galaxy has an asymptotic rotational velocity of about 112 kms ^ 1 . The M_ dyn / M_ HI =9.5 and M_ dyn /L_ B =9.5 ( yock1999observation , van2001kinematics ). Mass modelling of IC 5249 indicated the presence of a dark matter halo with R_ =2.9 kpc and _ 0 =0.026 M_ pc^ 3 banerjee2016mass .

IC5249 Our calculations show that the central value of the stellar vertical velocity dispersion of the thick disc is 20.6 0.6 kms ^ 1 , which falls off exponentially with scale length (2.2 0.2) R_ d1 . The same for the thin disc is found to be 9.3 0.4 kms ^ 1 with an exponential fall-off scale length of (7.5 0.2) R_ d2 . At R > R_ d1 , the density averaged vertical velocity dispersion converges to the value of the vertical velocity dispersion profile of the thick disc, which is hot, diffuse and extended. The central value of HI dispersion is 12.4 0.5 kms ^ 1 with _ HI = - 0.9 0.1 and _ HI =-0.04 0.01, thus indicating that the HI dispersion remains almost constant with R .

sources.summary: The paper reports a high-quality fit for IC 5249, scale length and height measurements consistent with earlier work, and describes it as a superthin galaxy.

quotes.quote: IC 5249 The overall quality of the fit is very good. The scale length is h_ R ,* =6828 340 pc, while the scale height is h_ z ,* =242 20 pc. Similar results have also been found in previous studies. Carignan1983 analysed the galaxy in the B-band and distinguished two components wityh scale lengths 18 kpc and 2.5 kpc. Wainscoat1986 confirmed these results using H, I and K-band data.

This galaxy is an example of a so-called superthin galaxy. vanderKruit2001A , who found a scale lenght of about 7 kpc attributed this to the relatively long scale length combined with the low (face-on) surface brightness.

sources.summary: The paper models IC 5249's dark matter halo, finding large uncertainties because the useful rotation-curve and vertical-force measurements begin far from the galaxy center.

quotes.quote: IC 5249 We have been moderately successful in modelling the dark matter halo of galaxy IC 5249. The decomposition of both the optically thin and self-absorbing results is shown in Figure fig:IC5249decomposition . As is clear from the figure, the uncertainties in the stellar halo contribution, and subsequently the dark matter halo contribution are quite severe. This is mostly due to the fact that the measurements of both the vertical force gradient, as well as the rotation curve, start relatively far out (near 5.0-5.5 kpc). The data of the inward parts of the galaxy were too uncertain for a reliable measurement of the tracers.

sources.summary: The paper studies IC 5249 as a superthin edge-on spiral, measuring its HI rotation, disk structure, surface brightness, stellar velocity dispersions, and unusually extended low-surface-brightness disk.

quotes.quote: We present spectroscopic observations of the stellar motions in the disk of the superthin edge-on spiral galaxy IC 5249 and re-analyse synthesis observations of the HI. We find that the HI rotation curve rises initially to about 90-100 km/s, but contrary to the conclusion of Abe et al. (1999) flattens well before the edge of the optical disk. Over most part of the optical disk we have been able to establish that the (tangential) stellar velocity dispersion is 25-30 km/s.

The very thin appearance of IC 5249 on the sky is the result of a combination of a low (face-on) surface brightness, a long scalelength and a a sharp truncation at only about 2.5 scalelengths. In terms of the ratio of the radial scalelength and the vertical scaleheight of the disk, IC 5249 is not very flat; in fact it is slightly fatter than the disk of our Galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper finds IC 5249 does not require a thick dust disk: although its thick optical depth exceeds the thin component, its extended feature can be explained by the standard PSF wing.

quotes.quote: However, it is found that the rest three targets (IC 5249, NGC 24, and NGC 4173) do not necessarily need a thick dust disk to model the ultraviolet (UV) halo, because its contribution is too small and the UV halo may be caused by the wing part of the point spread function.
IC 5249 is the only target whose _B^ thick is greater than _B^ thin , and its IC 5249's h_s is much larger than others .

The data profiles of IC 5249 and NGC 24 seen in Figure fig-diff show hints of extended, decreasing wing-like features, but with intensities comparable to or lower than the background upper bound. The feature for IC 5249 can be explained by the standard PSF wing solely.

gmw_00001220.name: ESO 115-G021
gmw_00001220.overview: ESO 115-G021 is a nearby, slow-rotating dwarf galaxy [arXiv:1303.2463, arXiv:1608.05559] classified as an SBdm [arXiv:1303.2463] or Scd type [arXiv:1003.3110]. It features a symmetric, well-resolved neutral hydrogen disk [arXiv:1003.3110] with visible flaring [arXiv:1003.3110] and an associated gas cloud that has negligible stellar content [arXiv:1303.2463].

sources.summary: The paper describes ESO 115-G021 as a nearby, well-resolved SBdm galaxy with an associated H I gas cloud, an asymmetric warped disk, solid-body rotation, and localized star formation.

quotes.quote: ESO 115-G021 is a nearby SBdm galaxy at a distance of only 4.9 Mpc. A lot of data was available, totaling to 83.5 hours. Due to this, it is a very well resolved galaxy. The plane of the galaxy is over 80 beams wide and the galaxy is 10 beams in height (see figure fig:ESO115-G021-DSS ).

This close proximity to the galaxy in both velocity and distance indicates the gas is associated with the galaxy. The cloud is invisible in the DSS, I and V bands paper IV, indicating that it has negligible stellar content. The gas cloud has a H I mass of 3.75 10^5 M _ . The PV-diagram (figure fig:ESO115-G021-PV ) shows an asymmetry, with the high velocity side being more extended than the low velocity side.

sources.summary: The paper reports unstable halo modeling for ESO 115-G021, with a problematic vertical force gradient and best-fit oblate halos under both self-absorbing and optically thin assumptions.

quotes.quote: ESO 115-G021 We have not been very successful in modelling ESO 115-G021. As can be seen from the results in Figure fig:ESO115decomposition , the observed vertical force gradient has a nearly flat slope near the inner parts of the galaxy ( R<4 kpc). This is problematic to fit to, as we always expect the vertical force gradient to get increasingly strong near the inner parts. We have attempted fitting only beyond R=3.5 kpc, but this left only 2 kpc in which we could fit the data, which did not result in a stable fit. Smoothing was applied on the input parameters, but this did not improve the quality of the observed vertical force gradient.

The self-absorbing model of the galaxy results in a dark matter core density _0 of 0.015^ +0.001 _ 0.001 M _ /pc ^3 , a scale length R_c of 3.02^ +0.03 _ 0.08 kpc and an oblate shape of q=0.5^ +0.1 _ 0.1 . The optically thin model produces a more massive central core density _0=0.022^ +0.004 _ 0.001 M _ /pc ^3 , a shorter scale length of R_c=2.30^ +0.10 _ 0.15 and a less oblate halo shape q=0.7^ +0.1 _ 0.1 .

sources.summary: The paper describes ESO 115-G021 as a symmetric, slow-rotating galaxy with a well-fit rotation curve and about a quarter of its H I hidden by self-absorption.

quotes.quote: ESO 115-G021 Galaxy ESO 115-G021 is a symmetric, slow rotating galaxy which we have managed to fit well (see Figures fig:ESO115-G021-velocitydispersion-thin and fig:ESO115-G021-velocitydispersion-thick ). The rotation curve rises from an initial 30 km/s in the inner parts almost linearly to 65 km/s around 8 km/s. The only other analysis of the rotation curve of this galaxy is by OBrien2010C , who report a very similar rotation curve.
The thickness of this galaxy is remarkably well behaved and consistent between both fits. Starting near 700 pc in the inner parts, it rises linearly to 1.5 kpc at a radius of 8 kpc.
Comparing the total masses, the optically thin model yields 5.6 0.2 10^9 M _ and 7.2 0.1 10^9 M _ for the self-absorption model. Thus about a quarter of the is hidden in the model.

sources.summary: The paper reports ESO115-G021 as a quiescent Scd galaxy with a symmetric HI disk, resolved vertical structure, visible flaring, and no barred-gas kinematics.

quotes.quote: ESO115-G021 This Scd galaxy extends to a maximum radial extent of 445 \ or 9.7 kpc (assuming a Hubble flow distance of 4.5 kpc inferred from the v_ Gal ). The scalelength of the projected distribution, h_X , is 209 \ or 4.6 kpc, with the edge of the \ disk at 2.1h_X . This \ column density map (Fig. fig:ch2-eso115-g021-combi-plot ) displays a very symmetric disk which appears to be relatively quiescent with no obvious \ extensions above and below the inner disk.

The vertical structure is well-resolved, extending to a height of 1.7 kpc, equivalent to 8.5 FWHM beamwidths or 2.9 h_z , where h_z is the projected scaleheight of 26.7 arcsec or 583 pc . The flaring of the vertical \ distribution can clearly be seen in channel maps at 459, 469, 479, 548, 558, 568 , particularily on the eastern side where the flatter domain of the rotation curve is more extended (as inferred from the XV map shown in Fig. fig:ch2-eso115-g021-combi-plot ). The XV diagram does not display barred gas kinematics; however a small galaxy like ESO115-G021 with a rotation speed of 62 \ is unlikely to show the strong orbital patterns of a gas bar.

sources.summary: The paper says ESO 115-G021 required heavy masking but was successfully modeled as a clumpy dwarf with small stellar disc dimensions, faint R-band luminosity, and high HI-to-light ratio.

quotes.quote: ESO 115-G021 A large amount of masking was required for ESO 115-G021 (Figure fig:stellar-ESO115-G021 ). The dwarf galaxy has been modelled successfully, although it is still clumpy. The stellar disc scale length h_ R ,* is only 1108 280 pc, while the scale height h_ z ,* is only 149 parsec. The galaxy is very faint and emits only 5.0 10^7 L _ in the R-band. In comparison to the neutral hydrogen mass, the M_ HI /L_ R is 12.5, the highest in our sample.

gmw_00001221.name: ESO 138-G014
gmw_00001221.overview: ESO 138-G014 is a large Sd-type disk galaxy [arXiv:1003.3110] with a thick stellar disk [arXiv:1605.08209, arXiv:1608.05559] and a distinctly prolate dark matter halo [arXiv:1605.08209]. Its gas kinematics suggest the presence of a central bar [arXiv:1003.3110], while its outer disk features a warp [arXiv:1303.2463] and a bright star-forming region [arXiv:1303.2463].

sources.summary: The paper identifies ESO 138-G014 as the least observed sample galaxy but still well resolved, with an asymmetric PV diagram, a warp lacking an optical counterpart, and a star-forming bright region.

quotes.quote: ESO 138-G014 is the least observed galaxy in our sample. Nevertheless, the galaxy is well resolved by the telescope. The minor axis of the galaxy in particular is covered by roughly 6 beams. The PV-diagram ( fig:ESO138-G014-PV ) is asymmetric, with the receding side showing an extended component.

No optical counterpart to this warp is known Sanchez2003A . The maximum brightness temperature map resembles a plateau for most of the galaxy, averaging between 80 and 100 K (figure fig:ESO138-G014-maxtemperature ). The only exception is at the left side, where a peak of 140 K is attained. This corresponds to the bright region in the south-west region in the DSS image (figure fig:ESO138-G014-DSS ), and is most likely a star forming region.

sources.summary: The paper finds ESO 138-G014 difficult because of inclination and thickness issues, but fits its rotation curve and vertical force gradient with a distinctly prolate halo.

quotes.quote: Galaxy ESO 138-G014 was initially hard to model, as the total observed vertical force gradient was already weaker than the contribution from the neutral hydrogen alone. As we noted before in Paper III, the galaxy seems to have quite a thick layer (see Figure 13 of Paper III). The most likely explanation for this is that the galaxy is not seen completely edge-on. This is consistent with the observed stellar disc from Paper IV, where we measured i=86.8^ (Table 2 of Paper IV). We have attempted to correct for this by lowering the observed thickness by 30 , but will treat the results are uncertain in view of this inclination issue.

In both cases, the rotation curve and the vertical force gradient have been successfully fitted. Only at the larger radii do observed and theoretical curves of the vertical force gradient start to deviate. The halo is distinctly prolate, with the optimal solution located at the boundary condition of q =2.0^ +0.1 _ 0.2 .

sources.summary: The paper characterizes ESO138-G014 as a larger Sd galaxy whose HI disk is resolved and whose XV diagram suggests barred gas kinematics, though missing short spacings limit the data.

quotes.quote: ESO138-G014 ESO138-G14 is a larger Sd galaxy, with a maximum rotation speed of 120.4 \ and a \ disk extending to 23 kpc. The \ column density map in Fig. fig:ch2-eso138-g014-m0 shows the \ disk is well-resolved by the \ synthesised beam of 10.7 \ or 960 pc. The XV diagram in Fig. fig:ch2-eso138-g014-combi-plot shows depressions in the \ flux at radii of 10 kpc, with the indication of a ``figure-8'' structure in the gas kinematics. This suggests that the galaxy is actually barred, as ``figure-8'' structures are typically seen in gas kinematics of barred edge-on systems bf1999 .
