We were able to observe this galaxy only in the 1.5A and 6A ATCA array configurations. Due to the lack of short spacing observations we are missing much of the extended structure on large spatial scales. The channel maps show that the \ disk clearly flares vertically with radius, but the missing extended structure will hamper accurate measurement of the flaring.

sources.summary: The paper models ESO 138-G014 as a well-resolved galaxy with a steeply rising rotation curve, disk flaring affected by a warp, and 28 percent hidden H I.

quotes.quote: ESO 138-G014 Galaxy ESO 138-G014 has been well resolved. The optically thin model and the self-absorption model are shown in Figure fig:ESO138-G014-velocitydispersion-thin . The rotation curve rises steeply to 70 km/s in the inner parts, and gradually increase to a maximum of 105 km/s near 8 kpc. Beyond this radius, it levels off. The curve is very similar to that measured by OBrien2010C .
The disc is reasonably thick, starting at 1.1 kpc near 2.5 kpc radius and increasing linearly towards 1.8 kpc at 9 kpc (for the optically thin model).

Comparing the two mass estimates, our optically thin model has a total mass of 3.4 0.1 10^9 M _ , while our self-absorption model finds 4.6 0.2 10^9 M _ . The total hidden fraction is thus 28 1 of the total .

sources.summary: The paper uses ESO138-G014/G104 as a dark-matter-halo lensing example, comparing Burkert-model Einstein-ring radii and giving distance, halo scale radius, and density values.

quotes.quote: Here, we restrict ourselves to the Burkert model to show the Einstein ring angular radius for gravitational lensing in the Milky Way, Andromeda galaxy(M31), Virgo galaxy(M87), and ESO138-G014 galaxy.

In the ESO138-G104 galaxy, the total mass of Hydrogen Intensity in the 21kpc range is M=4.6 10^9M_ , the distance between the observer and the lens plane is D_ OL =18.57 Mpc, the characteristic radius and density of the dark matter halo are h=7.5 kpc, _0=1.3 10^7M_ / kpc ^3 .

As shown in Table Table galaxy , by taking D_ LS /D_ OS as 0.2 , 0.5 , and 0.8 respectively, we can obtain the angular radius values of Einstein rings in different galaxies. And we can see that the gravitational lensing of Milky Way has the largest angular radius of the Einstein ring, followed by the Virgo galaxy(M87), the ESO138-G104 galaxy smaller, and the Andromeda galaxy(M31) smallest.

sources.summary: The paper says ESO 138-G014 needed extensive masking but fit well, with a stellar disc about twice as long as ESO 115-G021 and smaller than an earlier dust-free model.

quotes.quote: ESO 138-G014 ESO 138-G014 also required extensive masking (Figure fig:stellar-ESO138-G014 ). Despite this, the overall fit is good. With h_ R ,* =2288 59 pc and h_ z ,* =217 10 pc, the stellar disc is twice as long as ESO 115-G021 and slightly thicker. Kregel2002 previously modelled this galaxy without dust and reported a larger disc with h_ R ,* =3779 pc and h_ z ,* =382 10 pc.

gmw_00001222.name: ESO 274-G001
gmw_00001222.overview: ESO 274-G001 is a nearby, patchy late-type galaxy [arXiv:1303.2463, arXiv:1608.05563] located in the Centaurus A group [arXiv:1003.3110]. It features a lopsided, warped neutral hydrogen disk [arXiv:1003.3110] that exhibits gas flaring [arXiv:1003.3110, arXiv:1608.05559] and is surrounded by an oblate dark matter halo [arXiv:1605.08209].

sources.summary: The paper treats ESO274-G001 as the nearest sample galaxy, with high-resolution HI imaging, missing extended flux, an asymmetric rotation curve, visible flaring, and a lopsided warped disk.

quotes.quote: ESO274-G001 is the closest galaxy in our sample, at a distance of 3.4 Mpc in the Centaurus A group. Its proximity allows the \ disk to be examined at 160 pc resolution with the 9.8 \ resolution of the \ synthesised beam. To image this galaxy we used two partial observations in 1.5 km ATCA array configurations from the ATCA archive, and one new 12-hour synthesis observation in the ATCA 6A array configuration. From the \ total column density map in Fig. fig:ch2-eso274-g001-m0 we can see that the vertical axis is very well-resolved, although it is clear from the diffuse structure that the significant extended \ emission is not observed due to the lack of observations in short array configurations.

Comparison of the \ flux integral with that measured from the \ Parkes All-Sky Survey (HIPASS) data shows that about 30 of the \ flux is not measured. From the XV map in Fig. fig:ch2-eso274-g001-combi-plot we see that the rotation curve of this small Sd galaxy rises steeply to a radius of 1-2 kpc and then flattens on one side, but continues to rise with a shallower slope on the other side. In these outer velocity channel maps, where the \ spans a larger range along major axis, it is possible to see clear flaring of the gas thickness with radius. However, the galaxy is lopsided kinematically, and also slightly lopsided in its \ distribution with an indication of a warp on the NE side.

sources.summary: The paper presents ESO 274-G001 as the closest and least H I massive sample galaxy, with a possible central AGN and a notably regular H I disk.

quotes.quote: ESO 274-G001 is an SAd galaxy. At only 3.02 Mpc, it is the closest galaxy in our sample. It is also has the least H I mass of the galaxies in our sample (table tbl:HISpectrumProperties ). Due to it's proximity, it is still the largest galaxy in terms of angular size, spanning 13 arcminutes in width.

The most likely explanation is a active galactic nucleus (AGN). It is likely at the galactic center (see table tbl:opticalproperties ) and we have therefore manually centered the galaxy on this position. AGN are known to exert strong influence on the ISM of the galaxy (e.g. Shulevski2012A and references therein). It is therefore interesting to note how regular the H I disk is, both in the zeroth moment as in maximum temperature.

sources.summary: The paper reports that ESO 274-G001 has central absorption from a continuum source, unusual flaring and velocity-dispersion behavior, and 25 percent hidden H I.

quotes.quote: ESO 274-G001 As we discussed in Section 5.7 in Paper I, galaxy ESO 274-G001 has a strong continuum source at its central position. This causes strong absorption, leading to our inability to fit the central radii of the galaxy accurately. We trust our results beyond 1.5 kpc. We show our optically thin and self-absorption fits in Figures fig:ESO274-G001-velocitydispersion-thin and fig:ESO274-G001-velocitydispersion-thick .

The flaring is very different from that measured by OBrien2010C , whom found 100 pc near the inner parts and flaring out linearly towards 1.8 kpc in the outer parts. Both our models find a velocity dispersion that is dropping with radius. The optically thin model starts near 13 km/s and drops towards 7 km/s at 6 kpc. The self-absorption model drops towards 6 km/s at that radius. Beyond this radius, the velocity dispersion is unreliable. The velocity dispersion measured by OBrien2010C shows a very different behavior, staying constant around 6.5 km/s up to 6 kpc.
The optically thin model has a total mass of 3.1 0.02 10^8 M _ . The self-absorption model has a total mass of 4.1 0.1 10^8 M _ . A total of 25 1 of the is thus hidden by self-absorption.

sources.summary: The paper reports successful modelling of ESO 274-G001 despite foreground masking, finding small stellar disc scales, a flattened bulge, slow rotation, patchiness, and low HI-to-light ratio.

quotes.quote: ESO 274-G001 Galaxy ESO 274-G001 is very close to the Galactic plane, with a Galactic latitude of only 9.3^ . The four bands therefore required an exceptional amount of masking (Figure fig:stellar-ESO274-G001 ), but the fit was still successful. The galaxy has a stellar disc scale length h_ R ,* of 1270 59 pc and a scale height h_ z ,* of 161 2 pc, which is very similar to ESO 115-G021. The overall appearance is different from that galaxy, as ESO 274-G001 has a far more flattened bulge. Similar to the ESO 115-G021 and ESO 146-G014, the galaxy is a slow rotator, with a maximum circular velocity of v_ max =103.9 km/s.
Moreover, similar to those two galaxies, ESO 274-G001 is patchy in nature. The galaxy has the highest luminosity compared to its optically thin HI mass: M_ HI / L_ R =0.8

sources.summary: The paper identifies ESO 274-G001 as one of its best cases, reproducing its rotation curve and vertical force gradient and finding an oblate dark matter halo.

quotes.quote: ESO 274-G001 is one of the two galaxies from Paper III for which we could accurately measure the velocity dispersion. We model the galaxy in the standard way, setting the M_*/L_ R lower boundary at 0.5. Both the rotation curve and vertical force decomposition are shown in Figure fig:ESO274decomposition , where we show the results for both the optically thin and self-absorbing models. Both models have reproduced the rotation curve and the vertical force gradient reasonably well, although the self-absorbing model was more successful at the vertical force gradient.

The halo is oblate with q =0.7^ +0.1 _ 0.1 , _ 0, halo = 0.150^ +0.020 _ 0.022 M _ /pc ^3 and R_c = 1.10^ +0.04 _ 0.03 kpc. The shape of the halo is identical to the optically thin model, with an oblate shape of q=0.7^ +0.1 _ 0.1 .

sources.summary: The paper identifies a central radio source near ESO 274--G001 as likely unrelated, noting the emission-line morphology follows the optical major axis and subtracting the point source.

quotes.quote: LVHIS 072: ESO 274--G001 There is a strong radio source close to the optical centre of ESO 274--G001 (see Figure fig:cont_1 ). However, it is unlikely associated with the galaxy itself. The \ image provided by 2003A&A...406..505R shows the lack of \ peak at the centre of the galaxy.

The clumpy \ blobs are actually distributed along the optical major axis, consistent with the extended radio continuum emission. Given the morphology, we suggest it is a strong background radio source. We subtract the central point source before calculating the radio flux density.

sources.summary: ESO 274 G001 has an unusually high bright-line abundance, but line ratios suggest a supernova remnant; as an edge-on dwarf, it may help test abundance gradients.

quotes.quote: While the bright-line abundance for ESO 274 G001 \ region No. 4 implies an unusually high oxygen abundance (compared to the derived lower limit), our measured intensity ratios (Table table_data2 ) suggest that the nebula is a supernova remnant; see also the discussion in . If the object in question is a supernova remnant, the measured I ( )/ I ( ), I ( )/ I ( ), and the model grid from their Fig. 8 dopita84 yield an estimate of 12 + log(O/H) 7.8 0.1 for the oxygen abundance. As an edge-on dwarf galaxy, ESO 274 G001 is reminescent of NGC 55 in the Sculptor group and NGC 1560 in the IC 342 group. These galaxies may provide further opportunities to investigate the possibility of abundance gradients in low-luminosity late-type galaxies.

gmw_00001223.name: VCC 135
gmw_00001223.overview: VCC 135 is a blue compact dwarf galaxy located in the Virgo cluster [arXiv:2008.02212]. It features a nuclear star-forming region overlaid on an older, red stellar population and exhibits an inverted metallicity gradient [arXiv:2008.02212].

sources.summary: The paper presents VCC 135 as a Virgo star-forming dwarf with an inverted metallicity gradient, blue compact dwarf classification, and high radial velocity.

quotes.quote: firstpage - lastpage We present integral field spectroscopy observations of two star-forming dwarf galaxies in the Virgo cluster (VCC 135 and VCC 324) obtained with PMAS/PPak at the Calar Alto 3.5 meter telescope.
We measure gradient slopes of 0.20 0.06 and 0.15 0.03 dex/ R_e for VCC 135 and VCC 324, respectively.
Classified as S pec/blue compact dwarf (BCD), it hosts a nuclear star-forming region overlaid on a red ( g - i 1 mag) stellar population top-left panel of Fig. fig:met_img 2014A&A...562A..49M .

gmw_00001224.name: VCC 324
gmw_00001224.overview: VCC 324 is a blue compact dwarf galaxy located in the Virgo cluster [arXiv:2008.02212]. It is moderately deficient in neutral hydrogen, with its atomic gas extending to about twice the optical radius and showing an elongated distribution toward the southwest [arXiv:2008.02212].

sources.summary: The paper presents VCC 324 as a Virgo blue compact dwarf with an inverted metallicity gradient, moderate H I deficiency, and disturbed atomic gas.

quotes.quote: firstpage - lastpage We present integral field spectroscopy observations of two star-forming dwarf galaxies in the Virgo cluster (VCC 135 and VCC 324) obtained with PMAS/PPak at the Calar Alto 3.5 meter telescope.
The galaxy is classified as a BCD 1985AJ.....90.1681B,2014A&A...562A..49M .

VCC 324 is moderately \ deficient (Def _ HI = 0.4 ). \ observations with the Very Large Array VLA; 1987ApJ...314...57L show that the atomic gas extends out to about 2 times the optical radius ( 1 ^ ) and the outer \ distribution is elongated to the south-west.

gmw_00001225.name: IC 0214
gmw_00001225.overview: IC 0214 is a late-stage merging galaxy dominated by star formation [arXiv:2209.04002]. The system contains two compact radio regions, including a fainter starburst-dominated nucleus and a brighter background source with no optical or infrared counterpart [arXiv:2209.04002].

sources.summary: The paper identifies two compact radio regions in IC 0214, classifying the fainter region as a starburst nucleus and the brighter counterpart-free source as background.

quotes.quote: IC 0214 : Two regions are identified in this late-stage merger at both 15 and 33 GHz. The fainter region aligns with the optical and IR peak of the system, and the brighter region lies outside of the galaxy, with no visible IR or optical counterpart. No evidence for AGN has been reported in the literature and the high PAH 6.2 m equivalent width measured with Spitzer indicates that this system is dominated by star formation, which is consistent with optical BPT diagnostics using VLT/MUSE (ID: 097.B-0427, PI: G. Privon). Therefore here we classify the fainter region as the starburst-dominated nucleus.

gmw_00001226.name: NGC 5257
gmw_00001226.overview: NGC 5257 is a massive spiral galaxy [arXiv:2006.12523] that forms the interacting pair Arp 240 alongside NGC 5258 [arXiv:1811.04158]. The galaxy exhibits disk-wide star formation [arXiv:1811.04158], centrally concentrated molecular gas [arXiv:2006.12523], and possible LINER activity in its nucleus [arXiv:1204.5520].

sources.summary: The paper finds NGC 5257 is a massive spiral component of Arp 240 with centrally concentrated molecular gas measured through ALMA CO observations and gas concentration analysis.

quotes.quote: Arp 240 is composed two massive spiral galaxies, NGC 5257 and NGC 5258. The two galaxies are intertwined in HI Iono_2005 but well separated in optical images. The projected distance of the two galaxies is about 40 kpc, which is larger than the separation of Arp 55 ( 9 kpc). Simulations Privon_2013 show the two galaxies are in the early stage of the merging process and have just been through first passage.

For NGC 5257, the gas is clearly concentrated in the center. To quantitatively learn about the gas concentration degree, we calculated _ mol, 500 pc / _ mol , R25 , which is the ratio between the gas surface density in the central 500 pc and within isophotal radius R25, which is 53.3 arcsec for NGC 5257 Fuentes-Carrera_2019 . Due to the limited sensitivity, we do not detect molecular gas out to the isophotal radius and there are lot of blank pixels in the outer regions which have values below the 2 RMS threshold cut. In fact, some regions are even out of our field of view.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 5257 as part of the 100 Mpc Arp 240 pair, finding disk-wide star formation and non-circular motions tied to spiral arms, HII regions, the bar, and tidal response.

quotes.quote: The pair of galaxies Arp 240 is an interacting galaxy pair consisting of two spiral galaxies: NGC 5257 and NGC 5258 (Figure arp240_img ). Using the line-of-sight (LOS) radial velocities of the galaxies, corrected for the Local Group infall onto Virgo (6798 \ km \ s ^ 1 and 6757 \ km \ s ^ 1 , for NGC 5257 and NGC 5258, respectively Values taken from the LEDA database. ), the galaxy pair is located at a distance of 100.0 \ Mpc. This implies linear diameters of 51.7 kpc (106.6 ) and 48.3 kpc (99.6 ), for NGC 5257 and NGC 5258, respectively, considering the D_ 25 values by 1991rc3..book.....D , and a projected separation between the two galaxies of 37.8 kpc (78 ).

In the case of NGC 5257, the SF is spread over the whole disc of the galaxy, while for NGC 5258, the SF is mainly confined to a particular region in the southern arm where gas is being fueled towards the centre. Non-circular motions are detected in both galaxies. For both galaxies, in the inner parts of the discs, these motions are typically associated to spiral arms, and HII regions, and to streaming motion along the bar. For the outer parts of each galaxy, they are associated with the presence of the companion and the tidal response of the disc.

sources.summary: The paper singles out NGC 5257 as a LIRG double system whose mid-infrared emission is dominated by an off-nuclear spiral-arm region despite regular disks and unclear triggering.

quotes.quote: Moreover, three LIRGs show also dominant off-nuclear 24 m emission that is very likely associated with extended star formation in spirals arms: Arp 256 North ( > 70 of 24 m in spiral arm), NGC 5257 ( > 80 from outer spiral arm), and NGC 6670 West ( 50 from spiral arm). In particular one LIRG double system, namely NGC 5257, stands out from the rest of our sample, as it shows that almost all of its mid-IR emission is generated at the end of one of the spiral arms (in both galaxies), 7 kpc away from the center (Fig. NGC5257 , note that only one galaxy is shown due to the limited FOV of NICMOS). This seems to be very surprising given their very regular spiral disks and that no visible counterpart is seen at the center of the mid-IR emission, neither in the H- nor in the B-band image. This phenomenon might be also associated with Tidal Dwarf Galaxy (TDG) formation, which is also suggested for other interacting galaxies Duc98, Duc00 . Since NGC 5257 consists of two galaxies with a projected separation distance of 50 kpc, the exact cause of the enhanced disk emission is not clear.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 5257 (NW) as the observed northwest companion of an interacting system, with 1.49 GHz emission tracing spiral-arm gas and a flat central radio source classified as starburst-like.

quotes.quote: NGC 5257 (NW) Only the north-west companion of this interacting galaxy-system has been observed at 8.44 GHz. This radio source has a complex radio structure where the radio at 1.49 GHz traces the gas in the spiral arms of the NW galaxy. Only the nucleus was observed at 8.44 GHz due to the smaller field of view of the 8.44 GHz observations compared to the one at 1.49 GHz. In Fig. app:amaps we give just the radio source associated with the nucleus for presentation issues. The map and histogram show a flat distribution, and thus we classify it as a radio-SB.

sources.summary: The paper resolves two radio regions in NGC 5257, placing one at the optical/IR peak and one on a spiral arm, with starburst and star-forming classifications.

quotes.quote: Arp 240 (NGC 5257/8) : Both the eastern and western components in this early-stage merger have been observed. We detect two regions in the western component NGC 5257, both at native and at matched resolutions. These two regions are about 12 apart along the N-S direction, and the northern region coincides with the optical and IR peak of the galaxy while the southern region lies at the tip of a spiral arm. This galaxy is classified as a starburst galaxy in the optical veilleux95 , MIR stierwalt13 and radio vardoulaki15 , therefore we classify the northern region as ``SBnuc" and the southern region as extra-nuclear ``SF". For the eastern component NGC 5258, we detect two extra-nuclear regions in one of the spiral arms, but only at 15 GHz due to limited sensitivity at 33 GHz.

sources.summary: NGC 5257 is presented as a widely separated Arp 240 component with tidal tails, a bridge to NGC 5258, arm star formation, older nuclear populations, and possible LINER activity.

quotes.quote: IRAS F13373+0105 W (NGC 5257) Although NGC 5257 and its equal mass partner NGC 5258 are still widely separated and retain much of their structure, they exhibit interaction features including tidal tails and a bridge between the two galaxies. The spiral arms show regions of strong star formation with some signatures of post-starburst populations away from the nucleus. The nuclear regions of NGC 5257 are dominated by an older stellar population as the intense nuclear starburst associated with the later stages of major mergers has not yet begun. The extinction is higher in the nuclear regions and the measurable line-ratios place the nucleus in the composite region of the standard diagnostic diagrams, indicating possible LINER activity-previous nuclear observations and integrated spectrophotometry are consistent with the higher nuclear extinction and overall line ratios we observe Veilleux95,Kewley01b,Moustakas06 .

gmw_00001227.name: NGC 5331S
gmw_00001227.overview: NGC 5331S is a starburst galaxy that forms a mid-merging galaxy pair with its northern companion [arXiv:2209.04002]. Its nuclear radio knots lie along the edge of an inclined rotating nuclear disk, and the galaxy shows no signs of active galactic nucleus activity [arXiv:2209.04002].

sources.summary: The paper detects radio emission in NGC 5331S, identifies seven regions, connects nuclear knots to an inclined rotating disk, and classifies its regions as star-forming.

quotes.quote: NGC 5331 : In this mid-merging galaxy pair, we only detect radio emission in the southern galaxy NGC 5331S given the sensitivity limits. At both 15 and 33 GHz, we identify 7 regions in total in NGC 5331S, with 6 regions residing within the bulk of MIR emission at the galaxy center, and 1 region on the northern spiral arm. Comparison with high-resolution ALMA CO(J=2-1) data (2017.1.00395.S, PI: T. D i az-Santos) indicates that the nuclear radio knots lie along the edge of a inclined rotating nuclear disk. This galaxy is classified as a starburst galaxy in the optical wu98 , in agreement with a high PAH 6.2 m equivalent width stierwalt13 , and no AGN is detected in the X-rays torres-alba18 .

gmw_00001228.name: J0906+41
gmw_00001228.overview: J0906+41 is a Fanaroff-Riley class 0 radio galaxy [arXiv:2105.05396] associated with a host galaxy at a redshift of 0.027 [arXiv:2105.05396]. It features a core-dominated, two-sided parsec-scale jet structure [arXiv:2105.05396, arXiv:2302.12657] and exhibits an inverted radio spectrum at low frequencies [arXiv:2105.05396, arXiv:2302.12657].

sources.summary: The paper describes J0906+4124 as an FR 0 host at z=0.027 with compact low-frequency emission, a flat 5-8 GHz spectrum, and a two-sided parsec-scale EVN jet.

quotes.quote: J0906+4124 The source J0906+4124 is associated with a host galaxy at redshift z = 0.027 1999PASP..111..438F . The 150-MHz LOFAR image and 1.4-GHz NVSS image show a compact unresolved core with flux densities of 17.0 mJy and 51.8 mJy 1998AJ....115.1693C,2020A&A...642A.107C . Its total flux density spectrum is inverted at low frequencies ( ^ 150 MHz-1.4 GHz = 0.55) 2020A&A...642A.107C . The total flux density spectrum is flat ( ^ 4.87-8.37 _ VLBA = 0.22, ^ 4.99-8.42 _ EVN = 0.26) between 5 and 8 GHz, suggesting an emission peak at > 5 GHz. The EVN images at 5 and 8 GHz (first row of Figure fig:VLBI ) display a two-sided jet structure extending in the northwest-southeast direction on parsec scales, in agreement with the previous results 2018ApJ...863..155C .

sources.summary: The paper treats J0906+4124 as a blazar with changing peak frequency and VLBI core-jet morphology at 8.3 GHz.

quotes.quote: Quasi-simultaneous spectra of the source J0906+4124 do not have a constant shape, the peak frequency changes. This source has core-jet morphology at 8.3 GHz 2018ApJ...863..155C . The object is a blazar according to .

sources.summary: The paper describes J0906+41 as an FR0 radio source, using VLBI/JVLA structure, flux ratios, spectra, and jet sidedness to infer compact parsec-scale radio morphology.

quotes.quote: J0906+41 This is a core-dominated source with a two-sided symmetric structure. The ratio of the NVSS flux density at 1.4 GHz to the total correlated flux density at 8.4 GHz is 1.7--1.8 suggesting an inverted radio spectrum. The jet-to-counter-jet ratio is low, 3.9, and no relativistic boosting is necessary.

gmw_00001229.name: J1025+10
gmw_00001229.overview: J1025+10 is a Fanaroff-Riley type 0 radio galaxy [arXiv:2103.06573] located at a redshift of approximately 0.046 [arXiv:2105.05396, arXiv:2103.06573]. It features a flat-spectrum radio core [arXiv:2105.05396] and a two-sided jet structure [arXiv:2105.05396, arXiv:2302.12657] that may indicate restarted central activity [arXiv:2105.05396].

sources.summary: The paper describes J1025+1022 as a z=0.046 FR 0 with rich two-sided parsec-scale jets, a flat-spectrum core, and possible restarted activity producing an X-shaped source.

quotes.quote: J1025+1022 The source J1025+1022 is at a redshift of 0.046 2012ApJS..199...26H . The source is unresolved at 150-MHz LOFAR observation and 1.4-GHz NVSS observation 1998AJ....115.1693C,2020A&A...642A.107C . Our observation shows a rich two-sided structure with the jet extending roughly in the northwest-southeast direction on parsec scales. The inner jet follows a straight trajectory along the east-west direction ( 90 ) until 3 mas, but the outer jet has a position angle P.A.= 50 (or 130 ) and extends from northwest to southeast.

We calculated the total flux density spectrum ^ 4.99-8.42 _ EVN = 0.13, with a flat radio spectrum. The central component C shows the most compact size, the highest flux density and a flat spectrum ( ^ 4.87-8.37 _ VLBA = 0.49, ^ 4.99-8.42 _ EVN = 0.17) and is identified as the core.
J1025+1022 could be a restart of the central power source at a different jet axis direction, leading to an X-shaped source.

sources.summary: The paper describes ASK 378291.0 as a z=0.04580 Fanaroff-Riley type 0 radio galaxy whose double-horned H I absorption is robustly detected by FAST.

quotes.quote: ASK 378291.0 The ASK 378291.0 at redshift z= 0.04580 has been classified as a newly-defined Fanaroff-Riley type 0 Baldi2018 galaxy by , with a matched NVSS source exhibiting a 76.6 mJy flux at the 1.4 GHz band, and a well-aligned jet and counterjet Cheng2018 on its position. Its absorption line was detected by both of the wide band receiver, as well as the 19-beam array of FAST. As shown in Fig. figASK , the frequency and the double-horned profile in data from FAST and Arecibo show rough agreements to each other, thus proving the robustness of FAST detections. However, the low frequency peak in FAST data shows a significantly larger depth than ALFALFA's results. As can be seen in Fig. figASK_WSRT , this discrepancy exist even if the FAST data are binned to resolution similar to that of ALFALFA.

sources.summary: The paper describes J1025+10 as an FR0 radio source, using VLBI/JVLA structure, flux ratios, spectra, and jet sidedness to infer compact parsec-scale radio morphology.

quotes.quote: J1025+10 Unresolved in EVN observations at 1.7 GHz, it is resolved with a two-sided structure (13 pc in size) in the EVN 5 GHz image by cheng21 . In their image the inner core is extended at PA = 90 ^ and the two-sided jets are at PA 130 ^ , suggesting a Z-shaped structure in the inner ( 1 pc) region. The source size in the 5 GHz image is in agreement with the unresolved structure at 1.7 GHz. In our image no dynamic range problem is present. The unresolved source is the only visible structure, also in large field images.

gmw_00001230.name: J1230+47
gmw_00001230.overview: J1230+47 is a radio galaxy located at a redshift of z = 0.039 [arXiv:2105.05396]. Classified as an FR0 radio source [arXiv:2302.12657], it hosts a compact core [arXiv:2105.05396] and a slow, two-sided jet extending in the northwest-southeast direction [arXiv:2105.05396, arXiv:2302.12657].

sources.summary: The paper describes J1230+4700 as a z=0.039 galaxy with an unresolved low-frequency source, a two-sided NW-SE EVN jet, flat radio spectrum, compact core, and slow jet.

quotes.quote: J1230+4700 The source J1230+4700 is associated with a galaxy at redshift z = 0.039 2004ApJ...607..202M . The source is unresolved in 150-MHz LOFAR observation and 1.4-GHz NVSS observation 1998AJ....115.1693C,2020A&A...642A.107C . Our 5- and 8- GHz EVN images show a two-sided structure with jet extending in the northwest-southeast direction, consistent with the previous results 2018ApJ...863..155C . The source has a flat spectrum with a spectral index of = 0.14 between 150 MHz and 1.4 GHz 2020A&A...642A.107C , a spectrum with = 0.20 between 1.4 GHz and 5 GHz 2019A&A...631A.176C , and a flat spectrum with ^ 4.99-8.42 _ EVN = 0.21. Although the spectrum of the central component C shows a slightly steeper spectrum ( ^ 4.87-8.37 _ VLBA = 0.59, ^ 4.99-8.42 _ EVN = 0.58), the compact size and highest flux density are identified as the core.

Using the core as a reference, the linear regression fitting gives the proper motion velocities of three components: 0.073 0.014 mas yr^ 1 for S2, 0.088 0.022 mas yr^ 1 for N, and 0.064 0.027 mas yr^ 1 for S1. These angular velocities correspond to 0.22 , 0.16 , and 0.21 for S2, N and S1, respectively. The low radial velocity obtained from proper motion measurements suggests an intrinsic low jet speed.

sources.summary: The paper describes J1230+47 as an FR0 radio source, using VLBI/JVLA structure, flux ratios, spectra, and jet sidedness to infer compact parsec-scale radio morphology.

quotes.quote: J1230+47 The extended symmetric structure in the EVN images at 1.7 GHz is more than 70 pc in size (Fig. Fig9 ). The central core is resolved in the images by cheng18 in a two-sided structure 7-8 pc in size at the same PA of the more extended structure, suggesting a possible recurrent activity. The source is unresolved in JVLA images by baldi19 .

gmw_00001231.name: J1604+17
gmw_00001231.overview: J1604+17 is classified as an FR0 radio source [arXiv:2302.12657]. Its radio morphology displays a core-like structure, while a tail-like structure interacts with a nearby galaxy [arXiv:2302.12657].

sources.summary: The paper describes J1604+17 as an FR0 radio source, using VLBI/JVLA structure, flux ratios, spectra, and jet sidedness to infer compact parsec-scale radio morphology.

quotes.quote: J1604+17 Only a core-like structure is visible in the image reported by cheng18 , even if in the table it is reported as a jet-like structure. The correlated flux is in agreement with the NVSS flux density measure for this source. A tail-like structure interacting with a nearby galaxy is present in the LOFAR image in capetti20 .

gmw_00001232.name: J1621+25
gmw_00001232.overview: J1621+25 is classified as an FR0 radio galaxy [arXiv:2302.12657]. High-resolution observations reveal a short, symmetric nuclear structure that extends into a symmetric double-jet structure spanning 170 parsecs [arXiv:2302.12657].

sources.summary: The paper describes J1621+25 as an FR0 radio source, using VLBI/JVLA structure, flux ratios, spectra, and jet sidedness to infer compact parsec-scale radio morphology.

quotes.quote: J1621+25 In the 5 GHz and 1.5 GHz high-resolution VLBA images this source shows a short (28 pc in size) symmetric nuclear structure at PA = 90 ^ . At lower resolution (HPBW = 12 x 12 mas), a symmetric complex double-jet structure 170 pc in size is clearly evident at PA 130 ^ . The jet is transversely resolved and its extension is noise limited (see Fig. Fig11 ). The correlated flux density is 2.7 times the unresolved core estimated at 7.5 GHz in JVLA data by baldi19 , suggesting an extended steep spectrum structure.

gmw_00001233.name: J1628+25
gmw_00001233.overview: J1628+25 is an elliptical galaxy [arXiv:2306.01050] and a group member [arXiv:2306.01050] classified as a LINER-type active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2306.01050]. It is a candidate gamma-ray emitting FR0 radio galaxy [arXiv:2108.11701] that exhibits a compact, symmetric parsec-scale radio structure [arXiv:2302.12657].

sources.summary: The paper treats LEDA 58287 as a candidate gamma-ray emitting FR0: its association is complicated by a nearby BL Lac, but its position and Swift ratio favor a misaligned-jet origin.

quotes.quote: The case of LEDA 58287 is complex due to the presence of a BL Lac object, RX J1628.6+2527 ( z=0.22 ), at the edge of the 95 uncertainty region (Figure  fig:ts_map , right panel). The BL Lac is slightly brighter at 1.4 GHz with flux density 46 mJy compared to LEDA 58287 ( F_ 1.4 GHz 25 mJy). However, the FR0 galaxy is closer to the optimized ray position being located inside 68 uncertainty region while the BL Lac object is outside of it. With the collection of more LAT data, it will be possible to further reduce the uncertainty region size and identifying the true counterpart of the ray source. Therefore, the FR0 radio galaxy LEDA 58287 can be considered as a candidate ray emitter.

Using the Swift X-ray telescope observations, R_ /X was determined as 1.7, 1.9, and 1.4 for LEDA 55267, LEDA 57137, and LEDA 58287, respectively, similar to other FR0 galaxies. Altogether, the ray emission in FR0 systems is likely to be originated from misaligned jets.

sources.summary: J1628 + 2529 is described as a group-member elliptical LINER with a low-power radio continuum and a blueshifted, single-peak absorption line possibly tracing outflowing gas.

quotes.quote: The source J1628 + 2529 is a member galaxy in a galaxy group with a spectroscopic redshift of z=0.04010 0.00001 Ahumada2020 . The host galaxy is an elliptical galaxy with a high stellar mass of log (M_ /M_ )=11.1 Salim2016 . Classified as a LINER-type AGN Jeong2013 , J1628 + 2529 has a 1.4 GHz continuum flux of 25.2 mJy Condon1998 , corresponding to a radio power at 1.4 GHz of log (P_ 1.4 GHz / W Hz ^ 1 )=22.98 .

As shown in Figure fig:abs_profile , the \ absorption line profile of J1628 + 2529 is Doppler corrected and rebinned to a velocity resolution of 20 km s ^ 1 , and the zero-point is corrected to the optical redshift of the host galaxy. Since this \ absorption line shows a single-peak profile, we apply a single-component Gaussian fit to measure the peak optical depth _ peak =0.072 0.017 and the integrated optical depth _ int =6.46 0.97 km s ^ 1 .

sources.summary: The paper describes J1628+25 as an FR0 radio source, using VLBI/JVLA structure, flux ratios, spectra, and jet sidedness to infer compact parsec-scale radio morphology.

quotes.quote: J1628+25 This source is unresolved in JVLA high-resolution images baldi19 and in the EVN image. At higher resolution (VLBA images) it is resolved in a symmetric structure. The inner 10 pc are in PA 115 ^ (1.5 and 5 GHz image), but the more external regions visible only at 1.5 GHz rotate to a PA 90 ^ (Fig. Fig12 ).

gmw_00001234.name: J1703+24
gmw_00001234.overview: J1703+24 is classified as a Fanaroff-Riley class 0 radio source [arXiv:2302.12657]. Very Long Baseline Array and European VLBI Network observations reveal a complex, symmetric two-sided jet structure with varying position angles [arXiv:2302.12657].

sources.summary: The paper describes J1703+24 as an FR0 radio source, using VLBI/JVLA structure, flux ratios, spectra, and jet sidedness to infer compact parsec-scale radio morphology.

quotes.quote: J1703+24 This is a complex source. In the VLBA images we see a complex two-sided structure oriented in E-W (Fig. Fig14 , top panel). In the EVN image a symmetric two-sided structure is confirmed, but at a different PA: 70 ^ (Fig. Fig14 , bottom panel).

gmw_00001235.name: J2346+00
gmw_00001235.overview: J2346+00 is classified as an FR0 radio galaxy [arXiv:2302.12657]. Very Long Baseline Array observations reveal a two-sided symmetric emission spanning 50 parsecs, with a Z-shaped structure suggested by the orientation of its inner nuclear emission and extended jets [arXiv:2302.12657].

sources.summary: The paper describes J2346+00 as an FR0 radio source, using VLBI/JVLA structure, flux ratios, spectra, and jet sidedness to infer compact parsec-scale radio morphology.

quotes.quote: J2346+00 We observed this source with the VLBA at 1.5 and 5 GHz. The images show a two-sided symmetric emission 50 pc in size. The inner nuclear emission ( 10pc) is at PA 90 ^ ; the two-sided extended jet emission is at PA 105 ^ , suggesting a Z-shaped structure (Fig. Fig15 ). It is unresolved in JVLA images by baldi15 .

gmw_00001236.name: J125954.0+335653
gmw_00001236.overview: J125954.0+335653 is a radio galaxy at a redshift of 0.63 [arXiv:astro-ph/0508579]. It is classified as a compact steep-spectrum source and features a double-lobe radio structure spanning a projected linear extent of 1.6 kiloparsecs [arXiv:astro-ph/0508579].

sources.summary: The paper identifies J125954.0+335653 as the strongest 1.4 GHz DEVOS NGP galaxy, with redshift 0.63, a MERLIN double-lobe structure, VLBI-detected components, and CSS-source radio properties.

quotes.quote: J125954.0+335653 This galaxy is the strongest source at 1.4 GHz in the DEVOS NGP sample. A redshift of z=0.63 was measured by Allington-Smith et al. ( ). It was resolved by MERLIN into a double-lobe radio structure.

We could detect both components separated by 240  mas with VLBI, although heavily resolved (Fig.  vplot3 ). This corresponds to a projected linear extent of 1.6  kpc, assuming a flat cosmological model with _ m =0.3 , _ =0.7 and H_ 0 =70  km s ^ 1  Mpc ^ 1 . Based on the steep spectrum (radio spectral index 0.6 ) and the small size, the object can be identified as a CSS source. Its 5-GHz rest-frame radio luminosity is 4.2 10^ 26  W Hz ^ 1 .

gmw_00001237.name: J130129.1+333700
gmw_00001237.overview: J130129.1+333700 is a radio source that exhibits a compact radio structure [arXiv:astro-ph/0508579]. It has demonstrated strong flux density variability, with its 5-GHz flux density nearly doubling between March 2001 and May 2002 [arXiv:astro-ph/0508579].

sources.summary: The paper reports strong MERLIN-to-VLBI flux variability for J130129.1+333700, interprets it as consistent with compact radio structure, and flags it as a source worth further study.

quotes.quote: J130129.1+333700 The integrated 5-GHz flux density of this source measured in our MERLIN experiment was 62.8 mJy in March 2001. About a year later (in May 2002), a flux density of 127.0 mJy was determined with VLBI. This indicates significant flux density variability, consistent with the compact radio structure. Note that -- according to what is expected in the absence of variability -- none of the other sources showed VLBI peak brightness higher than the MERLIN value (Fig.  dist1 ).

The results of this pilot study can already be valuable in their own right since there are a couple of individual sources that may be worth studying further (e.g. J125858.6+325738 and J130129.1+333700).

gmw_00001238.name: NGC 4826
gmw_00001238.overview: NGC 4826 is an early-type spiral galaxy [arXiv:2210.04916, arXiv:2306.12902] classified as a LINER or transition object [arXiv:1503.00280, arXiv:astro-ph/0405047]. It is characterized by two nested, counter-rotating gas disks [arXiv:1503.00280, arXiv:2108.10909] and a thick dust lane [arXiv:1112.4058, arXiv:astro-ph/0606480].

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4826 as the nearby Black/Evil Eye LINER with counter-rotating gas disks, a compact circumnuclear molecular disk, spatially coincident gas/SFR morphologies, and a CO-transition-independent K-S relation near 60 pc.

quotes.quote: NGC 4826, also known as the ``Black Eye'' or ``Evil Eye'' galaxy due to its optical appearance, is the closest target ( 5 Mpc) of the NUGA core sample, and its nucleus is classified as a LINER type ho97 . It hosts two nested counter-rotating atomic and molecular gas disks of comparable mass 10 ^ 8 M _ , braun92,casoli93,braun94 . The inner disk has a radius of 50 ( 1.3 kpc), while the outer one from 80 \ to 9 8 ( 2.1 -- 15.3 kpc). By studying the stellar kinematics along the principal axes of NGC 4826, rix95 found that the stars rotate at all radii in the same direction as the inner disk, providing strong evidence that stars and gas are coplanar. NUGA observations have shown a high concentration of molecular gas (in ^ 12 CO(1--0), ^ 12 CO(2--1), and ^ 12 CO(3--2)), within a radius of 80 pc, forming a circumnuclear molecular disk.

The panels of the fourth line of Fig. fig:overlays show the comparison between \ and \ distributions in NGC 4826 at a resolution of 2 1 ( 56 pc from ^ 12 CO(1--0)), 0 7 ( 17 pc from ^ 12 CO(2--1)), and 2 3 ( 59 pc from ^ 12 CO(3--2)). Unlike NGC 4569 and NGC 4579, the morphologies of \ and \ in NGC 4826 are spatially coincident and characterized by a structured disk.
The results obtained for N_ fit and r_ corr therefore suggest that NGC 4826 has a K-S relationship independent of the ^ 12 CO transition at resolutions as high as 60 pc.

sources.summary: The paper finds M64 has counter-rotating gas, truncated star formation, and an anti-truncated profile likely marking a disk-to-halo transition after merger-driven quenching.

quotes.quote: M64 (NGC 4826) is a nearby Sa galaxy, alternately referred to as the ``Black Eye'' or ``Evil Eye'' galaxy else the ``Sleeping Beauty'' galaxy; rubin94 due to the prominent dust lane near the bulge on the northeast side. Interest in the galaxy peaked when braun92 discovered that the inner ( <50 , 1 kpc) and outer ( >50 ) H 1 disks counter-rotated with respect to each other. Subsequent observations by rix95 revealed that the entire stellar disk co-rotates with the inner gas disk, implying that the outer gas disk is an accretion relic. Detailed study of the H 1 rubin94 and CO garcia03 showed evidence of shocks and radial inflow in the inner disk originating from the boundary between the two counter-rotating systems.

M64 shows a stark star formation truncation only a few kpc from the center, with a low H 1 column density beyond this radius, and a sharp anti-truncation in the stellar surface brightness beginning around 400 \ (9kpc). We trace this anti-truncated disk to 19kpc, or 13 inner disk scale lengths. M64's strongly anti-truncated profile is likely the signature of a transition from the galaxy's disk to its diffuse stellar halo rather than being a true upbending of the disk surface brightness profile. The recent merger event in M64 appears to have disrupted its gas disk and truncated star formation in all but the inner few kiloparsecs, leading to the galaxy's very flat and red color profile.

sources.summary: The paper studies M64's outer disk and Type III break, finding metal-poor halo stars and M64-GC1, then arguing the break is halo-dominated and the gas ring likely has an external origin.

quotes.quote: M64 is a nearby spiral galaxy with a Type III anti-truncation component. To trace the origin of the Type III component, we present Hubble Space Telescope /Advanced Camera for Surveys F606W/F814W photometry of resolved stars in a field located in the outer disk ( 2 5 6 5 ) of M64. At 5 5 (7 kpc) to the east, we discover a new metal-poor globular cluster ( R_ eff =5.73 0.02 pc and M_V=-9.54 0.09 mag), M64-GC1. This is the first globular cluster found in M64.

The radial number density profile of the metal-rich RGB is described by an exponential disk law, while that of the metal-poor RGB is fit well be a de Vaucouleurs's law. This shows that the metal-rich RGB represents the disk population with high metallicity, while the metal-poor RGB denotes the halo population with low metallicity. We derive a TRGB distance to M64, d=4.33 0.18 Mpc ( (m-M)_0 = 28.18 0.03( ran) 0.08( sys) . From these results, we conclude that the origin of the Type III break in M64 is a halo rather than a disk or a bulge.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4826 as a medium-inclination SAab galaxy with counter-rotating gas disks, measured radio fluxes, a flat central spectrum, and centrally dominated radio/IR emission.

quotes.quote: A separate flat spectrum region is also seen to extend from centre towards East (seen along Dec 41 ^ 39 ' from centre), which could be an arm of the galaxy or a region of triggered star formation due to merger of one of its companion galaxy in the past Lawrence2020 (vi) NGC 4826: This is a SAab type of galaxy with medium inclination. This galaxy shows two counter-rotating gas-disks Braun1992Natur . This galaxy was observed in L-band using VLA Condon1987ApJS , and measured flux density to be 103 mJy. Its flux density as measured by Sings survey is 110 10 mJy at 1.365 GHz Braun2007A&A .

Our measured flux densities for this galaxy are 201 11 and 99 3 mJy from 0.33 GHz GMRT image and the image made from VLA 1.4 GHz archival data. Galaxy integrated spectral index from our measurements is 0.49 .06 . The central part of the galaxy does show a flatter spectrum of 0.4 0.07 (Fig. 19).

sources.summary: The paper uses VGT to make the first magnetic-field map of NGC 4826, finding complex central orientations, an hourglass circumnuclear morphology, and radial-to-tangential transition.

quotes.quote: In this work, we apply the VGT to five nearby Seyfert galaxies, M51 , NGC 1068 , NGC 1097 , NGC 3627 , and NGC 4826 , by using molecular emission line data of CO isotopologs obtained with ALMA and PAWS archives . We compare our results of the first four galaxies with the dust polarization data from the SOFIA legacy program and synchrotron polarization data from the Very Large Array (VLA) . We demonstrate that the synergy of the VGT using gas tracers and polarization presents valuable information on magnetic fields in different gas phases and their role in fueling nuclear activity in Seyferts.

Prediction of magnetic field morphology towards NGC 4826: We apply the VGT to obtain the first magnetic field map of NGC 4826, for which polarization measurements are not yet available. Fig. ngc4826 shows the VGT-predicted magnetic field orientations using ^ 12 CO emission line, which have a complex pattern in the central molecular gas reservoir. An hourglass-shaped magnetic field morphology is seen in the circumnuclear region. We find a transition from radial magnetic fields in the inner molecular gas disk with the radius <0.75 kpc to tangential magnetic fields in the outer region (see Fig. AM_rad2 in the Supplementary).

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4826 as a transition object with an abundant, lopsided circumnuclear molecular disk but finds its gas kinematics and stellar torques do not currently feed the AGN.

quotes.quote: NGC 4826 The first NUGA maps of the transition object NGC 4826 were published in paper I. The CO images showed already a large concentration of molecular gas ( 1600M _ /pc ^ 2 ) in the 160 pc-diameter circumnuclear disk (CND) of this galaxy. The distribution of molecular gas in the inner CND is significantly lopsided with respect to the position of the AGN; this suggests that m=1 instabilities may be at work at radial distances of 50--60 pc from the central engine. With the newest 0.5 ^ (10 pc) resolution ^ 12 CO(2--1) observations the distribution of molecular gas in the CND is fully resolved; the CND appears in the new maps as an off-center ringed disk (Fig. fig:feeding-4826 a ). The dynamical center of the galaxy determined from these observations coincides within the errors with a blue point source identified in the B-I HST color map of Fig. fig:feeding-4826 a .

Based on the CO maps of NGC 4826 we find little evidence of ongoing AGN feeding at scales < 150 pc from the AGN. While the molecular gas reservoir of NGC 4826 is abundant ( > 3 10 ^ 7 M _ ) close to its central engine ( < 150 pc), the analysis of gas kinematics provides no evidence that ongoing AGN feeding is at work in this transition object.

sources.summary: The paper includes NGC 4826 in its six-galaxy LBT outer-disk sample, finding marginally resolved star clusters beyond R_25 that trace H I gas and imply low-level cluster formation.

quotes.quote: Expanding on the pilot project of , we present deep ( 27.5 mag V band point-source limiting magnitude) U \ and V band imaging of six galaxies: IC 4182, NGC 3351, NGC 4736, NGC 4826, NGC 5474, and NGC 6503. We find that the outer disk of each galaxy is populated with marginally-resolved star clusters with masses 10^3 M_ and ages up to 1 Gyr (masses and ages are limited by the depth of our imaging and uncertainties are large given how photometry can be strongly affected by the presence or absence of a few stars in such low mass systems), and that they are typically found out to at least 2 R_ 25 but sometimes as far as 3 to 4 R_ 25 - even beyond the apparent H 1 disk. The mean rate of cluster formation for 1 R_ 25 R 1.5R_ 25 is at least one every 2.5 Myr and the clusters are spatially correlated with the H 1 , most strongly with higher density gas near the periphery of the optical disk and with lower density neutral gas at the H 1 disk periphery.

We present new observations of six galaxies (IC 4182, NGC 3351, NGC 4736, NGC 4826, NGC 5474, NGC 6503) with the LBC-Blue imaging camera on the LBT on the dates listed in Table tab:gals . These systems comprise a representative sample of nearby disk galaxies by spanning a range of physical disk sizes. All are nearby ( D 15 Mpc) and only two, NGC 4826 and NGC 6503, are seen at high inclination ( > 60^ ).

sources.summary: The paper derives center estimates and harmonic-decomposition results for NGC 4826, describing its disk morphology, non-circular motions, and whether the inferred potential is consistent with being round.

quotes.quote: NGC 4826 NGC 4826 (Fig. fig:ngc-4826 ) a) Center estimates [ 1.5ex ] NGC 4826 shows no sign of spiral structure in the 3.6 m IRAC image, but has two counter-rotating gas disks braun-1994 . The center of NGC 4826 is well-defined by the central, compact source visible in the radio continuum, and the IRAC 3.6 m image, whose coordinates agree to within 1 . We use the position of the radio continuum source ( =2 4 , =8 5 ) as an initial estimate for an unconstrained \ fit. We derive the dynamical center by averaging the , \ values inwards of 60 , i.e., over the radial range of the inner disk.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC4826 as an Sab galaxy with a dust lane and counter-rotating gas disks, finding its photometric data consistent with an abnormally low M/L ratio.

quotes.quote: NGC4826 is an Sab-type galaxy with a thick dust lane northeast of the nucleus. The rotation of the outer gas disk in NGC 4826 is opposite to that of the inner one (Braun et al. 1994). A detailed study of the H_ kinematics shows that the transition between the two disks occurs near the dust lane at a distance 50''<<70'' from the center (Rubin 1994). The velocity v_ max disk calculated from the R-band photometric parameters of the disk (see Table table3 ) and the (B-V) and (B-R) colors beyond the dust lane turns out to be higher than the maximum rotational velocity v_ max (see Table table4 ). This conclusion remains valid even if we use the model by Bell and de Jong (2001) with lower metallicity ( Z = 0.008 ). Thus, our photometric data agree with the abnormally low M/L ratio in this galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper says NGC 4826 has a steep H I decline unlikely to trace ionization, connecting it instead to counter-rotation and possible merger or retrograde gas infall.

quotes.quote: The profile of NGC 4826 declines steeply around 5 10^ 19 cm ^ 2 . However, it is unlikely that we are tracing effects due to ionization by extragalactic photons. Kinematical studies by braunetal94 and by rixetal95 revealed that the inner disk is counter-rotating with respect to the outer disk. As reported by rixetal95 , this counter-rotation may indicate that the outer disk of NGC 4826 has been built from the merging of gas rich dwarf galaxies or from the infall of H i gas with a retrograde orbit. Regardless of the exact cause, it is conceivable that we are tracing the relics of a merging event or episodic gas infall rather than the effects of photoionization.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4826's two RGB populations and uses the low-metallicity halo population to derive a PHANGS-HST TRGB distance of 4.41 Mpc.

quotes.quote: NGC 4826, also known as the Black Eye galaxy due to its prominent dust lanes, is the closest PHANGS-HST target with a previously determined TRGB distance of D = 4.40 0.18 Mpc on the CMDs/TRGB catalog (from GO-10905, PI: R. Tully). The PHANGS-HST data samples a region of the galaxy where there are two distinct RGBs. This includes a low-metallicity population within the halo of the galaxy, as well as a high-metallicity population from the outer disk. There is evidence of a Type-III anti-truncation component 2020ApJ...897..106K , and this new parallel field will allow us to trace this component at further galactocentric radii (to be presented in a later work). For our TRGB analysis, we select the lower metallicity red giant population by limiting the analysis to the far half of the field. We find m_ TRGB = 24.22 0.01 mag. From this, we determine D = 4.41 0.19 Mpc, which is nearly identical to the existing determination on the CMDs/TRGB catalog. We note that our measurement of the TRGB is consistent within the small uncertainties with the recent determination of , who measure m_ TRGB = 24.21 0.03 mag from archival HST data. We adopt the new PHANGS-HST TRGB determination for the distance to this galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes NGC 4826 (M 64) as a spiral target, estimating its globular-cluster count, distance, SMBH mass, luminosity, and globular-cluster specific frequency.

quotes.quote: In this paper, we present new measurements and analyses of the globular cluster systems of the Sab galaxies NGC 3368 (M 96), NGC 4736 (M 94) and NGC 4826 (M 64), and the Sm galaxy NGC 4395. Their surface brightness fluctuations (SBF) distances are 10.4 ^ +1.1 _ 1.0 Mpc for NGC 3368, 5.0 0.4 Mpc for NGC 4736, and 7.3 0.7 Mpc for NGC 4826 tonr01,blak10 . NGC 4395 is completely devoid of a bulge, and hence lacks an SBF measurement; we adopt a Cepheid distance of 4.3 0.4 Mpc thim04 . The masses of their central supermassive black holes are (3.6 1.1) 10^5 \ M_ for NGC 4395 pete05 ; (7.5 1.5) 10^6 \ M_ for NGC 3368; 6.8 ^ +1.5 _ 1.6 10^6 \ M_ for NGC 4736, and (1.6 0.4) 10^6 \ M_ for NGC 4826 korm13a .
Hence, we get N_ GC = 35^ +20 _ 16 for NGC 3368, N_ GC = 15 7 for NGC 4395, N_ GC = 46 18 for NGC 4736, and N_ GC = 69^ +31 _ 28 for NGC 4826.

For NGC 4826, rc3 give B_ T,0 = 8.82 0.24 mag and ( B \ V ) _ T,0 = 0.71 0.01 mag; M_V = -21.21 0.32 mag, and S_N = 0.23 0.09 or S_N = 0.23 0.11 , respectively, without and with the uncertainty in the number of obscured clusters.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4826/M64 as the closest, relatively isolated spiral host, with strong HI sensitivity and ELVES-vetted gaseous satellite candidates inside and beyond R200.

quotes.quote: NGC 4826 NGC 4826 (M64) is the closest spiral host in our sample, which gives it a large projected area (Figure fig:virgo_field_sky_distribution ), and a better local HI detectability M_ HI 10^ 6.2 M_ (Figure fig:survey_sensitivity_with_count ). It is in a relatively isolated environment with few massive nearby galaxies 1976ApJS...32..409T . As an overlapping host between ALFALFA and ELVES, our V_ 300 object, AGC 742601 (IC 3840), is the only ELVES-identified gaseous satellite within R_ 200 . The V_ esc object (AGC 742601) being rejected by ELVES can be explained by the F_ d_ 3D test using the updated distances 2020ApJ...905..104K , as described in appendix:Fd3d . Extending to 2R_ 200 , we find AGC 221120 (equivalently F575-3 or KDG 215), a satellite with the highest 3D proximity confidence in the sample, F_ d_ 3D =87.3 (Table table:2R200_satellites ), located at D=4.68 0.19 Mpc 2020ApJ...905..104K,schombert_anomalous_2021 .

sources.summary: The NUGA paper presents NGC 4826 as a counter-rotating LINER with a lopsided molecular disk and spiral modes that currently inhibit gas inflow to its AGN.

quotes.quote: Paper I (Garc a-Burillo et al. ) focused on the counter-rotating LINER NGC 4826: a prototype for m =1 modes. The BCD-maps of NGC 4826 show that the molecular gas is distributed in a 40 pc-radius lopsided disk and two m =1 spirals (Fig. Fig:1 ). Streaming motions linked with the m =1 perturbations are compatible with the inner modes being fast trailing waves. This implies that the lopsided instability may slow down or even temporarily halt gas infall. An independent confirmation that AGN feeding is inhibited at present in NGC 4826 has been obtained from an evaluation of the stellar gravitational torques exerted on the gas.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4826 as a LINER/transition object whose circumnuclear CO disk has m=1 instabilities, perturbed gas kinematics, weak stellar torques, and little current AGN fueling.

quotes.quote: The circumnuclear molecular gas disk of the LINER/transition object NGC 4826 shows the prevalence of m =1 instabilities (see figure fig:torques , adapted from ). Gas kinematics are perturbed by streaming motions related to m =1 modes . The non-circular motions associated with the inner m =1 perturbations (lopsided instability and inner m=1 spiral--Arm-I) fit the pattern expected for a trailing wave developed outside corotation ('fast' wave). A paradoxical consequence is that the inner m =1 perturbations would not favour AGN feeding. An independent confirmation that the AGN is not being generously fueled at present is found in the low values of the gravitational torques exerted by the stellar potential for R < 530 pc (figure fig:torques ).

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 4826 as an anemic spiral whose star formation is concentrated inside 5 kpc, making its radial IRX track move toward bulge-like colors outward.

quotes.quote: The SINGS sample also includes some peculiar objects that do not follow these smooth trends. This is the case of NGC 4826, which is a clear example of an anemic spiral (van den Bergh 1976). Although it has a global radial extent of 13 kpc at 3.6 , the bulk of the star formation activity as traced by the FUV or 24 images, for instance seems to be restricted to the central 5 kpc. Therefore, as we move away from the center towards the outer and more quiescent regions, the track followed by this galaxy in the IRX- diagram heads towards the region populated by bulges.

sources.summary: The paper reports that NGC 4826 only has inner CO velocity-dispersion coverage and is an exception where molecular dispersion is much smaller than HI because of a steep radial gradient.

quotes.quote: However, the maps of NGC 4736 and NGC 4826 contain no data at larger radii, and so for these two galaxies we quote disk velocity dispersions excluding only a central 9 pixel diameter region. The disk velocity dispersions of these two galaxies are likely biased to be somewhat higher than those of the other galaxies in our sample and thus we do not include them in calculating the average outer disk velocity dispersion.

The exception to this trend is NGC 4826, for which the cloud-cloud velocity dispersion is a factor of 5 times smaller than the the HI velocity dispersion. However, this galaxy has quite a steep radial gradient in the observed velocity dispersion (Figure fig-radial ) and so the average disk value depends sensitively on what aperture is used.

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC4826 as a fitting example with surface-brightness deviations, a revised mask for the third fit, and outer residuals informing uncertainties.

quotes.quote: However, for NGC4826 the situation was different, as it can be seen from the average surface brightness profile (black line), that there are deviations from an exponential disk profile inside R_ max . Thus, a new mask was used, with all the pixels beyond R_ mask excluded from the 3rd run fit. We have used the positive sky residuals in the outer parts of galaxies (such as the one noticeable in the surface brightness profiles of NGC4826 or even NGC3031 towards R_ max and beyond) to estimate the systematic errors in bulge-to-disk ratios.

One could also use a 3rd function during the fitting procedure, to potentially improve the fit in the outskirts of some of the galaxies, as it would be the case for NGC4826 and a few other galaxies, when various features and deviations from an exponential disk are present, such as truncations / antitruncantions, rings, noise etc.

sources.summary: The paper notes NGC 4826 as an Sb LINER galaxy with dust features and poor-group membership, and finds it among the strongest individual SZ-correlation detections.

quotes.quote: NGC 4826 (M 64): This is a Sb galaxy that has a LINER designation and with prominent dust features. It is the brightest member of a poor group Tully2008 .

The six objects with the most significant correlations are NGC 253 (chance probability or p-value of 5.9 ), NGC 3031 (p = 9.2 ), NGC 5236 (p = 1.3 ), and three objects with p < 0.2 : NGC 891, NGC 4826, and NGC 5055.
The confidence for the fit is highest for NGC 891 (chance probability is 0.02 , or 3.56 ), followed by NGC 4826 (1.5 or 2.16 ), which agrees with visual inspection.

sources.summary: The paper disputes NGC4826's pseudobulge classification, arguing it follows the Kormendy relation, has n greater than 2, and is not a Faber-Jackson low outlier.

quotes.quote: After inspecting such list given also by , we noticed that the bulge of NGC4826 was classified as a pseudobulge because the center of the galaxy is dominated by Population I material and there is no sign of a merger in progress, besides NGC4826 is rotation-dominated and is a low- outlier in the Faber-Jackson Relation (FJR) according to Kormendy_et_al_2011 . However, for us NGC4826 follows the KR, it has n> 2 and =96 km s^ 1 (in fact, they also report such value). Nevertheless, in a plot of the FJR (Paper II), it can be seen that it is not a low- outlier. As we see, velocity dispersion information is the only point in common we do agree with, so a possible explanation for this discrepancy may be related to the difference in the surface brightness modelling, since 1D decomposition was used by , unlike the 2D approach presented in this paper.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes NGC 4826 as an early-type spiral with counter-rotating gas disks and strong orientation-angle changes around the transition radius.

quotes.quote: NGC 4826 NGC 4826 is an early-type spiral galaxy known for its counter-rotating gas disks, with a high-column density inner disk rotating in the same direction as the stars, and a low-column density outer disk rotating in the opposite direction Braun_etal_1994 . The inner disk has an high-column density disk and it is associated with the bright stellar disk and rotates in the same direction. The outer, much lower column-density disk, rotates in the direction opposite to that of the stars. The transition between the two disks occurs around 100'' radius that is about 1/3 of the optical radius R_ 25 310'' .

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4826 as the isolated Evil Eye Sab spiral, with a dust lane, transition-object nucleus, radio counterpart, clumpy emission, and good RC-CO correlation.

quotes.quote: NGC 4826 . The 'Evil Eye' galaxy is a relatively isolated Sab spiral with a conspicuous dust lane. , analysing spectroscopic parameters, have classified the nucleus as a transition object, which has a radio counterpart in our 20 cm image (Fig. figura1a ). Archival observations at higher resolution, performed with VLA-A array, revealed the presence of clumps of emissions. In the upper- and lower-right panels of Fig. 2 (l) we can see that the point-by-point and also the radial profile show a good correlation between the RC and CO emission.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 4826 as a distinct, high-density starburst system whose homogenized clouds have larger line widths and surface densities and a nearly continuous, centrally concentrated molecular disk.

quotes.quote: For example, we note in Figure fig:orig_vs_common that clouds in NGC 4826 clearly show larger line widths and surface densities relative to the other targets after homogenization.

The CO emission morphologies of these two systems are distinct, with NGC 4826 showing a nearly-continuous molecular disk where the catalogued clouds may blend together low-mass neighbouring clouds and contributions from a diffuse molecular medium.
NGC 4826 is a high density, centrally concentrated molecular gas disc that may be strongly affected by (or even result from) a recent interaction.

sources.summary: The atlas identifies NGC 4826 as the Black Eye/Evil Eye LINER, describing its counter-rotating central gas disk, oppositely rotating diffuse outer H i disk, and bright inner radio disk.

quotes.quote: Also known as the ``Black Eye'' or ``Evil Eye galaxy'', this LINER exhibits a counter-rotating gaseous disk in its central region (see e.g. Braun et al. ; Garc ia-Burillo et al. ). The high column density H i in the central regions rotates in one sense, while the diffuse outer H i disk in the other. Our radio continuum image shows a high-surface brightness inner disk in this system.

gmw_00001239.name: VCC 0308
gmw_00001239.overview: VCC 0308 is a round dwarf elliptical galaxy located in the outskirts of its cluster [arXiv:1210.3591]. It features a blue core with a strong central hydrogen-alpha peak that suggests a younger stellar population, and it exhibits a mild level of rotation [arXiv:1210.3591].

sources.summary: VCC 0308 is the sample's only blue-core dwarf elliptical, with a central H peak suggesting younger stars and mild rotation in a round outer-cluster system.

quotes.quote: VCC 0308 is designated dE(bc,di) in the notation of L06, and it is the only blue-core (bc) galaxy in our sample. The H map shows a strong peak in the center, suggestive of the presence of a younger population, consistent with the '(bc)' classification. It is a round galaxy in the outskirts of the cluster, showing a mild level of rotation, signs of which have also been seen by T11.

gmw_00001240.name: VCC 0523
gmw_00001240.overview: VCC 0523 is a nucleated dwarf elliptical galaxy located in the inner regions of its cluster [arXiv:1210.3591]. The galaxy is rotationally supported and features a disk and a bar structure, which causes a misalignment between its photometric and kinematic major axes [arXiv:1210.3591].

sources.summary: VCC 0523 is described as a nucleated dwarf with disk and bar structure, misaligned photometric and kinematic axes, and rotational support.

quotes.quote: VCC 0523 is a mildly flattened, nucleated dwarf in the inner parts of the cluster; L06 found a disk and a bar in this galaxy based on photometric decomposition, so the galaxy is denoted dE(di). We confirm this result by showing how the photometric and the kinematic major axes of the galaxy are misaligned due to the bar. The galaxy is rotationally supported, in agreement with T11 results from their mean velocity and velocity dispersion curves.

gmw_00001241.name: VCC 0929
gmw_00001241.overview: VCC 0929, also known as NGC 4415, is a dwarf elliptical galaxy located in the Virgo cluster [arXiv:2308.12449, arXiv:1210.3591]. It is a fairly round but strongly rotating galaxy with flat line-strength maps that show only a mild central increase in metal lines [arXiv:1210.3591].

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 4415 as SN 2022pul's elliptical Virgo-cluster host, gives its redshift, size, offset from the supernova, and adopts a 16 Mpc distance.

quotes.quote: The host galaxy, NGC 4415, has a redshift of z = 0.00301 Francois19 and a distance of 16 Mpc (see s:dist ). Like other 03fg-like SNe, this SN has a large spatial offset from its host galaxy, possibly indicating that it could have originated in a low-metallicity environment Khan11 .

Extinction, Distance, and Luminosity \ is located in the outskirts of its elliptical host galaxy NGC 4415 in the Virgo cluster and its offset is 2.207 arcminutes (projected distance of 9 kpc). The SDSS band Petrosian radius of NGC 4415 is 2.3 kpc SDSS_dr6 , so we assume that the extinction by dust from the host galaxy is minimal and the main source of extinction will come from the local environment of the SN itself.

The distance to NGC 4415 is somewhat uncertain; it is projected close to M49 and thus likely part of the Virgo B subcluster. Using the surface brightness fluctuation (SBF) method, Jerjen2004 find a distance of 14.9 1.0 Mpc, whereas the fundamental plane (FP) gives 17.1 2.0 Mpc = 31.17 0.25 mag; Tully2013 . Combining these with a conservative uncertainty estimate, we adopt a distance of 16 2 Mpc (distance modulus = 31.02 0.27 mag) for .

sources.summary: VCC 0929 is presented as a fairly round but strongly rotating dwarf elliptical whose line-strength maps are mostly flat with mild central metal-line increases.

quotes.quote: VCC 0929 is a fairly round galaxy with a high level of rotation. Previous results also indicate rotation, though to a lesser degree (P02) or showing large uncertainties (S02). Line-strength maps are essentially flat, showing only a mild increase towards the center in the Fe5015 and Mg b maps.

gmw_00001242.name: VCC 1036
gmw_00001242.overview: VCC 1036 is a flat dwarf elliptical galaxy located in the central region of the Virgo Cluster [arXiv:1210.3591, arXiv:1401.7336]. It is a fast-rotating system [arXiv:1210.3591] that exhibits an asymmetric rotation curve [arXiv:1401.7336] and a central kinematic anomaly [arXiv:0812.3272, arXiv:1401.7336].

sources.summary: VCC 1036 has an inclined disc, nearly solid-body inner rotation, constant outer velocity dispersion, and a prominent kinematically decoupled core.

quotes.quote: VCC 1036 = NGC 4436 (Fig figvcc1036 ). The inclined disc is reported by . Nearly solid-body rotation out to 1 r_e changes into the flattening of the rotation curve outwards. The velocity dispersion stays constant beyond 0.3 r_e at a level of 50 km s ^ 1 . This galaxy exhibits a prominent KDC.

sources.summary: VCC 1036 is a flat central-cluster dE(di) whose maps confirm fast rotation, reject a reported central dispersion drop, and show central metal-line peaks.

quotes.quote: VCC 1036 , of the dE(di) type, is a very flat object that lies in the central parts of the cluster. It is a well-studied galaxy, previously analyzed by S02, G03, Z04, and C09. In agreement with these studies we find that the galaxy is fast-rotating. We do not confirm the presence of a central drop reported by C09. The H map is flat, and Fe5015 map shows a central increase/peak, which is yet more pronounced in the Mg b map.

sources.summary: The paper treats VCC 1036 as an unusual Virgo dwarf whose asymmetric rotation curve contains a significant central kinematic anomaly that does not resemble a symmetric KDC.

quotes.quote: The remaining galaxy, VCC 1036, is different from the other galaxies (see Figure appVCC1036 ). The rotation curve of VCC 1036 is asymmetric in two ways: (1) one side is rotating more rapidly than the other; and (2) one side is kinematically regular in the center while the other side is not. The statistical parameter S(R) for this galaxy has only three values where V_ inner (<R) and V_ outer (>R) have opposite signs. There is not a clear peak among these three values. Thus, while there is a statistically significant anomaly in the center of this galaxy, it does not resemble a symmetric KDC.

gmw_00001243.name: VCC 1087
gmw_00001243.overview: VCC 1087 is a flattened, nucleated dwarf elliptical galaxy [arXiv:0812.3272, arXiv:1205.3176] that exhibits no internal substructures [arXiv:0812.3272, arXiv:1210.3591]. The galaxy displays very little rotation in its inner regions [arXiv:0812.3272, arXiv:1210.3591], although its outer globular cluster system shows rotational support [arXiv:1205.3176, arXiv:1210.3591].

sources.summary: VCC 1087 is a flattened nucleated dwarf with little rotation; low-SNR data complicate population gradients, and SDSS and HFA population estimates differ because of seeing effects.

quotes.quote: VCC 1087 = IC 3381 (Fig figvcc1087 ). This flattened nucleated dwarf with no substructures reported by has very little if any rotation. The low signal-to-noise ratio of the data makes difficult to study the variations of the stellar population along the radius. We notice that the stellar population parameters derived from the SDSS DR6 spectrum fitting are very different from those estimated from the HFA spectra. This, however, may be explained as the seeing effect, which smoothed the nucleus.

sources.summary: The paper discusses VCC 1087 as one of three Virgo dEs whose rotating globular-cluster systems suggest a discy origin, while its blue-cluster frequency T_N=19 challenges harassment-driven mass-loss scenarios.

quotes.quote: For instance, Beasley2009 show that the GCSs of three Virgo dEs (VCC 1261, VCC 1087 and VCC 1528) exhibit significant rotational support, possibly indicating a discy origin for these systems. They propose that a combination of mass loss due to harassment and subsequent stellar population aging can explain the high (v/ ) of their GCSs, and their compatibility with the Tully-Fisher relation of late-type galaxies. Data from the ACSVCS however indicate that VCC 1261, VCC 1087 and VCC 1528 have T_ N,blue = 6, 19 and 23, respectively. These GC frequencies are 2-7 times larger than the typical values found in field disc galaxies (see Fig. fig:harass_lsb ), so any mass loss scenario would require the progenitors to preferentially lose disc mass (with respect to GCs) by these factors in order to produce comparable remnants -- an unfeasible scenario according to our analysis.

sources.summary: VCC 1087 is treated as a central flattened nucleated dwarf with little inner rotation but significant globular-cluster rotation at larger radii.

quotes.quote: VCC 1087 , centrally located, is another well-known flattened nucleated dwarf, this time, however, with no substructures (L06) and little apparent rotation as reported previously by S02, G03, C09 and T11. Our maps do show some level of rotation at the edge of the field, it is, however, almost consistent with zero. Interestingly, results from the analysis of globular clusters located at up to 4--7 effective radii reveal a significant level of rotation at those larger distances. It is, thus, possible that our maps look flat due to insufficient radial coverage.

gmw_00001244.name: VCC 1261
gmw_00001244.overview: VCC 1261 is a flattened dwarf elliptical galaxy in the Virgo Cluster [arXiv:1210.3591, arXiv:1401.7336] that exhibits no large-scale rotation [arXiv:0812.3272, arXiv:1210.3591]. It hosts a small, rapidly rotating kinematically decoupled core [arXiv:0812.3272, arXiv:1401.7336] associated with a central velocity dispersion drop [arXiv:0812.3272, arXiv:1210.3591] and faint emission lines [arXiv:0812.3272].

sources.summary: VCC 1261 resembles an enlarged VCC 917, with a small rapidly rotating kinematically decoupled core, a dispersion drop, young metal-rich center, and faint emission lines.

quotes.quote: VCC 1261 = NGC 4482 (Fig figvcc1261 ). No substructures were reported by in this galaxy. This object looks like an enlarged version of VCC 917, giving another example of a small rapidly rotating KDC in a flattened spheroid with a very low, if any, large-scale rotation. As in VCC 917, the KDC is associated with a velocity dispersion drop, younger and much more metal rich stellar population than in the peripheral parts of the galaxy. Faint emission lines are seen in the fitting residuals, suggesting the presence of warm ISM in this object.

sources.summary: VCC 1261 is highlighted as a flattened dwarf with no rotation within one effective radius, a possible extended central dispersion drop, and central H and Mg b peaks.

quotes.quote: VCC 1261 is the only galaxy in our sample, which, despite significant flattening, truly exhibits no signs of rotation out to 1 effective radius (previously noted by G03, Z04, C09 and T11). C09 velocity curve and sigma profile suggest a possible presence of a small kinematically decoupled component. Our data, as well as those of G03 and T11, do show some signs of a central drop, though it is more spatially extended and not as deep. Our line-strength maps show a central peak in the H and Mg b maps, suggestive of a rather strong positive age and negative Z gradient. The galaxy's GCs were studied by with the similar findings as for VCC 1087 above.

sources.summary: The paper discusses VCC 1261 as a Virgo dE with a literature KDC, two projected neighbors, and radial-velocity differences indicating the three galaxies are not bound.

quotes.quote: VCC 1261 VCC 1261 has a radial velocity of 1799.4 0.3 km s ^ 1 (Paper II) and has two close neighbors in projection. VCC 1200 is an irregular galaxy located at 42 kpc ( 9' ) from VCC 1261 and has a radial velocity of 123 km s ^ 1 . VCC 1269 is a dE located at 71 kpc ( 15' ) and has a radial velocity of 633 km s ^ 1 . The large velocity differences among these three galaxies indicate that they are not gravitationally bound to one another in the present time.

Combined with the KDCs found in VCC 510 by Thomas06 , VCC 917 and VCC 1261 by Chil09 , and in VCC 1475 by Koleva11 , this makes a total of six dEs (defined as early-type galaxies in the luminosity range 19 < M_r < -16 ) with a significant or marginal detection of a KDC in the Virgo cluster.

sources.summary: The paper discusses VCC 1261 as one of three Virgo dEs whose rotating globular-cluster systems suggest a discy origin, while its blue-cluster frequency T_N=6 challenges harassment-driven mass-loss scenarios.

quotes.quote: For instance, Beasley2009 show that the GCSs of three Virgo dEs (VCC 1261, VCC 1087 and VCC 1528) exhibit significant rotational support, possibly indicating a discy origin for these systems. They propose that a combination of mass loss due to harassment and subsequent stellar population aging can explain the high (v/ ) of their GCSs, and their compatibility with the Tully-Fisher relation of late-type galaxies. Data from the ACSVCS however indicate that VCC 1261, VCC 1087 and VCC 1528 have T_ N,blue = 6, 19 and 23, respectively. These GC frequencies are 2-7 times larger than the typical values found in field disc galaxies (see Fig. fig:harass_lsb ), so any mass loss scenario would require the progenitors to preferentially lose disc mass (with respect to GCs) by these factors in order to produce comparable remnants -- an unfeasible scenario according to our analysis.

gmw_00001245.name: VCC 1431
gmw_00001245.overview: VCC 1431 is a very round, non-rotating dwarf elliptical galaxy located in the center of its cluster [arXiv:1210.3591, arXiv:1506.05810]. It is composed exclusively of an old stellar population, with no star formation activity occurring for at least the last 10 billion years [arXiv:1506.05810].

sources.summary: The paper describes VCC 1431 as an old dwarf elliptical lacking recent star formation, consistent with early stripping plus tidal heating or compact in-cluster evolution.

quotes.quote: One of our cluster objects, VCC 1431, appears to be composed exclusively of an old population ( 10-12 Gyr).
