Combining this with our earlier dynamical results, we conclude that the galaxy was either ram-pressure stripped early on in its evolution in a group environment and subsequently tidally heated, or that it evolved in situ in the cluster's central parts, compact enough to avoid tidal disruption.

VCC 1431 is an interesting case since here the recovered age of the younger population practically coincides with that of the old one. No significant SF activity seems to have taken place there at least in the last 10 Gyr in the center and 12-13 Gyr in the outskirts.
Finally, one of our galaxies, VCC 1431, does not show any sign of younger populations across the probed spatial extent ( 1.5 effective radii).

sources.summary: VCC 1431 is described as a round, central, non-rotating dwarf with a small central dispersion increase and a Fe5015 peak but flat H and Mg b maps.

quotes.quote: VCC 1431 is a very round, non-rotating galaxy in the center of the cluster. The galaxy has previously been analyzed by T11. We note a small increase in towards the galaxy's center, which is however, consistent with zero to within the errors. The line-strength maps show a clear central peak in the Fe5015 map, while the H and Mg b maps are flat.

gmw_00001246.name: VCC 1861
gmw_00001246.overview: VCC 1861 is an almost perfectly spherical dwarf elliptical galaxy [arXiv:1210.3591]. It is classified as a non-rotator with no detected substructures, though it exhibits weak negative metal-line gradients [arXiv:1210.3591].

sources.summary: VCC 1861 is an almost spherical non-rotator whose apparent lack of rotation may be projection-related, with no reported substructures and weak negative metal-line gradients.

quotes.quote: VCC 1861 is an almost perfectly spherical non-rotator (with an average V< 10 km/s), but it must be remembered that, like in the case of VCC 1431 above, we may be viewing the galaxy face-on, in which case we would be unable to detect any (possible) rotation. No substructures are present as reported by L06. Our line-strength maps show a small negative Fe5015 and Mg b gradient.

gmw_00001247.name: VCC 2048
gmw_00001247.overview: VCC 2048 is a strongly flattened, nucleated early-type galaxy located in the outskirts of its cluster [arXiv:0812.3272, arXiv:1210.3591]. It features an edge-on morphology [arXiv:2010.12208] with a fast-rotating inner region that is kinematically decoupled from its outer body [arXiv:0812.3272].

sources.summary: VCC 2048 is a strongly flattened nucleated galaxy with a possible bar or embedded disc; its fast-rotating inner region appears kinematically decoupled from the outer body.

quotes.quote: VCC 2048 = IC 3773 (Fig figvcc2048 ). There is an evidence for a bar or edge-on embedded disc reported by . This nucleated galaxy is strongly flattened. Its inner region, corresponding to the disc demonstrates fast solid-body rotation and looks kinematically decoupled from the outer parts of the galaxy rotating in the same sense, similar to the structures observed by in the two dE galaxies in groups. There is a pronounced drop in the central region of the galaxy.

sources.summary: VCC 2048 is described as an outskirts flattened disky rotator and possible S0-to-dE transition object, with mostly flat line strengths except central Mg b.

quotes.quote: VCC 2048 is a flattened disky rotator situated in the cluster's outskirts. Previous kinematic analyses are found in S02 and C09. The latter finds a pronounced drop in the center. The drop would be undetectable for us at the claimed minimum level (20 km/s). Our line-strength maps seem fairly flat, with the exception of the Mg b map which shows a slight increase towards the center. VCC 2048 is the galaxy considered by Kormendy Bender (2011) to be a transition object and a ``missing link'' between the S0 and dE (Sph in their notation) classes.

sources.summary: The paper treats IC 3773 as a CIR-correlation outlier, noting its central spheroidal component behaves unlike a bulge and its edge-on morphology may affect CIR estimation.

quotes.quote: In the case of the third outlier IC 3773 (marked 6 in Figure fig:figure1 (b)), the central spheroidal component is known to behave not like a bulge 2012ApJS..198....2K .

In this case, six galaxies ( IC 3773 (marked 6 in Figure fig:figure1 ()) NGC 0404 (1), NGC 0855 (7), NGC 1316 (8), NGC 2787 (9) and NGC 5866 (5)) are showing deviation from the linear relation. Edge-on like morphology of NGC 5866, IC 3773 and NGC 0855 may affect the estimation of their CIR.
Galaxies NGC 5866 (marked 5 in Figure fig:figure2 ) and IC 3773 (6) are outliers here also.

gmw_00001248.name: NGC 3073
gmw_00001248.overview: NGC 3073 is a dwarf galaxy [arXiv:1509.00350] classified as an early-type or S0 system [arXiv:1210.3591] that exhibits high levels of star formation [arXiv:1509.00350] and strong emission lines [arXiv:1706.00014, arXiv:1210.3591]. It features a long, curved, cometary H I tail [arXiv:1509.00350, arXiv:0908.3979] likely produced by ram-pressure stripping from its larger neighbor, NGC 3079 [arXiv:2009.09047, arXiv:0908.3979].

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 3073 as a dwarf SAB0 west of NGC 3079 with young stars, high star formation, and a long, curved two-stream H I tail.

quotes.quote: NGC 3073 is a dwarf SAB0 galaxy located about 10 arcmin ( 50 kpc) to the west of NGC 3079. The optical spectrum of NGC 3073 shows a young stellar population and high levels of star formation for its kind Ho2009,McDermid2015 . b1 revealed an elongated \ tail in this galaxy pointing away from NGC 3079, but aligned with the nucleus of NGC 3079.

The \ tail is clearly visible and turns out to be even longer than in b1 . However, the tail also seems to consist of two streams. This can be seen in the channel maps shown in Fig. \ f:2tails (from 1132 to 1198 ) and in Fig. \ f:tail . The significant curvature of the tail is also visible in Fig. \ f:tail . The tail extends to a projected length of about 5 arcmin (25.5 kpc) and it bends at the end giving a ``cometary" appearance to the galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 3073 as the one sample galaxy needing significant emission correction and compares its derived line-strength indices with prior survey measurements despite emission-related uncertainty.

quotes.quote: The only significant emission is present in one of our field galaxies (NGC 3073, see Fig. example-spectra ), with a few more objects showing moderate amounts of emission and/or presence of [ O iii ] lines. The majority of galaxies are essentially emission-free.

The only galaxy for which a significant emission correction was necessary was the field galaxy NGC 3073, both in the as well as [ O iii ] . NGC 3073 was also observed as part of the survey, with 2.5 hrs exposure time (vs. 5.5 hrs in our program). The comparison of calculated 1 integrated line-strengths shows a discrepancy for the and Fe5015 lines, with our measurements being ca. 0.7 higher, which constitutes a significant difference. However, given the galaxy's particularly strong emission, the removal of which is the largest source of uncertainty in the derived line strengths, which we estimate can be up to 0.5 , combined with the fixed continuum correction uncertainty of 0.1 (assumed after kuntschner:2010 ) and the calculated random error of 0.12 , the two measurements can be reconciled.

sources.summary: NGC 3073 is a field galaxy near NGC 3079, with ambiguous early-type/dwarf classification, gas-distorted isophotes, an H I tail, and confirmed low-level rotation.

quotes.quote: NGC 3073 is a field galaxy in the vicinity of the larger NGC 3079 (with their projected separation of 10 arcmin). The galaxy has previously been observed by T11 and . The classification of this galaxy varies from source to source: SAB0 ^- in , S0 in , and T11 classify it as dE(di). The galaxy's integrated intensity isophotes are distorted by the presence of gas which is later seen in the spectra in the form of strong emission lines . show the H i pattern for NGC 3073 where a tail is present pointing away from its large neighbor. Small level of rotation was previously detected by S02 and T11, and is indeed confirmed here.

sources.summary: The paper discusses NGC 3073 as NGC 3079's companion, arguing its cometary H I tail is more plausibly stripped by NGC 3079's wind than by the hot halo alone.

quotes.quote: The wind also likely affects the companion galaxy, NGC 3073. Shafi2015 argued that an ambient hot density of 1.3 10^ 2 cm ^ 2 is needed to explain the cometary H 1 tail of NGC 3073 through ram-pressure stripping due to infall. The hot density profile shows that the hot density around NGC 3073 (beyond 25 kpc from NGC 3079) is at least an order of magnitude too small. In contrast, the ram pressure from v_ wind 500 km s ^ 1 and a density of 5 10^ 4 cm ^ 3 is likely, but barely, sufficient to produce the tail based on the arguments in Irwin1987 . If NGC 3073 is in the middle of the wind, then v_ wind could be substantially higher, which would strengthen the case for wind stripping.

sources.summary: The paper reports a long curved HI tail in NGC 3073, interpreting its cometary appearance as ram-pressure effects from the super-wind of NGC 3079.

quotes.quote: NGC 3073: Interestingly enough, an extended HI tail was seen in NGC 3073 that is much longer than seen in previous observations with significant curvature (see Fig. f:totHI ). As suggested by the HI tail of this galaxy can be explained by ram pressure due to the supper-wind from NGC 3079. While the wind ram pressure seems to be responsible for the 'Cometary' appearance of NGC 3073, we have seen a drop in the surface brightness of NGC 3073 along the tail that can not be explained by a steady wind or constant density. Perhaps temporal changes in the wind properties are responsible for this drop.

gmw_00001249.name: ID 0650
gmw_00001249.overview: ID 0650 is an isolated field galaxy with no galactic neighbors within a 30-arcminute projected radius [arXiv:1210.3591]. Kinematic data reveals a large-scale counter-rotating component spanning approximately 1.5 kiloparsecs, alongside slight central peaks in its Fe5015 and Mg b maps [arXiv:1210.3591].

sources.summary: ID 0650 is an isolated field galaxy with first-time kinematic data revealing a large counter-rotating component and slight central Fe5015 and Mg b peaks.

quotes.quote: ID 0650 is a truly isolated field galaxy, with no galactic neighbors within 30 arcmin projected radius. This is the first kinematic data for this object available in the literature. We find a large-scale counter-rotating component with an approximate diameter of 12 arcsec (or 1.5 kpc). The Fe5015 and Mg b maps show a slight central peak.

gmw_00001250.name: ID 0918
gmw_00001250.overview: ID 0918 is an isolated field dwarf elliptical galaxy [arXiv:1506.05810, arXiv:1210.3591] that exhibits misaligned isophotes and a strong counter-rotating core [arXiv:1210.3591]. This kinematically decoupled core correlates with a break in the galaxy's stellar population profiles, suggesting a merger origin [arXiv:1506.05810].

sources.summary: The paper links ID 0918 stellar-population gradients and a kinematically decoupled core to a likely merger origin despite its isolated field environment.

quotes.quote: For one of our field galaxies, ID 0918, we find a correlation between its stellar population and kinematic properties, pointing to a possible merger origin of its kinematically-decoupled core.

The relative isolation of the galaxies - no similarly sized or larger objects within a projected radius of 20 arcmin - makes the suggested merger in principle a highly unlikely event. ID 0918 must, therefore, be a result of a particular coincidence of progenitor galaxies' initial orbits which led to the suggested merger. No other theory currently exists that would be able to explain the above findings.

We find a correlation between the stellar population and kinematic properties of the field galaxy ID 0918. The metallicity and age profiles show a break at a radius which corresponds to the size of the KDC we earlier found in this galaxy rys:2013 . We believe it to be an argument in favor of a merger origin of the KDC.

sources.summary: ID 0918 is a field galaxy with a nearby projected companion, misaligned isophotes, a newly discovered strong counter-rotating component, and central Fe5015 and Mg b peaks.

quotes.quote: ID 0918 is a field galaxy with the closest galactic companion at a projected distance of 20 arcmin, also with no kinematic data available so far. The integrated intensity isophotes show a slight misalignment of the inner and outer parts, the latter agree with the galaxy's rotation pattern. We report a discovery of a strong counter-rotating component, whose approximate size is 14 arcsec (or 1.1 kpc) along the major axis. The line-strength maps show a strong central peak in the Fe5015 and Mg b maps.

gmw_00001251.name: NGC 2778
gmw_00001251.overview: Although historically classified as an elliptical galaxy, NGC 2778 is better described as a lenticular galaxy [arXiv:1110.1275] or an S0 galaxy with a large-scale disk [arXiv:1311.1530]. It contains a weak bar structure [arXiv:0801.1548] and hosts a supermassive black hole with an unresolved sphere of influence [arXiv:1011.5077].

sources.summary: The paper finds NGC 2778 has an unresolved sphere of influence; adding a dark halo improves the fit but removes the formal significance of the black-hole detection.

quotes.quote: NGC 2778. This galaxy already had the least confident black hole detection in G03. Assuming the value for M_ of our no DM halo model, we do not resolve the black hole's sphere of influence ( R_ inf /d_ res =0.2 ). Including a DM halo in the model improves the fit significantly, but the significance of the black hole detection disappears. However, the previous M_ estimate is still fully consistent with the 1 upper limit of M_ , up =2.99 10^7 M_ that we derive for NGC 2778 under the presence of a DM halo. This behavior might indicate the need to properly resolve R_ inf when a DM halo is included to properly determine M_ .

sources.summary: The paper gives K-band decomposition notes for NGC2778, describing its morphology, fitted components, residual structures, and why the adopted model is preferred over simpler alternatives.

quotes.quote: NGC2778 (Fig. fig:NGC2778 ) is classified as an elliptical in the RC3 catalog, but we unambiguously identify it as an S0 with a large-scale disk. Furthermore, the 1D profiles (in particular the inflection in surface-brightness and the peak in ellipticity at 6 ) and residual image reveal a bar. Although the ^2 decreases only slightly by including the latter, we adopt the improved model on grounds that the bulge flux is overestimated by 30 when the bar is omitted.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 2778 as a disk/barred galaxy with a weak bar and a large offset from the barless black-hole relation.

quotes.quote: M32 may be a stripped S0 galaxy (Bekki et al. \ 2001; Graham 2002), while NGC 2778 is a disk galaxy (Rix, Carollo Freeman 1999) as is NGC 4564 (Trujillo et al. \ 2004; see also figure 6 in Graham Driver 2007a).

Intriguingly, all of these five disk galaxies have been identified in the literature as containing bars. They are: the Milky Way (e.g., L o pez-Corredoira et al. \ 2007, their Fig.5); NGC 1023 (Debattista et al. \ 2002, their Fig. 4b; Sil'chenko 1999); NGC 2778 (weak bar peaks at 5 ; Rest et al. \ 2001, their Fig.
However the barred galaxy NGC 1023 has an offset of nearly 0.7 dex but M_ NC /M_ bh 0.1 , while the barred galaxy NGC 2778 has no nuclear cluster but is offset by 0.9 dex.

sources.summary: The paper discusses NGC2778 as formerly classified elliptical but better described as a lenticular-like bulge-plus-disk system, supported by kinematic studies.

quotes.quote: NGC2778 has been classified as an elliptical galaxy but GD07 GD07 derive their major-axis surface brightness profiles via fitting elliptical isophotes using IRAF ELLIPSE . showed that it can be described better with a S e rsic bulge n_ bulge =1.6 plus a exponential disk which indicate a lenticular galaxy. This conclusion is also supported also from kinematical studies ( tex:RC99 ).

gmw_00001252.name: NGC 4473
gmw_00001252.overview: NGC 4473 is an elongated early-type elliptical galaxy in the Virgo cluster [arXiv:1004.4439, arXiv:1310.2607] featuring disky isophotes [arXiv:1105.4809, arXiv:1110.1275] and a central stellar disk [arXiv:1011.5077]. It is a rare double-sigma galaxy [arXiv:1506.07882, arXiv:1601.02597] containing two counter-rotating stellar components [arXiv:astro-ph/0703533, arXiv:1310.2607] and a complex, triaxial outer halo [arXiv:1308.3531, arXiv:1712.05833].

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 4473 as a double-sigma galaxy whose globular-cluster kinematics show persistent counter-rotating structure, a sharp radial transition, and a distinct halo out to large radii.

quotes.quote: NGC 4473 is a so--called double sigma (2 ) galaxy, i.e. a galaxy with rare, double peaks in its 2D stellar velocity dispersion. Here, we present the globular cluster (GC) kinematics in NGC 4473 out to 10 R_e (effective radii) using data from combined HST /ACS and Subaru/Suprime--Cam imaging and Keck/DEIMOS spectroscopy. We find that the 2 nature of NGC 4473 persists up to 3 R_e , though it becomes misaligned to the photometric major axis. We also observe a significant offset between the stellar and GC rotation amplitudes. This offset can be understood as a co--addition of counter--rotating stars producing little net stellar rotation.

We identify a sharp radial transition in the GC kinematics at 4 R_e suggesting a well defined kinematically distinct halo. In the inner region ( <4 R_e ), the blue GCs rotate along the photometric major axis, but in an opposite direction to the galaxy stars and red GCs. In the outer region ( >4 R_e ), the red GCs rotate in an opposite direction compared to the inner region red GCs, along the photometric major axis, while the blue GCs rotate along an axis intermediate between the major and minor photometric axes.

sources.summary: The paper finds NGC 4473 has decreasing major-axis velocity, strong k5, residuals suggesting a counter-rotating component, and likely two-component stellar rotation.

quotes.quote: NGC 4473 is our final example. The decrease in the velocity along the major axis coincides with the rise of the flattening and k_5 . The kinematic flattening does not match well the flattening of the photometric ellipses, although the position angles agree very well in the region of the velocity decrease.

NGC 4473 is not as clear a case. The velocity map (Fig.  f:residuals ) does not show any clear signature of the two kinematic components which are inferred from the higher-order kinemetric terms. The residual map (Fig.  f:residuals ), however, shows that the residuals are opposite to the bulk rotation, suggesting that the two components are actually counter-rotating. This configuration can naturally explain the observed decrease of rotation with radius. It is thus likely that NGC 4473 is made up of two components: a dominant one responsible for the bulk rotation of the stars, and a lighter counter-rotating one (perhaps a disk because of its confined contribution along the major axis) which brings more weight to large radii.

sources.summary: The paper places NGC 4473 in class E, describing it as an old rare 2-sigma galaxy with central mild rotation and a complex largely non-rotating halo.

quotes.quote: Three galaxies (NGC 3608, 4365 and 4473) have been assigned class E. The mass growth of class E galaxies is expected to be dominated by recent gas-poor major mergers, although minor mergers also contribute. They should contain predominately old stars with little, or no, younger stars. This class of slow rotators includes simulated galaxies that reveal kinematic twists, minor axis rotation, KDCs and the rare 2- galaxies.

Indeed, NGC 3608 and NGC 4365 are known to harbour KDCs, and NGC 4473 is one of only 4 of galaxies in the ATLAS ^ 3D sample to reveal a double peak in the 2D velocity dispersion map (i.e. a 2- galaxy). All three have old mass-weighted ages (McDermid et al. 2015). The kinematics of the stars and globular clusters in NGC 4473 reveal mild rotation in the very central regions, which gives way to a complex largely non-rotating halo (Foster et al. 2013; Alabi et al. 2015).

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 4473 as a special early-type system with tangential anisotropy, peculiar major-axis kinematics, and a counterrotating stellar component contributing about 30 percent of the modeled mass.

quotes.quote: However two galaxies, NGC 4473 and NGC 4550, stand out for being dominated by tangential dispersion. Understanding what makes some object special is key to understand the normal galaxies. In fig:models we show the data versus model comparison for the galaxy NGC 4550, while the same comparison for NGC 4473 was shown in Fig. 2 of cmd05 . Along the major axis both galaxies show a peculiar decrease in the V field and a corresponding increase in the field.

Integrating the solution over the whole model we find that in NGC 4450 the counterrotating component constitutes about 50 of the total mass, while in the case of NGC 4473 the counterrotating component is about 30 of the total mass.

Remarkably, only two galaxies fall significantly below the magenta line for 0.3 . These are the fast-rotator NGC 4473 (marginally) and the special slow-rotator NGC 4550. This was expected from the fact that these two galaxies are dominated by tangential anisotropy ( fig:anisotropy ), due to the presence of two counterrotating disks ( fig:integral_space ).

sources.summary: The paper highlights NGC 4473's counter-rotating inner discs, complex outer rotation, triaxial elliptical interpretation, and possible kinematically decoupled halo.

quotes.quote: In the same vein, Foster13 used kinemetry adapted to sparsely sampled data also see Proctor09,Foster11 to confirm the presence of two counter-rotating disc components in the inner regions of the fast rotator NGC 4473. This galaxy is a so-called 2 galaxy as it exhibits two clear maxima in its velocity dispersion map. They also found a statistically significant minor axis rotating component in its outskirts.

Regardless of the number of sub-components in NGC 4473, it is clear that the combination of both minor and major axis rotation in the outskirts is an unstable configuration and would be short lived in a flattened system. Hence, it is a tell-tale sign of triaxiality and Foster13 concluded that NGC 4473 is a centrally fast rotating triaxial elliptical galaxy.
Again, a case in point is NGC 4473, which shows a very constant photometric position angle at all radii despite its complex distribution of observed kinematic position angles.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 4473 as a massive Virgo early-type galaxy with Fe and Mg residuals indicating Mg/Fe enhancement, translating Yamada et al.'s proxy to about +0.25.

quotes.quote: Our subsample of galaxies (NGC 4387, NGC 4473, NGC 4489 and NGC 4621) cover a range in age, metallicity, abundance ratio and mass, as these galaxies were selected alongside the Colour-Magnitue Relation of the Virgo cluster. For the most massive galaxies, NGC 4473 and NGC 4621 we see in Fig. fig:galaxies positive residuals for the Fe features, e.g., Fe4383, Fe5270, Fe5335, and negative residuals for the Mg feature at 5170 . This shows the well known [ Mg/Fe ] enhancement, which is characteristic of massive galaxies (e.g., Peletier 1989; Worthey, Faber Gonz alez 1992; Gonz alez 1993).

As an example, Table 5 of Yamada et al. lists [ Z _ Mg /Z _ Fe ] 0.6 for NGC 4473 and NGC 4621. Following Fig. fig:heuristic this translates to [ Mg/Fe ] +0.25 on the abundance ratio scale of Thomas et al. (2003).

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 4473 to set its tightest dust-grain lifetime constraint, deriving a silicate-grain upper limit below 46 Myr and discussing dependencies on uncertainty and dust temperature.

quotes.quote: Our dust mass detection limit of M_ d 8.7 10^3 M_ for silicate grains results in grain lifetimes as given in Table table:1 . The tightest constraint, of <46 25 Myr , is provided by NGC 4473. The error reflects the uncertainties in v_ and L_4 . In Table table:3 we provide the values used to arrive at this grain lifetime and their dependencies. For dust temperatures in the range 20-40 K , for example, the grain lifetime lies in the range 69-31 Myr .

sources.summary: The paper links NGC 4473 black-hole mass behavior to a nuclear disk, models its central exponential disk, and says this disk affects the difference between halo models.

quotes.quote: The most extreme case is NGC 4473 probably due to the presence of a nuclear disk, with an increase of a factor of 1.8 when a DM halo is included.

NGC 4473. NGC 4473 shows evidence for a central stellar disk both in the imaging and the kinematics, as discussed by G03. We followed G03 and include a central exponential disk and also assumed a galaxy inclination of 71^ , as found for the disk component. Thus this galaxy is the only case in our sample not modeled with an edge-on inclination. The presence of the disk has a distinct influence on the measured black hole mass causing a relatively large difference between the models with and without a DM halo.

sources.summary: NGC 4473 is an E5 galaxy with discy isophotes, merger-related peculiarities, cold stellar-disc evidence, a younger metal-rich core, and declining metallicity.

quotes.quote: NGC 4473 = VCC 1231 This is an E5 galaxy with discy isophotes. pinkney2003 , argue that this galaxy underwent a recent merger, because of its photometric peculiarities. They also find photometric and kinematic evidences for a cold stellar disc. Our analysis reveals a younger and more metal rich core. The metallicity decreases outwards, changing its slope at 5 arcsec, where the maximum velocity ( 60 ) is observed. The age is constant outside of this radius, but drops to about 6 Gyr inwards.

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC4473 as an elliptical with disky isophotes, unusual surface-brightness structure, a counter-rotating stellar disk, and differing Sersic-index measurements.

quotes.quote: NGC4473 is an elliptical galaxy with primarily disky isophotes ( tex:BD88,tex:BF94 ). Its unusual surface profile brightness has aroused plenty of interest, e.g., tex:BG96,tex:FC06a,tex:KC06 . The distribution of light in the inner part of the galaxy is dominated by a counter-rotating stellar disk ( tex:CE07 ). KF09 modelled the galaxy by excluding the inner 23   arcsec and measured a S e rsic index of n=4.0 in agreement with the 2D profiling of H09. GD07 found a S e rsic index of n=2.73 .

sources.summary: The paper uses wide-radius stellar kinematics to show that NGC 4473, a fast-rotator elliptical, undergoes an outer kinematic transition with minor- and major-axis rotation, implying a triaxial kinematically distinct halo.

quotes.quote: Here we study the Fast Rotator elliptical galaxy: NGC 4473. Using slitlets, we obtain galaxy light kinematics out to unprecedentedly large galactocentric radii (2.5 effective radii). While we confirm the IFU results in the central regions, we find that at large galactocentric radii NGC 4473 exhibits a kinematic transition. In the outskirts, we observe clear minor and major axis rotation, a tell-tale sign of triaxiality, which agrees well with the galaxy's Hubble type. This outer `kinematically distinct halo' (KDH) may be expected from simulations of galaxy formation, and in this system contains around one-third of the stellar light.

sources.summary: NGC 4473 is described as an elongated disky elliptical with off-center dispersion peaks, two counter-rotating disks, declining inner rotation, and a triaxial kinematically distinct halo.

quotes.quote: This elongated elliptical (E5) galaxy presents disky isophotes Bender:1988vj . It is an interesting case of a so-called `` 2 '' galaxy Krajnovic:2011jj which has off-center velocity dispersion peaks. The favored explanation for this feature is the presence of two counter-rotating disks Cappellari:2007bn . The inner rotation quickly declines with radius, eventually giving way to two-component retrograde major-axis and minor-axis rotation in the outskirts. This outer kinematic configuration is a tell-tale sign of triaxiality, and the galaxy overall may be considered a case of a ``kinematically distinct halo'' Foster:2013 .

sources.summary: NGC 4473 is presented as a more massive Virgo elliptical where strong CN and red-side-band absorption affect H measurements, and SSP models fit as well as complex histories.

quotes.quote: Since the over-abundance of CN and the resulting absorption tends to increase with the mass of the galaxy see e.g. sbla03,tol09 , the inclusion of the more massive NGC 4473 serves to show how significant the effect can be. The 0.6 \ difference in H given by the side band method can be seen to be generated by the increase in absorption in the red side-band region 4120 , of NGC4473. We note again that the CN absorption, identified by is higher in NGC 4473 (CN _1 =0.138 mag), than in NGC 4478 (CN _1 =0.077 mag).
For some galaxies, such as NGC 4365, NGC 4387 and NGC 4473, an SSP is as good as the other models presented here and in fact the parameters recovered by complex SFHs essentially reduce to an SSP.

sources.summary: The paper gives K-band decomposition notes for NGC4473, describing its morphology, fitted components, residual structures, and why the adopted model is preferred over simpler alternatives.

quotes.quote: NGC4473 (Fig. fig:NGC4473 ) is a flattened ( 0.5 ) early-type galaxy with disky isophotes. Its surface-brightness profile is not perfectly fit by a \ model, and the ellipticity profile peaks twice, at 3 and 45 , possibly suggesting the presence of two disks (we note that a disk was also suggested by Ferrarese_etal06b based on HST images). An inner disk is in fact clearly seen in the residuals from a single- \ model.

sources.summary: The paper reports NGC 4473 kinematics, describing its rotation, velocity dispersion, halo behavior, and relevant photometric or interaction features in the early-type-galaxy survey.

quotes.quote: This Virgo galaxy is an E5 disky elliptical. It is well known for its multicomponent central kinematics 2004MNRAS.352..721E : it is a fast but non-regular rotator ( V_ rot ^ MAX =63 , along the photometric major axis) and the velocity dispersion map displays a double peak, which have been interpreted as the presence of a counter-rotating disk 2007MNRAS.379..418C , maybe formed in binary disk major mergers 2011MNRAS.416.1654B . We detect a low amplitude rotation of 30 along a direction tilted with respect to the photometric major axis. The kinematics from integrated light of 2013MNRAS.435.3587F already proved this object to be triaxial.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 4473 as the low-UV-upturn early-type comparison case, with FUV luminosity plausibly dominated by hot subdwarf stars and later augmented in a GC-based model.

quotes.quote: Relevant ETGs representing the bottom and top of this range are NGC 4473 and NGC 4649, respectively. We exclude the strong UV upturns in NGC 1399 and M87 from this exercise, since their FUV emission is due in part to unresolved nuclear emission cart+11 . These two galaxies have the same SSP age to within the uncertainties as determined from their Lick indices (see Tables t:IUEsample and t:SAURONsample ), suggesting that their production rates of EHB stars by stellar evolution are consistent with each other as well. Since NGC 4473 has \ = 6.90 0.06 within R_ eff /2 bure+11 , it constitutes a relevant example of an ETG for which a significant fraction of the FUV luminosity may be due to hot subdwarf stars produced in binary systems.

To determine how many ``average'' FUV-bright massive metal-rich GCs as seen in M87 are needed to produce the ``extra'' FUV luminosity in NGC 4649 relative to NGC 4473, we iteratively add such GCs (for which M_V^0 = -9.64 and M_ FUV ^0 = -4.82 , see above) to NGC 4473 and re-evaluate its integrated M_V and M_ FUV until we produce a galaxy with \ = 5.51.

sources.summary: The paper says VCC 1231 has distinct kinematic components, peculiar photometric and kinematic data, younger central populations, and GC kinematics suggesting a gas-rich major merger.

quotes.quote: VCC 1231 (NGC 4473). This elliptical galaxy is well known for its kinematically distinct components, namely double peaks in the velocity dispersion map. The galaxy shows peculiarities in both its kinematical and photometrical data, which could originate from a recent merger 2003ApJ...596..903P . The spectroscopic result by 2011MNRAS.417.1643K shows that the central part of this galaxy consists of younger and more metal-rich stellar populations compared to the outer part. 2015MNRAS.452.2208A suggested that GCs follow peculiar stellar kinematics, which is evidence of a gas-rich major merger event.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4473 as a core fast rotator with embedded and counter-rotating discs whose profile fitting can yield core, extra-light, or mixed classifications.

quotes.quote: NGC 0524 and NGC 4473 have very clear evidence for embedded discs, both morphologically and kinematically. NGC 0524, has a nuclear dust disc which makes the analysis of the nuclear light profile difficult, but is the only core galaxy with molecular gas 2011MNRAS.414..940Y , while dynamical models for NGC 4473 require two counter-rotating discs 2007MNRAS.379..418C . The cores in these two galaxies seem to be well defined, but the cores might also depend on the actual choice of the fitting range.

For example, 2009ApJS..182..216K showed that in the case of NGC 4473, it is possible to fit the surface brightness profile such as to recover a core, an extra-light (core-less) or a combination of an extra-light profile with an indication for a core (their Figs. 58 (top), 17, and 58 (bottom), respectively).

sources.summary: The paper finds NGC 4473 globular clusters have stronger red-color CaT values than predicted, bluer low-CaT colors than other galaxies, and higher low-CaT Na82 strengths.

quotes.quote: At blue colours the GCs of NGC 1407 and NGC 4278 have weaker CaT values than predicted by Equation eq:CaT_colour . For red colours, NGC 1407, NGC 4278, NGC 4365 and NGC 4649 GCs have weaker CaT strengths than predicted from their colours while NGC 3115, NGC 3377, NGC 4473 and NGC 4494 have stronger than predicted CaT values. The shapes of the colour--CaT relations of NGC 1407 and NGC 4494 are radically different as first noticed by 2011MNRAS.415.3393F . As seen in Figure fig:stacking_CaT1 , at low CaT values NGC 3115, NGC 3377 and NGC 4473 show bluer colours compared to the other galaxies while at high CaT values NGC 3377 and NGC 4494 also show bluer colours than the other galaxies. Additionally, at low CaT values NGC 3115, NGC 3377 and NGC 4473 show higher Na82 strengths than the other galaxies.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4473 as a highly elongated Virgo elliptical whose HST data had reported bimodality; this analysis finds non-compelling bimodality but similar mean color peaks.

quotes.quote: NGC 4473 This is a highly elongated Virgo elliptical. kun99 reports bimodality from HST data. Our detection of bimodality is statistically not compelling, but we find similar mean color peaks to Kundu.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4473 as having a complex velocity-dispersion field with major-axis high dispersion, a central drop, outward velocity decrease, and lower h4 region.

quotes.quote: The velocity dispersion field exhibits a complex morphology, with a region of high dispersion along the major-axis which widens at larger radii, and a central drop inside 3 , not observed in BSG94 . The widening in the dispersion map is particularly significant outside 10 \ where the mean velocity is observed to decrease outwards. This also corresponds to a region of lower h_4 .

sources.summary: The paper says NGC 4473 is well described by a small-parameter Nuker law, while the alternate VCS model overestimates its central cusp slope.

quotes.quote: In the cases of NGC 4473, 4478, and 4486B, fitted models to galaxies that we classified as core systems. NGC 4473 is well described by a Nuker law with a small parameter. The model in contrast clearly over estimates the central '; again the pattern of residuals that we see here is obvious in VCS Figure 103 as well.

gmw_00001253.name: NGC 4649
gmw_00001253.overview: NGC 4649, also known as M60, is a giant elliptical galaxy located in the Virgo Cluster [arXiv:1705.02389, arXiv:2111.02430]. It hosts a low-luminosity active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2311.06126] and is currently undergoing a tidal interaction with its companion galaxy, NGC 4647 [arXiv:1306.6490, arXiv:1401.3747].

sources.summary: The paper studies NGC4649's hot gas to infer a central black-hole mass, arguing that the temperature spike and agreement with stellar kinematics show nearly hydrostatic gas in a relaxed giant elliptical.

quotes.quote: We present a \ study of the hot ISM in the giant elliptical galaxy . In common with other group-centred ellipticals, its temperature profile rises with radius in the outer parts of the galaxy, from 0.7 keV at 2 kpc to 0.9 keV by 20 kpc. However, within the central 2 kpc the trend reverses and the temperature peaks at 1.1 keV within the innermost 200 pc. Under the assumption of hydrostatic equilibrium, we demonstrate that the central temperature spike arises due to the gravitational influence of a quiescent central super-massive black hole.

We constrain the black hole mass ( ) to (3.35^ +0.67 _ 0.95 ) 10^9 \ (90 confidence), in good agreement with stellar kinematics measurements. This is the first direct measurement of \ based on studies of hydrostatic X-ray emitting gas, which are sensitive to the most massive black holes, and is a crucial validation of both mass-determination techniques. This agreement clearly demonstrates the gas must be close to hydrostatic, even in the very centre of the galaxy, which is consistent with the lack of morphological disturbances in the X-ray image. \ is now one of only a handful of galaxies for which \ has been measured by more than one method.

sources.summary: The paper studies NGC 4649 as a Virgo elliptical, mapping its LMXB and globular-cluster populations and linking outer LMXB excesses to possible companion accretion or tidal encounters.

quotes.quote: We investigate the radial distribution of the low-mass X-ray binary (LMXB) population in the elliptical galaxy NGC 4649, using Chandra and Hubble data to separate the field and globular cluster (GC) populations. GCs with LMXBs have the same radial distribution as the parent red and blue GCs. The radial profile of field LMXBs follows the V band profile within the D25 of NGC 4649.

Our large coverage with both Chandra and HST of the Virgo elliptical NGC 4649 2012ApJ...760...87S, 2013ApJS..204...14L , enables us to revisit this question, with a rich population of several hundreds LMXBs, and a homogeneous and complete GC population. The present work is based on the X-ray source catalog of the elliptical galaxy NGC 4649 by 2013ApJS..204...14L , and on the associated optical catalog of globular clusters detected with HST by 2012ApJ...760...87S .

An alternate possibility is that of a rejuvenation of the field LMXB population of NGC 4649 at large radii, caused by tidal encounters or accretion of companion or satellite galaxies. Such an interaction may be presently beginning with NGC 4647 2013ApJ...768...90L . This scenario is also suggested by the anisotropy we find in the two-dimensional distribution of GCs and LMXBs in NGC 4649 at large radii (D'Abrusco et al 2013, in preparation).

sources.summary: The paper uses deep Chandra data to study NGC 4649, finding cavities, ripples, a ring-like structure, radiatively inefficient nuclear accretion, and evidence for AGN feedback.

quotes.quote: Box 208121, New Haven, CT 06520-8121 4 Department of Physics and Astronomy, Michigan State University, East Lansing, MI 48824, USA 5 Department of Astronomy Astrophysics, 525 Davey Lab, The Pennsylvania State University, University Park, PA 16802, USA Using the deepest available Chandra observations of NGC 4649 we find strong evidences of cavities, ripples and ring like structures in the hot interstellar medium (ISM) that appear to be morphologically related with the central radio emission. The nucleus of NGC 4649 appears to be extremely radiatively inefficient, with highly sub-Bondi accretion flow. NGC 4649, also known as M60, is a nearby We assume a distance to NGC 4649 of ( 16 Mpc ).

Thanks to the relatively small distance and large supermassive black hole (SMBH) mass ( few 10 ^9 M_ ) of NGC 4649, Chandra resolves radii close to the Bondi accretion radius, r_ acc 100-200 pc , at which the gravitational binding energy of a gas element becomes larger than its thermal energy 1952MNRAS.112..195B . Due to its low radio power, and its non-being a central dominant galaxy, NGC 4649 is also an ideal case to investigate whether these correlations, mostly found for radio-bright central dominant galaxies in groups or clusters, work equally well in more ``normal'' elliptical galaxies. In this paper we revisit the properties of the hot ISM of NGC 4649, using much deeper Chandra data with respect to previous studies and updated atomic databases.

Thanks to the long cumulative Chandra pointing on NGC 4649 we have determined the hot gas density and temperature in a wide range of radii, discovering cavities and a ring-like higher-pressure ripple. The clear evidence for AGN feedback on the large scale in NGC 4649 (i.e., the series of cavities, likely more numerous than the N and S ones that will be investigated in detail in Sect. sec:jet_power ) provides support to the idea of repeated AGN heating occurring at the center, possibly producing the temperature peak, for example through shock activity.

sources.summary: The paper studies NGC 4649's globular clusters, finding bimodal colors, gradients, rotation, and kinematic substructure; gas-poor major merger plus satellite accretion reproduces its observations.

quotes.quote: We present new wide-field photometry and spectroscopy of the globular clusters (GCs) around NGC 4649 (M60), the third brightest galaxy in the Virgo cluster. Imaging of NGC 4649 was assembled from a recently-obtained HST /ACS mosaic, and new Subaru/Suprime-Cam and archival CFHT/MegaCam data. About 1200 sources were followed up spectroscopically using combined observations from three multi-object spectrographs: Keck/DEIMOS, Gemini/GMOS and MMT/Hectospec. We confirm 431 unique GCs belonging to NGC 4649, a factor of 3.5 larger than previous datasets and with a factor of 3 improvement in velocity precision.

We confirm significant GC colour bimodality and find that the red GCs are more centrally concentrated, while the blue GCs are more spatially extended. We infer negative GC colour gradients in the innermost 20 kpc and flat gradients out to large radii. Rotation is detected along the galaxy major axis for all tracers: blue GCs, red GCs, galaxy stars and planetary nebulae. We compare the observed properties of NGC 4649 with galaxy formation models.

sources.summary: The paper models M60-UCD1 as an ultra-compact dwarf with an SMBH candidate, predicting cold-cloud formation in its inner galaxy from stellar and accretion radiation conditions.

quotes.quote: We consider two examples: the Nuclear Star Cluster (NSC) in the Milky Way central region (including the gaseous Mini-spiral of Sgr A*), and the Ultra-Compact Dwarf galaxy M60-UCD1.

In M60-UCD1 our model predicts spontaneous formation of cold clouds in the inner part of the galaxy. These cold clouds may survive since the cooling timescale is shorter than the inflow/outflow timescale.

M60-UCD1 is an exceptional example with tentative evidence, based on stellar kinematics, for a supermassive black hole in the core strader2013,seth2014,reines14 . The object has also been detected as a variable X-ray source Luo2013 . We can thus apply to this source a similar analysis as in the previous section. In the first step we again construct a model of the broad-band spectrum emitted from the accretion inflow from moscibrodzka2012 . But in case of M60-UCD1, the X-ray luminosity between 2--10 keV is 1.3 10^ 38 erg s ^ 1 strader2013 , i.e., higher than in Sgr A*. Therefore, we re-scale the broad-band spectra used in the Sgr A* calculations to obtain the observed value of the X-ray luminosity between 2-10 keV for this source. All eight different luminosity states used in our photoionization calculations are presented in Fig. fig:spec2 . In the second step we model the stellar emission of M60-UCD1 nuclear star cluster with Starlight fernandes2011 , taking the total mass of the stars to be 1.2 10^8 M_ seth2014 and assuming the age of the stars to be 13 Gyr strader2013 .

sources.summary: The paper focuses on Chandra observations of M60, finding a leading cold front, gas stripping, a diffuse tail, and filamentary wings consistent with Kelvin-Helmholtz instabilities during infall toward M87.
sources.paper_coordinate_text: M60 ( =12^ h 43^ m 39^ s .6, =11^ 33^ ' 09 ), is a massive elliptical galaxy

quotes.quote: We present Chandra observations of hot gas structures, characteristic of gas stripping during infall, in the Virgo cluster elliptical galaxy M60 (NGC4649) located 1 Mpc east of M87. 0.5-2 keV Chandra X-ray images show a sharp leading edge in the surface brightness 12.4 0.1 kpc north and west of the galaxy center in the direction of M87 characteristic of a merger cold front due to M60's motion through the Virgo ICM. We measured a temperature of 1.00 0.02 keV for abundance 0.5 inside the edge and 1.37^ +0.35 _ 0.19 keV for abundance 0.1 in the Virgo ICM free stream region. We find that the observed jump in surface brightness yields a density ratio n_ in /n_ out =6.44^ +1.04 _ 0.67 between gas inside the edge and in the cluster free stream region. If the edge is a cold front due solely to the infall of M60 in the direction of M87, we find a pressure ratio of 4.7^ +1.7 _ 1.4 and Mach number 1.7^ +0.3 _ 0.3 .

Surface brightness profiles also show the presence of a faint, diffuse gaseous tail. We identify filamentary gaseous wing structures caused by the galaxy's motion through the ICM. The structure and dimensions of these wings are consistent with simulations of Kelvin-Helmholtz instabilities as expected if the gas stripping is close to inviscid.

sources.summary: The paper measures NGC 4649’s FUV and V photometry within half an effective radius, finding a stronger UV upturn than NGC 4473 and modeling it with dissolved FUV-bright clusters.

quotes.quote: To derive \ within R_ eff /2 for NGC 4649, we download GALEX dataset GI3 041008 NGC4621 from the http://galex.stsci.edu/GR6/ STScI/MAST GR6 archive , with an exposure time of 1658 s in the NUV and FUV passbands. This GALEX dataset is available at the MAST archive at 10.17909/T9V68G doi.org/10.17909/T9V68G . Following bure+11 , we carry out surface photometry of NGC 4649 using the ellipse task in http://bit.ly/2i6m11Q IRAF/STSDAS , using the NUV image to perform ellipse fitting followed by imposing those ellipses on the FUV image.

To obtain V band photometry for NGC 4649, we use the B band surface photometry tables of pele+90 in conjunction with the B V colors from burs+87 , interpolating in galactocentric radius. Adopting R_ eff = 74 3 for NGC 4649 burs+87 , we obtain M_ FUV, AB ^0 = -15.50 , M_V^0 = -21.01 , and \ = 5.51 within R_ eff /2 , i.e., 1.39 mag bluer in \ than NGC 4473.

To determine how many ``average'' FUV-bright massive metal-rich GCs as seen in M87 are needed to produce the ``extra'' FUV luminosity in NGC 4649 relative to NGC 4473, we iteratively add such GCs (for which M_V^0 = -9.64 and M_ FUV ^0 = -4.82 , see above) to NGC 4473 and re-evaluate its integrated M_V and M_ FUV until we produce a galaxy with \ = 5.51. We find that this would require 7789 such GCs. These would produce M_V = -19.41 , which is 1.60 mag fainter than the actual light within R_ eff /2 in NGC 4649, equivalent to a V band luminosity fraction f_V = 0.23 .

sources.summary: The paper compares M60-UCD1 with other Virgo UCDs, reporting radio non-detection, Chandra X-ray detections, flux variability, and fundamental-plane tension.

quotes.quote: Here we consider the radio and X-ray emission from three massive UCDs around the Virgo galaxies M59 and M60: M59cO, M59-UCD3, and M60-UCD1, which all have dynamical evidence for SMBHs. We note that no deep radio data exist for the other UCDs with evidence of SMBHs. M60-UCD1 is also undetected, with a local rms of 2.4 Jy bm ^ 1 and a corresponding upper limit of < 7.2 Jy bm ^ 1 (L < 1.17 10^ 34 ) at 5.8 GHz.

M60-UCD1 is detected in all six observations. The total merged dataset, representing 308 ksec of Chandra data, is deep enough to allow spectral fitting. The individual unabsorbed 0.5--10 keV fluxes for M60-UCD1 range over 1.8-- 7.5 10^ 15 erg s ^ 1 cm ^ 2 ( L_X = 0.6 -2.4 10^ 38 erg s ^ 1 , depending on the epoch. The average flux is (3.3^ +0.8 _ 0.7 10^ 15 erg s ^ 1 cm ^ 2 ( L_X = 1.1^ + 0.3 _ 0.2 10^ 38 erg s ^ 1 ). There is compelling evidence for X-ray variability of M60-UCD1, but only at a single epoch: five of the six epochs are consistent with the mean flux, while one (Obs ID 12976) is 6 higher compared to the mean flux.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4649 as a Virgo-cluster E2/S0 galaxy paired with NGC 4647, with misaligned outer PNe/GC kinematics and a likely major-merger origin.

quotes.quote: NGC 4649 (M60) is an elliptical galaxy of E2/S0 morphological type deVaucouleurs1976 , belonging to the Virgo galaxy cluster. NGC 4649 is in a pair with the nearby smaller spiral galaxy, NGC 4647. However there are no clear signs of interactions between the two galaxies, whose optical light morphology does not look disturbed (e.g. Pota2015 ).

Specifically, NGC 4649 shows the largest scatter in the PA _ kin of the PNe and blue GC sub-population with respect to the underlying stars, with clear kinematic twists and minor-axis rotation beyond 2 R_ e . On the other hand, the red GCs of NGC 4649 have PA _ kin that is slightly offset from that of the stars, but it does not show significant variations as a function of the radius (Fig. fig:1D_kinematic_profiles and A7).

NGC 4649 is the only cluster galaxy in our sample and the most massive together with NGC 5846. Therefore, NGC 4649 should be ex-situ dominated at all radii (Class IV galaxy; Pulsoni2020 ). This galaxy is expected to have had a very gas-poor accretion history, with only very small fraction of accreted gas (i.e. <20 ), and to have experienced at least one gas-poor major merger in recent times, i.e. z<1 , that contributed to a large fraction (up to 40 ) of the ex-situ stars Pulsoni2020 .

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 4649 as the reference for a synthetic elliptical-galaxy image, adopting its magnitude, size, velocity dispersion, SBF magnitude, distance, and Sersic-index range.

quotes.quote: Reference galaxy data In order to study the reliability when obtaining SBFs, we have created a synthetic galaxy image using NGC 4649 (VCC 1978) as reference. The apparent i- band magnitude of the galaxy is m_ i = 9.493 0.001 mag (AB) from Sloan Digital Survey abazajian2009seventh , its effective radius is R_ eff =82 arcsec van1991velocity and its velocity dispersion is =330.5 4.6 km s ^ 1 davies1987spectroscopy . The rest of data used to define the galaxy are derived from the work of cantiello2018next and CFHT/MegaCam imaging data from the NGVS survey ferrarese2012next . The MegaCam general specifications state the plate scale at the centre of the field is 0.187 arcsec/pixel, so the effective radius is R_ eff 438 pixels. According to cantiello2018next the SBF magnitude of the galaxy is m_ i = 30.64 0.07 mag (AB) and its distance, derived from the SBF magnitude, is D=16.7 0.6 Mpc.

Aside from the observational values we have gathered, we need other additional parameters to model a synthetic galaxy, such as a Sersic index and a PSF. The observed values of the Sersic index found in the literature for NGC 4649 range approximately from n=3.4 0.5 vika2013megamorph to n=5.36^ +0.38 _ 0.32 kormendy2009structure . So, we choose an intermediate Sersic index value of n=4 , typical of an elliptical galaxy de1953distribution, caon1993shape .

sources.summary: The paper models NGC 4649 as a Virgo giant elliptical using globular-cluster kinematics, comparing Newtonian constant-M/L and MOND Jeans fits and finding both can match the observed dispersion.

quotes.quote: In this paper, we discuss the early-type galaxy NGC 4649 (M60) belonging to the Virgo cluster, for which new observational data have become available (Lee et al. 2008a). This galaxy is more typical than either a field giant elliptical (NGC 3379) or the central galaxy of a cluster (NGC 1399), since it is a typical cluster member. The plan of the paper is as follows: in Sect. 2, we present the data related to NGC 4649; in Sect. 3, we calculate the total mass of this galaxy for Newtonian gravity and the MOND approach and solve the Jeans equation for the constant mass-to-light ratio and MOND approach to determine the best-fit parameters.

The data used in this paper are based on observations of GCs in NGC 4649 presented by Lee et al. (2008a). The sample consists of 121 GCs (83 blue and 38 red GCs). In all of our calculations, we always consider the entire sample, i.e. we do not divide the data into subsets, so that we are able to have a larger number of GCs per bin in our analyses. NGC 4649 (M60) is a giant elliptical galaxy in the Virgo cluster that has a nearby companion, NGC 4647 (Sc galaxy at 2.'5 from the center of NGC 4649).

sources.summary: The paper models NGC 4649 as a rotating elliptical with inward-flowing X-ray gas, a central black hole, a cooling-flow problem, and X-ray isophote flattening from inflow spin-up.

quotes.quote: We describe two-dimensional gasdynamical computations of the X-ray emitting gas in the rotating elliptical galaxy NGC 4649 that indicate an inflow of 1 M_ yr ^ 1 at every radius. Such a large instantaneous inflow cannot have persisted over a Hubble time. The central constant-entropy temperature peak recently observed in the innermost 150 parsecs is explained by compressive heating as gas flows toward the central massive black hole. Since the cooling time of this gas is only a few million years, NGC 4649 provides the most acutely concentrated known example of the cooling flow problem in which the time-integrated apparent mass that has flowed into the galactic core exceeds the total mass observed there.

The recent deep 81ks Chandra observation of the nearby X-ray luminous elliptical galaxy NGC 4649 by Humphrey et al. (2008) led to the detection of a massive central black hole ( M_ bh = 3 10^ 9 M_ ). This supermassive black hole was found for the first time directly from X-ray data by assuming that the hot gas is very nearly in hydrostatic equilibrium. The increased quality of the X-ray observations of NGC 4649 has motivated us to develop a dynamical model for the hot gas flow in this rotating galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4649 as a massive Virgo giant elliptical with nearby NGC 4647, hot X-ray gas, discrete sources, and a well-studied bimodal globular cluster system.

quotes.quote: NGC 4649 Also known as M60, this Virgo Cluster member is a giant elliptical (E2) within the Virgo cluster and located in a sub-group to the East of the main cluster concentration. NGC 4647, a spiral some 2.5 arcmin to the NW of NGC 4649, appears to be a background object since there is no evidence of reddening produced by this spiral on NGC 4649 ( WKC2000 ) and no evidence of interaction between them. The GMOS fields presented here, and in our earlier work ( FFFBBGHSZG04 ), were chosen to minimize the effect of possible contamination of the NGC 4649 GC system by NGC 4647 GCs. With a luminosity of M _B = --21.43, NGC 4649 is the most massive galaxy in our sample.

Based on spectra of the central region, TF2002 used Lick indices to measure an old age of 11 Gyr and a metallicity of [ Fe/H ] = 0.3. NGC 4649 shows strong X-ray emission arising from a hot gas halo ( OFP2001 ). Chandra observations reveal the presence of numerous discrete sources that are mostly identified as low mass binaries (LXMBs) within NGC 4649's globular clusters ( SKISBR2003,RSI2004 ).

sources.summary: The paper models NGC 4649 with NMAGIC, comparing PN, X-ray, and globular-cluster constraints to infer its halo mass, dark matter fraction, and orbital anisotropy.

quotes.quote: We create dynamical models of the massive elliptical galaxy, NGC 4649, using the N-body made-to-measure code, NMAGIC, and kinematic constraints from long-slit and planetary nebula (PN) data. We explore a range of potentials based on previous determinations from X-ray observations and a dynamical model fitting globular cluster (GC) velocities and a stellar density profile. The X-ray mass distributions are similar in the central region but have varying outer slopes, while the GC mass profile is higher in the central region and on the upper end of the range further out. Our models cannot differentiate between the potentials in the central region, and therefore if non-thermal pressures or multi-phase components are present in the hot gas, they must be smaller than previously inferred.

Estimating the mass in stars using photometric information and a stellar population mass-to-light ratio, we infer a dark matter mass fraction in NGC 4649 of 0.39 at 1 R_e (10.5 kpc) and 0.78 at 4 R_e . We find that the stellar orbits are isotropic to mildly radial in the central 6 kpc depending on the potential assumed. Further out, the orbital structure becomes slightly more radial along R and more isotropic along z , regardless of the potential assumed.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes X-ray spectra of the Virgo elliptical NGC 4649/M60, finding declining ISM abundances, dilution by subsolar external gas, and a complex flow and enrichment history.

quotes.quote: We present our X-ray imaging spectroscopic analysis of data from deep Suzaku and XMM-Newton Observatory exposures of the Virgo Cluster elliptical galaxy NGC 4649 (M60), focusing on the abundance pattern in the hot interstellar medium (ISM). All measured elements show a radial decline in abundance, with the possible exception of Oxygen. We construct steady state solutions to the chemical evolution equations that include infall in addition to stellar mass return and SNIa enrichment, and consider recently published SNIa yields. By adjusting a single model parameter to obtain a match to the global abundance pattern in NGC 4649 we infer that introduction of subsolar metallicity external gas has reduced the overall ISM metallicity and diluted the effectiveness of SNIa to skew the pattern towards low /Fe ratios, and estimate the combination of SNIa rate and level of dilution.

As is the case for NGC 4472, NGC 4649 is a giant elliptical galaxy in the Virgo cluster with an old stellar population enhanced in elements -- but with several notable differences both optically and in X-rays. In particular NGC 4649 has a substantial major axis rotation (Brighenti et al. 2009, and references therein), and is considerably more compact in X-rays. We have adjusted our spectral analysis procedures in response to the distinctive X-ray characteristics of NGC 4649, and have revised and expanded our models to consider possible episodes of inflow, and a recently published set of SNIa yields.

sources.summary: The paper reports a 21-million-solar-mass black hole in M60-UCD1, using adaptive-optics kinematics to argue that the bright ultracompact dwarf is a stripped galaxy nucleus.

quotes.quote: Adaptive optics kinematic data of M60-UCD1 show a central velocity dispersion peak above 100 km/s and modest rotation. Dynamical modeling of these data reveals the presence of a supermassive black hole with mass of 21 million solar masses. This is 15 of the object's total mass. The high black hole mass and mass fraction suggest that M60-UCD1 is the stripped nucleus of a galaxy.

The object M60-UCD1 is the brightest ultracompact dwarf galaxy (UCD) currently known with a luminosity L_V = 4.1 10^7 L _ and radius r_e = 24 pc. It lies at a projected distance of 6.6 kpc from the center of the massive elliptical galaxy M60 (Fig. 1), and 16.5 Mpc from us . We obtained integral field spectroscopic data between 2 and 2.4 m of M60-UCD1 with Gemini/NIFS.

sources.summary: The paper uses resolved HST halo stars to measure a TRGB distance to M60, addressing PNLF/SBF discrepancies and noting that the Virgo elliptical hosts rich stellar systems and companions.

quotes.quote: There is a well-known discrepancy in the distance estimation of M60, a giant elliptical galaxy in Virgo: the planetary nebula luminosity function (PNLF) distance moduli for this galaxy are, on average, 0.4 mag smaller than the values based on the surface brightness fluctuation (SBF) in the literature. We present photometry of the resolved stars in an outer field of M60 based on deep F775W and F850LP images in the Hubble Space Telescope obtained as part of the Pure Parallel Program in the archive. Detected stars are mostly old red giants in the halo of M60. With this photometry, we determine a distance to M60 using the tip of the red giant branch (TRGB).

In this study we selected M60 (NGC 4649, VCC 1978), a giant elliptical galaxy in Virgo, to resolve the distance discrepancy between PNLF distances and SBF distances. Basic parameters of M60 are listed in Table tab-info . M60 has been a target of numerous studies because it shows several interesting features.

sources.summary: The paper presents VLBA imaging of M60, a nearby elliptical LLAGN, resolving a compact centi-parsec radio core interpreted as a jet base or ADAF near its SMBH.

quotes.quote: M60, an elliptical galaxy located 16.5 Mpc away, has an active nucleus with a very low luminosity and an extremely low accretion rate. Its central supermassive black hole has a mass of M_ BH 4.5 10^ 9 M_ and a Schwarzschild radii corresponding to R_ S 5.4 as . To investigate the nature of its innermost radio nucleus, data from the Very Long Baseline Array (VLBA) at 4.4 and 7.6 GHz were reduced. The VLBA images reveal a compact component with total flux densities of 20 mJy at both frequencies, a size of 0.27 mas (99.7 confidence level), about 0.022 pc ( 50 R_ S ) at 7.6 GHz, and a brightness temperature of 6 10^ 9 K.

This suggests that the observed centi-parsec-scale compact core could be attributed to a nonthermal jet base or an advection-dominated accretion flow (ADAF) with nonthermal electrons. The extremely compact structure also supports the presence of an SMBH in the center. Our results indicate that M60 is a promising target for broad-band VLBI observations at millimeter wavelengths to probe ADAF scenarios and tightly constrain the potential photon ring (about 28 as) around its SMBH.

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 4649 as an X-ray bright E2 elliptical with diffuse hot gas, many LMXB candidates, variable sources, radial temperature structure, and Chandra-confirmed radial features.
sources.paper_coordinate_text: R.A. \ = \ 12 ^ h 43 ^ m 40 02, and Dec. \ = \ +11 33 10 2 (J2000; Condon, Frayer, Broderick 1991)

quotes.quote: We present an XMM-Newton X-ray observation of the X-ray bright E2 elliptical galaxy NGC 4649. In addition to bright diffuse emission, we resolve 158 discrete sources, 50 of which are likely to be low-mass X-ray binaries (LMXBs) associated with NGC 4649. We find evidence for variability in three sources between this observation and a previous Chandra observation. Additionally, we detect five sources that were not detected with Chandra despite its better detection limit, suggesting that these sources have since brightened.

A deprojection of the diffuse emission revealed a radial temperature gradient that is hot in the center, drops to a minimum at about 20-50 \ (1.6-4.1 kpc), and rises again in the outer regions. The diffuse emission appears to require a two-temperature model with heavy element abundance ratios that differ from the solar values. We have verified the existence of faint radial features extending out from the core of NGC 4649 that had previously been seen with Chandra .

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 4649 (M60) as the nearby elliptical companion to NGC 4647, notes compatible distances and velocities, and tests CO and H I gas-transfer claims.

quotes.quote: It is also notable for being in a close pair with the elliptical galaxy NGC 4649 (M60); the two are 2.6 \ apart or 13 kpc in projection (Figure fieldofview ). The galaxies are close in radial velocity as well, with NGC 4647 at 1415 4 \ and NGC 4649 at 1114 17 \ RC3 . Surface brightness fluctuations give the distance to NGC 4649 to be 16.8 1.2 (random) 1.2 (systematic) Mpc tonry01 , so this independent distance measurement is also consistent with the two galaxies being physically close to each other. We therefore adopt a distance of 17 Mpc to both galaxies and entertain the possibility that they are gravitationally interacting.

This coverage was designed to give sensitivity to CO emission throughout the disk of NGC 4647 as well as in the region between the two galaxies and the central regions of NGC 4649. sage89 reported a single-dish CO detection of the elliptical NGC 4649, so the mosaic observations were partly designed to investigate those claims.
Our present images show no sign of any HI extension towards NGC 4649, so we conclude that we are not presently observing gas transfer from the spiral to the elliptical.

sources.summary: The paper treats M60 as the dominant galaxy in the nearest compact-group candidate, but finds SBF distances put it behind the M59 plus NGC 4660 pair.

quotes.quote: Taken at face value, the SBF distances suggest that M59 and NGC 4660 lie roughly 2 Mpc to the foreground of M60, NGC 4638, and the bulk of the Virgo cluster. The statistical analysis confirms that the CG is, indeed, the result of a chance alignment of its galaxies, with NGC 4638 lying at least 800 kpc further away (with 99 confidence) than either M59 or NGC 4660. The first two galaxy distances are consistent with M59 and NGC 4660 constituting a tight pair. The dominant galaxy, M60, is at least 440 kpc more distant (95 confidence) than the M59+NGC 4660 pair, and over 1 Mpc (99 confidence) more distant if one uses the broken linear calibration of the SBF distances.

Moreover, using the broken-linear or linear SBF calibrations, M60 must lie at least 0.82 Mpc (99 confidence) further away than either M59 or NGC 4660. On the other hand, the SBF distances determined with the polynomial or mixed calibrations produce consistent distances between M60 and either M59 or NGC 4660. However, one can combine the distances to M59 and NGC 4660 to obtain a 2 smaller uncertainty in the distance of that galaxy pair. M60 then turns out to be 440 or 520 kpc further away than the pair (at the 95 confidence level), depending on which of the polynomial or broken-linear SBF calibrations is used to estimate the distance of the pair.

sources.summary: The paper reports the strongest positive NUV residual in NGC4649, measures FUV and NUV residual magnitudes, and argues star formation can only partly explain the FUV excess.

quotes.quote: In a number of other galaxies (e.g. NGC1407, NGC4365, NGC4649, NGC4839 and NGC4889) the NUV residual image shows extended positive excess NUV emission, which we attribute to recent star formation in the core. This will also contribute to the FUV residual, but cannot account for it all. For instance for NGC4649, which shows the strongest positive NUV residual, we find from the fits to the residual images described below that the magnitude of the excess is 16.26 in FUV and 17.13 in NUV.
Thus even in the unlikely event of the NGC4649 NUV excess being due to such a young starburst, this can still contribute at most 50 of the excess flux in the FUV.

NGC4649 The fit to this galaxy is complicated by the close companion (NGC4647). However the extended NUV residual does not appear to be caused by a poor S ersic fit in the H-band, but by a low level of ongoing star formation, as suggested by Magris Bruzual (1993).

sources.summary: NGC 4649's globular-cluster system is the clearer bimodal case: its (g-K) distribution is better fit by two peaks, simulations preserve bimodality under realistic scatter, and the paper infers genuine metallicity bimodality.

quotes.quote: The two most cluster-rich galaxies in our sample, NGC 4486 and NGC 4649, show some interesting differences. The (g-K) distribution of NGC 4649 is better described by a bimodal distribution, while this is true for the (g-K) distribution of NGC 4486 GCs only if restricted to a brighter sub-sample with small K-band errors ( < 0.05 mag).

For NGC 4486 and NGC 4649 a linearly transformed bimodal distribution in (g-z) remains bimodal both in (g-K) and (z-K) even when realistic photometric scatter is added. This is attested by the statistically significant values of P(KMM), P(GMM) and P(kurt) ( 0.05) for the great majority of the cases. Nonetheless, the separation between the peaks is not as significant, P(DD) > 0.05.
The underlying metallicity distribution of the GC system of NGC 4649 appears to be a genuine case for bimodality.

sources.summary: The paper includes NGC 4649 as a giant Virgo elliptical with the minimum radial globular-cluster coverage, using a power-law substitute when limited extent prevented a beta-model fit.

quotes.quote: NGC 4649 is a giant Virgo elliptical galaxy. The minimum radial extent is for NGC 4649 which reaches to 8.75 arcmin using data from Lee et al. (2008). For NGC 4649 the limited radial extent did not allow for a beta model fit for the blue GCs. In this case we fit a simple power-law fit to the data beyond 4 arcmin which gives an equivalent beta slope of 0.55 0.09 and is quoted in Table 3.

The galaxy profiles are from Goudfrooij et al. (1994) for NGC 720; Spitler et al. (2012) for NGC 1407; Blom, Spitler Forbes (2012) for NGC 4365; Caon et al. (1994) for NGC 4472 and NGC 4636 and MacDonald et al. (2011) for NGC 4649. Although the galaxy starlight does not extend as far as the GC data (particularly for NGC 720), the GCs and the starlight show similar slopes in their outer regions.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 4649 as an elliptical galaxy at 16.5 Mpc observed repeatedly with Chandra, hosting a globular-cluster ultraluminous X-ray source and two additional ULX candidates.

quotes.quote: Here we report the discovery and characteristics of a new, variable ULX associated with a GC in the elliptical galaxy NGC 4649. We assume a distance of d = 16.5 Mpc to NGC 4649 in this work Blakeslee09 . NGC 4649 has been observed six times by the Chandra ACIS-S detector, for a combined exposure of 300 ks.

Remarkably, given that only three ULX candidates were detected in NGC 4649, a second candidate ULX (CXOU J124347.0+113237 with L_ X 10^ 39 erg s^ 1 ) lies less than 4 arcseconds from the position of our object. Through a cross-correlation of the region around the Ca II triplet of stellar absorption features we measure a heliocentric radial velocity of 1025 8 km s ^ 1 , consistent with the systemic velocity of NGC 4649 ( 1117 6 km s ^ 1 ; Trager00 ). This provides strong evidence that the optical counterpart is indeed a GC associated with NGC 4649.

sources.summary: The paper finds complex GC and LMXB asymmetries in NGC4649, including an eastern arc, and interprets them as evidence for mergers or satellite accretion during its evolution.

quotes.quote: We report significant anisotropies in the projected two-dimensional (2D) spatial distributions of Globular Clusters (GCs) of the giant Virgo elliptical galaxy NGC4649 (M60). Similar features are found in the 2D distribution of low-mass X-ray binaries (LMXBs), both associated with GCs and in the stellar field. Deviations from azimuthal symmetry suggest an arc-like excess of GCs extending north at 4-15 kpc galactocentric radii in the eastern side of major axis of NGC4649. This feature is more prominent for red GCs, but still persists in the 2D distribution of blue GCs.

These results suggest that NGC4649 has experienced mergers and/or multiple accretions of less massive satellite galaxies during its evolution, of which the GCs in the arc may be the fossil remnant. We speculate that the observed anisotropy in the field LMXB spatial distribution indicates that these X-ray binaries may be the remnants of a star formation event connected with the merger, or maybe be ejected from the parent red GCs, if the bulk motion of these clusters is significantly affected by dynamical friction.

sources.summary: The paper discusses NGC4649 as a giant Virgo elliptical with GC and LMXB inhomogeneities, a nuclear radio source, hot halo, and likely tidal interaction with NGC4647.

quotes.quote: NGC4261 and NGC4649 are giant ellipticals with L_ K = 11.4 \ L_ K, , while NGC4278 is of intermediate stellar mass, with L_ K = 10.8 \ L_ K, devaucouleurs1991 ; all of them have old stellar populations, with ages ranging from 12 to 16.3 Gy see compilation in boroson2011 . While NGC4649 is in Virgo, NGC4261 and NGC4278 are in small groups see garcia1993 . NGC4261 hosts a luminous radio AGN 3C270, birkinshaw1985 , NGC4649 hosts a low-luminosity nuclear radio source dressel1985 and NGC4278 has a LINER nucleus, which is also detected in the radio younes2010,terashima2003 . Of the three galaxies, NGC4649 is the only one associated with a prominent luminous extended hot halo e.g., humphrey2013 .

Recent kinematical measurements arnold2013 suggest that NGC4649 might be a major dry merger remnant, showing disk-like outer rotation, which suggests a massive lenticular galaxy (S0) progenitor. NGC4649 has a near neighbor, the spiral galaxy NGC4647, with which it is likely to be tidally interacting degrijs2006 .

sources.summary: The paper analyzes NGC4649 hot-accretion density data alongside NGC 1316, arguing R^3/2 profiles match the observations better than classical Bondi expectations.

quotes.quote: Figure 4 shows the inferred volumetric electron density profiles from Pl s ek et al. (2022) for NGC 1316 and NGC4649, points with error bars, together with R^ 3/2 fits to the observed profiles. In all cases for the innermost points, the reported relative radial errors are of 100 , for which reason the innermost points have been ignored in the fits. It is clear that the fits are very accurate representations of the data over the close to two orders of magnitudes in radius covered by the observations.

Also, the Bondi radii of the two galaxies shown in the figure are of 7.4^ +1.2 _ 1.3 pc for NGC 1316 and 111 19 pc for NGC 4649, as inferred by Pl s ek et al. (2022). Thus, a Bondi fit to the observed profiles would appear close to a horizontal line for the case of NGC 1316 over the radial range covered by the data, and beyond 1 kpc a solution rapidly convergent to a constant value for the case of NGC4649. The rapid flattening of the Bondi profile outwards of the Bondi radius, as seen in figure (3), shows that once constrained by the inferred values of the Bondi radii, the two cases shown above will yield significantly poorer fits to classical Bondi profiles than to the expected R^ 3/2 density scalings of the solution in eqs. (13) and (14).

sources.summary: The paper lists M60-UCD1 among observed UCDs with confirmed black holes and notes its 2 x 10^7 solar-mass black hole matches the high end of simulated stripped-nucleus predictions.

quotes.quote: Figure fig:bhmasses plots derived black hole masses against derived total masses for simulated stripped nuclei with black hole masses above 10 6 M _ for the seven massive clusters in EAGLE and UCDs whose black hole content has been studied. The 5 UCDs that are confirmed to contain black holes are M60-UCD1 Seth2014 , VUCD3, M59cO Ahn2017 , M59-UCD3 Ahn2018 and UCD3 Afanasiev_2018 . Also included are UCD 330 and UCD 320 Voggel_2018 , for which only upper limits on black hole masses have been found. Figure fig:bhmasses shows that the black hole masses of the simulated stripped nuclei are consistent with the black hole masses of observed UCDs. The masses and mass fractions of these black holes in UCDs are a reliable indicator of them being stripped nuclei.
This range is consistent with the most massive black hole found in a UCD, M60-UCD1, which has a mass of 2 10 7 M _ and is found in the massive Virgo cluster.

sources.summary: The paper compares NGC 4649 with M87 and NGC 4472, finding a sparser globular-cluster system and weaker blue-tilt slopes sensitive to measurement choices.

quotes.quote: However, both restrict the analysis of blue tilts in individual galaxies to the three most luminous (massive) candidates M49 (NGC 4472), M87 (NGC 4486) and M60 (NGC 4649). Consequently these are also the most GC rich galaxies in the sample, although NGC 4649 and NGC 4472 have fewer than half the number of GCs as M87 within the ACS field of view.

For the sparser NGC 4649 cluster system, using different magnitude cutoffs and sampling frequencies, Strader et al. (2006) and Meiske et al. (2006) measure somewhat different but weaker slopes of -0.037 and -0.028 0.009 respectively.

Thus variations in such quoted values of the slope of the `blue-tilt' between NGC 4472 and the similarly rich NGC 4649 cluster population, based on an arbitrary faint cutoff magnitude do not provide any convincing evidence of differences in the `blue-tilt'. It simply reflects the fact that as compared to M87 a smaller fraction of the blue GCs in the sparser NGC 4472 and NGC 4649 cluster systems are well resolved.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 4649 to test completeness assumptions, finds its alternate source distribution has modest effects, and notes its broad GCLF influences the composite fit.

quotes.quote: We have explicitly confirmed that starting from one magnitude fainter than this limit makes no measurable difference to our key results for one galaxy, NGC 4649.

In order to assess how significantly this may affect our results, we recomputed the incompleteness correction for one system, NGC 4649, but distributing the simulated sources as a model, with =0.46 and core radius 0.81 . Such a model approximately fits the distribution of the LMXBs in this system. We found \ changed by 0.20 and \ by 0.026, which are consistent with the statistical errors, and other sources of systematic uncertainty for this object.
kundu01b found that NGC 4472 and NGC 4649, which account for 20 of the detected GCs, may have _V 1.5.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes NGC 4649 (M60) using X-ray and globular-cluster tracers, finding little dark matter inside three effective radii but increasing dark-matter importance farther out.

quotes.quote: In this paper we analyze the problem of the total mass and the total mass-to-light ratio of the early-type galaxy NGC 4649 (M60). We have used two independent techniques: the X-ray methodology which is based on the temperature of the X-ray halo of NGC 4649 and the tracer mass estimator (TME) which uses globular clusters (GCs) observed in this galaxy. We calculated the mass in Newtonian and MOdified Newtonian Dynamics (MOND) approaches and found that interior to 3 effective radii ( R_e ) there is no need for large amounts of dark matter. Beyond 3R_e dark matter starts to play important dynamical role.
