We find that galaxies showing one single burst of star formation (Ursa Minor and Leo II) require a dark halo slightly larger that the current estimates for their tidal radii, or the presence of a metal rich selective wind, which might carry away much of the energy output of their supernovae before this might have interacted and heated the gas content, for the gas to be retained until the observed stellar population have formed.

sources.summary: The paper treats Leo II as a distant Galactic dSph, arguing that weak tidal effects, its ordinary placement among dSphs, and its M/L value favor a dark-matter interpretation.

quotes.quote: Second, the Galactic dSphs Leo I and Leo II have Galactocentric distances that exceed 200 kpc. Thus neither of these dSphs are subject to Galactic tides to anything like the same extent as the inner dSphs (R 65 -- 90 kpc, excluding Sgr). Yet neither Leo I nor Leo II lies in a distinctly different location, relative to the other Galactic dSphs, in Fig. \ 1, for example. Further, both Leo systems have M/L values that imply the presence of dark matter.

sources.summary: The paper describes Leo II as a distant satellite with a first proper-motion estimate, an outermost-satellite speed exception, and an orbital pole aligned with the common direction.

quotes.quote: The first PM estimates have also been published for the two most distant MW satellites Leo I 2013ApJ...768..139S and Leo II 2011ApJ...741..100L .
With the exception of the two outermost MW satellites Leo I and Leo II, all satellites fall well below this speed.

The orbital poles of at least six satellites (LMC, SMC, Ursa Minor, Leo II, Draco and Fornax) are closely aligned with each other, being inclined by less than 27^ \ from their average direction (which points to [ l,b ] = [ 177^ ,-3^ ] ) \ and close to the minor axis of the VPOS.

sources.summary: Leo II is modeled as a Milky Way dSph; its dark halo parameters are mapped, and its lower-M/L halo is described as less flattened than high-M/L systems.

quotes.quote: In this section, we describe the best-fit dark halo model for each of seven MW dSphs (Carina, Fornax, Sculptor, Sextans, Draco, Leo I, and Leo II) and five M31 dSphs (And I, And II, And III, And V, and And VII) and present confidence maps between each parameter.

In Figure  fig:fig2 and  fig:fig3 , we present 68  ( 1 ), 95  , and 99  confidence levels of contours in the two-dimensional plane of Q- _z , _0- _z , _z , b_ halo _z , and i- _z for all sample dSphs: Carina, Fornax, Sculptor, Sextans, Draco, and Leo I are shown in Figure  fig:fig2 , and Leo II, And I, And II, And III, And V, and And VII are shown in Figure  fig:fig3 .

In light of these predictions, it is interesting to remark that the dark halos of Fornax, Sculptor, Leo I, and Leo II with relatively low M/L s tend to be less flattened than Carina, Sextans, and Draco with high M/L s.

sources.summary: The paper finds Leo II has the highest specific orbital angular momentum in the satellite sample, making it the object that brackets the upper Milky Way mass range.

quotes.quote: We also find that high j satellites (i.e. Leo II) constrain the upper limits for the MW's mass and low j satellites rather than the highest speed satellites (i.e. Leo I and LMC), set the lower mass limits.

Contrary to previous studies, Leo II, rather than Leo I, puts limits on the MW's mass. Leo II has the highest specific orbital angular momentum of the satellites considered in this analysis, so it brackets the upper end of the MW's plausible mass range.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes Leo II as a dwarf spheroidal with published stellar kinematics, finding it substantially denser than Fornax and noting comparison models that predict a cusp.

quotes.quote: For the stellar-kinematic data, we use the published spectroscopic samples of 2009AJ....137.3100W for Carina, Fornax, Sculptor and Sextans, of mateo08 for Leo I, of spencer17 for Leo II, and of 2015MNRAS.448.2717W for Draco.

Nonetheless, we remain able to detect that Leo I and Leo II are substantially more dense than Fornax, while Sextans and UMi favour a density similar to Sculptor and Carina that lies in-between that of Draco and Fornax.

Similarly, 2015MNRAS.450.3920B used their DM heating models, combined with abundance matching, to predict DM cores in WLM and Fornax, cusps in Draco, Leo I, Leo II and UMi, and something in-between for Sculptor and Aquarius.

sources.summary: The paper flags Leo II as an outlier in inferred Si/Fe_CC and Ca/Fe_CC, suggesting the bi-linear model may be less suited to its extended 5 Gyr star-formation history.

quotes.quote: Leo II is an outlier in Si/Fe _ CC and Ca/Fe _ CC . It is possible that our bi-linear model is not as well suited to Leo II's more extended SFH compared to the other dSphs.
The dSphs in our sample formed the middle two thirds of their stars in durations ranging from 1 Gyr in Sculptor to 5 Gyr in Leo II wei14 .

sources.summary: The paper uses Leo II as a distant dwarf-spheroidal constraint, arguing that massive extended primary halos and eccentric orbits are needed for tidal stirring at Leo II distances.

quotes.quote: The transformation requires the orbital time of the dwarf to be 3-4 Gyr, which implies a halo as massive and extended as predicted by hierarchical models of galaxy formation to explain the origin of even the farthest dSph satellites of the Milky Way, Leo I and Leo II.
The apocenter of this orbit is still sufficiently large to be consistent with the distance of the second most distant dSph satellite, Leo II ( 200 kpc).
However, the farthest dSphs, Leo I and Leo II, must be moving on fairly eccentric orbits (apo/peri > 3 ) in order to be transformed.

gmw_00001259.name: NGC 3162
gmw_00001259.overview: NGC 3162 is classified as an intermediate-type spiral galaxy that features a weak bar and a bulge [arXiv:0807.2699]. The galaxy may also host a central ring, and its nucleus appears fairly undisturbed despite having somewhat asymmetric spiral arms [arXiv:0807.2699].

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 3162 as one endpoint of the bar-strength relation, describing it as an intermediate spiral with a weak bar, prominent bulge, possible ring, and undisturbed nucleus.

quotes.quote: Since the correlation is significant but not very strong we examined the two galaxies that define the higher and lower limits of Q_g and A_2 . We looked at them closely to see if they are odd in some way, and not characteristic of the rest of the sample. (i) NGC 3162 ( _ e /v_g =1.13) : This is an intermediate type spiral galaxy with a weak bar and prominent bulge. There may also be a ring in the center. Though the spiral arms are somewhat asymmetric, the nucleus is fairly undisturbed.

gmw_00001260.name: NGC 4314
gmw_00001260.overview: NGC 4314 is a nearly face-on, early-type barred spiral galaxy located in the Virgo cluster [arXiv:0704.2839, arXiv:1002.1259]. It is a prototypical barlens galaxy [arXiv:1505.00590] featuring a LINER-type nucleus [arXiv:0807.2699] and a young, star-forming nuclear ring [arXiv:1803.01501, arXiv:astro-ph/0604487].

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4314 as a low-inclination Sa barlens galaxy with a red central peak, star-forming nuclear ring, dusty spiral arms, and profiles matching barlens simulations.

quotes.quote: NGC 4314 (RP+nr): has a Hubble stage Sa, and has an inclination inc = 20.4 ^ . This is an example of barlens galaxies which have a red central peak in the color profile along the bar major axis (in g- and i-z ). The central peak appears within = 5 , and has colors of (g-) 0.92 and (i-z) 0.35, which are redder than the mean values in normal elliptical galaxies. The red peak is surrounded by a star forming nuclear ring visible in (g-) . Outside the nuclear ring spiral arms appear, which are red in (g-) probably being a manifestation of dust.

The nuclear ring and the nuclear bar of this galaxy have been previously discussed in the optical by see references there erwin2003 , and in the infrared by lauri2011 . In see their figure 1 lauri2014 the observed surface brightness profiles along the bar major and minor axis were shown and compared with those predicted by the simulation model by atha2013b . They concluded that these profiles are similar to those predicted by the simulation models.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4314 as a high apparent star-formation case whose ring has cold gas, but whose face-on geometry makes young stars weakly affect stellar dispersion.

quotes.quote: In Figure 3, we present the cases of NGC 4245, NGC 4274, and NGC 4314 to illustrate the most common properties of the stars and gas in these SF regions. There is a remarkable correlation between the dust and the regions where SF is very intense (i.e., large \ flux). At the same time, the / \ ratio is very low at the same locations, confirming the presence of star formation. The velocity dispersion of the ionised gas is also low ( 50 ) along the star-forming rings. We are thus seeing stars being formed from dynamically cold gas.

In NGC 4314, one of the galaxies with highest apparent SF activity, the presence of young stars hardly affects _ * . In practice, these features strongly depend not only on the number of young stars present, but also on the inclination of the galaxy, as in face-on configurations (e.g., NGC 4314) the contribution of the young stars to the line-of-sight radial velocity is much smaller than that of the surrounding bulge.

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 4314 as a well-studied barred Virgo galaxy with an inner star-forming ring, nearby inner spiral, younger stars strongest at the ring, and old central populations.

quotes.quote: NGC 4314 is a low-inclined, well studied barred galaxy in the Virgo cluster (Benedict et al. 1996, P erez-Ram rez et al. 2000), mainly known for a inner star-forming ring in the inner 10 . Benedict et al. analyse ground-based (1992) and HST-images (1993) of this object. They detect a inner star forming ring in optical and near-IR colors, together with a inner spiral just exterior of the ring. They conclude from the optical and near-infrared photometry that a pattern of younger stars exist, stronger at the ring, and almost disappearing at the linear dust lanes.

They find that the stellar populations are consistent with an old age (10 ^ 10 years). Gonz alez-Delgado et al. (2004) measure absorption line indices from central STIS spectra, and classify it as a galaxy with old stellar populations and weak [ OI ] .

sources.summary: The paper models NGC 4314 with nuclear X-ray and diffuse-gas components, finds only a radio ring around the nucleus, and sets a radio-core upper limit.

quotes.quote: NGC 4314 is located at a distance of 13.1 Mpc 2002AJ....123.1411B . The spectra of all three available observations were fitted by an absorbed power law with a photon index of approximately two plus a diffuse X-ray emission component from ionized gas, modeled using a single temperature mekal model, with T 0.4 keV. The 0.5-10 keV mean value for the source unabsorbed luminosity -- calculated for the 3 \ extraction region from the observations, and only for the power law component -- is 3.88 10^ 38 erg s ^ 1 ; total 0.5-10 keV source luminosity for the entire 30 \ region (for all and observations), is 1.38 10^ 39 erg s ^ 1 .

As already mentioned by , a ring of radio emission is (marginally) detected around the position of the galaxy nucleus. However, we do not detect radio emission at a position coincident with the optical core also in agreement with 1991A&A...244..257G . Combining our two spectral bands and using natural weighting, we obtain a RMS noise level of 7.6 Jy leading to a 3 upper limit of 23 Jy.

sources.summary: The paper examines NGC 4314 as the opposite endpoint, describing a bright early-type barred galaxy with a large bulge, LINER nucleus, nuclear rotation, and star-forming ring.

quotes.quote: (ii) NGC 4314 ( _ e /v_g =0.18) : This is a bright, early type galaxy with a strong bar and large bulge with a LINER type nucleus. There may be significant rotation in the nuclear region which may lower the _ e . Also, the mass may be more widely distributed over the bucle and hence not as centrally concentrated as in NGC 3162. There appears to be a ring of star formation in the nucleus as well (Gonzalez Delgado et al. 1997). Both galaxies are thus fairly normal and not unusually different from the rest of the sample galaxies.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4314 as a strong-bar R1' comparison galaxy whose broader spiral structure and star-formation-poor outer disk are better matched by higher initial velocity dispersion.

quotes.quote: We include two additional galaxies, NGC 4314 and NGC 4548, that are not classified as ring galaxies, but have strong bars and display R1' type ring morphology.
NGC 4314 and NGC 1300 have stronger bars with Q_T = 0.44 and 0.54 respectively.

NGC 4314's spiral structure is not as narrow as for the other galaxies. We find a better match of morphologies between NGC 4314 and our simulation when we increase the initial velocity dispersion of our simulation from 0.04 to 0.07. The outer disk of NGC 4314 is devoid of star formation suggesting that an initial velocity dispersion typical of a stellar disk rather than a gaseous one should be used.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 4314 as a nearly face-on barred spiral with boxy outer isophotes, a short spiral continuation of the bar, and chaotic stellar orbits supporting the morphology.

quotes.quote: A standard example is the nearly face-on, early type barred-spiral galaxy NGC 4314. In Fig. n4314 a we give an R-image (Gadotti de Souza 2006) with overplotted isophotes showing the boxiness in discussion. The spiral structure appears as a continuation of the bar and is confined azimuthally in an angle less than /2 . Patsis et al. (1997b), have shown that the stellar orbits associated with the observed boxy isophotes are chaotic. The potential used for this study has been estimated by Quillen et al. (1994) from the K image of NGC 4314.

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 4314 as a characteristic barlens example, describing its bar as a short, fat lens-like component plus a longer thin component, and comparing it with a strong-bar simulation.

quotes.quote: Here we display in Fig. fig:NGC4314 the NIRS0S image of NGC 4314, a characteristic barlens example (reproduced from Laurikainen.SBK.11 ). In this and many other cases one can see that the bar can be described as a sum of two components, a short, fat, lens-like component, and a thin and much longer component. It is the former of these components that is the barlens.
The third row compares the t =6 Gyr image of gtr114 with the S ^4 G image of NGC 4314. Both have a fairly round barlens and a strong thin bar component.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 4314 as an extreme young nuclear-ring galaxy, offset from ring star-formation trends because of its low ring mass, young age, high specific SFR, and high YMC fraction.

quotes.quote: The two youngest nuclear rings in our sample, NGC 1512 and NGC 4314, which have the most extreme physical properties, represent the young extremity of the nuclear ring age distribution.

Interestingly, these subgalactic regions exhibit a similar correlation as their full counterparts, except for NGC 1512 and NGC 4314, which are also the two least massive rings with the youngest ages in our sample.

Figures 6 to 8, show that the ring properties in NGC 1512 and NGC 4314 are outliers in almost all cases. These are the least massive and youngest rings with the highest specific SFRs and YMC mass fractions (for which the relatively lower ring stellar masses may play a crucial role in determining their physical properties). We have marked these two rings separately in Figs 6--8.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4314 as an intermediate or evolved bar case, with minor-axis profiles suggesting the disk dispersion is lower than the bar dispersion.

quotes.quote: NGC 4314 has intermediate values for both _ z, bar and _z and we do not reach far out enough along the bar major axis. However, the _z radial profiles along the bar minor axis show an estimate of _z in the disk substantially lower than those that refer to the bar. Taking this single point into account it seems that the more appropriate is to consider its bar an evolved one. It can in fact be an example where bar evolution is at an intermediate stage.

sources.summary: NGC 4314 is presented as a low-inclination barred Virgo galaxy known for a nuclear star-forming ring, with regular stellar and gas kinematics and a ring visible in emission maps.

quotes.quote: NGC 4314 is a low-inclined, well studied barred galaxy in the Virgo cluster bsk96, pkpldn00 , mainly known for a nuclear star-forming ring in the inner 10 . The stellar kinematics displays regular rotation around the minor axis of the galaxy. The kinematics of the ionised gas is consistent with that of the stars. The presence of the nuclear ring is clearly visible in the \ flux and velocity dispersion maps.

sources.summary: The paper finds only sparse soft diffuse emission in NGC 4314 and emphasizes its young circumnuclear star-forming ring as the likely LINER power source.

quotes.quote: NGC 4314 (L2, SBa) has no point sources, just sparse soft diffuse emission. NGC 4314 is one of the nearest examples of galaxies hosting a circumnuclear ring of star formation. Its diameter is 10 , which falls within the central kiloparsec. The age of the stellar population in the nuclear ring has been estimated to be very young (1--15 Myr, Benedict et al. 2002).

sources.summary: The paper reports larger-radius gas knots in NGC 4314, uses an off-center aperture for detected emission, and groups it with LINERs showing positive gradients.

quotes.quote: For seven galaxies (NGC 1433, NGC 1530, NGC 1832, NGC 2665, NGC 2935, NGC 3081 and NGC 4314) we have also found knots of gas emission at larger radii, corresponding to one or several crowded \ regions.
For NGC 4314 we show the emission line strengths for the innermost aperture (0.8 arcsec from the galaxy center) with detected emission.
For LINERs, we find a positive gradient for three galaxies (NGC 1433, NGC 4314 and NGC 4394) and none of them has a negative gradient.

sources.summary: The review identifies NGC 4314 as a prototypical barlens galaxy with a structure linking the barlens to the bar and a star-forming nuclear ring inside the boxy bulge.

quotes.quote: Two prototypical barlens galaxies, NGC 936 and NGC 4314, are shown in Figures 4 and 10.
The unsharp mask image of NGC 4314 also shows a structure connecting the barlens to the more elongated part of the bar (see Fig. 10).
An example of a barlens galaxy with similar characteristics is NGC 4314, showing a star forming nuclear ring inside the boxy bulge.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 4314 as a difficult bar-length case, noting a strong bar without a clear ellipticity maximum and spiral arms that keep the position angle nearly constant.

quotes.quote: In NGC 4314 the bar is strong, but has no clear maximum in its ellipticity profile, instead a broad bump appears. The visual barlength corresponds to the radius where the spiral arms start on both sides of the bar. The strong spiral arms prevent the position angle profile from changing, which is almost constant up to =100 .

gmw_00001261.name: NGC720
gmw_00001261.overview: NGC 720 is an elongated, early-type elliptical galaxy [arXiv:1310.2607, arXiv:astro-ph/0605279] that serves as the brightest member of its group [arXiv:astro-ph/0605279]. It is a strong X-ray source [arXiv:astro-ph/0401103] hosting a massive, quasi-hydrostatic hot gas halo [arXiv:1010.6078] and an elliptical dark matter halo [arXiv:1011.2907, arXiv:2207.10638].

sources.summary: The paper reexamines NGC 720's X-ray isophote and halo-shape constraints on self-interacting dark matter, finding 1 cm^2/g likely inconsistent but 0.1 cm^2/g as consistent as CDM.

quotes.quote: In this section, we focus on the shape measurement of the X-ray emission about the elliptical galaxy NGC 720. The inferred shape of the matter distribution of this system has been used to set constraints on self-interacting dark matter in the recent past feng2009,feng2010d,buckley2010b,mcdermott2011 . The data set used by buote2002 for the shape measurement of NGC 720 is a 40 ks exposure of the inner 5^ of the galaxy with the ACIS-S3 camera on the Chandra telescope. The data included in the fit are contained within a 35 ^ 185^ annulus from the center of the galaxy, which corresponds to 4.5-22.4 kpc.

While our comparison to buote2002 is not sufficiently sharp, the weight of the arguments suggests that /m = 1 cm ^2/ g is not likely to be consistent with the measured shape of NGC 720. However, based on our existing simulations, /m = 0.1 cm ^2/ g is as consistent as CDM for the shape of the NGC 720 isophotes. It is interesting to note in this regard that there is no hint of a large core ( 30 kpc) in the results of buote2002 or humphrey2006 . The core sizes for /m = 0.1 cm ^2/ g are smaller, 7 kpc, for the same virial mass range (Rocha et al, 2012), comparable to the effective radius of the stars in NGC 720.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 720 as the benchmark elliptical for X-ray halo-shape constraints, comparing its Chandra-derived ellipticity with CDM and self-interacting dark-matter simulations.

quotes.quote: Applying this method to the NGC 720 elliptical galaxy using Chandra X-ray data, Ref. determined the ellipticity of the DM halo was roughly 0.35-0.4. Simulations for cross-sections of /m = 0, 0.1, 1 cm ^2 /g showed that the NGC 720 ellipticity was consistent with an interaction cross-section over mass of /m 0.1 cm ^2 /g, as well as with CDM ( /m=0 cm ^2 /g). While these results presented convincing evidence that /m=1 cm ^2 /g was incompatible with the NGC 720 observation, strong assertions for lower cross-sections are difficult to make given the singular observation and significant scatter in the ellipticities of the simulated halos.

Still, the results of the X-ray ellipticity measurements of NGC 720 have been useful in applications to a wide range of DM models including hidden sector hydrogen , dissipative and ``double-disk'' dark matter , charged DM , as well as alternative gravity theories for DM phenomena such as MOND. In figure fig:galaxy_ellips we show the ellipticities for each galaxy and mass distribution configuration. For comparison, we also show the ellipticity for NGC 720 determined in as horizontal colored lines.
The values found in our analysis for NGC 720 tend to be slightly lower, although this could potentially be due to exposure corrections (see section 2 of ) or calibration effects.

sources.summary: The paper uses deep Chandra and Suzaku data to model NGC 720's hot gas, dark halo, and baryon fraction, finding a Milky Way-mass isolated elliptical can retain a massive quasi-hydrostatic gas halo.

quotes.quote: To begin to address this question, in this paper we present a detailed X-ray study of the isolated elliptical galaxy NGC 720, using deep \ and \ observations (see Table table_observations ). The complementary characteristics of these satellites, \ the excellent spatial resolution of \ (enabling the inner parts of the X-ray halo to be studied in detail) and the low, stable background of \ (which is helpful for studying the low surface-brightness outer parts of the halo), make a combination of these data ideal for studying the hot gas properties over as wide a radial range as possible.

Assuming an NFW dark matter halo, we obtained unprecedented, tight constraints on both the virial mass and concentration for such a low-mass system ( = 1.6 0.2 10^ 12 \ with systematic errors typically 20 ; =3.1^ +0.4 _ 0.3 10^ 12 ). This confirms that an isolated elliptical galaxy that is not at the centre of a massive group can maintain a substantial dark matter halo. 4. The total gas mass inferred within \ substantially exceeds the stellar mass, with most of the gas lying outside 100 kpc. This supports theoretical predictions that Milky Way-mass galaxies can host massive, quasi-hydrostatic hot gas halos.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 720 as a nearby non-cluster Chandra comparison galaxy, degrading its image to show how Perseus conditions would hide most of its LMXB halo.

quotes.quote: NGC 720 lies at a distance of 28 Mpc, is not in a cluster and has a Chandra observation of 40 ks length (OBSID 492). To account for the effect of distance on the spatial scale, we binned the image of NGC 720 by a factor of 3, and smoothed it by 1 arcsec to account for the PSF at the position of NGC 1278. From this image we subtracted a flat background, then multiplied the image by a factor of 2.3 to account for the difference in exposure time and distance between the Perseus and NGC 720 observations. We added a flat background component to account for the projected intracluster medium in Perseus. Finally we generated an image by making a Poisson realization of our model, to account for counting statistics.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 720 as a well-studied elliptical galaxy whose X-ray isophotes and elliptical dark matter halo constrain charged dark matter through halo relaxation times.

quotes.quote: The elliptical galaxy NGC 720 is well-studied . In Ref. , X-ray isophotes were used to extract the ellipticity of the underlying matter distribution, and the DM halo of NGC 720 was found to be elliptical at 5 kpc and larger radii. At 5 kpc , the DM density is _ tot =4 GeV/cm^3 , and the radial velocity dispersion v^2_r =v^2_0()/2 (240 km/s )^2 . To derive the constraints on for the given from the observed halo shapes, we require ^ 1 _e>10^ 10 years . That is, the average time scale to create O (1) change in the DM particle momentum must be greater than the galaxy's lifetime.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC720 halo ellipticity to constrain mirror-electron dark-matter self-interactions, while noting that a single-galaxy constraint may be weakened by density evolution and special features.

quotes.quote: Galactic halos have a triaxial structure and self-interactions reduce the anisotropy of the DM velocity distribution, reducing the ellipticity of the halos. The measured ellipticity of NGC720 has led to constraints on self-interactions . Remarkably, for m_ e' = 225 GeV, our predicted value for ' is right on the upper limit .

One caveat is that the constraint results from calculations of the time scale for the halo velocity distribution to become isotropic, whereas the measured ellipticity is probing the mass density distribution, which could evolve on a longer time scale.

Also, the constraint relies on observations of a single galaxy, NGC720, which might have special features, for example low ellipticity; a more robust result would require analysis of further galaxies.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 720 as a bright X-ray active galaxy aligned with RXJ 0152.7-1357 and an X-ray BSO, arguing the configuration is unusually improbable by chance.

quotes.quote: It is conspicuously elongated, however, toward the bright, X-ray active galaxy NGC 720 about 14 arcmin away. At the same distance on the other side of NGC 720, and almost perfectly aligned, is an X-ray BSO of 5.8 cts/ks.

In fact there was a very bright Shapley Ames galaxy, NGC 720 ( B_T = 11.15, z = .0057). NGC 720 is strongly active with an X-ray filament extending from the nucleus and curving southward as it emerges (Buote and Canizares 1996).
The X-ray galaxy cluster is 14.2' from NGC 720 and the two are almost exactly aligned with each other across the galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper applies its X-ray image-modeling method to NGC 720, measuring a hot gaseous halo and arguing against a baryon-complete halo inferred from prior spectral analyses.

quotes.quote: We calibrate and demonstrate this method against a variety of simulated images, and then apply it to Chandra observations of the hot gaseous halo around the elliptical galaxy NGC 720. We are able to follow the X-ray emission below a tenth of the background, and to infer a hot gas mass within 35 kpc of 4-5 10^9 M_ , with some indication that the profile continues to at least 50 kpc and that it steepens as the radius increases. We derive much stronger constraints on the surface brightness profile than previous studies, which employed the spectral method, and we show that the density profiles inferred from these studies are in conflict with the observed surface brightness profile. We therefore conclude that, contrary to a previous claim, the X-ray halo of NGC 720 does not seem to contain the full complement of missing baryons from this galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 720 as a bright elliptical active galaxy with strong ROSAT emission, a twisted nuclear X-ray jet or filament, and nearby ULX/quasar candidates.

quotes.quote: NGC 720: These two IXO's lie very close to a bright ( B_T = 11.15 mag) E galaxy. The galaxy is a very strong ROSAT X-ray source which emits 148 cts/ks. Moreover, there is a strong, ``twisted'', X-ray jet/filament which curves southward as it emerges from the nucleus (Buote and Canizares 1996). In addition to the two ULX's, X-ray quasar candidates of similar apparent magnitude are conspicuous at larger radial separations from NGC 720 (see ``Catalogue of Discordant Redshift Associations'', Arp 2003).

sources.summary: The paper cites NGC720 as an elliptical whose hot X-ray gas traces its potential; published deprojections find MOND stellar potentials too round for the observed X-ray isophotes.

quotes.quote: They argue that, if the hot gas in NGC720 is in hydrostatic equilibrium, then p_ gas =- which implies that = 0 . Thus, the X-ray isophotes from the hot gas in NGC720 trace the gas density, which in turn traces the underlying gravitational potential. By deprojecting the stellar potential they show that the stars in NGC720 cannot produce a flat enough potential in MOND to produce the observed X-ray isophotes. While they have to assume hydrostatic equilibrium and some simple form for the deprojected stellar potential, their assumptions are quite conservative.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 720 as a large elliptical whose X-ray isophote misalignment provides confident evidence for dark matter in MOND comparisons.

quotes.quote: The closest anyone has come is the study of the large elliptical NGC 720 by Buote Canizares (1994, 2002) which has T 0.6keV. They showed that the major axis of the isophotes of the X-ray emission were significantly offset from the major axis of the galaxy requiring dark matter of at least 4 times the mass of the visible matter. NGC 720 is currently the only elliptical galaxy for which this misalignment can be attributed to dark matter with reasonable confidence.

sources.summary: NGC 720 is described as an elongated E5 galaxy with moderate central rotation whose slow but significant rotation remains nearly constant to the SLUGGS edge.

quotes.quote: This is a highly elongated E5 galaxy possibly residing in a triaxial halo Buote:2002a . Cappellari:2007bn found clear but moderate central rotation within 1 , and similarities to both fast and slow rotators. Our kinematic data show that this slow but significant rotation continues essentially constant out to the edge of the SLUGGS data around 2 .

sources.summary: The review cites NGC720 as an X-ray halo-shape constraint on self-interacting dark matter, where isophote twisting implies a halo ellipticity consistent with CDM-like elliptical halos.

quotes.quote: Constraints on scattering cross sections from shapes of halos have been obtained using the X-Ray measurements of the shapes of the hot halo gas of NGC720. The shape and the twisting of X-Ray isophotes carry information about the three dimensional shape of the potential. Measurements from NGC720 constrain the halo ellipticity to be 0.35 0.03 , consistent with elliptical halos from CDM, this values is consistent with /m_ DM < 1 cm ^2 /g .

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 720 as the early-type brightest galaxy of its group, centrally located in projection but velocity-offset, with much fainter companions suggesting a fossil group.

quotes.quote: NGC 720 Group find this group to have extended intra-group X-ray emission. The brightest galaxy in the group, NGC 720, is an early-type galaxy and is spatially coincident with the centre of the group (within 21 kpc). However, it is offset in velocity by 230 km s ^ 1 , nearly twice the velocity dispersion. The other 5 group members are all >2 magnitudes fainter than NGC 720 itself, suggesting that this could be a fossil group.

gmw_00001262.name: UGC 7369
gmw_00001262.overview: UGC 7369 is a nearby early-type lenticular dwarf galaxy [arXiv:2211.01384] that exhibits a smooth, elliptical shape [arXiv:astro-ph/9910178]. The galaxy features a bright, nucleated star cluster [arXiv:2211.01384, arXiv:astro-ph/9910178] and hosts a notably rich population of globular clusters [arXiv:2211.01384].

sources.summary: The paper models UGC 7369 as a GC-rich lenticular dwarf, arguing that its mass segregation and nuclear star cluster are naturally reproduced in a cuspy dark matter halo.

quotes.quote: Here we present the results of our simulations of UGC 7369, a nearby early-type dwarf galaxy. Unlike Fornax, UGC 7369 is nucleated, and because it is relatively GC rich, we are able to gain a clearer understanding of its GC population and thus make more robust constraints on the nature of its halo. Briefly, we argue that the mass and projected orbital radius of the NSC in UGC 7369 are plausibly explained by GC-merger-dominated formation, but only in a cuspy halo.

UGC 7369 is a lenticular dwarf galaxy with a measured stellar mass of M_ = 1.72 10^8 Georgiev_sample3 . As demonstrated in Figure fig:MGC_Mstar , this galaxy is notable for its GC richness. It hosts 21 GCs, by far the most out of any galaxy in this sample, which is important because segregation effects are only robustly detectable in a sufficiently large population of varied GC masses.

sources.summary: The paper describes UGC 7369 as a smooth elliptical-looking galaxy with a bright nucleus, shallow reddening color profile, possible dwarf elliptical status, and exponential surface-brightness profile.

quotes.quote: UGC 7369 : is a smooth, elliptically shaped galaxy, with a bright nucleus. It has a shallow color profile, becoming redder with radius, and a slightly blue nucleus. It is classified in the RC3 as an elliptical, but at v_ hel =333 km s^ 1 it could be a dwarf elliptical. Its SBP is close to exponential.

gmw_00001263.name: PRIMER-COS 3866
gmw_00001263.overview: PRIMER-COS 3866 is a bright, X-ray-detected active galactic nucleus at a redshift of z=4.66 [arXiv:2404.03576]. Its mid-infrared emission is dominated by the active nucleus, while its rest-frame ultraviolet light is dominated by the host galaxy [arXiv:2404.03576].

sources.summary: The paper describes PRIMER-COS 3866 as a bright z=4.66 X-ray-detected LRD whose multi-band imaging and SED modeling indicate AGN-dominated mid-infrared emission and host-galaxy UV light.

quotes.quote: PRIMER-COS 3866 was first identified as an X-ray emitter by Elvis09 as CXOC100024.2+022510 and later spectroscopically confirmed to be at z=4.66 by . The source has a F444W magnitude of 19.75, making it the brightest object in our sample. Images of PRIMER-COS 3866 in multiple NIRCam bands are shown in Figure fig:galfit_montage . In addition to its strong NIRCam detection, the source is also detected in the rest-frame UV with Hubble ACS imaging and in the near- and mid-infrared with Spitzer IRAC and MIPS imaging. In Figure fig:cigale_fit , we show the best-fit galaxy plus AGN SED model produced by fitting the available photometry with CIGALE v2022.1 using the parameters listed in 2.2.

We find the 3-30 m emission of the source is well-fit, and dominated by, AGN emission that is moderately reddened by polar dust with an E(B-V)=0.40 0.01 . The skirtor2016 model used for the AGN component is based on a clumpy torus model, which would suggest the observed extinction is due to torus-level obscuration as opposed to galaxy-wide obscuration. The CIGALE results suggest the relatively blue color of the source in the rest-frame UV is due to emission from the host galaxy at <1 m . CIGALE places the stellar mass of the underlying galaxy at log (M_*/ M _ )=10.60^ +0.2 _ 0.4 .

gmw_00001264.name: JADES 21925
gmw_00001264.overview: JADES 21925 is an active galactic nucleus at a redshift of z=3.1 [arXiv:2404.03576]. Its mid-infrared emission is dominated by the active nucleus, while the host galaxy dominates the rest-frame ultraviolet emission and may harbor an overmassive black hole [arXiv:2404.03576].

sources.summary: The paper presents JADES 21925 as a z=3.1 X-ray-detected LRD with JWST photometry, AGN-dominated mid-infrared emission, host UV emission, moderate attenuation, and a possible overmassive black hole.

quotes.quote: JADES 21925 was first identified as an X-ray emitter by Giacconi02 as CXOCDFSJ033220.9-275223 in the 1 Msec Chandra Deep Field South observations and more recently detected in the 7 Ms observations of the field presented in Luo17 . Images of JADES 21925 in multiple NIRCam bands are shown in Figure fig:JADES21925_images . The source shows clear signs of an extended, blue structure surrounding a reddened point source that only becomes prominent at wavelengths longer than 2 m. The photometric redshift of the source was reported as z=1.5 in the Luo17 catalog, which we revise upward to z=3.1 with the inclusion of JWST photometry from JADES.

Like PRIMER-COS 3866, we find the 3-30 m emission of JADES 21925 is dominated by AGN emission, while the rest-frame UV emission is attributed to the host galaxy. We find the extinction of the AGN emission by polar dust to be E(B-V)=1.39 0.5 and CIGALE's estimate for the stellar mass of the underlying galaxy is log (M_*/ M _ )=8.84^ +0.15 _ 0.38 .

The results of this analysis indicate JADES 21925 is moderately attenuated, with a best-fit column density of (N_ H / cm ^ 2 )=22.72^ +0.13 _ 16 , resulting in an obscuration-corrected X-ray luminosity of (L_ 2-10 keV / erg s ^ 1 ) = 43.73 0.06 . The ratio of our measured extinction and column density again favors a E(B-V)/N_ H ratio below that of the Galactic standard value. Using the median of the ratios reported in and our measured E(B-V) extinction would predict a column density of (N_ H / cm ^ 2 ) = 22.5 , which agrees with our measured value, within the errors, better than the 21.6 cm ^ 2 obtained using the Galactic standard ratio. Our measured X-ray luminosity, coupled with the relatively low stellar mass that we infer for the host from our SED fit, indicates that JADES 21925, like PRIMER-COS 3866, may harbor an overmassive black hole.

gmw_00001265.name: JADES GN 1146115
gmw_00001265.overview: JADES GN 1146115 is a high-redshift galaxy at z=6.7 that hosts a dormant supermassive black hole [arXiv:2404.11643]. The galaxy has experienced a low, nearly constant star formation rate of approximately 1 solar mass per year for the past 100 million years [arXiv:2404.11643].

sources.summary: The paper models JADES GN 1146115 as a compact z=6.7 galaxy hosting an extremely overmassive dormant black hole, now near the boundary where efficient global star formation can resume.

quotes.quote: In this Note, we apply this model to JADES GN 1146115, a dormant supermassive black hole at z=6.7 whose mass is 40 of the host's mass in stars and accreting at 2 of the Eddington limit. The host has been forming stars at the low rate of 1 yr^ 1 for the past 100 Myr. Our model suggests that this galactic system is on the verge of a resurgence of global star formation activity. This transition comes after a period of domination by the effect of its overmassive black hole, whose duration is comparable to typical quasar lifetimes.

In summary, JADES GN 1146115 is at the threshold between regions of efficient and inefficient star formation. JADES GN 1146115 is a galaxy characterized by an instantaneous star formation rate (SFR) of 1.38^ +0.92 _ 0.45 yr^ 1 , lower by a factor of 3 compared to similar galaxies. Furthermore, this rate has been nearly constant in the last 100 Myr Juodzbalis_2024 . This time scale is intriguingly similar to typical quasar lifetimes, i.e., 10^7-10^8 yr, which is the duration of rapid black hole growth Hopkins_2005 .

gmw_00001266.name: NGC 3610
gmw_00001266.overview: NGC 3610 is a giant elliptical galaxy and a prototype intermediate-age merger remnant [arXiv:1701.02056, arXiv:astro-ph/0702467]. It features a warped inner disk, boxy isophotes, and an outer envelope with tidal plumes and tails [arXiv:1701.02056, arXiv:astro-ph/0702467].

sources.summary: NGC 3610 is modeled as a galaxy with combined boxy and disky isophotes, consistent with a thick boxy bulge, where higher harmonics reveal structure missed by standard modeling.

quotes.quote: A second galaxy, NGC 3610, which displays a combination of boxy and disky isophotes was modelled in this section. This galaxy is consistent with having a thick boxy bulge, i.e., the extent of the disk component is less than or comparable to that of the bulge.

The data was again retrieved from the HLA, and consisted of an I band image taken with WFPC2 (filter F814W; PI: B. Whitmore). The resultind models and residual maps corresponding to this galaxy are displayed in Figure fig:n3610 . Once again, standard can not model the higher harmonic orders, and the residual image is dominated by an artificial streak along the disk plane.

Figure fig:bn_coeff displays the B_4 and B_6 coefficients of NGC 2549 and NGC 3610, computed with , as they change with radius (along the major axis). The coefficients are re-normalised by the local gradient and local semi-major axis length (Equation equ:normalisation ), such that B_n B_n a I a ^ 1 , where a is the local semi-major axis length of the ellipse. This is a common way of expressing the coefficients as (dimensionless) deviations from ellipticity, as opposed to concrete intensity corrections. The transition between boxy and disky isophotes ( B_4 crossing 0) occurs in both galaxies, and also B_6 has non-zero amplitude for most of the radial range, which indicates that it plays a part in capturing the isophote shape.

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 3610 as a prototype intermediate-age merger remnant with a poor globular-cluster system, embedded disk and spheroid, boxy isophotes, fine structure, and asymmetric colors supporting a recent merger.

quotes.quote: We present a photometric study of the prototype merger remnant NGC 3610 and its globular cluster (GC) system, based on new GEMINI/GMOS and ACS/HST archival images. Thanks to the large FOV of our GMOS data, larger than previous studies, we are able to detect a `classical' bimodal GC colour distribution, corresponding to metal-poor and metal-rich GCs, at intermediate radii and a small subsample of likely young clusters of intermediate colours, mainly located in the outskirts. The extent of the whole GC system is settled as about 40 kpc. The GC population is quite poor, about 500 110 members that corresponds to a low total specific frequency S_N 0.8 .

The large-scale galaxy surface-brightness profile can be decomposed as an inner embedded disc and an outer spheroid, determining for both larger extents than earlier research (10 kpc and 30 kpc, respectively). We detect boxy isophotes, expected in merger remnants, and show a wealth of fine-structure in the surface-brightness distribution with unprecedented detail, coincident with the outer spheroid. The lack of symmetry in the galaxy colour map adds a new piece of evidence to the recent merger scenario of NGC 3610.

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 3610 as an intermediate-age giant elliptical merger remnant with blue colors, an inner twisted disk, tidal plumes and tails, and globular-cluster properties shaped by merger-driven evolution.

quotes.quote: The Advanced Camera for Surveys on board the Hubble Space Telescope has been used to obtain deep high-resolution images of the giant elliptical galaxy NGC 3610, a well-established remnant of a dissipative galaxy merger. These observations supersede previous, shallower observations which revealed the presence of a population of metal-rich globular clusters of intermediate age ( 1.5 - 4 Gyr).

Several optical imaging studies have shown that NGC 3610 is a bona fide inter medi ate-age merger remnant. Its colors are anomalously blue for a giant elliptical galaxy of its luminosity schsei92,goud+94a,idia+02 , suggesting recent star formation activity. Its inner region features a small, strongly twisted inner disk scoben90,whit+97 . Such disks are a natural byproduct of gas-rich galaxy mergers in simulations barn02 . The galaxy's outer envelope is extremely boxy relative to its inner body and shows several non-concentric plumes and tails seisch90 which are most likely remnants of tidal perturbations.

sources.summary: The paper revises NGC 3610 distance and globular-cluster implications using ACS SBF data, while its population models favor subsolar-to-near-solar metallicity over a broad age range.

quotes.quote: One of the main exceptions to the universality of the GCLF is NGC 3610, whose TOM is 2 magnitudes fainter than expected. richtler03 argues that one of the possible causes of such mismatch is the presence of a population of intermediate-age metal-rich clusters, resulting in a fainter TOM. However, even though only the blue subpopulation of clusters is taken into account, there is still a large offset between NGC 3610 and the other galaxies.

The difference between our distance and the estimation from T01 is probably due to the much lower quality of the NGC 3610 ground-based data used by T01, compared to these high resolution ACS images. Inspecting the data quality flags from T01 (see their Table 1, Q and PD values), we find that the SBF magnitude of NGC 3610 should be considered as poorly constrained. Moreover, the T01 distance of the other galaxy NGC 3613, which is classified as same group member of NGC 3610, agrees within uncertainties with our new SBF distance.

sources.summary: The paper selects NGC3610 as a possible UHE cosmic-ray source, describes it as a merger-remnant elliptical with a black hole and warped disk, and reports MAGIC non-detections and flux limits.

quotes.quote: NGC3610 is a merger remnant elliptical galaxy with a black hole of 0.5 10^ 8 solar masses M_ . The maximum accessible cosmic ray energy is estimated to be 4.4 10^ 19 eV. This galaxy has attracted attentions because of its fine structure and prominently warped disk, implying that a dynamical event occurred in a few 10^ 9 years ago .

Following up with AGASA-HiRes quartet detection, we searched the possible UHE cosmic ray sources, nearby quasar remnants NGC3610 and NGC3613 and a starburst galaxy Arp299, for VHE emission. In 200 - 500 GeV energies, the upper limits on the gamma ray flux from each source is placed against each object and were 8 12 Crab flux at a 95 CL. Over the region observed, there are no positive signals for the VHE emission by effectively 15 hour observation.

sources.summary: The review highlights NGC 3610 as a discrepant GCLF-distance case whose intermediate-age red globular clusters, likely tied to a merger, make its turnover magnitude fainter than expected.

quotes.quote: Among the elliptical galaxies, the largest deviation is shown by NGC 3610, admittedly with a large error. But more interesting is the fact that this galaxy violates one important condition for the TOM to be a viable distance indicator, namely that its GC population is old. NGC 3610 is known to host GCs of intermediate-age. Whitmore et al. and Whitmore et al. estimate an age of about 4-6 Gyr for the red (and presumably metal-rich) GCs which plausibly have their origin in a merger event (Schweizer Seitzer ).

We therefore can conclude that in the case of NGC 3610, a large part of the deviation of the GCLF distance from the SBF distance comes from the fact that the contribution of the presumably younger red clusters causes a fainter TOM than from the blue, metal-poor and presumably older clusters alone. But also when we use only the TOM of the blue clusters to determine the distance modulus, which then would be 32.94, a significant difference remains to the SBF modulus, which is 31.65.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 3610 as an intermediate-age merger remnant where globular-cluster luminosity functions test whether red cluster systems survive and evolve toward old elliptical populations.

quotes.quote: Intermediate-age remnants, such as NGC 1316 and NGC 3610 , offer the possibility of actually observing this process in action. While the blue, metal-poor GCs in these galaxies should have an LF similar to that of old GCs in elliptical galaxies (peaking at M_V 7.2 ), the LFs of the red, metal-rich clusters are expected to extend to both brighter and fainter luminosities. This prediction has recently been verified for two intermediate-age merger remnants, providing important evidence that mergers can indeed create ``red'' GC subsystems that survive destruction.

Likewise, Whitmore et al. \ performed deep WFPC2 photometry of the GC system of the candidate 4-Gyr-old merger remnant NGC 3610 , . They found a very similar result, with the red GCs still showing a power-law LF shape (see Fig. f:GCLFs ,d).

gmw_00001267.name: Z229 15
gmw_00001267.overview: Z229 15 is a nearby Seyfert 1 active galaxy [arXiv:1103.2153, arXiv:1907.07048] hosting a supermassive black hole estimated at approximately 10 million solar masses [arXiv:1103.2153, arXiv:2012.04906]. Its optical and X-ray emissions exhibit stochastic variability [arXiv:1612.06817] characterized by distinct spectral states [arXiv:1907.07048] and a five-day accretion-disk timescale [arXiv:1409.1613, arXiv:1704.05717].

sources.summary: The paper presents optical reverberation mapping of the Seyfert 1 galaxy Zw 229-015, identifies its aliases, measures a 3.86-day line lag, and estimates a 10^7 solar-mass black hole.

quotes.quote: Here, we present the results of a six-month, ground-based, optical reverberation-mapping program for the \ target Zw 229-015 at z=0.0275 ; falco1999 , with the goal of determining the black hole mass in this galaxy from its \ reverberation lag. This galaxy (also known as CGCG 229-015 or 2MASX J19052592+4227398) was first identified as a Type 1 Seyfert by proust1990 . Since then, no follow-up observations of this AGN have been reported in the literature at all, aside from its inclusion in ROSAT All-Sky Survey source catalogs voges1999,zimmermann2001 . Nevertheless, Zw 229-015 is one of the nearest and brightest AGNs in the \ field, and it was selected as a monitoring target for the first two cycles of \ observations.

To summarize our conclusions: we have measured a rest-frame reverberation lag of =3.86_ 0.90 ^ +0.69 days for the \ line in Zw 229-015. Combining this result with the broad \ line width, we obtain a virial estimate of 10^7 \ for the black hole in this galaxy, under the assumption of a virial normalization factor of f=5.25 . The mass estimate does not change significantly if we adopt the method of marconi2008 to obtain a mass estimate corrected for the dynamical influence of radiation pressure in the BLR. The \ target Zw 229-015 is destined to be one of the most important AGNs for investigations of the origin and characteristics of optical continuum variability.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes Zw 229-15 Kepler photometry, finding an unprecedented long, precise light curve and a roughly 5-day optical PSD bend linked to accretion-disk timescales.

quotes.quote: Mushotzky Department of Astronomy, University of Maryland, College Park, MD 20742-2421, USA We present time series analyses of the full \ dataset of . This \ light curve --- with a baseline greater than three years, composed of virtually continuous, evenly sampled 30-minute measurements --- is unprecedented in its quality and precision. We utilize two methods of power spectral analysis to investigate the optical variability and search for evidence of a bend frequency associated with a characteristic optical variability timescale.

More importantly both also show strong bends ( 2 ) at timescales of 5 days, a feature similar to those seen in the X-ray PSDs of AGN but never before in the optical. This observed 5 day timescale may be associated with one of several physical processes potentially responsible for the variability. A plausible association could be made with light-crossing, dynamical or thermal timescales, depending on the assumed value of the accretion disk size and on unobserved disk parameters such as and H/R . This timescale is not consistent with the viscous timescale, which would be years in a 10^7 M_ AGN such as .

sources.summary: The paper analyzes XMM-Newton and Kepler data for Zw 229.015, finding weak soft excess, acceptable thermal-comptonisation or blurred-reflection fits, X-ray lags between energy bands, and stochastic optical/X-ray variability.

quotes.quote: We analyse the spectra of the archival XMM-Newton data of the Seyfert 1 AGN Zw 229.015 in the energy range 0.3-10.0 keV . When fitted with a simple power-law, the spectrum shows signatures of weak soft excess below 1.0 keV . We find that both thermal comptonisation and relativistically blurred reflection models provide the most acceptable spectral fits with plausible physical explanations to the origin of the soft excess than do multicolour disc blackbody and smeared wind absorption models.

Our analysis reveals that the variation in the 3.0-10.0 keV band lags that in the 0.3-1.0 keV by 600^ +290 _ 280 s , while the lag between the 1.0-10.0 keV and 0.3-1.0 keV is 980^ +500 _ 500 s . This implies that the X-ray emissions are possibly emanating from different regions within the system.

Based on this analysis, we infer that the X-ray as well as optical lightcurves of Zw 229.015 are dominated plausibly by stochastic noise rather than deterministic chaos. The implication of this could be that advection is an important component of the accretion flow dynamics with random cooling processes general advective accretion flow; 2010MNRAS.402..961R destroying any signature of self-similarity in the flux variability, making it stochastic.

sources.summary: The paper treats 229 as a nearby Seyfert 1 AGN with a reverberation-mapped black-hole mass, analyzing multi-epoch variability and finding two spectral states driven by power-law continuum changes.

quotes.quote: 229 (CGCG 229-015, KIC 006932990, Zw 229-15) is a nearby ( z=0.0279 ) Seyfert 1 AGN with the reverberation mapped black hole mass of M_ BH 10^ 7 M_ Barth+2011 and a moderate value of Galactic absorption column density N_ H    5.83   10^ 20 cm^ 2 Kalberla+2005 . A considerable amount of work has been done on this source in the past decade as a result of being in the field of view. 229 is one of the AGN with the most complete, continuous, high quality and high resolution light curves that enabled the numerous studies on its optical variability properties Mushotzky+2011, Carini+2012, Edelson+2014, Kasliwal+2015, Kasliwal+2017, Adegoke+2017, Dobrotka+2017 .

We have carried out a systematic analysis of the nearby (z=0.0279) active galaxy 229 using multi-epoch, multi-instrument and deep pointed observations with , , and . Spectral and temporal variability are examined in detail on both the long (weeks-to-years) and short (hours) timescales. A deep observation of the source shows two distinct spectral states; a bright-soft state and a dim-hard state in which changes in the power law component account for the differences. Partial covering, blurred reflection and soft Comptonisation models describe the X-ray spectra comparably well, but the smooth, rather featureless, spectrum may be favouring the soft Comptonisation scenario.

sources.summary: The paper reports dust reverberation mapping of the Seyfert 1 galaxy Z229 15, deriving optical/NIR lags, host-galaxy corrections, coordinates, and a dust-torus radius near the AGN continuum source.

quotes.quote: Using cross-correlation function analysis we found significant time delays between the optical V and the AD corrected J, H and K_s light curves. The lags in the rest frame of the source are 12.52^ +10.00 _ 9.55 days (between V and J), 15.63^ +5.05 _ 5.11 days (between V and H) and 20.36^ +5.82 _ 5.68 days (between V and K_ s ). Given the large error bars, these lags are consistent with each other. However, considering the lag between V and K_s bands to represent the inner edge of the dust torus, the torus in Z229 15 lies at a distance of 0.017 pc from the central ionizing continuum.
Located at _ 2000 = 19:05:25.94 and _ 2000 = +42:27:39.76, Z229 15 has a black hole mass of 1.00^ +0.19 _ 0.24 10^7 M_ 2011ApJ...732..121B and a Gaia G band brightness of 16.44 mag.

The host galaxy is prominently seen in the observed B and V band images. We devised a procedure to remove the contribution of the host galaxy to the observed optical light curves and get pure light curves of the optical continuum source.

sources.summary: The paper reanalyzes ZW229-15 variability, finding optical and possible X-ray break frequencies and arguing that its behavior supports a hot inner corona interpretation.

quotes.quote: The former is supported by detection of the same break frequencies in the \ X-ray data of ZW229-15. Subsequent re-analysis of ZW229-15 \ data by edelson2014 revealed a characteristic break frequency suggesting the existence of time-scale of 5 day in the PDS. This PDSs shape upgrade in interacting binaries together with the re-analysis of the ZW229-15 data by edelson2014 and the discovery of a more complex PDS structure motivated us to revisit all four AGNs already studied by mushotzky2011 , searching for multiple-components in the PDS.

Break frequencies in the ZW229-15 \ PDS The two break frequencies we have found do not agree within the errors, suggesting that we found PDS components different from those found by edelson2014 . We analysed long-term data of ZW229-15 from \ satellite and we conclude that despite the low quality and quantity of data, the presence of both break frequencies is possible in the corresponding X-ray PDS. This paper supports the corona interpretation at least in ZW229-15 because of positive detection or at least some presence indication of both optical break frequencies also in X-rays.

sources.summary: The paper reanalyzes ZW229-15 timing data, comparing apparent multicomponent PDS features with instrumental artifacts and arguing that independent detections can still reflect real AGN variability.

quotes.quote: firstpage - lastpage 2018 We analysed the light curves of four active galactic nuclei (AGN) from the \ field, and find multicomponent power density spectra with characteristic frequencies that are surprisingly similar to other \ AGNs (including ZW229-15). An identical time series analysis of randomly selected planet candidate stars revealed the same features, suggesting an instrumental origin for the variability. This result is enigmatic, as these signals have been confirmed for ZW229-15 using independent observations from .
Subsequent re-analysis of one of the four objects ZW229-15 performed by edelson2014 revealed a break frequency at 5 days.

\ Reanalysis of ZW229-15 The presence of very similar PDS components in all eight \ AGNs from revalski2014 and dobrotka2017 implies the signals are instrumental in origin. Additional support for an astrophysical origin in \ data of ZW229-15 was presented in dobrotka2017 through additional PDS calculations using \ data. We found the same PDS components, which supports the features having a physical origin.

sources.summary: The paper treats Zw 229-15 as a supermassive-scale Seyfert comparison with a years-long stitched Kepler light curve, long baseline, global SF breaks, and fitted timescale-dependent slopes.

quotes.quote: Among them, the Seyfert galaxy Zw 229-15 (at z = 0.0275 and with M_ BH 10^7 M_ ; Barth2011ApJ...732..121B ) has been observed in four quarters, within which changes from 1.48 to 1.65 ; the Seyfert galaxy W2R 1904+37 (at z = 0.089 with M_ BH 10^ 7.66 M_ ; Smith2018ApJ...857..141S ) has been observed in three quarters, within which changes from 1.30 to 1.38 ; the Seyfert galaxy W2 1925+50 (at z = 0.067 with M_ BH 10^ 8.04 M_ ; Smith2018ApJ...857..141S ) has been observed in three quarters, within which changes from 1.37 to 1.48 ; and the Seyfert galaxy W2R 1858+48 has been observed in two quarters, within which transitioned from 1.44 to 1.49 . Except the last one, the other three AGNs, i.e., Zw 229-15, W2R 1904+37, and W2 1925+50, have years-long light curves stitched by The light curves of W2R 1858+48 can not be stitched according to .

In the top panels of Figures fig:sf_dis to fig:sf_dis_more_5548 , we show the observed SFs (crosses) for Zw 229-15, W2 1925+50, W2R 1904+37, and NGC 5548, respectively. For the two AGNs with the longest baselines, i.e., Zw 229-15 over 3.3 yrs and W2 1925+50 over 2.8 yrs, their SFs intuitively have at least two breaks near 10^ 5-6 s and 10^ 6-7 s, indicating that a single could be not enough for describing their global SFs. Using a single power-law (SPL) function fitting to the SF in three distinct timescale ranges, we find that with increasing timescales changes from 1.8 , through 1.3 , to 0.7 for both Zw 229-15 and W2 1925+50 (see Table tab:smbh for the selected timescale ranges and the corresponding best-fit ). Note that owing to the significance of the noise contribution within the smallest timescale range, a noise term has been included in the fit, that is, the nominated _n + SPL fit (cf. the dotted curves in Figures fig:sf_dis and fig:sf_dis_more_kepler2 for Zw 229-15 and W2 1925+50, respectively).

sources.summary: The paper uses Zw 229-015 as the uniquely ground-monitored Kepler AGN, finding CBV corrections likely removed real variability while recovering its known optical power-spectrum break.

quotes.quote: However, since the majority of these AGN were not known before the launch of Kepler , Zw 229-015 (KIC 6932990) is unique in this sample for its simultaneous ground-based monitoring (from the Lick AGN Monitoring Project; ), which allows us to determine whether or not the application of CBVs has overlapped with and removed real signal.

In KIC 6932990 (Zw229-015), the already-known break is detected in our light curve with greater significance than in past studies, which reported only a slightly better fit with a bending power law. In this object, real variability was almost certainly removed, but only the existing break was recovered. The uncorrected light curve does not actually pass the Uttley2002 test, despite being the light curve in which the bending power law was discovered in past papers, so the Uttley2002 method is stricter. Because of what happened with Zw229-015, there is some basis for accepting breaks that are significant in CBV-treated light curves but insignificant in the original.

sources.summary: The paper reports REMAP optical and near-infrared monitoring of Z229-15, finding variability while leaving detailed lag analysis still in progress.

quotes.quote: Z229-15 Z229-15 is a Seyfert 1 galaxy at a redshift of z = 0.028. About 25 epochs of observations were accumulated in the optical V and B-bands and in the NIR J, H and K _s bands. The contribution of accretion disk to the NIR flux was corrected as per the details outlined in Section 4.3.1.
Preliminary light curves in the optical B-band and NIR K _s band are shown in Figure 4. Further analysis of the data acquired on this object is in progress.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes Zw 229-15 as a narrow-line Seyfert 1 observed by Kepler, measuring its stitched light curve and structure functions.

quotes.quote: The stitched light curve of Zw 229-15, spanning 3.3 years with a time resolution of 30 minutes, is presented in Figure fig:zw229-15-lc-eps . Zw 229-15 is a narrow-line Seyfert 1 galaxy, observed by Kepler during 13 quarters (Q4 Q16, the most among Kepler AGNs). Figure fig:zw229-15-sf-eps shows structure functions of both the original and smoothing-corrected light curves of Zw 229-15. The original SF( ) shows a rise towards longer timescales with a gradually decreasing slope.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes the Seyfert 1 AGN Zw 229-15 light curve with C-ARMA modeling, explaining variability slopes and recovering reported 5.6-day and 67-day timescales.

quotes.quote: We analyze the light curve of the AGN Zw 229-15 and find that the observed variability behavior can be modeled as a damped harmonic oscillator perturbed by a colored noise process. The model powerspectrum turns over on time-scale 385 d. On shorter time-scales, the log-powerspectrum slope varies between 2 and 4 , explaining the behavior noted by previous studies. We recover and identify both the 5.6 d and 67 d timescales reported by previous work using the Green's Function of the C-ARMA equation rather than by directly fitting the powerspectrum of the light curve.

sources.summary: The paper finds Zw 229-015 has remarkably well constrained BLR parameters from a single spectrum, attributed to significant line-profile asymmetry and structure.

quotes.quote: Zw 229-015 The parameters for this object are remarkably well constrained and agree with the full light-curve result within the uncertainties. As for Mrk 141, this is likely due to the information associated with the significant asymmetry and structure of the line profile in this AGN. The only exception is , which is not constrained for Zw 229-015 or any of the AGN in our sample. We find = zw229_opening degrees, = zw229_inc degrees, = zw229_kappa , = zw229_eps , = zw229_fellip , \ is constrained to be > 0.5 i.e. signatures of outflowing trajectories, = zw229_thetae degrees and = zw229_rmin light-days. The posterior probability distributions is shown in Fig. posterior_zw229 .

gmw_00001268.name: (VV2010c) J093313.9+015858
gmw_00001268.overview: The galaxy (VV2010c) J093313.9+015858 is a dubious Type-1 active galactic nucleus candidate [arXiv:2005.02052]. Its emission line spectrum and diagnostic diagrams instead favor an H II region classification [arXiv:2005.02052].

sources.summary: The paper flags (VV2010c) J093313.9+015858 as a dubious Type-1 AGN candidate whose spectrum and diagnostic diagrams favor HII emission rather than an AGN NLR.

quotes.quote: Two examples of objects that may have broad lines but be impostors, are the galaxies (VV2010c) J093313.9+015858 and NGP9 F270-0377962. Their spectra are shown in Figure voigt . The P-Cygni profiles observed in the H profiles of (VV2010c) J093313.9+015858 and NGP9 F270-0377962 are almost never associated with quasar spectra.

In the case of VV2010c, diagnostic diagrams based on [ OIII ] /H vs. [ OII ] /H \ and and [ OIII ] /H vs. [ NII ] /H \ positively identify the emitting regions as HII. The only discordant diagnostic diagrams is [ OIII ] /H vs. [ OI ] /H \ due to the significant [ OI ] emission. The extremely low FWHM 200 km/s also disfavors the possibility that the emission line spectrum is due to the NLR of an AGN.

gmw_00001269.name: NGP9 F270-0377962
gmw_00001269.overview: NGP9 F270-0377962 is an emission line galaxy that has been identified as a dubious Type-1 active galactic nucleus candidate [arXiv:2005.02052]. Its spectral line diagnostics place it as a LINER, with some low-ionization nuclear activity supported by a line width of 400 km/s [arXiv:2005.02052].

sources.summary: The paper treats NGP9 F270-0377962 as a dubious Type-1 AGN candidate, finding SN-like spectral features and line diagnostics partly consistent with LINER activity.

quotes.quote: Two examples of objects that may have broad lines but be impostors, are the galaxies (VV2010c) J093313.9+015858 and NGP9 F270-0377962. Their spectra are shown in Figure voigt . The P-Cygni profiles observed in the H profiles of (VV2010c) J093313.9+015858 and NGP9 F270-0377962 are almost never associated with quasar spectra.

The case of NGP9 F270-0377962 is different. This source is placed at the boundary between HII and transition objects in the d.d. [ OIII ] /H vs. [ NII ] /H ; in the [ OIII ] /H vs. [ OI ] /H \ and [ OIII ] /H vs. [ SII ] /H \ d.d. the source is classified as a LINER. In the diagram [ OII ] / [ OIII ] vs. [ OIII ] /H the data point is not localted along the correlation expected for HII region. The FWHM 400 km/s is also consistent with some low-ionization nuclear activity.

gmw_00001270.name: AzTEC/C159
gmw_00001270.overview: AzTEC/C159 is a star-forming disk galaxy located at a redshift of approximately 4.57 [arXiv:1709.04954, arXiv:1710.10181]. This gas-rich system features an extended rotating molecular reservoir [arXiv:1710.10181], a flat rotation curve [arXiv:1709.04954], and star formation efficiency comparable to merger-driven starbursts [arXiv:1710.10181].

sources.summary: AzTEC/C159 is a z=4.567 SMG with high star formation; the tilted-ring analysis finds the best evidence for a flat rotation curve and possible dark matter content.

quotes.quote: AzTEC/C159 Located in the COSMOS field, AzTEC/C159 ( z=4.5670 ) is an SMG discovered at 1.1 mm in the AzTEC/ASTE survey arex11 . smol15 derived a dust temperature of T_ dust =39 2 K, SFR 700 200 M _ year ^ 1 , and M_ * =1.1 10^ 11 M _ .

AzTEC/C159 shows the most likely evidence for a flat rotation curve at large radius. The use of a zeroth moment image here is not very helpful, as the integrated emission is nearly circular ( 0.27'' 0.23'' ). This introduces a large uncertainty in both inclination angle and position angle. However, all derived quantities agree with those found with rotcur .

Since we have estimates of the stellar, dust, and molecular gas masses (Table fitres2 and references therein), we may estimate the total dark matter content ( M_ DM ) of the inner 4 kpc by assuming M_ dyn =M_ dust +M_ * +M_ H_2 +M_ DM + where we are neglecting the mass of the ionized medium ( M_ HII ) and cold medium ( M_ HI ), for which we have no estimates. By inverting this equation, we find that M_ DM 10^ 11 M _ .

sources.summary: The paper studies AzTEC/C159 as a z=4.567 star-forming disk with broad CO lines, an extended rotating molecular reservoir, high gas mass, and extreme star-formation efficiency.

quotes.quote: We studied the molecular gas properties of AzTEC/C159, a star-forming disk galaxy at z=4.567 , in order to better constrain the nature of the high-redshift end of the sub-mm selected galaxy (SMG) population. We secured ^ 12 CO molecular line detections for the J =2 1 and J =5 4 transitions using the Karl G. Jansky Very Large Array (VLA) and the NOrthern Extended Millimeter Array (NOEMA) interferometer. The broad ( FWHM 750 km s ^ 1 ) and tentative double-peaked profiles of both ^ 12 CO lines are consistent with an extended molecular gas reservoir, which is distributed in a rotating disk as previously revealed from [ CII ] 158 m line observations. Based on the ^ 12 CO(2 1) emission line we derived L'_ CO =(3.4 0.6) 10^ 10 K km s ^ 1 pc ^ 2 , that yields a molecular gas mass of M_ H_2 ( _ CO /4.3)=(1.5 0.3) 10^ 11 M _ and unveils a gas-rich system with _ gas ( _ CO /4.3) M_ H_2 /M_ =3.3 0.7 . The extreme star formation efficiency (SFE) of AzTEC/C159, parametrized by the ratio L_ IR /L'_ CO =(216 80) L _ (K km s ^ 1 pc ^ 2 )^ 1 , is comparable to merger-driven starbursts such as local ultra-luminous infrared galaxies (ULIRGs) and SMGs.

Based on mass budget considerations we constrained the value for the L'_ CO -- H _2 mass conversion factor in AzTEC/C159, i.e. _ CO =3.9^ +2.7 _ 1.3 M _ K ^ 1 km ^ 1 s pc ^ 2 , that is consistent with a self-gravitating molecular gas distribution as observed in local star-forming disk galaxies. Cold gas streams from cosmological filaments might be fueling a gravitationally unstable gas-rich disk in AzTEC/C159, which breaks into giant clumps forming stars as efficiently as in merger-driven systems and generate high gas excitation. These results support the evolutionary connection between AzTEC/C159-like systems and massive quiescent disk galaxies at z 2 .

sources.summary: The paper presents AzTEC/C159 as a COSMOS 1.1 mm source with stated coordinates, deboosted flux, a nearby radio counterpart, and a DEIMOS spectroscopic redshift of 4.569.

quotes.quote: AzTEC/C159 is a 3.7 detection in the ASTE/AzTEC-COSMOS 1.1 mm survey of the inner COSMOS 1 deg ^2 ( ) \ located at _ 2000.0 =09^ h 59^ m 29 75 , _ 2000.0 =+01 55 35 . Its deboosted 1.1 mm flux density is 3.3 1.3 mJy (deboosted by a factor of 1.5 ). Here we associate it with a radio source within the AzTEC beam, 12 away from the centroid of the AzTEC detection at 34 resolution. A DEIMOS spectrum for this source yields a spectroscopic redshift of z_ spec =4.569 (see sec:specdata ).

gmw_00001271.name: NGC7319
gmw_00001271.overview: NGC 7319 is a spiral galaxy in Stephan's Quintet [arXiv:1505.01166] that hosts a Seyfert active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2207.13067] and a low-power radio jet [arXiv:2208.04835]. Past interactions with neighboring galaxies stripped most of its neutral gas [arXiv:1105.5840, arXiv:2208.04835], creating tidal tails [arXiv:1512.02633] and a gas bridge connecting to the group's large-scale shock [arXiv:1909.05797].

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 7319 as the most massive Quintet member, with continuous star formation, older interactions with NGC 7320C and NGC 7318A, and depleted neutral hydrogen.

quotes.quote: NGC 7319 The analysis of Figure fig:N7319 shows that star formation in NGC 7319 has been going on throughout its history, reflected in the continuous distribution of SCCs along the evolutionary track. However, some peculiarities are observed. For example, the fraction of SCCs younger than 100 Myr is lower than found in NGC 7318A/B and NSBR. Our analysis of Figure fig:mass.v.age (which gives us the approximate values of the masses and ages of SCCs above the 50 completeness level) shows that we should be able to detect a 100 Myr old clusters down to 10^ 4.0 , relatively modest-sized clusters that are massive enough to have a fully sampled initial mass function and therefore 'normal' colors (as investigated by Silva-Villa2011 ). Thus, the low number of 100 Myr old clusters is likely to be real.

In light of the models of interaction found in the literature, our results are consistent with the following scenario: the first traceable interaction for NGC 7319 was with NGC 7320C and it happened 500 Myr ago. It was the cause of the initial increase in the star formation rate. At the same time, this interaction stripped a large quantity of the gas from NGC 7319 and deposited it into the intergalactic medium (IGM), between galaxies NGC 7319 and NGC 7318A ( moles98 ).

sources.summary: The paper discusses NGC 7319 as a core spiral involved in tail-forming encounters, hosting a Seyfert 2 bridge, possible collision history, JWST PAH structures, and unusually inefficient star formation.

quotes.quote: The fiducial model in Renaud2010 involves a number of events in the last half Gyr, including collisions between spiral galaxies NGC 7319 and NGC 7320c creating the so-called outer tail, between NGC 7319 and the early-type galaxy NGC 7318a creating the inner tail, and between the high-speed intruder NGC 7318b and the intragroup medium producing shocks in the gas stripped by the previous interactions. However, the hydrodynamical simulations in Hwang2012 favor a different scenario in which both the outer tail and inner tail were triggered simultaneously by a close encounter between NGC 7320c and NGC 7319 about 9 10^8 years ago.
The radiations are mainly from two locations: (1) the large shock front, (2) the so called ``bridge'' that links the Seyfert 2 nucleus of NGC 7319 and the shock front Cluver2010 .

If the involved core galaxy were NGC 7319, then its gas disk had been already distorted when NGC 7320c arrived. Alternatively, NGC 7319 could have had been involved in a collision with the elliptical galaxy NGC7317 which seems to be linked with NGC 7319 with a diffuse optical halo Moles1997, Duc2018 . The recently published JWST MIRI images of SQ in the JWST Early Release Observations Pontoppidan2022 show some peculiar features in NGC 7319 in the 7.7 m band in incredible details, which is presumably dominated by the emission of PAH molecules. These features don't look like any tidal arms/tails at all, but are very similar to the spokes in a ring galaxy which are triggered by a head-on collision with an intruder galaxy (e.g. those in the Cartwheel Galaxy; Renaud2018 ). The rather high molecular gas content and very low SFR in the disk of NGC 7319 Gao2000, Yttergren2021, Xu2005 correspond to a very low star formation efficiency, similar to the situation in Arp 142, a ring galaxy involved in a head-on collisions previously XU2021 .

sources.summary: The paper discusses NGC 7319 as a Seyfert 2 galaxy tied to AGN-like MIR lines, nearby bridge emission, concentrated CO, and a dusty tidal feature.

quotes.quote: In addition we detect strong emission from the star forming region, SQ-A, as well as associated with NGC 7319. We discuss further details of the AGN-like MIR emission lines from NGC 7319 in Appendix A. Figures 2a-d reveal a new structure running eastward from the ``main'' shock ridge.

The S(0) and S(1) maps demonstrate that the in the bridge terminates in a large clump a few arcsecs west of the nucleus of the Seyfert 2 galaxy NGC 7319, and in a small detour to the North (especially in the S(1) map which has the highest S/N).

As observed by Yun97 , the CO around NGC 7319 is concentrated in two regions. The dominant complex is North of the nucleus residing in a dusty tidal feature. The nuclear CO is elongated perpendicular to the stellar disk suggesting a deficiency of ongoing star formation in the disk. t The CO distribution does not correlate with the location of warm emission, particularly around NGC 7319.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 7319 as the main Stephan Quintet galaxy, linked to the group core, tidal tails, and interactions with NGC 7320c and NGC 7318b.

quotes.quote: Stephan's Quintet ( St77 ; hereafter SQ), also known as Hickson Compact Group 92 Hi82 , is the first discovered compact galaxy group. It is located in the constellation Pegasus. SQ consists of five galaxies (NGC 7319, NGC 7318a, NGC 7318b, NGC 7317, and NGC 7320, cf. also Fig. morph ) with an estimated distance to earth of 94 Mpc MoMa98,ApXu06 . SQ is famous for a physical adjacency between four of the galaxies (NGC 7319, 7318a, 7318b, and 7317), whereby strong interactions between three of these members are apparently causing tidal tails, a strong group-wide shock visible in X-ray PiTr97,SuRo01 and radio emission AlHa72,XuLu03 , and a region of active star formation northern of the colliding galaxies Xu05 .

The fifth galaxy, NGC 7320, is observed to be a much closer foreground galaxy and is not part of the interacting group Sh74,AlSu80,MoSu97 . It has a recessional velocity of 740 km s ^ 1 FaKu99 . Three of the four physically related galaxies, the main galaxy NGC 7319 as well as NGC 7317 and NGC 7318a, have a similar recessional velocity of 6640-6670 km s ^ 1 FeGa11 and represent the core of the compact group (.f. Fig. morph ). NGC 7318b is observed to be a high-speed intruder ( 5770 km s ^ 1 ) and seems to interact with the IGM within the main system for the first time MoSu97,FeGa11 .
