Finally, there is a sixth galaxy, NGC 7320c, which shows a similar recessional velocity ( 5990 km s ^ 1 ) as the core of the compact group FeGa11 and is therefore suggested to interact with NGC 7319 MoSu97 , because it reveals connected tidal features in the eastern large tidal tail (cf. the outer tail in Fig. morph ). Therefore, it is most likely also part of the compact group Ar73 .

sources.summary: The paper analyzes NGC 7319 as a Seyfert 2 galaxy where outflow gas emission requires both AGN photoionization and shock excitation, with shocks spanning roughly 200-1000 km/s.

quotes.quote: The observed line intensities and widths in the outflow of NGC 7319 are consistent with the spectra of radiative shocks with velocity v _ s > 100 km s ^ 1 1993ARA&A..31..373D . Typically, the shock velocity (v _ s ) can be diagnosed using H , 6300, 3727 and 5007 based on the emission-line velocity dispersion and/or the FWHM. Nevertheless, as it was previously discussed, we do not have enough spectral resolution to determine the velocity dispersion of the gas or to resolve any line splitting in the data.

The observed line ratios are thus consistent both with shock and AGN models, but neither of these possibilities can explain the observational results exclusively. Even the nucleus emission, classified as Seyfert 2, is not plainly consistent with AGN models, which may be caused by a partial contamination by shock emission that we observe projected on the nucleus. We reach the conclusion that the excitation mechanism of the gas in the galaxy is a mixture of AGN photoionization and shocks, and the resulting emission spectra are generated by the combination of both ionization mechanisms. Thus, while A96 scenario considered the AGN photoionization as the only responsible mechanism for exciting the gas, the spatial resolution and wide FoV of our data have allowed to detect the presence of shocks as another source of ionization, and to study the coexistence of these two processes in the observed gas emission of the galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper maps NGC7319 as Stephan’s Quintet’s active galaxy, finding a large-scale nuclear wind, Seyfert 1 signatures, decoupled gas and stellar disks, and strong nearby CO emission.

quotes.quote: We map the large-scale nuclear wind in NGC7319 and its decoupled gas and stellar disk. With our high resolution data we can, for the first time, reveal the Seyfert 1 nature of NGC7319 and fit the narrow-line region and broad-line region of the H line. While the ^ 12 CO (1-0) map shows significant emission in the area in or near NGC7319, the bridge, and the star-forming ridge, the ^ 12 CO (2-1) emission shows a prevalence to the star-forming ridge, an area south of the NGC7318 pair, and shows an extension towards NGC7317 -- connecting NGC7317 to the centre of the group, indicating a previous interaction.

Classified as a Seyfert 2 galaxy, NGC7319 is the only confirmed active galaxy in the group. The extensive emission in this galaxy allowed for a spaxel-by-spaxel fitting of the ionised gas emission lines and the stellar continuum. Although there are parts of NGC7319 that display dual gas emission lines, the higher signal-to-noise of the 1.5'' region spectra is required for a proper fit of the dual lines; therefore, the continuum-subtracted line-emission maps are created with an allowance for a single broader line. When studying the multiple velocity components in or near NGC7319, we find that they can often be related to the line-of-sight velocities of not only the central velocity of NGC7319 but also NGC7320C (likely in the creation of the inner tidal tail). From the pPXF fit of the stellar kinematics we obtain the mean stellar line-of-sight velocity and velocity dispersion in the central 1.5'' of NGC7319 as 6814 12 km/s and 159.4 13.0 km/s, respectively.

sources.summary: The ERO paper describes NGC7319 as a merging Stephan’s Quintet galaxy hosting a Seyfert 2 AGN and radio jet, targeted with NIRSpec to illustrate AGN growth and interactions.

quotes.quote: Stephan's Quintet is a well-known Hickson Compact Group (HCG), consisting of at least 4 individual large galaxies at an average distance of 88.6 Mpc DuartePuertas19 , which are likely actively merging. One of the merging galaxies, NGC7319, harbors a bright Seyfert 2 active galaxtic nucleus associated with a radio jet Aoki99 . A fifth galaxy, NGC7320, is not interacting with the group, as it is much closer Burbidge61 , and is an apparent chance alignment (Fig.  fig:stephan_field ). In the mid-infrared, Stephan's Quintet is characterized by a large-scale shock at the interface between the NGC 7318 and NGC 7319 galaxies, with strong, extended emission from rotational H _2 lines Cluver10 , matching similarly strong X-ray emission O'Sullivan09 . The objective of the ERO was to illustrate the energetic interactions in the compact group, and show the active growth of an AGN in NGC 7319, all within the context of a field of distant background galaxies.

We obtained a single NIRSpec IFU tile centered on the Seyfert 2 AGN in NGC7319. We use the PRISM for maximal sensitivity and instantaneous spectral range (0.7-5.2 m). It is noteworthy that the redshift of NGC7319, albeit relatively small, allows the rest-frame optical H line to be included in the NIRSpec coverage.

sources.summary: The paper uses JWST MIRI spectroscopy to study NGC 7319, finding that molecular gas and dust lanes decelerate its low-power radio jet and shape ionized and molecular outflows.

quotes.quote: We present JWST MIRI MRS spectroscopy of NGC 7319, the largest galaxy in the Stephan's Quintet, observed as part of the Early Release Observations (ERO). NGC 7319 hosts a type 2 active galactic nucleus (AGN) and a low-power radio jet ( L_ 1.4 GHz =3.3 10 ^ 22 W Hz ^ 1 ) with two asymmetric radio hotspots at 430 pc (N2) and 1.5 kpc (S2) projected distances from the unresolved radio core.

NGC 7319 is the largest galaxy in the Stephan's Quintet interacting group (Arp 319, HCG 092; d=98 Mpc). NGC 7319 lost most of its atomic neutral gas during the interactions Williams2002 , but some atomic ionized and cold molecular gas is still present in its nuclear region RodriguezBaras14, Gao2000 . This galaxy hosts a type 2 active galactic nucleus (AGN), which dominates the energy output of this object since no strong nuclear starburst signatures are found Sulentic2001 .

We have analyzed the low-power ( L_ 1.4 GHz =3.3 10 ^ 22 W Hz ^ 1 ) jet--ISM interaction in the Seyfert 2 galaxy NGC 7319 using MIRI MRS data. We find evidence suggesting that molecular gas in dust lanes decelerates the jet at the northern hotspot, N2, which is three times closer to the nucleus than the southern hotspot, S2.

sources.summary: The paper models NGC 7319 as part of a closely interacting, star-forming system and concludes all torus models require line-of-sight N_H variability despite model differences.

quotes.quote: NGC 7319 Data reduction/fitting: We used an annulus of radii 10 20 to extract the background, in order to avoid a nearby source. Similarly, we used a circular source extraction region of only 15 for , to avoid both extreme soft excesses and CCD lines present around the source. No source was detected in Obs. ID 0021140401, and therefore it is not used in this analysis. A double- apec model was used to fit the soft emission of this galaxy, since it is part of a closely-interacting system, which is known to increase star forming activity.

Analysis of results: The data is well-fitted by all models. However, yields significantly different values for N_ H,los for the observations. Similarly to the case of 3C 445, it models the observed flux by using both lower N_ H,los and lower intrinsic flux values. This scenario is more similar to the best-fit Zhao2020 found for the source using . They detected no significant N_ H,los variability between and , while needing a much lower intrinsic flux for the observation. We recovered this solution with and , with worse statistics. Interestingly, the Zhao2020 / solution is statistically the best when not accounting for the soft X-ray emission (for and ). However, this solution always has N_ H,av at the maximum value allowed by the models. Despite the mentioned differences, all models agree that N_ H,los variability is required to explain the data, although this effect is larger for and . We thus classify this source as ` N_ H,los variable'.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 7319 as the Seyfert member whose tidal debris and molecular gas participate in Stephan's Quintet's shock, bridge, and stripped-gas structures.

quotes.quote: This IGM material is likely to be gas that has been tidally stripped from NGC 7319 by a past interaction with another galaxy, NGC 7320c see for a plausible dynamical scenario Moles1997, Sulentic2001 . These ideas have been further explored recently with numerical simulations. Both N-body Renaud2010 and hydrodynamical models Hwang2011 of the Quintet suggest a sequence of serial collisions between various group members culminating in the current high-speed shock-heating of gas as NGC 7318b strikes the pre-existing tidal debris from NGC 7319.

Before the discovery of warm H_2 in the IGM of the SQ group, previous observations were mostly concentrated on the Seyfert galaxy NGC 7319, on the southern tidal features, or on the northern starburst region (SQ-A). The first millimetre CO observations in SQ detected cold molecular gas in NGC 7319 Yun1997, Verdes-Montenegro1998, Leon1998 , which contains 4.8 10^9 M _ Smith2001a . Using the BIMA Berkeley Illinois Maryland Association, http://bima.astro.umd.edu/bima.html interferometer, Gao2000, Petitpas2005 showed that 3/4 of the CO gas in NGC 7319 is lying outside its nucleus, mostly in an extended northern region (see green contours on Figure fig:CO_beams_on_HST ).

The gas associated with the intruder NGC 7318b ( 5700-6000 km s ^ 1 ) and with the intra-group medium ( 6700 km s ^ 1 ) are both detected in the ridge and in the eastern feature (the bridge) towards NGC 7319.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 7319 as SQ's Seyfert 2 AGN galaxy, with comparable MIR/FIR output, interaction-triggered AGN activity, and FIR dust morphology that does not follow cold gas.

quotes.quote: This is consistent with a picture where MIR emission is produced by regions very close to young stars whilst FIR emission and PAHs emission come from dust illuminated by dilute radiation fields, as observed in galaxy disks (e.g. bendo08 ) AGN galaxy NGC 7319 The observed SED of the Seyfert 2 galaxy NGC 7319 differs markedly from the SEDs we obtained for the other sources in SQ, for which the power emitted at 24 m is typically a factor ten or more smaller than that observed at 70 and 160 m . For NGC 7319 the amount of energy emitted at MIR wavelengths is comparable to the FIR luminosity.
In the case of NGC 7319, the interactions have apparently also induced gas flows that have triggered AGN activity.

6) We have found that the morphology of the FIR dust emission in the Seyfert 2 galaxy NGC 7319 does not correspond to the cold gas distribution. Therefore, the FIR emission in this galaxy does not seem to be related to star formation.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 7319 as a Stephan's Quintet spiral type 2 Seyfert with radio jet structure, hard X-ray point source, soft extended emission, and nuclear-power variability.

quotes.quote: NGC 7319 NGC 7319 is a spiral galaxy located in the Stephan's Quintet, a group composed by six galaxies including a core of three galaxies trinchieri2003 . These three galaxies were also observed at radio wavelenghts with VLA aoki1999 and later with MERLIN xanthopoulos2004 , revealing a jet structure in NGC 7319. It has been optically confirmed as a type 2 Seyfert see an optical spectrum in Appendix multiimages rodriguezbaras2014 . The nucleus of this galaxy is not detected at UV frequencies with the OM (see Table obsSey ). At X-rays, a point-like source is observed in the 4.5--8.0 keV energy band, and it shows extended emission at soft X-ray energies (Appendix multiimages ).

sources.summary: The paper links NGC 7319 to an eastern bridge of warm molecular gas, CO clumps from its Seyfert nucleus, and possible AGN-driven molecular outflow or heating.

quotes.quote: These data (which will be discussed in much more detail in a companion paper; Guillard et al. in preparation) imply a coherent string of multi-phase gas emission extending from the Seyfert nucleus of NGC 7319 into the intra-group medium. This raises the possibility that there is an atomic and molecular outflow associated with the nucleus of NGC 7319.

Our analysis shows that the H _2 gas continues to be warm and excited far to the east of the main filament, in the region of the bridge that connects to NGC 7319. Some of this may be related to a known collimated radio jet. Our [ C 2 ] observations show an extension of diffuse gas towards the nucleus of this galaxy associated with a kinematically shifting component, but the observations do not extend far enough to conclusively associate this with an outflow.

sources.summary: The paper links NGC7319 to SQ interaction shocks, identifies it as a core spiral member, and finds an H bridge between the shock and its AGN nucleus.

quotes.quote: SQ is at an advanced state of interaction, evidenced by the existence of, for example, at least young and old tidal tails with ages of 150-200 Myr and 400-500 Myr, respectively 2011AJ....142...42F ; a large-scale shock region LSSR, e.g. 1972Natur.239..324A,1998ApJ...492L..25O,2012A&A...539A.127I produced by an ongoing interaction between NGC7318B and both NGC7319 and debris material from previous interactions; a diffuse gas halo close to NGC7317 composed by old stars that indicates that the formation of SQ took place several Gyr ago 2018MNRAS.475L..40D .

The core of SQ (i.e. NGC7317, NGC7318A, and NGC7319) and NGC7320c show a mild radial velocity dispersion, whereas NGC7318B has an unusually high radial velocity dispersion compared to the others ( V 1000 km s^ 1 ), indicating that it is not bound to the SQ 2005A&A...444..697T .

We found five radial velocity components in SQ: i) v= [ 5600-5900 ] km s^ 1 , associated with NI and SDR; ii) v= [ 5900-6100 ] km s^ 1 , associated with the NSQA and SSQA and the strands that are connected to these zones; iii) v= [ 6100-6600 ] km s^ 1 , associated with the strands located in the shock; iv) v= [ 6600-6800 ] km s^ 1 , associated with YTT, SQA, NGC7319, and the north lobe; and v) v= [ 6800-7000 ] km s^ 1 , associated with the strands that connect the shock with SQA. When considering a contrast (H ) 2 and binning 6x6, we do not detect gas in the old tail, in the vicinity of NGC7318A nor in NGC7317. We also do not have evidence that there is any connection between i) the north lobe in NGC7319 and the left side of SQA; or ii) the shock and the right part of NI nor SDR. Conversely, we find a link between the shock and the NGC7319 AGN nucleus (H bridge).

sources.summary: The paper places NGC 7319 among the main group galaxies and links its tidal tail, nucleus, northern disk, and bridge to observed ionized gas in Stephan's Quintet.

quotes.quote: When excluding the large foreground dwarf galaxy NGC 7320, the main group is dominated by four large massive galaxies. Three of them, NGC 7317, NGC 7318a and NGC 7319, have heliocentric radial velocities in the range V _ helio = 6599-6747 , and together they define the main barycentric velocity of the group (at around 6600 ). A fourth galaxy, NGC 7318b (V _ helio = 5774 , is often described as an intruder galaxy because it appears to be colliding into NGC 7319's tidal tail in the intra-group medium at a relative velocity of 1000 see Fig fig:SQ_contours_COS_on_WFC3Ha and Xu2003 . The collision of NGC 7318b with the gas in the intra-group medium is believed to be responsible for a striking feature of the group, namely a galaxy-wide shock ( 15 35 kpc ^ 2 ; Fig. fig:SQ_contours_COS_on_WFC3Ha ) seen at many wavelengths.

For context, the radial velocity of the intruder galaxy, NGC 7318b is V _ sys = 5774 km s ^ 1 , and it is thought to be entering the group from behind with a blue-shifted discrepant velocity of almost 1000 \ relative to the rest of the group members and the group-wide gas Xu2003,Hwang2012 . A component of the ionized gas (Fig. fig:VIRUS-P a and b) follows the spiral arm and \ regions seen in NGC 7318b, as expected if some of the gas was part of that galaxy. Gas is also seen from the nucleus and northern disk of NGC 7319, (Fig. fig:VIRUS-P , d and e), but with significant emission from the main North-South shocked filament. Gas at intermediate velocities (Fig. fig:VIRUS-P and d) is spread along the filament, but also in the bridge between NGC 7318b and NGC 7319. The bridge is fragmentary in nature at H , and occupies as narrower range of velocities compared with the main filament.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 7319 as the Seyfert member of Stephan’s Quintet whose tidal tail is struck by NGC 7318b, producing shock emission and adjacent H2, UV, and mid-IR structures.

quotes.quote: A remarkable feature of SQ is that a giant ( 15 35 kpc) shock is created by an intruding galaxy, NGC 7318b (Sbc pec), colliding into NGC 7319 's tidal tail, at a relative velocity of 1 000 km s ^ 1 . Evidence for a group-wide shock comes from observations of an extended X-ray ridge containing shock-heated ( 5 10^6 K) gas Pietsch1997, Trinchieri2003, Trinchieri2005, O'Sullivan2009 , strong radio synchrotron emission from the radio emitting plasma Allen1972, Sulentic2001, Williams2002, Xu2003 and shocked-gas excitation diagnostics from optical emission lines Xu2003 .
The H _2 emission is extended not only over the whole ridge, but in several other structures, including an extension towards the Seyfert galaxy NGC 7319, and the intergalactic starburst SQ-A Xu1999 .

If one excludes the foreground galaxy NGC 7320 (Sd), most of the UV emission is associated with the two spiral members of the group, NGC 7319 and NGC 7318b, and the intragroup medium starburst SQ-A. The galaxy-wide shock structure, which shows up in H , radio and X-ray observations, is barely visible on the UV images. Based on the comparison between ISO and GALEX data, Xu2005 concluded that most of the UV emission in the ridge is not associated with the large-scale shock itself, but with H ii regions along the spiral arm of the intruder NGC 7318b.

sources.summary: The paper links one shocked ionized component and high-velocity H I structures to gas formerly in NGC 7319, a luminous spiral AGN host stripped during interactions.

quotes.quote: The recent hydrodynamical simulations of Hwang et al. (2011) suggest that the gas dynamically associated to our two shocked ionized components comes mostly from NGC 7318b (although a small fraction also from NGC 7319) in the case of component A, and from NGC 7319 in the case of component B.

NGC 7319 is a high luminosity M_ B = -21.68 according to LEDA. spiral galaxy that hosts an AGN in its nucleus. The fact that no H i was detected in the disk of this galaxy (Williams et al. 2002) supports the idea that this gas was removed from the disk in one of the interactions experienced by this galaxy and can now be found in the form of the neutral filaments seen around the main bodies of the galaxies.

This shocked ionized component is proposed to be the link between the NW-HV and Arc-S and Arc-N H i structures reported by Williams et al. (2002), and its oxygen abundance is consistent with being formed out of gas formerly in the disk of NGC 7319.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 7319 as gas-deficient after interactions, with a bright radio core and a Faraday-depth peak tied to the Quintet magnetic structure.

quotes.quote: The gas-deficient galaxy NGC 7319 is located eastward of the shock. Its gaseous content was nearly entirely swept away during the previous interactions, forming the tidal tails moles98 .
The highest peak flux density is attributed to the core of NGC 7319, and is equal to 27 2 mJy/beam.

Throughout the Quintet area, the position of the second peak remains quite stationary: it reaches +230 \ at the position of NGC 7319, +250 \ at the position of SQ-R, +260 \ at the position of NGC 7318A and the highest value of +275 \ is found in the shock area.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 7319 as a higher-luminosity Seyfert 2 comparison at larger distance, with similar black-hole mass but high-ionization lines lacking the broad widths or strong blueshifts seen in the LLAGN.

quotes.quote: We also compare these data to public JWST data of a higher-luminosity AGN, NGC 7319.
The high ionization potential lines in NGC 7319 show neither broad widths or significant blue shifts.

In this paper, we contrast these two LLAGN with the higher luminosity and Eddington ratio Seyfert 2 AGN in NGC 7319 part of the JWST Early Release Observations; Ponto2022 , which is also at a much larger distance. All three galaxies have similar BH masses, thus the primary differences between these AGN are that they have Eddington ratios spanning 4 dex.

sources.summary: The paper places NGC 7319 in Stephan's Quintet's interaction history, saying a collision stripped much of its gas and produced tidal arms with star-forming knots extending eastward.

quotes.quote: They suggest that a few times 10 ^8 yr ago the group experienced a collision with NGC 7320c, a galaxy 4 arcmin to the east of NGC 7319 but with a very similar recession velocity 6583 , Sulentic01 to the other galaxies in SQ. This collision removed most of the gas of NGC 7319 towards the west and east, and produced the eastern tidal tail which connects to NGC 7319.

The first collision created the very faint tidal arm east of the interloper NGC 7320, whereas the second interaction produced the tidal arm which stretches from NGC 7319 eastwards. This violent dynamical history has induced star formation at various places outside the individual galaxies. ISOCAM mid-infrared and H observations have revealed a starburst region object A in hereafter called SQ A Xu99 at the intersection of two faint optical arms stemming north from NGC 7318a/b. Several knots of star formation are visible in the tidal arm extending from NGC 7319 to the east. At this position, identified as B by Xu99 and hereafter called SQ B, there is also mid-infrared and H emission, although much weaker than in SQ A.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 7319 as the progenitor of the optical tidal tail; matching gas components and solar metallicity indicate the molecular and H I gas came from its inner regions.

quotes.quote: We have mapped with the IRAM interferometer at Plateau de Bure (PdBI) the ^ 12 CO emission towards intergalactic star forming regions located in the tidal tail stemming from NGC 7319, in the Stephan's Quintet compact group of galaxies.

The optical tidal tail stems from NGC 7319, which points at this galaxy as its progenitor. Since the gas and the optical tail are related, their origin is expected to be the same which suggests that the gas also comes from NGC 7319. The solar metallicity observed in SQ B furthermore indicates that the gas comes from relatively inner regions of this galaxy since an enrichment by recent SF is not sufficient to explain these values see Braine01 .

sources.summary: The paper treats 7319 as a Stephan's Quintet member with a young tidal tail, an AGN bridge to 7318B, and nuclear Seyfert-like emission-line shoulders indicating outflowing material.

quotes.quote: Many results have also been drawn from spectroscopic studies of the Quintet. trancho12 targeted compact sources in the shock, the star-forming bow and debris region south of 7318A, as well as the young tidal tail extending to the east of 7319.

The spatial coverage is densest in the shock region, however, we sample the rest of the system quite well, with targets in the southern debris region (SDR), SQ-A xu99 , and the ``AGN bridge'' between the intruder, 7318B, and 7319 a broad-line excitation molecular region connecting the two galaxies in projection; cluver10 .

The other two galaxies covered show no surprising traits: the Seyfert signature is evident in the bright lines emerging from the nuclear region of 7319 (also shown on Figure fig:7318a ), along with blue and red `shoulders' on the nebular lines indicative of out-flowing material as noted by aoki98 .

sources.summary: The paper identifies HCG 92C, also called NGC 7319, as a strong late-type AGN responsible for luminosity excess and notes Fabry-Perot evidence for prominent tidal tails.

quotes.quote: The two known strong \ AGNs HCG 16B osullivan2014a,osullivan2014b and HCG 92C (NGC 7319). The former is a single nuclear \ point source. As shown in T14 the CG environment is very rarely host to unambiguously strong nuclear AGN emission; for this reason we have not attempted to exclude nuclear point-source emission in this work. These are the only late types in our sample that are strong AGNs, clearly the primary cause of the observed excess. HCG 16B is also strongly interacting with its neighbor 16A (classification LINER/weak AGN in T14), to which it is linked by a bridge of diffuse ( \ and optical) emission jeltema2008 . The Fabry-Perot analysis of M98 detects a highly disturbed velocity field in HCG 16B, kinematic warping and G/SMM. Similar analysis for 92C (NGC 7319) also detects prominent tidal tails hereafter TF09 torres-flores2009 .

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 7319 as a type 2 AGN with weak nuclear PAH emission, likely suppressed short-wavelength PAH bands, and clear 11.3 micron detection.

quotes.quote: NGC 7319 harbours a type 2 AGN with no evidence of starburst (SB) activity Sulentic01 .

In the case of NGC 7319 (see Fig. nuclear_fit2 ), there is either no nuclear star formation activity or the 6.2 and 7.7 m PAH features have been destroyed in the inner 200 pc (alternatively, these features could be diluted by the strong AGN continuum; e.g.

In the case of NGC 7319, only the 11.3 m PAH feature is clearly detected in the nuclear region, and its continuum-subtracted residuals show tentative detection of the 12.7 m PAH band (see Fig. nuclear_residuals ).

gmw_00001272.name: NGC7317
gmw_00001272.overview: NGC 7317 is a mildly rotating elliptical galaxy [arXiv:2109.13757] in Stephan's Quintet [arXiv:1206.1234] that lacks gas emission [arXiv:1405.0880, arXiv:1909.05797] and polarized radio emission [arXiv:2008.01881]. It is surrounded by an off-centered, diffuse stellar halo of old stars [arXiv:1712.07145] and is connected to the group center by CO emission [arXiv:2109.13757].

sources.summary: The paper reports no significant gas emission from NGC 7317 itself, but identifies a nearby concordant-redshift emission-line region likely tied to tidal interaction in Stephan's Quintet.

quotes.quote: The elliptical galaxy NGC 7317 shows no significant gas emission by itself. Nevertheless, we do detect a peculiar emission-line source within this pointing: a region located at the southwest of NGC 7317 that can also be observed in the HST image (see Fig. pointings ), and at first sight may be confused as a background galaxy. An analysis of the spectra of this region reveals that its redshift is concordant with that of NGC 7317, therefore this emitting region is located close to the elliptical galaxy. The maximum visible length of the region is of 8 arcsec, which, adopting a distance of 93.3 Mpc to NGC 7317 (taken from NED and confirmed spectroscopically), corresponds to a physical length of 0.35 Mpc.

NGC 7317 showed no significant gas emission by itself, and we could not obtain maps or diagnostic diagrams for this pointing. Nevertheless, an emitting region was detected southwest to the galaxy. Its integrated spectrum was extracted, and we estimated its metallicity and the ionizing star cluster mass. We postulate that this cluster might be the result of a tidal interaction with the rest of the members in the SQ group.

sources.summary: The paper newly detects NGC 7317 in total power, finds no polarized emission, and questions whether its bridge-like connection is real or envelope contamination.

quotes.quote: NGC 7317 The elliptical NGC 7317 was previously xu03, osullivan09, bnw13b believed to be a radio quiet galaxy. The high sensitivity of the new WSRT data allows us to detect it in our total power map (Fig. tp_full ). There are no traces of polarised emission. As early-type galaxies are considered to be lacking mechanisms that could effectively order the magnetic field beckrev , this is not an unexpected result. The real character of the bridge that connects NGC 7317 to the common envelope is uncertain.

Whereas this galaxy is believed to be a part of the original triplet of galaxies that formed what is today called the Stephan's Quintet moles98 , the radio contours are relatively concave on the northern side of the connection area. Keeping in mind the large beam size (comparable to the observed structure) another explanation seems to be more plausible: that this galaxy is detached from the other ones, and the richness of radio emission on its east side originates from an extension of the radio envelope in this direction.

sources.summary: The paper identifies a diffuse, reddish, off-centered stellar halo around NGC 7317 and argues it indicates an early interaction and an older formation history for Stephan's Quintet.

quotes.quote: An extended diffuse, reddish, lopsided, halo is detected towards the early-type galaxy NGC 7317, the role of which had so far been ignored in models. The presence of this halo made of old stars may indicate that the group formed earlier than previously thought.

The diffuse halo SQ appears to be embedded in a diffuse reddish halo, which is especially prominent towards the South-West, near NGC 7317. This early-type galaxy is off-centred with respect to the outer isophotes of the halo. The diffuse emission extends up to 60 kpc from the galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC7317 as connected to Stephan’s Quintet by CO emission, likely affected by past harassment, and showing unusual mildly rotating elliptical-galaxy kinematics.

quotes.quote: the ^ 12 CO (2-1) emission shows a prevalence to the star-forming ridge, an area south of the NGC7318 pair, and shows an extension towards NGC7317 -- connecting NGC7317 to the centre of the group, indicating a previous interaction. NGC7317 may also be a prime candidate for studies of the process of galaxy harassment.

The optical spectrum of NGC7317 (Fig. fig:7317_spec ) reveals a quiescent galaxy with a line-of-sight velocity and velocity dispersion in the central 1.5 '' of 6734 5 km/s and 262.7 6.9 km/s, respectively. Having observed NGC7317 at one slit position allows plotting the line-of-sight velocity and velocity dispersion as a function of the offset from the galaxy centre along the slit. These velocity-position plots are presented in Figs. fig:7317_vlos and fig:7317_sig , and they reveal an usual elliptical galaxy, mildly rotating and potentially still in morphological transition. These peculiarities may be due to the effects of galaxy harassment from when the galaxy passed through SQ in the past. NGC7317 may be an optimal candidate for studying the effect of galaxy harassment.

sources.summary: The paper notes NGC 7317 is an early-type member often neglected in simulations, surrounded by diffuse halo emission, and possibly linked to NGC 7319 through an earlier collision.

quotes.quote: In both models, the role played by NGC 7317, an early-type galaxy, is neglected because of a lack of observed connection to any other SQ members. Recently a reddish, low surface brightness stellar halo surrounding SQ was discovered in deep optical imaging observations Duc2018 , which corresponds spatially very well to the diffuse X-ray halo Trinchieri2005 , in particular around NGC 7317.

Anon 4 and 6000 component One of the possible scenarios proposed by Xu2022 for the formation of the large diffuse feature in the 6600 component (Figure fivecontour ) is that, like the large Leo Ring Michel-Dansac2010 , the diffuse feature could be the product of a high-speed head-on collision between an unknown old intruder and one of the core members of SQ (i.e. NGC 7319, NGC 7318a, and NGC 7317). In this scenario, the collision triggers an expanding density wave that pushes gas in an extended HI disk of the target galaxy outwards to form a very large ring, of which the diffuse feature is the high-density part.
Alternatively, NGC 7319 could have had been involved in a collision with the elliptical galaxy NGC7317 which seems to be linked with NGC 7319 with a diffuse optical halo Moles1997, Duc2018 .

sources.summary: The paper lists NGC 7317 as a physical Stephan Quintet core member with similar recessional velocity, while noting its weak present influence and exclusion from simulations.

quotes.quote: SQ consists of five galaxies (NGC 7319, NGC 7318a, NGC 7318b, NGC 7317, and NGC 7320, cf. also Fig. morph ) with an estimated distance to earth of 94 Mpc MoMa98,ApXu06 . SQ is famous for a physical adjacency between four of the galaxies (NGC 7319, 7318a, 7318b, and 7317), whereby strong interactions between three of these members are apparently causing tidal tails, a strong group-wide shock visible in X-ray PiTr97,SuRo01 and radio emission AlHa72,XuLu03 , and a region of active star formation northern of the colliding galaxies Xu05 . Three of the four physically related galaxies, the main galaxy NGC 7319 as well as NGC 7317 and NGC 7318a, have a similar recessional velocity of 6640-6670 km s ^ 1 FeGa11 and represent the core of the compact group (.f. Fig. morph ). Furthermore, NGC 7317 does not show any visible features of an ongoing or past interaction.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 7317 as an elliptical in Stephan's Quintet whose star cluster population is old, centrally concentrated, and likely dominated by globular clusters.

quotes.quote: NGC 7317 NGC 7317 is an elliptical galaxy and, as expected for a galaxy of that type, hosts mostly old star clusters. The distribution of these clusters in color space is concentrated near \ \ = 1.1 mag and \ \ = 0.8 mag (Figure fig:N7317_SDR ). The minimal reddening that would be expected in NGC 7317, as well as the spatial distribution of the clusters, which are concentrated around the core of the galaxy, suggest that a large number of them can be considered globular clusters.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC7317 as an elliptical SQ member, links a nearby old-star halo to early group formation, and reports no ionized gas in its vicinity.

quotes.quote: SQ is composed of elliptical (NGC7317 and NGC7318A) and spiral galaxies (NGC7319, NGC7320c, and NGC7318B).

SQ is at an advanced state of interaction, evidenced by the existence of, for example, at least young and old tidal tails with ages of 150-200 Myr and 400-500 Myr, respectively 2011AJ....142...42F ; a large-scale shock region LSSR, e.g. 1972Natur.239..324A,1998ApJ...492L..25O,2012A&A...539A.127I produced by an ongoing interaction between NGC7318B and both NGC7319 and debris material from previous interactions; a diffuse gas halo close to NGC7317 composed by old stars that indicates that the formation of SQ took place several Gyr ago 2018MNRAS.475L..40D .
When considering a contrast (H ) 2 and binning 6x6, we do not detect gas in the old tail, in the vicinity of NGC7318A nor in NGC7317.

gmw_00001273.name: NGC7318B
gmw_00001273.overview: NGC 7318B is a gas-rich spiral galaxy [arXiv:1104.4701, arXiv:1909.05797] currently colliding with the intragroup medium of Stephan's Quintet at a high velocity of approximately 1000 km/s [arXiv:0912.0282, arXiv:2102.06843]. This ongoing collision drives a large-scale shock wave [arXiv:1701.03226, arXiv:1206.1234] and triggers star formation along its disturbed spiral arms [arXiv:0904.4239, arXiv:1004.0677].

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 7318b as Stephan's Quintet's intruder galaxy, linking its high-speed collision with a tidal filament to shock heating, star-forming spiral arms, and continuum contamination.

quotes.quote: The significance of the molecular hydrogen lines over other measured sources of cooling in fast galaxy-scale shocks may have crucial implications for the cooling of gas in the assembly of the first galaxies. galaxies: evolution -- galaxies: individual (NGC 7318b; NGC7319) -- galaxies: interactions -- galaxies: intergalactic medium -- physical data and processes: shock waves Stephan's Quintet (hereafter SQ) is a strongly interacting compact group which has produced a highly disturbed intragroup medium (IGM) Xu03,Xu05 through a complex sequence of interactions and harrassment All80,Will02 . This interplay has produced a large-scale intergalactic shock-wave, first observed as a narrow filament in the radio continuum All72 , and subsequently detected in the X-ray Piet97,Trin05,Osul09 . The high-velocity ( 1000 km s ^ 1 ) collision of an intruder galaxy, NGC 7318b, with the intergalactic medium of the group Sul01,Xu03 is believed to be responsible for the shock-heating of the X-ray emitting gas.

NGC 7318b, the intruder galaxy, lies to the West of the shock, while the large Seyfert 2 galaxy Dur94 , lies to the East. Other members of the group are also indicated. The peculiar extranuclear star formation region, named SQ-A Xu99,Xu03 , lies at the extreme northern end of the main shock wave. NGC 7318a (west of NGC 7318b) is also a strongly interacting group member, with NGC 7317 further away from the core. t The unexpected discovery of extremely powerful, pure-rotational H _2 line emission from the center of the shock App06 , using the Spitzer Space Telescope Wer04 , has sparked intense interest in the SQ system.

Are these results consistent with the hypothesis that the shock is formed where the intruding galaxy NGC 7318b collides with a pre-existing tidal filament of drawn out of NGC 7319 in a previous interaction with another group member Mol97, Trin03 ? tb This basic mechanical picture appears plausible as can be seen in Figure fig:HI+H2 which shows that the distribution ``fills in'' the gap in the tidal tail as observed by the VLA Will02 . The implication is that the has been converted into both a hot X-ray component and a warm component by the collision of the intruder with the now missing H i . Part of the puzzle of how this high-speed ( V_ s 700-1000 km s ^ 1 ) shock can lead to both X-ray and very strong molecular line emission is presented in a model by Pg09 . The high-speed collision of NGC 7318b with the filament (assumed to be composed of a multiphase medium) leads to multiple shocks passing through and compressing denser clumps (which become dusty nucleation sites for formation) as opposed to the lower-density gas, which is shock-heated to X-ray tempertures.

sources.summary: The paper models NGC 7318b in Stephan's Quintet as a gas-rich spiral plunging hypersonically through H I gas, forming a large-scale bow shock with a calculated standoff distance of about 7.4 kpc.

quotes.quote: In the simplest scenario, as report, the shock results from the high-velocity collision of the gas-rich spiral galaxy NGC 7318b with previously stripped H i gas in SQ. For an upstream H i temperature of 100 K (and number density of about 6.5 10^ 3 cm ^ 3 ), the velocity of the intruding galaxy is highly hypersonic ( 1400 km s ^ 1 ), resulting in an upstream Mach number in the galaxy's rest frame of M_ 930 . The gas inside the bow shock is then heated up to an (observed) energy of 0.5 keV.

To model the scenario in SQ, where a large-scale bow shock wave is believed to be formed by the gas-rich spiral galaxy NGC 7318b as it plunges through 100 K H I gas Tri:03 , the upstream Mach number has chosen to be M_ =930 . We assumed a plane ( j=0 ) supersonic flow of perfect monoatomic gas ( =5/3 ) and parametrized the bow shock time-dependently (e.g. by ^2=1.81 z for t=130 Myr after the start of the interaction). Results are plotted in Figs. im:analyt and im:flowfield . The standoff distance of the shock from the nose of the projectile (in this case from the leading edge of the galaxy NGC 7318b of SQ) can be immediately determined to be _0 2.3 10^ 22 cm ( 7.4 kpc).

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 7318B as a high-speed intruder whose largely unperturbed spiral structure implies first infall and whose collision produced the X-ray shock front.

quotes.quote: The fourth galaxy, NGC 7318B, is a high-speed intruder (with v_ R = 5766 ; Table tab:tab1 ) that is apparently interacting with the core for the first time ( mendes94 ; moles97 ).

Thirdly, previous work ( moles97 ) has found that NGC 7318B is interacting with SQ for the first time. They draw this conclusion mainly from two observations: the v_ R of NGC 7318B with respect to SQ ( v 900 ) and its mostly unperturbed spiral structure, a sign that this galaxy has not experienced many interactions in its recent history. Thus, considering that galaxy interactions usually trigger star formation, without recent interactions with other galaxies, NGC 7318B will be expected to have a small number of SCCs younger than 2 Gyr above our luminosity limit, consistent with a galaxy with continuous star formation typical of an isolated spiral. The gap can therefore be used to age-date the interaction from the onset of enhanced star formation.

The last region, the X-Ray Shock Front, is defined as a contour of soft X-Ray emission ( 0.5 -- 2.0 keV) with the value of 0.3 count pix ^ 1 from the Chandra image in the eastern part of NGC 7318B. This shock wave is the result of a high-speed collision between NGC 7318B, which is blueshifted by 900 \ ( mendes94 ) with regards to the average radial group velocity, and the cold intergalactic medium seen in H \ ( shostak84 ).

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC7318B as the high-velocity intruder in Stephan’s Quintet, interacting with the IGM and NGC7318A and creating the shock-induced star-forming ridge.

quotes.quote: This structure is created by the collision of the IGM and the intruder galaxy NGC7318B, which is entering the group from behind at a relative line-of-sight velocity of 900 km/s xu2003 . The star formation near the shocked ridge towards NGC7318B, in the south-west (SW) tail and the north-west (NW) tail, is what is called the SF ridge.

The ionised gas in NGC7318A and B presents line-of-sight velocities of 6003 28 kms/s and 6142 26 km/s, respectively, giving an indication that the ionised gas of NGC7318B is either mixed with a higher velocity IGM or decoupled from the stellar disk due to the interaction. The CO gas shows congregations at 5815 16 km/s and 5800 25 km/s at the location of NGC7317A and B, respectively, as well as fainter emission at higher velocities. In region 18 i-iii we find a combination of gas at velocities matching both NGC7318A and B. The CO emission in the NW tail (marked in Fig. fig:structures ), region 18 iii, indicates that there is a mix of high temperature molecular gas from NGC7318B coexisting with lower temperature molecular gas from NGC7318A. In addition, there is a lack of gas in the area between the two galaxies. Considering the line-of-sight velocities in the bridge, the SF ridge, and the west ridge, we can extrapolate a picture of a galaxy, NGC7318B, entering the group from behind, slightly from the SW. NGC7318B collides with the IGM at high speed, creating the shock-induced SF ridge. As NGC7318B passes through the group, its ISM is pulled out and back towards NGC7319 and NGC7318A, forming arms and tails that connect the structures and contributing to the bridge and the gas deposit at the lower velocity in front of NGC7318A.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 7318b as the high-velocity new intruder whose collision drives Stephan's Quintet's large shock, associates it with the 5700 km/s H I component, and critiques simulations of its gas removal.

quotes.quote: According to Williams2002 , the 5700 component is associated with the ``new intruder'' NGC 7318b whose radial velocity is 5774 km s ^ 1 . Its HI mass measured by FAST is M_ HI = 2.43 0.24 10^9 M_ .

Long history of interactions in SQ Moles1997 proposed a comprehensive picture for the interaction history of SQ based on a `two-intruders' scenario also see Sulentic2001 : an old intruder (NGC 7320c) stripped most of the gas from group members, and a new intruder (NGC 7318b) is currently colliding with this gas and triggering the large scale shock. The `old intruder' passed the core of SQ twice, one in 5 7 10^8 yr ago and pulled out the outer tail (also known as `old tail'), another about 2 10^8 yr ago and triggered the inner tail (`young tail') .

However, it cannot reproduce the overall HI distribution in SQ, in particular it predicts that NGC 7319 and NGC 7318b still retain most of their gas, in contradiction with the observations. For NGC 7318b, the fiducial model of Hwang2012 assumes that it has a relative radial velocity with NGC 7318a of v 300 km s^ 1 , which is significantly lower than the observed value ( v 900 km s^ 1 ). This may cause an under-estimate of the strength of density waves triggered by the head-collision and result in less gas removal in NGC 7318b.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 7318b as SQ's high-velocity intruder, with UV-delineated elongated features and compact MIR/FIR star-forming regions on and north of its arms.

quotes.quote: One galaxy, NGC 7318b, is apparently unbound, entering the group at a high relative velocity of 1000 km/s and colliding with the group IGM, as evidenced by a 32 kpc north-south long ridge prominent in radio continuum ( Xu03 ), optical line emission ( Xu99 ), X-ray ( T05 ) and recently also powerful mid-infrared rotational hydrogen lines ( A06 ; Cluv10 ).

As can be seen from the MIR maps, dust emission from compact star formation regions are detected all over the group and especially on the elongated features of the intruder galaxy NGC 7318b which are most prominently delineated in the UV. Here the similarity is strongest between the UV and the 8 m band though the most prominent discrete sources are also clearly seen at 24 and 70 m . The most prominent such MIR/FIR source is SQ A, the star formation region located to the north of NGC 7318b, already detected by ISO, which can also be seen at 160 m . From optical spectra ( Xu03 ) and radio observations ( Lis02 , Wi02 ) it is known that star formation in this region is associated with gas at radial velocities corresponding to both the intruder galaxy and the IGM of the group. Several further compact star formation regions are located on the southern arms of NGC 7318b.

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 7318b as a high-speed intruder in Stephan Quintet whose collision with the IGM produces the observed shock and enhanced X-ray/radio emission.

quotes.quote: The fifth galaxy, NGC 7320, is observed to be a much closer foreground galaxy and is not part of the interacting group Sh74,AlSu80,MoSu97 . It has a recessional velocity of 740 km s ^ 1 FaKu99 . Three of the four physically related galaxies, the main galaxy NGC 7319 as well as NGC 7317 and NGC 7318a, have a similar recessional velocity of 6640-6670 km s ^ 1 FeGa11 and represent the core of the compact group (.f. Fig. morph ). NGC 7318b is observed to be a high-speed intruder ( 5770 km s ^ 1 ) and seems to interact with the IGM within the main system for the first time MoSu97,FeGa11 . Finally, there is a sixth galaxy, NGC 7320c, which shows a similar recessional velocity ( 5990 km s ^ 1 ) as the core of the compact group FeGa11 and is therefore suggested to interact with NGC 7319 MoSu97 , because it reveals connected tidal features in the eastern large tidal tail (cf. the outer tail in Fig. morph ). Therefore, it is most likely also part of the compact group Ar73 .

North of NGC 7318a and NGC 7318b there are two tidal arms extending from the galaxies, which belong to a region of active star formation GaCh01,Xu05 . A region of enhanced X-ray and radio emission is observed between NGC 7319 and NGC 7318b. This region is widely interpreted as a large shock front (cf. Fig. morph ) resulting from a collision of the high-speed intruder NGC 7318b with the cold IGM ShAl84 .

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 7318b as the high-velocity intruder in Stephan's Quintet whose collision with intragroup gas drives the large shock and whose spiral-arm gas appears in ionized maps.

quotes.quote: When excluding the large foreground dwarf galaxy NGC 7320, the main group is dominated by four large massive galaxies. Three of them, NGC 7317, NGC 7318a and NGC 7319, have heliocentric radial velocities in the range V _ helio = 6599-6747 , and together they define the main barycentric velocity of the group (at around 6600 ). A fourth galaxy, NGC 7318b (V _ helio = 5774 , is often described as an intruder galaxy because it appears to be colliding into NGC 7319's tidal tail in the intra-group medium at a relative velocity of 1000 see Fig fig:SQ_contours_COS_on_WFC3Ha and Xu2003 . The collision of NGC 7318b with the gas in the intra-group medium is believed to be responsible for a striking feature of the group, namely a galaxy-wide shock ( 15 35 kpc ^ 2 ; Fig. fig:SQ_contours_COS_on_WFC3Ha ) seen at many wavelengths.

For context, the radial velocity of the intruder galaxy, NGC 7318b is V _ sys = 5774 km s ^ 1 , and it is thought to be entering the group from behind with a blue-shifted discrepant velocity of almost 1000 \ relative to the rest of the group members and the group-wide gas Xu2003,Hwang2012 . A component of the ionized gas (Fig. fig:VIRUS-P a and b) follows the spiral arm and \ regions seen in NGC 7318b, as expected if some of the gas was part of that galaxy. Gas is also seen from the nucleus and northern disk of NGC 7319, (Fig. fig:VIRUS-P , d and e), but with significant emission from the main North-South shocked filament. Gas at intermediate velocities (Fig. fig:VIRUS-P and d) is spread along the filament, but also in the bridge between NGC 7318b and NGC 7319. The bridge is fragmentary in nature at H , and occupies as narrower range of velocities compared with the main filament.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 7318b as the current intruder in Stephan's Quintet, with a distinct recession velocity, gas being removed in the ongoing collision, and shocks linked to its motion.

quotes.quote: Presently, the group is experiencing another collision with the ``intruder'' galaxy NGC 7318b which strongly affects the interstellar medium (ISM) removed during the first collision. NGC 7318b has a recession velocity of 5765 , in contrast to the other members of the group which have velocities close to 6600 .

Sulentic01 suggest that the gas at 6700 \ was stripped during the earlier visit of NGC 7320c and part of it has become shock-ionized due to the present interaction with NGC 7318b. In this view, the emission at 6700 \ traces the shock front. Strong support for this shock hypothesis is the fact that X--ray (tracing hot gas) and radio continuum emission (tracing cosmic ray electrons produced in shocks) are coincident with the H emission. The gas at velocities around 6000 \ originates from NGC 7318b and is being removed from this galaxy in the ongoing collision.

Sulentic01 suggest that the apparent spatial coincidence is a projection and that NGC 7318b, and thus the shock caused by it, are moving along the line of sight. The presence of molecular gas indicates a rather cool environment. It is therefore surprising to find large amounts and extended CO emission at positions where shocks are present and are heating and ionizing the gas. J-shocks with velocities above about 50 \ are able to effectively destroy molecular gas Hollenbach80, Hollenbach89 . Shocks with these velocities are plausibly present given the large velocity difference (600 -- 1000 ) between the intruder NGC 7318b and the rest of the SQ galaxies.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 7318b as the high-velocity intruder in Stephan's Quintet, driving shocks into a tidal tail and showing disturbed spiral-arm gas and star formation.

quotes.quote: It is postulated that another galaxy, NGC 7318b (hereafter called the ``intruder''), is falling into the group with a very high relative velocity shocking a 25 kpc segment of that tidal tail 2003ApJ...595..665X . The observed difference in radial velocities between the tidal tail and the intruder is 1 000 km s ^ 1 . If this picture of two colliding gas flows is right, we expect two shocks: one propagating forward into the SQ intergalactic medium, and an other reverse shock driven into the intruding galaxy.
There are also large spatial variations of the extinction value for the intruder gas velocity close to this position, suggesting clumpiness of the gas within the disturbed spiral arm of NGC 7318b.
It provides an upper limit on the UV field in the shock because this area includes star forming regions in the spiral arm of NGC 7318b.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 7318b as the intruding galaxy in Stephan’s Quintet whose high-speed collision with NGC 7319’s tidal tail drives the large shock and associated UV/IR structures.

quotes.quote: A remarkable feature of SQ is that a giant ( 15 35 kpc) shock is created by an intruding galaxy, NGC 7318b (Sbc pec), colliding into NGC 7319 's tidal tail, at a relative velocity of 1 000 km s ^ 1 . Evidence for a group-wide shock comes from observations of an extended X-ray ridge containing shock-heated ( 5 10^6 K) gas Pietsch1997, Trinchieri2003, Trinchieri2005, O'Sullivan2009 , strong radio synchrotron emission from the radio emitting plasma Allen1972, Sulentic2001, Williams2002, Xu2003 and shocked-gas excitation diagnostics from optical emission lines Xu2003 .

If one excludes the foreground galaxy NGC 7320 (Sd), most of the UV emission is associated with the two spiral members of the group, NGC 7319 and NGC 7318b, and the intragroup medium starburst SQ-A. The galaxy-wide shock structure, which shows up in H , radio and X-ray observations, is barely visible on the UV images. Based on the comparison between ISO and GALEX data, Xu2005 concluded that most of the UV emission in the ridge is not associated with the large-scale shock itself, but with H ii regions along the spiral arm of the intruder NGC 7318b.

sources.summary: NGC 7318b is identified as the new intruder whose collision with Stephan's Quintet produced shocked ionized components, while some original ISM and H II regions survived.

quotes.quote: The two shocked components are interpreted as products of a collision between NGC 7318b new intruder and a debris field in its path. This has given rise to a complex structure of ionized gas where several components with different kinematical and physical properties coexist although part of the original ISM associated with NGC 7318b is still present and remains unaltered.

Spectra corresponding to 3 discrete emission features in pointings S and M: their recession velocities are consistent with that of NGC 7318b. The estimated oxygen abundances, 8.45 < 12 + O/H < 8.61 , are lower than the solar value and show a gradient along the spiral arm of NGC 7318b. We claim that these H ii regions survived the collision of NGC 7318b with SQ.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 7318B as a spiral galaxy entering Stephan's Quintet at high velocity, with young clusters tracing debris stripped into its spiral arm and tidal tail.

quotes.quote: NGC 7318A is an early type galaxy (found in a GMOS spectrum obtained by Konstantopoulos et al., in prep) while NGC 7318B is a spiral galaxy thought to be falling into SQ with a high velocity for the first time (Moles et al. 1997).

T119, T120, T121 and T123 are associated with the tidal tail/spiral arm emanating from NGC 7318B, while T111a-d and T112 all lie to the south of NGC 7318A, with its associated debris. All clusters in this region are young ( <20 Myr) and the clusters associated with the tidal tail of NGC 7318B follow a clear velocity gradient, as expected from numerical simulations (Renaud et al. 2010) and observed gas distributions (e.g., Williams et al.

Clusters/complexes T111a-d and T112 do not have similar velocities to NGC 7318A, but instead are consistent with NGC 7318B. This implies that they formed from debris stripped from this spiral galaxy, and form a continuation of the spiral arm and tidal tail originating from NGC 7318B (as also supported by Renaud et al. 2010).

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC7318B as the gas-rich new intruder, with unusually high radial velocity dispersion and interactions driving SQ shock and debris structures.

quotes.quote: NGC7318B is a gas-rich galaxy that has experienced several interactions throughout its evolution e.g. 1997ApJ...485L..69M,2001AJ....122.2993S .

All the galaxies of SQ, except NGC7318B, have evolved dynamically, losing their interstellar medium (ISM). proposed an evolutionary model for SQ based on two intruders, an old intruder (OI, NGC7320c) and a new intruder (NI, NGC7318B).

SQ is at an advanced state of interaction, evidenced by the existence of, for example, at least young and old tidal tails with ages of 150-200 Myr and 400-500 Myr, respectively 2011AJ....142...42F ; a large-scale shock region LSSR, e.g. 1972Natur.239..324A,1998ApJ...492L..25O,2012A&A...539A.127I produced by an ongoing interaction between NGC7318B and both NGC7319 and debris material from previous interactions; a diffuse gas halo close to NGC7317 composed by old stars that indicates that the formation of SQ took place several Gyr ago 2018MNRAS.475L..40D . From the kinematical point of view, SQ covers a large radial velocity range ( 5500 -- 6600 km s^ 1 ). The core of SQ (i.e. NGC7317, NGC7318A, and NGC7319) and NGC7320c show a mild radial velocity dispersion, whereas NGC7318B has an unusually high radial velocity dispersion compared to the others ( V 1000 km s^ 1 ), indicating that it is not bound to the SQ 2005A&A...444..697T .

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 7318b as the intruding Stephan's Quintet galaxy whose high-speed collision with NGC 7319 tidal debris drives the large shock and sets one gas velocity system.

quotes.quote: The most striking feature of the group is that a galaxy-scale ( 15 35 kpc ^ 2 ) shock is created by an intruding galaxy, NGC 7318b which is colliding into the intra-group gas at a relative velocity of 1 000 km s ^ 1 (see the background image in Figure fig:CO_beams_on_HST ).

Both N-body Renaud2010 and hydrodynamical models Hwang2011 of the Quintet suggest a sequence of serial collisions between various group members culminating in the current high-speed shock-heating of gas as NGC 7318b strikes the pre-existing tidal debris from NGC 7319.

These velocities match the redshifts of the two H i gas systems found in the same region Sulentic2001, Williams2002 , which correspond to the intruder galaxy, NGC 7318b, and NGC 7319's tidal tail in the IGM, respectively.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 7318b as the intruder galaxy whose collision with SQ intragroup gas heats the shock region, while star-forming regions on its southeast and southwest periphery show standard and enhanced SFRs.

quotes.quote: On the MIR and FIR maps of SQ, shown in Fig. fig1 together with optical, UV, 21 cm line and soft-X-ray data, several types of sources are detected: 1) bright compact SF regions located either in the intergalactic medium (SQ-A, SQ-B) or at the periphery of the intruder galaxy NGC 7318b (HII-SE, HII-SW); 2) dust emission from the region of the shock, where gas has been heated by the collision between NGC 7318b and SQ IGM; 3) an ``extended'' FIR emission component roughly coincident with the X-ray halo.

The source HII-SE, a SF region located to the south-east of the intruder galaxy NGC 7318b, presents also a standard SFR. This differs from what we found in NA10, because of the inclusion of new CO data from GU12. Instead, the source at the south-west of NGC 7318b, HII-SW, seems to be located far away from the area defined by the KS relations for local galaxies, presenting a much more efficient SFR.

sources.summary: The paper describes 7318B as the intruder galaxy driving Stephan's Quintet's high-speed shock, with star-forming regions tied to the intruder but no shocked regions inside 7318B.

quotes.quote: Their redshift distribution reveals interesting three-dimensional structure with respect to gas-phase baryons, with no H \ regions associated with shocked gas, no shocked regions in the intruder galaxy 7318B, and a sharp boundary between shocks and star formation.

One of the strongest shock regions in the local universe can be found in the site of the high-speed collision between 7318B and the intra-group medium (IGM) of Stephan's Quintet (to which we will sometimes refer as ``the Quintet''; see Figure fig:finder ), a nearby compact galaxy group (CG; u_R=6600 , d=94 Mpc if H_0=70 Mpc ^ 1 ).

This low star formation efficiency (SFE) has also been deduced with UV, optical and IR imaging, with xu05 reporting a star formation rate SFR =6.7 yr ^ 1 , of which 30 can be attributed to the bright starburst region known as SQ-A (or the northern starburst region, NSBR), which is thought to have resulted from an interaction between the intruder and 7318B, taking place before the collision that shocked the IGM renaud10, hwang12 .

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 7318b as the intruder galaxy whose collision drives shocks, turbulent energy transfer, and large-scale warm H2 excitation in Stephan's Quintet.

quotes.quote: NGC 7318b is colliding with intergroup gas at about 1000 km s ^ 1 and Guillard et al. (2009) proposed that, through a turbulent cascade, energy is being transferred to smaller scales and low velocities where a significant fraction of the energy escapes through rotational H _2 and far-IR fine structure emission (Appleton et al. 2013).
The mapped region extends over an area of at least 35 x 55 kpc ^2 , and includes the area dominated by shocked gas ahead of the intruder galaxy NGC 7318b.

The distribution of the lowest power-law values defines a curved band lying to the East and ahead of the intruder galaxy NGC 7318b, as expected if the galaxy is driving a large-scale disturbance into the group-wide gas.

gmw_00001274.name: NGC7318A
gmw_00001274.overview: NGC 7318A is a quiescent elliptical galaxy [arXiv:2109.13757, arXiv:1909.05797] that forms a core member of the Stephan's Quintet compact group [arXiv:1206.1234, arXiv:1909.05797]. It is strongly interacting with its neighbor NGC 7318B [arXiv:1201.5149, arXiv:2109.13757] and likely hosts a low-luminosity active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1701.03226].

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 7318A/B as the interacting pair driving Stephan's Quintet's shock front, finding shocked gas, multiple kinematic components, and low-density 200-400 km/s shock models.

quotes.quote: The PPAK pointing corresponding to NGC 7318 includes two objects: NGC 7318A which belongs to the group core, and NGC 7318B which is entering the group for the first time and colliding with it. The shock front generated by this collision is one of the most interesting features of the SQ, but until recently studies had been restricted to the brightest regions. 2012A&A...539A.127I , hereafter IP12, analysed for the first time IFS observations of the shock region in order to get a complete mapping of physical and dynamical properties, with very interesting results about the different kinematical components of the gas.

Gas emission is only detected in the shock front, and the spectra of both galactic nuclei show only stellar emission. In the south region of our pointing two regions are detected, coinciding with those mentioned by IP12 in their S pointing. Further north we observe the shocked gas emission, with an evident strong blending in H lines and in the doublet. This is due to the presence of the two different kinematical components studied by IP12, although our data do not have enough spectral resolution to develop a similar kinematic analysis.

The set of models more consistent with our observational data are those of shocks without precursor with solar metallicity and low pre-shock density (n=0.1 cm ^ 3 ), and with velocities between 200 and 400 km s ^ 1 .

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC7318A as a quiescent member of the NGC7318 pair whose interaction with NGC7318B affects gas kinematics and may contribute to tidal structures.

quotes.quote: Whether both the inner and outer tidal tail were created in the interaction of NGC7319 with NGC7320C or whether an interaction between NGC7319 and NGC7318A was involved as well is still unclear renaud2010,hwang2012 . NGC7319 is expected to have been involved in nearly all of the past interactions and is classified as a Seyfert 2 galaxy with a large-scale outflow aoki1996 . Furthermore, it is yet to be determined whether NGC7317 passed through the group in the past sulentic2001,rod2014 , though a diffuse stellar halo extending towards the galaxy indicates that an interaction has occurred duc2018 . There is galaxy-wide shocked ridge located between the NGC7318 pair and NGC7319 (as marked in Fig. fig:structures together with additional important structures in SQ), flanked by star formation towards NGC7318B and in the ends of the radio shock cluver2010 .

As the spiral galaxy NGC7318B enters SQ from behind it interacts primarily with the IGM and NGC7318A. Due to the high line-of-sight velocity dispersion between NGC7318A and B, the pair is not expected to merge. NGC7318A and B are both quiescent galaxies, as clear from their optical spectra presented in Fig. fig:18a and fig:18b , and their low molecular gas content (listed in the tables in Appendix app:tables_7318 ). The stellar continuum is strong with prominent absorption lines, which, fitted with the pPXF routine, provides maps of velocity dispersion and line-of-sight velocity as a function of position. These maps, at a S/N>2, are presented in Appendix app:7318_maps and provide the line-of-sight velocity and velocity dispersion in the inner 1.5 '' of NGC7318A as 6787 5 km/s and 263.6 10.7 km/s, and in the inner 1.5 '' of NGC7318B as 5973 4 km/s and 168.9 4.0 km/s. Naturally, the velocities in the NGC7318B map increase as we move closer to the SF ridge. Studying the velocity dispersion as a function of radius in NGC7318A, Fig. fig:7318a_sig , reveals a typical curve common for elliptical galaxies.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 7318a as a physically related core member of Stephan Quintet, included in simulations and associated with tidal arms and group interactions.

quotes.quote: Stephan's Quintet ( St77 ; hereafter SQ), also known as Hickson Compact Group 92 Hi82 , is the first discovered compact galaxy group. It is located in the constellation Pegasus. SQ consists of five galaxies (NGC 7319, NGC 7318a, NGC 7318b, NGC 7317, and NGC 7320, cf. also Fig. morph ) with an estimated distance to earth of 94 Mpc MoMa98,ApXu06 . SQ is famous for a physical adjacency between four of the galaxies (NGC 7319, 7318a, 7318b, and 7317), whereby strong interactions between three of these members are apparently causing tidal tails, a strong group-wide shock visible in X-ray PiTr97,SuRo01 and radio emission AlHa72,XuLu03 , and a region of active star formation northern of the colliding galaxies Xu05 .

Three of the four physically related galaxies, the main galaxy NGC 7319 as well as NGC 7317 and NGC 7318a, have a similar recessional velocity of 6640-6670 km s ^ 1 FeGa11 and represent the core of the compact group (.f. Fig. morph ). North of NGC 7318a and NGC 7318b there are two tidal arms extending from the galaxies, which belong to a region of active star formation GaCh01,Xu05 .

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 7318A as one of Stephan's Quintet's strongly interacting galaxies, identifying it as early type and relating nearby clusters and debris to the interaction history.

quotes.quote: NGC 7318A/B NGC 7318 A and B are the two galaxies within SQ that are currently strongly interacting. Because of their proximity we analyze the population of SSCs around these galaxies together, as did F11.

NGC 7318A is an early type galaxy (found in a GMOS spectrum obtained by Konstantopoulos et al., in prep) while NGC 7318B is a spiral galaxy thought to be falling into SQ with a high velocity for the first time (Moles et al. 1997).

T119, T120, T121 and T123 are associated with the tidal tail/spiral arm emanating from NGC 7318B, while T111a-d and T112 all lie to the south of NGC 7318A, with its associated debris. Clusters/complexes T111a-d and T112 do not have similar velocities to NGC 7318A, but instead are consistent with NGC 7318B.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 7318A as an elliptical, links it to the NGC 7318A/B region, and argues its interaction with NGC 7319 shaped the Young Tail.

quotes.quote: In the literature NGC 7318A is identified as an elliptical galaxy ( nilson73 ; rc3 ; moles98 ), which would explain the relatively large number of old SCCs observed in this region. Another interesting feature present in the color-color plot for NGC 7318A/B (Figure fig:N7318AB ) is a lack of detected SCCs between the ages of 400 Myr and 2 Gyr. Five out of the seven objects that are located in the box of 0.0< \ \ <1.1 and 0.5< \ \ <0.8 (i.e., approximately 1 Gyr old) are either coincident with bright sources in the H_ images or located in the actively star-forming regions, making them very likely to be reddened young clusters.

The models by renaud10 suggest that approximately 200 Myr ago NGC 7319 had another interaction, that time with NGC 7318A (see sec:YT ), triggering a second wave of star formation and depleting the remaining galactic gas reservoir.

sources.summary: The paper presents 7318A's first published flux-calibrated spectrum, confirms it as an early-type galaxy, and links dust/backlighting and SQ-A debris to interactions with 7318B and the IGM.

quotes.quote: Additionally, we show the flux-calibrated spectrum of 7318A in Figure fig:7318a , the first to be published of this system. The lack of emission lines confirms it as an early-type galaxy, as morphologically classified by sulentic01 . This indicates that the dust features seen across the face of this galaxy are in fact part of 7318B, back-lit by 7318A see for examples of this process keel13 .

These pieces of evidence, along with the slightly higher SQ-A velocity are consistent with the renaud10 and hwang12 models that trace the origin of SQ-A as debris ejected from 7318A before colliding with the IGM.

sources.summary: The paper reports silicate emission near NGC 7318a's center, weak nuclear X-ray and radio emission, and interprets the galaxy as likely hosting a low-luminosity AGN.

quotes.quote: We note the detection of Silicate emission peaking at approximately 10 m from spectra taken near the center of both NGC 7318a and NGC 7318b (Figure A1e and A1h). Silicate emission is relatively rare, and its detection usually indicates the existence of a warm dust torus surrounding an AGN but seen from above li08,hat15 . We note that both galaxies show weak nuclear X-ray emission (O'Sullivan et al. 2009-Figure 2 of that paper), although NGC 7318a is brighter (with L(0.3-3keV) = 9.4 10 ^ 39 erg s ^ 1 for D = 94 Mpc; Trinchieri et al. 2005), and also contains a (0.95 mJy at 20 cm) nuclear radio source (Xu et al. 2003) with a spectral index of 0.62. It is therefore likely that both galaxies contain low-luminosity AGN.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 7318A as an elliptical member of Stephan's Quintet whose Faraday spectrum has the shared second peak associated with the group.

quotes.quote: The other two members of the Quintet, the ellipticals NGC 7317, and NGC 7318A, are less pronounced.

Fig. pi_prof_sq shows the Faraday Dispersion Function (FDF) for the objects that belong to the Stephan's Quintet: NGC 7318A (uppermost), NGC 7319 (upper-middle) and the IGM shock region (lower-middle). The lowermost panel presents the profile extracted for the background radio galaxy SQ--R xu03 These four profiles show a double-peaked distribution.

Throughout the Quintet area, the position of the second peak remains quite stationary: it reaches +230 \ at the position of NGC 7319, +250 \ at the position of SQ-R, +260 \ at the position of NGC 7318A and the highest value of +275 \ is found in the shock area.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 7318a as an early-type member invoked in simulations and proposed to have triggered Stephan's Quintet's inner tail, with models pairing it in an encounter with NGC 7318b.

quotes.quote: The fiducial model in Renaud2010 involves a number of events in the last half Gyr, including collisions between spiral galaxies NGC 7319 and NGC 7320c creating the so-called outer tail, between NGC 7319 and the early-type galaxy NGC 7318a creating the inner tail, and between the high-speed intruder NGC 7318b and the intragroup medium producing shocks in the gas stripped by the previous interactions.
Xu2005 , based on newer observations, argued that it is more likely that the inner tail is triggered by NGC7318a which has a closer projected distance to NGC7319 than NGC7320c.

The fiducial model of Hwang2012 can reasonably reproduce the the positions of the galaxies involved in the encounter between NGC 7319 and NGC 7320c and that between NGC 7318a and NGC 7318b, and the locations and the orientations of the inner and older tails. However, it cannot reproduce the overall HI distribution in SQ, in particular it predicts that NGC 7319 and NGC 7318b still retain most of their gas, in contradiction with the observations. For NGC 7318b, the fiducial model of Hwang2012 assumes that it has a relative radial velocity with NGC 7318a of v 300 km s^ 1 , which is significantly lower than the observed value ( v 900 km s^ 1 ).

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC7318A as an elliptical core member of SQ, notes its mild radial velocity dispersion, and finds no ionized gas in its vicinity.

quotes.quote: SQ is composed of elliptical (NGC7317 and NGC7318A) and spiral galaxies (NGC7319, NGC7320c, and NGC7318B).

The core of SQ (i.e. NGC7317, NGC7318A, and NGC7319) and NGC7320c show a mild radial velocity dispersion, whereas NGC7318B has an unusually high radial velocity dispersion compared to the others ( V 1000 km s^ 1 ), indicating that it is not bound to the SQ 2005A&A...444..697T .
When considering a contrast (H ) 2 and binning 6x6, we do not detect gas in the old tail, in the vicinity of NGC7318A nor in NGC7317.

gmw_00001275.name: NGC7320C
gmw_00001275.overview: NGC 7320C is a barred spiral galaxy [arXiv:1909.05797, arXiv:1712.07145] that hosts an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1909.05797] but lacks detectable atomic gas [arXiv:1105.5840, arXiv:astro-ph/0407473]. Past gravitational interactions with NGC 7319 stripped its interstellar medium [arXiv:2109.13757] and created tidal tails [arXiv:2306.11231, arXiv:1206.1234].

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 7320c as the old intruder in interaction models, stripping gas and pulling tidal tails, while alternative simulations make its close passage by NGC 7319 the shared origin of both tails.

quotes.quote: The fiducial model in Renaud2010 involves a number of events in the last half Gyr, including collisions between spiral galaxies NGC 7319 and NGC 7320c creating the so-called outer tail, between NGC 7319 and the early-type galaxy NGC 7318a creating the inner tail, and between the high-speed intruder NGC 7318b and the intragroup medium producing shocks in the gas stripped by the previous interactions. However, the hydrodynamical simulations in Hwang2012 favor a different scenario in which both the outer tail and inner tail were triggered simultaneously by a close encounter between NGC 7320c and NGC 7319 about 9 10^8 years ago.

Long history of interactions in SQ Moles1997 proposed a comprehensive picture for the interaction history of SQ based on a `two-intruders' scenario also see Sulentic2001 : an old intruder (NGC 7320c) stripped most of the gas from group members, and a new intruder (NGC 7318b) is currently colliding with this gas and triggering the large scale shock. The `old intruder' passed the core of SQ twice, one in 5 7 10^8 yr ago and pulled out the outer tail (also known as `old tail'), another about 2 10^8 yr ago and triggered the inner tail (`young tail') . Xu2005 , based on newer observations, argued that it is more likely that the inner tail is triggered by NGC7318a which has a closer projected distance to NGC7319 than NGC7320c. However, the simulations of Renaud2010 and Hwang2012 found that, if indeed the inner tail is generated later in a different encounter, it is very difficult for the outer tail to survive the strong tidal influence during the second passage. Instead, Hwang2012 concluded that it is most likely that the two tails are triggered by the same passage of NGC 7320c from a very close vicinity of NGC 7319 about 9 10^8 years ago. The fiducial model of Hwang2012 can reasonably reproduce the the positions of the galaxies involved in the encounter between NGC 7319 and NGC 7320c and that between NGC 7318a and NGC 7318b, and the locations and the orientations of the inner and older tails.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 7320c as a likely compact-group member, modeled as interacting with NGC 7319 and connected to the large eastern tidal tail.

quotes.quote: The fifth galaxy, NGC 7320, is observed to be a much closer foreground galaxy and is not part of the interacting group Sh74,AlSu80,MoSu97 . It has a recessional velocity of 740 km s ^ 1 FaKu99 . Three of the four physically related galaxies, the main galaxy NGC 7319 as well as NGC 7317 and NGC 7318a, have a similar recessional velocity of 6640-6670 km s ^ 1 FeGa11 and represent the core of the compact group (.f. Fig. morph ). NGC 7318b is observed to be a high-speed intruder ( 5770 km s ^ 1 ) and seems to interact with the IGM within the main system for the first time MoSu97,FeGa11 . Finally, there is a sixth galaxy, NGC 7320c, which shows a similar recessional velocity ( 5990 km s ^ 1 ) as the core of the compact group FeGa11 and is therefore suggested to interact with NGC 7319 MoSu97 , because it reveals connected tidal features in the eastern large tidal tail (cf. the outer tail in Fig. morph ). Therefore, it is most likely also part of the compact group Ar73 .

At its eastern end, the inner tail is pointing towards NGC 7320c. Xu05 proposed that the outer tail is the result of an encounter between the galaxies NGC 7320c and NGC 7319. Based on the fact that both the outer and the inner tails are directed towards NGC 7320c, MoMa98 and also SuRo01 proposed a bound orbit of NGC 7320c around NGC 7319, where the first interaction created the outer and a subsequent passage the inner tail.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC7320c as the old intruder with mild velocity dispersion and maps its H/[N II] nuclear and spiral-arm emission, finding an AGN-like profile.

quotes.quote: proposed an evolutionary model for SQ based on two intruders, an old intruder (OI, NGC7320c) and a new intruder (NI, NGC7318B).

The core of SQ (i.e. NGC7317, NGC7318A, and NGC7319) and NGC7320c show a mild radial velocity dispersion, whereas NGC7318B has an unusually high radial velocity dispersion compared to the others ( V 1000 km s^ 1 ), indicating that it is not bound to the SQ 2005A&A...444..697T .

Figure fig:NG7320c shows the H flux map (upper left panel), the [ N ii ] 6584 flux map (upper right panel), the H radial velocity map (lower left panel), and the deep image from the SN2 filter (lower right panel) for NGC7320c.
