In this figure, we see the presence of H and [ N ii ] 6584 emission in the galactic nucleus, and H emission in several regions located in the spiral arms. Figure fig:spectroNGC7320c shows examples of spectra for several regions with H emission found in NGC7320c. Observing the fit for the galactic nucleus emission line (see upper panel in Fig. fig:spectroNGC7320c ), we ascertain that the spectrum has an AGN profile (and found a strong emission of [ N ii ] 6584).

sources.summary: The paper links NGC 7320C to formation of the Old Tail, gas stripping from NGC 7319, and loss of its own detectable H-alpha gas.

quotes.quote: The Old Tail is a low surface brightness tidal structure that originates presumably from NGC 7319 ( renaud10 ) and formed through the interaction with NGC 7320C ( moles97 ; sulentic01 ).

The oldest event that has left a significant trace in the SQ system was a gravitational encounter between NGC 7319 with NGC 7320C ( moles97 ; sulentic01 ) which caused the formation of the Old Tail. Our analysis of the color-color diagram of star clusters detected in the Old Tail indicate this event happened approximately 400 Myr ago. Moreover, this encounter is also responsible for stripping the ISM from NGC 7319 and depositing it to the west of the galaxy ( moles98 ). Since NGC 7320C has no detectable H \ ( sulentic01 ) and assuming that there was only one interaction between those galaxies (see sec:OT and sec:YT ), we conclude that this collision also stripped all gas from NGC 7320C.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC7320C as a Stephan’s Quintet member whose past passage deposited ISM, created tidal tails, and may explain velocity components near NGC7319.

quotes.quote: Stephan's Quintet consists of five galaxies, NGC7317 , NGC7318A , NGC7318B , NGC7319 , and NGC7320C (NGC7320 is a foreground galaxy at a much lower redshift; burbidge1961 ), and exhibits impressive tidal structures that reveal a rich history of past interactions (e.g. allen1972,allen1980,shostak1984,moles1997, moles1998,fedotov2011 ). In the past, NGC7320C passed through the centre of the group, west of NGC7319 moles1997,lisenfeld2004 , depositing most of its interstellar medium (ISM) into the IGM and creating large tidal tail(s) (as marked in Fig. fig:structures ). Whether both the inner and outer tidal tail were created in the interaction of NGC7319 with NGC7320C or whether an interaction between NGC7319 and NGC7318A was involved as well is still unclear renaud2010,hwang2012 .

When studying the multiple velocity components in or near NGC7319, we find that they can often be related to the line-of-sight velocities of not only the central velocity of NGC7319 but also NGC7320C (likely in the creation of the inner tidal tail). From the pPXF fit of the stellar kinematics we obtain the mean stellar line-of-sight velocity and velocity dispersion in the central 1.5'' of NGC7319 as 6814 12 km/s and 159.4 13.0 km/s, respectively.

sources.summary: The paper links NGC 7320c to Stephan's Quintet's outer tail, describing it as the barred galaxy whose past crossing shaped the group's dynamical history.

quotes.quote: The so-called outer tail, observed to the South, disappears on its Western side towards the foreground galaxy NGC 7320. The deep CFHT image undoubtedly confirms that it points to the East towards NGC 7320c, the barred galaxy believed to have crossed the group about 0.5 Gyr ago. Interestingly, the rather blue colour of this tail (u-=1.4, see Fig. fig:SQ , bottom-left), testifies the presence of intermediate age stars, but star formation seems to have stopped there: the Eastern tip of the tail is not detected in UV in GALEX images we collected from the archives.
The connection between the outer tail and NGC 7320c is clearly confirmed, proving the active role of this galaxy in the dynamical history of the group.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 7320c as an earlier visitor whose collision with Stephan's Quintet stripped gas from NGC 7319, produced tidal structure, and left gas later shock-ionized.

quotes.quote: They suggest that a few times 10 ^8 yr ago the group experienced a collision with NGC 7320c, a galaxy 4 arcmin to the east of NGC 7319 but with a very similar recession velocity 6583 , Sulentic01 to the other galaxies in SQ. This collision removed most of the gas of NGC 7319 towards the west and east, and produced the eastern tidal tail which connects to NGC 7319.
Sulentic01 , in a multi-wavelength study of the group, confirm this scenario and suggest that the group has been visited twice by NGC 7320c.

Sulentic01 suggest that the gas at 6700 \ was stripped during the earlier visit of NGC 7320c and part of it has become shock-ionized due to the present interaction with NGC 7318b. In this view, the emission at 6700 \ traces the shock front. Strong support for this shock hypothesis is the fact that X--ray (tracing hot gas) and radio continuum emission (tracing cosmic ray electrons produced in shocks) are coincident with the H emission. The gas at velocities around 6000 \ originates from NGC 7318b and is being removed from this galaxy in the ongoing collision.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 7320c as a dynamically associated spiral whose passage may have formed Stephan's Quintet's tidal tails, and whose missing H I may have been stripped.

quotes.quote: A fifth galaxy, NGC 7320c, situated 4 arcmin to the east, is also dynamically associated to the group.
Sulentic01 suggest that each of these tails was created by a passage of NGC 7320c, the galaxy towards which both tails are pointing.
Part of the HI could also have been stripped from NGC 7320c, the other spiral galaxy in the group which completely lacks atomic gas.

gmw_00001276.name: SDSS J0936 + 0913
gmw_00001276.overview: SDSS J0936 + 0913 is a massive early-type elliptical galaxy located at a redshift of z = 0.164 [arXiv:1306.2635, arXiv:1409.4197]. It acts as a gravitational lens and is characterized by a low dark matter fraction and a nearly spherical dark matter halo [arXiv:1409.4197].

sources.summary: The paper models SDSS J0936 + 0913 as a massive early-type lens galaxy, deriving tight stellar-mass and dark-matter constraints plus an IMF slope near Salpeter.

quotes.quote: In this Letter we follow an analogous approach: by taking advantage of high-quality X-Shooter spectroscopic observations, we perform a fully self-consistent joint L D analysis of two massive lens ETGs: SDSS J0936 + 0913 ( = 243 11 at  z = 0.164 ) and SDSS J0912 + 0029 ( = 326 12 at  z = 0.190 ), hereafter J0936 and J0912, respectively.

This is made possible by the exquisite quality of the data sets available for the two systems, J0936 and J0912, including HST high-resolution imaging and high signal-to-noise X-Shooter spectra. We model simultaneously the lensing and kinematic data sets by adopting a flexible, axisymmetric, two-component mass distribution for the dark halo and the luminous profile (both self-gravitating), and solving the Jeans anisotropic equations. We derive tight constraints on the total stellar mass and dark matter fractions (within one ) of the two galaxies, i.e. = 11.52^ +0.02 _ 0.04 (and = 0.06^ +0.10 _ 0.05 ) for J0936 and = 12.01^ +0.03 _ 0.03 (and = 0.18^ +0.08 _ 0.08 ) for J0912, independent of the IMF.

The modelling of the line-strength indices (adopting solar metallicity and a cut-off mass = 0.115 ) provides a direct determination of the IMF slopes for the two lenses, namely J0936 = 2.10 0.15 and J0912 = 2.60 0.30 . The total stellar masses inferred from this method are completely consistent within the one-sigma uncertainties with the results obtained from the combined lensing and dynamics analysis, which is a fully independent approach that makes no assumptions on the IMF. For both galaxies, an IMF with slope close to Salpeter ( = 2.35 ) provides a much better description than a Chabrier profile: statistical analysis based on the calculation of evidence ratios (i.e., Bayes factor) shows that a Salpeter IMF model is preferred over a Chabrier one with  89 and  99 probability for J0936 and J0912, respectively. Bottom-heavy IMFs significantly steeper than Salpeter, i.e. with slopes 3.0 , are ruled out with decisive evidence (Bayes factor B > 1000 ) for both galaxies.

sources.summary: The paper models SDSS J0936 + 0913 as a massive XLENS elliptical lens, finding baryon dominance within one effective radius, a low dark matter fraction, and a nearly spherical halo.

quotes.quote: Both massive ellipticals SDSS J0936 + 0913 (velocity dispersion 245 ; redshift z = 0.164 ) and SDSS J0912 + 0029 ( 325 ; z = 0.190 ) were first observed as part of the SLACS Survey Bolton2008a . The high-resolution images needed for the lensing modelling were obtained with HST ACS through the F814W filter. Elliptical B-spline models of the lens galaxies were subtracted off the images to isolate the structure of the gravitationally lensed background sources.

Both systems are found to be markedly dominated by the baryonic component within the inner , with a dark matter fraction = 0.06^ +0.10 _ 0.05 for J0936 and = 0.18^ +0.08 _ 0.08 for the more massive system J0912. The dark halo structure of both galaxies ( = 6.3^ +18.4 _ 4.1 and = 124^ +160 _ 88 for J0936; = 7.2^ +3.8 _ 2.7 and = 470^ +160 _ 130 for J0912) is consistent with the concentration--virial velocity relation predicted from pure N body simulations Maccio2008 : thus, within the fairly broad uncertainties, there is no obvious evidence for contraction or expansion of the dark halo in response to galaxy formation. The inner slope of the dark halo is basically unconstrained for J0936 ( = 0.92^ +0.72 _ 0.64 ), while for J0912 we can conclude that steep slopes are disfavoured ( = 0.46^ +0.41 _ 0.30 ). The two haloes differ in terms of flattening, with J0936 having a more spherical halo ( = 0.93^ +0.25 _ 0.18 ), while the halo of J0912 is clearly oblate ( = 0.54^ +0.10 _ 0.08 ).

gmw_00001277.name: SDSS J0912 + 0029
gmw_00001277.overview: SDSS J0912 + 0029 is a massive early-type elliptical galaxy [arXiv:1306.2635, arXiv:1409.4197] that acts as a strong gravitational lens [arXiv:1409.4197]. It features an oblate dark matter halo [arXiv:1409.4197], a high velocity dispersion [arXiv:1204.3823, arXiv:0908.0748], and two long lensed arcs [arXiv:0908.0748].

sources.summary: The paper treats SDSS J0912 + 0029 as a massive early-type lens galaxy, finding high-quality lensing, dynamics, and spectra favor a Salpeter-like IMF over Chabrier or steeper alternatives.

quotes.quote: In this Letter we follow an analogous approach: by taking advantage of high-quality X-Shooter spectroscopic observations, we perform a fully self-consistent joint L D analysis of two massive lens ETGs: SDSS J0936 + 0913 ( = 243 11 at  z = 0.164 ) and SDSS J0912 + 0029 ( = 326 12 at  z = 0.190 ), hereafter J0936 and J0912, respectively.

This is made possible by the exquisite quality of the data sets available for the two systems, J0936 and J0912, including HST high-resolution imaging and high signal-to-noise X-Shooter spectra. We model simultaneously the lensing and kinematic data sets by adopting a flexible, axisymmetric, two-component mass distribution for the dark halo and the luminous profile (both self-gravitating), and solving the Jeans anisotropic equations. We derive tight constraints on the total stellar mass and dark matter fractions (within one ) of the two galaxies, i.e. = 11.52^ +0.02 _ 0.04 (and = 0.06^ +0.10 _ 0.05 ) for J0936 and = 12.01^ +0.03 _ 0.03 (and = 0.18^ +0.08 _ 0.08 ) for J0912, independent of the IMF.

The modelling of the line-strength indices (adopting solar metallicity and a cut-off mass = 0.115 ) provides a direct determination of the IMF slopes for the two lenses, namely J0936 = 2.10 0.15 and J0912 = 2.60 0.30 . The total stellar masses inferred from this method are completely consistent within the one-sigma uncertainties with the results obtained from the combined lensing and dynamics analysis, which is a fully independent approach that makes no assumptions on the IMF. For both galaxies, an IMF with slope close to Salpeter ( = 2.35 ) provides a much better description than a Chabrier profile: statistical analysis based on the calculation of evidence ratios (i.e., Bayes factor) shows that a Salpeter IMF model is preferred over a Chabrier one with  89 and  99 probability for J0936 and J0912, respectively. Bottom-heavy IMFs significantly steeper than Salpeter, i.e. with slopes 3.0 , are ruled out with decisive evidence (Bayes factor B > 1000 ) for both galaxies.

sources.summary: The paper treats SDSSJ0912+0029 as a massive early-type SLACS lens with deep NaI absorption, high velocity dispersion, inferred bottom-heavy IMF, and lensing limits against steeper IMF slopes.

quotes.quote: As part of the XLENS The X-Shooter Lens Survey, Spiniello et al. \ (2011) project, we obtained a UVB-VIS X-shooter spectrum of the massive and luminous early-type SLACS (Sloan Lens ACS Survey, Bolton et al. 2006) lens galaxy SDSS J0912+0029 at z=0.1642 , with high enough signal-to-noise to perform stellar population analyses. The lens galaxy shows a surprisingly deep NaI0.82 feature (Fig. fig:spectra ), making it an extremely interesting target for studying the low-mass end of the IMF in ETGs. We measure the luminosity-weighted velocity dispersion of the lens galaxy from the reduced flux-calibrated 1D UVB--VIS spectrum using the Penalized Pixel Fitting (pPXF) code of Cappellari Emsellem (2004). We obtain _ * ( R_ eff )=325 10 12 , in agreement with the previously published value ( 313 12 ; Bolton et al. \ 2006).

A strong case against an extreme bottom-heavy IMF can be made using the system with the strongest NaI EW (Fig. fig:indices ), the XLENS galaxy SDSSJ0912+0029. This system provides a hard upper limit on the stellar mass inside its Einstein radius, no matter the IMF model. If we assume that the SSP models are correct and that this galaxy has a high [ Na/Fe ] abundance, we infer an IMF with a power-law slope x=3 - 3.5 (where the IMF follows dn/dm=m^ x , and the Salpeter slope is x=2.35 ).

sources.summary: The paper models SDSS J0912 + 0029 as the more massive XLENS elliptical, deriving baryon dominance, a moderate dark matter fraction, disfavored steep inner halo slopes, and an oblate halo.

quotes.quote: Both massive ellipticals SDSS J0936 + 0913 (velocity dispersion 245 ; redshift z = 0.164 ) and SDSS J0912 + 0029 ( 325 ; z = 0.190 ) were first observed as part of the SLACS Survey Bolton2008a . The high-resolution images needed for the lensing modelling were obtained with HST ACS through the F814W filter. Elliptical B-spline models of the lens galaxies were subtracted off the images to isolate the structure of the gravitationally lensed background sources.

Both systems are found to be markedly dominated by the baryonic component within the inner , with a dark matter fraction = 0.06^ +0.10 _ 0.05 for J0936 and = 0.18^ +0.08 _ 0.08 for the more massive system J0912. The dark halo structure of both galaxies ( = 6.3^ +18.4 _ 4.1 and = 124^ +160 _ 88 for J0936; = 7.2^ +3.8 _ 2.7 and = 470^ +160 _ 130 for J0912) is consistent with the concentration--virial velocity relation predicted from pure N body simulations Maccio2008 : thus, within the fairly broad uncertainties, there is no obvious evidence for contraction or expansion of the dark halo in response to galaxy formation. The inner slope of the dark halo is basically unconstrained for J0936 ( = 0.92^ +0.72 _ 0.64 ), while for J0912 we can conclude that steep slopes are disfavoured ( = 0.46^ +0.41 _ 0.30 ). The two haloes differ in terms of flattening, with J0936 having a more spherical halo ( = 0.93^ +0.25 _ 0.18 ), while the halo of J0912 is clearly oblate ( = 0.54^ +0.10 _ 0.08 ).

sources.summary: The paper singles out J0912+002 among SLACS lenses because it has two long arcs modeled as four doubles and the highest lensing-galaxy velocity dispersion in the sample.

quotes.quote: The doubly imaged systems among the SLACS lenses are J0037-094 , J0912+002 , J1330-014 and J2303+142 . All of them are quite typical with biases according to their observation method. J1330-014 is the nearest lens with z=0.08 and shows the smallest . Among these lenses J0912+002 takes a special position. It consists of two long arcs which are represented in this work as four doubles. Moreover, this lensing galaxy has the highest among all lenses of our sample.

gmw_00001278.name: DGSAT I
gmw_00001278.overview: DGSAT I is an isolated, quiescent ultra-diffuse galaxy located in a low-density environment near the Pisces-Perseus supercluster [arXiv:1601.06960, arXiv:2209.09910]. The galaxy is deficient in cold gas [arXiv:2001.05746, arXiv:2209.09910], yet it exhibits an extreme magnesium-to-iron enhancement [arXiv:2309.15148] and hosts a rich compact globular cluster system [arXiv:2209.09910].

sources.summary: The review describes DGSAT I as an isolated field ultra-diffuse galaxy at 78 Mpc, with low gas or star formation and unusually magnesium-enhanced stellar populations.

quotes.quote: The most important discovery so far of the DGSAT project (see Sec. 4) is a field ultra-diffuse galaxy named DGSAT I (Mart nez-Delgado et al. 2016). Its unresolved appearance in the amateur (Fig 12, left panel) and Subaru follow-up images, structural properties and absence of emission lines initially suggested an interesting case of an isolated dwarf galaxy 10 Mpc beyond the Local Group with a surface brightness and structural properties similar to those of the classical Milky Way dSph companions. However, spectroscopic follow-up observations with the BTA 6-meter telescope yielded a radial velocity of 5450 40 km s ^−1 . This revealed DGSAT I is actually a background system placed at a distance of 78 Mpc and likely associated with an outer filament of the Pisces-Perseus super-cluster projected in this direction of the sky.

Unlike other UDGs found in the Coma, Virgo and Fornax galaxy clusters (see E.K. Grebel review, this volume) at that time, DGSAT I is a rare example of isolated UDG that resides in a low density environment. The isolation of DGSAT I offers an opportunity to study the secular evolution of this type of object and to explore different formation scenarios for UDGs without confounding environmental effects. Since its discovery, DGSAT I has been observed with the Hubble Space Telescope (Romanowsky et al. in preparation), Keck Cosmic Web Imager (Martin-Navarro et al. 2019), Spitzer Space Telescope (Pandya et al. 2018) and it has also inspired theoretical simulations with the goal of explaining its formation (Di Cintio et al. 2017). Its estimated stellar mass is 4.8 10 ^8 M _ o , and it shows little sign of cold gas or current star formation (Papastergis et al. 2017).

sources.summary: The paper identifies DGSAT I as a quenched ultra-diffuse galaxy behind M31 in the Pisces-Perseus supercluster, with old stellar colors, large size, and a low-density environment.

quotes.quote: Its red color ( V-I = 1.0 ), shallow S ersic index ( n_V=0.68 ), and the absence of detectable emission are typical properties of dwarf spheroidal galaxies and suggest that it is mainly composed of old stars. Initially interpreted as an interesting case of an isolated dwarf spheroidal galaxy in the local universe, our radial velocity measurement obtained with the BTA 6-meter telescope ( V_h=5450 40 ) shows that this system is an M31-background galaxy associated with the filament of the Pisces-Perseus supercluster. At the distance of this cluster ( 78 ), \ would have an R_e 4.7 and M_ V 16.3 .

Its properties resemble those of the ultra-diffuse galaxies recently discovered in the Coma cluster. \ is the first case of these rare ultra-diffuse galaxies found in this galaxy cluster. Unlike the ultra-diffuse galaxies associated with the Coma and Virgo clusters, DGSAT I is found in a much lower density environment, which provides a fresh constraint on the formation mechanisms for this intriguing class of galaxy.
Alternatively, DGSAT I could be a nucleated UDGs, similar to those found in the Virgo cluster (Mihos et al. 2015; Beasley et al. 2015).

sources.summary: DGSAT I is described as an isolated ultra-diffuse galaxy with low surface brightness, little cold gas, unusual magnesium enhancement, and a high mass-to-light ratio.

quotes.quote: This relatively blue object DGSAT I was first identified by MD16 , and it is particularly interesting because it apparently resides in a low density environment, at least compared to the densities where UDGs are typically found e.g. vdB16,Roman17 . \ is a UDG likely associated with the outskirts of the Pisces-Perseus supercluster MD16 , with a central surface brightness of _V = 24.8 mag arcsec ^ 2 and a total V band luminosity of L_V=2.7 10^8 L_ . It has an optical (V-I) color of 1.0, and size of =4.7 kpc in the I band at a distance of 78 Mpc MD16,Viraj18 . The estimated stellar mass of DGSAT I is 4.8 10^8 , and it shows little sign of cold gas or current star formation Papastergis17 .

With a measured velocity dispersion of 56 10 , \ also shows a high mass-to-light ratio, which indicates that it is highly dark matter-dominated. The metal-poor conditions of DGSAT I may have enhanced the formation of massive stars, while at the same time, additional mechanisms are needed to prevent iron-rich yields from being recycled into stars.

sources.summary: The paper studies DGSAT I as an isolated quiescent UDG with a blue clump, rich compact globular-cluster system, high velocity dispersion, and likely failed-galaxy origin.

quotes.quote: We investigate the isolated, quiescent ultra-diffuse galaxy (UDG) DGSAT I and its globular cluster (GC) system using two orbits of Hubble Space Telescope Advanced Camera for Surveys imaging in the F606W and F814W filters. This is the first study of GCs around a UDG in a low-density environment. DGSAT I was previously found to host an irregular blue low surface brightness clump, that we confirm as very likely belonging to the galaxy rather than being a chance projection, and represents a recent episode of star formation ( 500 Myr ) that challenges some UDG formation scenarios.

DGSAT I is the best-studied example of a quenched UDG in a low density environment, discovered serendipitously as a background galaxy in a study of the M31 halo MD2016 . It is associated with the Pisces--Perseus supercluster at a distance D 80 kpc , is red ( V-I =1.0 ) and quiescent MD2016 and also deficient in cold gas papastergis2017 . MN2019 carried out integral-field spectroscopy of DGSAT I and measured a velocity dispersion of = 56 10 km s ^ 1 , which is currently the highest known for any UDG.

sources.summary: DGSAT I is an isolated UDG with extreme magnesium enhancement, unusual scaling-relation behavior, and stellar populations similar to Yagi358.

quotes.quote: In the case of the isolated UDG DGSAT I, it exhibits the most extreme [ Mg/Fe ] ever found in any type of galaxy Martin-Navarro2019 . We include the group UDG NGC 1052-DF2 Fensch2019 and the isolated UDG DGSAT I Martin-Navarro2019 . UDGs mostly cover the [ Mg/Fe ] range of GCs and dwarfs at all metallicities, and only the most extreme ones (DGSAT I and Yagi358) having [ Mg/Fe ] that do not match any galaxy in the comparison sets.

In particular, DGSAT I has been shown to be an extremely bizarre galaxy in many respects Martin-Navarro2019 . Given that they both have overall similar stellar population properties, we speculate that Yagi358 could have formed in a similar process to DGSAT I.

sources.summary: DGSAT I is cited as an isolated UDG with recent star-formation evidence; its HI upper limit remains consistent with simulated gas-rich isolated UDG analogues.

quotes.quote: Corroborating the idea that UDGs can form in isolation, delgado16 found a UDG in the outskirts of the Pisces-Perseus supercluster, DGSAT I, that shows a blue over-density compatible with recent star formation.

While most recent observations focused on UDGs in clusters, finding the not surprising result that those objects are gas-poor, there is no current evidence that isolated UDGs should be gas-poor: indeed, the only work that focused on isolated UDGs could only place an upper limit of < 10 ^ 8.8 \ on the expected amount of HI gas in DGSAT I delgado16 , suggesting that values of 7 log _ 10 ( / ) 9, as predicted by our simulations, are fully within observational constraints.

The bursty SFH of the UDG can be appreciated in panel b), including peaks in the last Gyr of its history, indicating recent episodes of star formation: this feature is also reflected in the blue off-centered over-density visible in the colour image of panel a), similar to DGSAT I UDG observed in delgado16 .

sources.summary: The paper uses DGSAT I as an isolated UDG comparison, noting it is younger than the sample, has extremely high Mg/Fe, and has similarly high stellar velocity dispersion.

quotes.quote: Yet our UDGs in the low-density environments are younger than most of the member UDGs in galaxy clusters/groups Gu18,Ferre-Mateu18,Fensch19,Ruiz-Lara18 but older than the isolated UDG DGSAT I Martin-Navarro19 , as shown in panel b of Fig. UDG_property .

As shown in panel d of Fig. UDG_property , our relatively-isolated UDGs have a lower [ Mg/Fe ] compared with the extremely-high [ Mg/Fe ] 1.5 0.5 of DGSAT I Martin-Navarro19 ; yet it is similar to the [ Mg/Fe ] of the member UDGs in clusters/groups Ferre-Mateu18,Fensch19 .

We find _ los 54 12 km/s for our UDGs We have used the mock spectra with the different input stellar dispersions but same S/N ( 30 ) of our stacked spectrum, and found that the pPXF fitting can well recover a dispersion >35 km/s; for _ los <35 km/s, the fitting slightly under-estimates (but still in 1 uncertainty range) the input dispersions cf. also Guerou17,Cappellari17 . , comparable to the high dispersion of DGSAT I Martin-Navarro19 .

sources.summary: DGSAT-I is listed as a field UDG near Pisces-Perseus; the paper rejects a backsplash classification and notes unusually luminous globular clusters.

quotes.quote: DGSAT- I DGSAT- I is listed as field although we note that it is located near the Pisces--Perseus supercluster and may potentially be a `backsplash' galaxy MartinezDelgado2016, Papastergis2017, Benavides2021 . The backsplash galaxy hypothesis has been disfavoured by Janssens2022 and thus we continue to list this galaxy as a field object. Note that some of the GCs counted are more luminous than expected given a traditional GC luminosity function Janssens2022 .

gmw_00001279.name: SDSS125343.71+122721.5
gmw_00001279.overview: SDSS125343.71+122721.5 is a cataloged quasar and active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1701.03694]. Although previously cataloged as a high-RFe source, reanalysis reveals that the object actually exhibits negligible Fe II emission [arXiv:1701.03694].

sources.summary: The paper treats SDSS125343.71+122721.5 as a cataloged high-RFe quasar whose reanalysis shows negligible Fe II emission, leading the authors to exclude it from mean values.

quotes.quote: Object SDSS125343.71+122721.5 has high R_ Fe in the Shen et al. catalog (see Table tab:objects ) but negligible R_ Fe in our fits, and visual inspection also does not show traces of Fe II emission. We discussed this issue with the authors of the catalog, and apparently, this is a mistake in their automatic recording routine (Yue Shen, private communications). We therefore ignored this object when giving any mean/median values, but we plot the source for the record, and as an example of weak Fe II emission.

gmw_00001280.name: SDSS125100.44+660326.8
gmw_00001280.overview: SDSS125100.44+660326.8 is an active galactic nucleus classified as a quasar [arXiv:1701.03694]. It exhibits narrow oxygen III lines and iron II pseudo-continuum features that yield different redshift estimates depending on the line system used [arXiv:1701.03694].

sources.summary: The paper finds SDSS125100.44+660326.8 difficult to fit at the catalog redshift, preferring shifted O III and Fe II systems and reporting multiple redshift estimates.

quotes.quote: Narrow [ OIII ] lines in object SDSS125100.44+660326.8 could not be fitted if assumed to be at the rest frame implied by the redshift taken from Shen et al. (2011). We considered the change of the source redshift but in this case the Fe II pseudo-continuum features, well visible in this source due to low broadening (only 700 km s ^ 1 ) were not reproduced at the proper positions. We thus allowed in this source for a redshift or a blueshift of both [ OIII ] lines, and Fe II lines, as we did in all our fits for the broad H component. We obtained much better fit for [ OIII ] lines shifted by 180 km s ^ 1 toward longer wavelengths.

Final reduced ^2 is now significantly lower (4.39). We thus obtained three values of the redshift in this source, depending on the line system used as a reference. Broad H line redshift is z = 0.2823532 , [ OIII ] lines give z = 0.2825588 , and Fe II implies the redshift z = 0.28015 .

gmw_00001281.name: SDSS150245.36+405437.2
gmw_00001281.overview: SDSS150245.36+405437.2 is an active galactic nucleus classified as an extreme Eigenvector 1 object [arXiv:1701.03694]. It features a very narrow H-beta line, weak [O III] emission, noticeable Fe II emission, and a significant contribution from disk emission [arXiv:1701.03694].

sources.summary: The paper identifies SDSS150245.36+405437.2 as an extreme EV1 object with the sample's highest RFe, narrow H beta, weak O III, noticeable Fe II, and important disk emission.

quotes.quote: The highest narrow component contribution (narrow to broad component ratio ratio of 1.24) is seen in SDSS150245.36+405437.2 which has the highest R_ Fe value in our sample. If R_ Fe is measured with respect to the total H flux, the new value is 1.4. This object would still, therefore, have been selected as an extreme EV1 case.

Some of the objects, such as SDSS121549.43+544223.9, or SDSS150245.36+405437.2 as mentioned before, have a very narrow H line, but their kinematic width is still somewhat broader than the width of [ OIII ] (870 km s ^ 1 and 665 km s ^ 1 , respectively). Intensities of the [ OIII ] lines are low, so the objects meet the criterion [ OIII ] /H < 3 (this ratio for the broad H component only is 1.85 and 1.29, respectively, for these two objects in our fits). The contribution of the disk emission is important, so the source cannot be considered as a Seyfert 2 galaxy. The relative shift between [ OIII ] and H is less than 45 km s ^ 1 , for both sources. The objects are well fitted (see Fig. fig:wszystkie for all fitted spectra and Fig. fig:narrow_line for an expanded version for SDSS150245.36+405437.2), with noticeable Fe II.

gmw_00001282.name: SDSS130112.91+590206.6
gmw_00001282.overview: SDSS130112.91+590206.6 is a quasar located at a redshift of 0.478 [arXiv:1701.03694, arXiv:astro-ph/0509539]. Its optical spectrum exhibits a high starlight fraction [arXiv:1701.03694] alongside strong Fe II contamination that obscures key absorption features [arXiv:1701.03694].

sources.summary: The paper uses SDSS130112.91+590206.6 to test starlight fitting, finding high starlight fractions but ambiguous absorption constraints because Fe II contamination buries key features.

quotes.quote: We selected quasar SDSS130112.91+590206.6 for which the starlight content in the first-stage fitting was 72.5 , and the last-stage fitting gave 65.6 for a Lorentzian fit, and 62.8 for a Gaussian fit (see Table tab:nasze ). We partially relied on the wide-range STARLIGHT results in selecting the dominant starlight components. We allowed them to vary and we performed an interactive fit of the two dominating components as well as of the total starlight normalization, and all the other parameters, including both starlight and power-law extinction, and selected emission lines.

The data do not convincingly indicate a better solution. The signal-to-noise ratio (S/N) is not a problem, the source SDSS130112.91+590206.6 has a g magnitude of 15.6, comparable to the source RE J1034+396 (g magnitude of 15.4) where the same absorption features were clearly visible (Czerny et al. 2016). The problem lies in the strong Fe II contamination. In the wavelength range 5100 - 5200 there are 10 Fe II transitions in our templates, some of them strong, and 15 transitions are in the range 3900 - 4000 . Using a single normalization for the whole Fe II template does not provide a precise fit to the data, and the possible absorption feature is buried in Fe II.

sources.summary: The paper includes PG 1259+593 as a quasar sightline from prior STIS BLA work, summarizing 29 candidates, tentative classifications, spectrum S/N, and line-density estimates.

quotes.quote: So far, two sight lines observed with STIS towards the quasars PG 1259+593 ( z_ em =0.478 ) and PG 1116+215 ( z_ em =0.176 ) have been carefully inspected for the presence of BLAs, and a number of candidates have been identified (Richter et al. 2004; Sembach et al. 2004).

Toward PG 1259+593 there are 29 BLA candidates along relatively long, unblocked redshift path of z=0.355 (Richter et al. 2004). Following the selection criteria presented above, 22 of these candidate BLA systems are tentative detections. The average S/N in the the PG 1259+593 spectrum is 12 , but note that the S/N varies within a factor of 1.5 along the spectrum. The number of reliably detected broad systems ( =7 ) corresponds to dN_ BL /dz 20 for log (N/b) 11.1-11.4 ( 82 including the uncertain cases).

gmw_00001283.name: SDSS133005.71+254243.7
gmw_00001283.overview: SDSS133005.71+254243.7 is a quasar characterized by an extremely broad hydrogen line [arXiv:1701.03694]. It exhibits a moderate iron emission ratio, which requires extreme broadening of the iron pseudo-continuum to model [arXiv:1701.03694].

sources.summary: The paper refits SDSS133005.71+254243.7 as a moderate-RFe, extremely broad-line object requiring very broad Fe II modeling, reducing its RFe below the catalog value.

quotes.quote: Object SDSS133005.71+254243.7 has moderate R_ Fe but an extremely broad H line. We thus refitted this object more carefully, searching for the best Fe II template among Bruhweiler Verner (2008) options and best broadening. In this object the Fe II pseudo-continuuum also required extreme broadening of 2300 km s ^ 1 , and the best fit template was d11-m10-20-5 (i.e., particle density 10^ 11 cm ^ 3 , turbulent velocity 10 km s ^ 1 , lower than typical, and ionization parameter, = 10^ 20.5 cm ^ 2 s ^ 1 ).

This template also gives a strong and broad contribution close to the [ OIII ] line which is clearly visible in the spectrum. Achieved ^2 is 870/700 \ d.o.f . The R_ Fe ratio is 0.9, somewhat higher than obtained from systematic fitting of all objects but nevertheless smaller than 1.93 reported by Shen et al. (2011).

gmw_00001284.name: SDSS J074834.64+444117.8
gmw_00001284.overview: SDSS J074834.64+444117.8 is a nearby disk galaxy containing two counter-rotating stellar disks [arXiv:2401.11179]. Its primary disk is older and counter-rotates with the ionized gas, while the younger, metal-poorer secondary disk co-rotates with the gas [arXiv:2401.11179].

sources.summary: The paper studies SDSS J074834.64+444117.8 as a nearby galaxy with counter-rotating stellar disks, finding an older primary disk, younger metal-poorer secondary disk, and likely external gas accretion.

quotes.quote: Using the integral field spectroscopic data from Mapping Nearby Galaxies at Apache Point Observatory survey, we study the kinematics and stellar population properties of the two counter-rotating stellar disks in a nearby galaxy SDSS J074834.64+444117.8. We disentangle the two stellar disks by three methods, including 8542 double Gaussian fit, pPXF spectral decomposition, and orbit-based dynamical model. These three different methods give consistent stellar kinematics.

The pPXF spectral decomposition provides the spectra of two stellar disks, with one being more luminous across the whole galaxy named primary disk, and the other named secondary disk. The primary disk is counter-rotating with ionized gas, while the secondary disk is co-rotating with ionized gas. The secondary disk has younger stellar population and poorer stellar metallicity than the primary disk. We estimate the stellar mass ratio between the primary and secondary disks to be 5.2. The DESI g , , z color image doesn't show any merger remnant feature in this galaxy.

gmw_00001285.name: BCG in A1835
gmw_00001285.overview: The brightest cluster galaxy in Abell 1835 is a luminous infrared galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0603656] with a compact, diffuse radio morphology [arXiv:1504.00921] and sub-solar metallicity [arXiv:1504.00921]. Its extended [OII] nebular gas spans 53 kpc [arXiv:2404.02212], aligned with X-ray cavities [arXiv:2404.02212], while its star formation is likely powered by cooling cluster gas [arXiv:astro-ph/0603656].

sources.summary: The paper presents 1916 as the best A1835 z=10 candidate, with photometric redshift z=9-11, a weak emission line implying z=10.00175, high lensing magnification, and faint intrinsic H/Ks magnitudes.

quotes.quote: This galaxy (called 1916) was the best candidate found in the field of the lensing cluster A1835, and a good example of the research procedure. In this case, the photometric redshift probability distribution shows a clear maximum at redshift z_ phot 9--11. This estimate is mainly corroborated by a strong break of 3.1 to 3.7 AB mags between VRI and H , which has a high significance independently of the definition used for the limiting magnitudes (see Fig. z10 ). The spectroscopic redshift determination was obtained with ISAAC/VLT between 29 June and 3 July 2003, under excellent seeing conditions, using a 1 arcsec slit width.

The observations resulted in the detection of one weak emission line at the 4-5 level with an integrated flux of (4.1 0.5) 10^ 18 erg cm ^ 2 s ^ 1 at a wavelength of 1.33745 \ (see Fig. \ z10 ), which appears on 2 different overlapping wavelength settings. When identified as in good agreement with the photometric SED, the observed line gives a redshift of z=10.00175 0.00045 , the most likely one given the data set (see a detailed discussion in Pell o et 2004). Given the location of the image on the lensing plane, the source is highly magnified by a factor of 25. Its intrinsic AB magnitude is 28.5 and 29 in the H and Ks bands respectively.

sources.summary: The paper maps the central galaxy in Abell 1835, finding extended [OII] nebular gas aligned with X-ray cavities and complex redshifted gas kinematics.

quotes.quote: Above a flux of 2.5 10^ 16 erg s ^ 1 cm ^ 2 , the stellar continuum is detected up to a radius of 20 kpc from the nucleus of the central galaxy. Half of the total stellar continuum flux is contained within a radius of 9 kpc ( 2.2 ) from the center. The middle and right panels of Fig. fig:flux:A1835 show the detected [ OII ] flux in and around the central galaxy.

The [ OII ] morphology observed in the core of Abell 1835 provides compelling evidence for the impact of X-ray cavities on gas dynamics. The right panel of Fig. fig:flux:A1835 shows that the nebular gas is extended along the direction of the two X-ray cavities. The detected [ OII ] emission spans 53 kpc along the axis of the bubbles, compared to 32 kpc in the perpendicular direction.

sources.summary: The paper identifies the A1835 BCG as an infrared-bright brightest cluster galaxy with a far-infrared SED peak, LIRG-level luminosity, and star formation possibly linked to cooling gas.
sources.paper_coordinate_text: A1835 & 14 01 02.0 & + 02 52 45; These coordinates indicate the positions of the BCGs.

quotes.quote: The exceptions, however, are the BCGs in the three X-ray-brightest clusters in the sample, A1835, Z3146, and A2390. These BCGs have a prominent far-infrared peak in their spectral energy distributions (SEDs), and two of them (those in A1835 and Z3146) can be classified as luminous infrared galaxies (LIRGs: L_ IR >10^ 11 L _ ). Although radio AGNs are found to be prevalent among the BCGs, the infrared luminosities of these three BCGs, judged from the infrared SED signatures, are likely to be powered by star formation. Considering the overall trend that clusters with shorter radiative gas cooling times harbor more infrared-luminous BCGs, the enhanced star formation may be caused by the cooling cluster gas accreting onto the BCGs.

The infrared-derived SFRs for the BCGs in A1835, Z3146, and A2390 are 125, 70, and 5 M _ yr ^ 1 , respectively, while the X-ray-derived mass deposition rates at the cluster center are <20 , 290, and 110 M _ yr ^ 1 , respectively, based on our own estimates. This indicates that the correspondence between the infrared-derived SFRs and X-ray-derived mass deposition rates is currently suggestive at best. Z3146 is the only case where the two quantities are broadly consistent (within a factor of several). A2390 shows a large difference (a factor of 20), which may be related to the strong radio AGN detected in the BCG of this cluster.

sources.summary: The paper describes the A1835 CCG as the sample’s most luminous H emitter, with elongated quiescent H kinematics and CO matching the ionized gas.

quotes.quote: At a redshift of z=0.2523 , A1835 is the most distant cluster presented in this study. It is also our most luminous H emitter, with an extinction-corrected luminosity of 3 10^ 42 and a visible star formation rate of 100 (Crawford et al. 1999; Allen 1995). The Spitzer MIPS photometry of Egami et al. (2006) reveal far infrared thermal dust emission with L_ IR = 7 10^ 11 , plausibly powered by star formation. In comparison with A1664 the kinematics are simple and relatively quiescent, requiring a single velocity component at each position. The H emission has a maximum extent of 8 (31 ) and is elongated in a NW-SE direction; the reconstructed continuum image shows a small companion galaxy 5 (20 ) north-west of the CCG.

sources.summary: The paper describes A 1835 as a BCG with burst histories to about 3.5 Gyr, sub-solar metallicity in comparison work, and compact diffuse radio morphology.

quotes.quote: While RXC J1504 is best fitted by a model with a single burst at 10 Myr, the other three seem to prefer multiple bursts with the shortest possible spacing (10 Myr), with ZwCl 3146 and A 1795 going up to the oldest age of 6 Gyr and A 1835 going up to the oldest age of about 3.5 Gyr.

Although the average mass-weighted metallicity of their sample is 1.5 , the three cool-core BCGs that overlap between our and their sample -- RXC J1504, ZwCl 3146 and A 1835 -- have sub-solar metallicities of around 0.5 (Fengshan Liu, private communication).

However, given the BCGs in our sample have very different radio morphologies varying from core dominated (RXC J1504), to compact and diffuse (PKS 0745-191 and A 1835), to small doubles (A 2597 and A 1795), to classic FR-Is (Hydra-A and A 2199), it is difficult to imagine a scenario where the power input in the large lobes is matched to the ongoing star formation.

sources.summary: The paper describes A1835 262 as an emission-line, X-ray-obscured QSO candidate with faint oxygen emission, high absorbing column, and intrinsic 2-10 keV luminosity of 1.10e44 erg s^-1.

quotes.quote: The H_ FWHM of GD153 236 (H2XMMJ125654.1+215318; z=0.909, AGN2) is <1000 km s ^ 1 . Faint [ OII ] and [ OIII ] emission is present in the spectrum of A1835 262 (H2XMMJ140130.8+024532; z=0.746, ELG). The latter source is obscured also in the X-ray band, having a 0.5-2 keV flux less than 20 times the observed 2-10 keV flux; to be more quantitative, the X--ray spectral fit yields a rest frame N _H (z) 7.9^ +3.3 _ 2.2 10^ 22 cm ^ 2 , fixing the spectral energy index _E (F(E) E ^ _E ) to 0.9. GD153 236 is less extreme in this regard, the X--ray spectral fit yields a rest frame N _H (z) = 0.3^ +0.4 _ 0.3 10^ 22 cm ^ 2 , fixing the spectral energy index _E to 0.9. The 2--10 keV intrinsic luminosity is 1.10 10^ 44 erg s ^ 1 for A1835 262 and 1.59 10^ 44 erg s ^ 1 for GD153 236, making them obscured QSO candidates.

gmw_00001286.name: BCG in Z3146
gmw_00001286.overview: The brightest cluster galaxy in Z3146 is classified as a luminous infrared galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0603656]. Its infrared luminosity is likely powered by star formation, which is broadly consistent with the central X-ray mass deposition rate of its host cluster [arXiv:astro-ph/0603656].

sources.summary: The paper describes the Z3146 BCG as an infrared-bright LIRG with a far-infrared SED peak; its star-formation rate is broadly consistent with central X-ray mass deposition.
sources.paper_coordinate_text: Z3146 & 10 23 39.6 & + 04 11 12; These coordinates indicate the positions of the BCGs.

quotes.quote: The exceptions, however, are the BCGs in the three X-ray-brightest clusters in the sample, A1835, Z3146, and A2390. These BCGs have a prominent far-infrared peak in their spectral energy distributions (SEDs), and two of them (those in A1835 and Z3146) can be classified as luminous infrared galaxies (LIRGs: L_ IR >10^ 11 L _ ). Although radio AGNs are found to be prevalent among the BCGs, the infrared luminosities of these three BCGs, judged from the infrared SED signatures, are likely to be powered by star formation. Considering the overall trend that clusters with shorter radiative gas cooling times harbor more infrared-luminous BCGs, the enhanced star formation may be caused by the cooling cluster gas accreting onto the BCGs.

The infrared-derived SFRs for the BCGs in A1835, Z3146, and A2390 are 125, 70, and 5 M _ yr ^ 1 , respectively, while the X-ray-derived mass deposition rates at the cluster center are <20 , 290, and 110 M _ yr ^ 1 , respectively, based on our own estimates. This indicates that the correspondence between the infrared-derived SFRs and X-ray-derived mass deposition rates is currently suggestive at best. Z3146 is the only case where the two quantities are broadly consistent (within a factor of several). A2390 shows a large difference (a factor of 20), which may be related to the strong radio AGN detected in the BCG of this cluster.

gmw_00001287.name: BCG in A2390
gmw_00001287.overview: The brightest cluster galaxy in Abell 2390 is a central cD galaxy [arXiv:1008.1963, arXiv:2403.03974] hosting a strong radio active galactic nucleus [arXiv:astro-ph/0603656] with compact, self-absorbed jets [arXiv:2403.03974]. It contains a massive, asymmetric molecular gas reservoir [arXiv:2210.14922] that forms a heavily one-sided plume [arXiv:2310.16892].

sources.summary: The paper uses Abell 2390 for the cluster’s BCG, finding a 2.2e10 solar-mass molecular reservoir, a one-sided plume, active-nucleus absorption, and possible jet or gravitational origins.

quotes.quote: In this paper we present new observations of the molecular gas in Abell 2390's brightest cluster galaxy, and discuss them in the context of pre-existing optical, X-ray, and low frequency radio observations. Our new data reveals 2.2 10 ^ 10 M _ of molecular gas. Half of this gas is in a heavily one-sided plume which has similar dynamics to the ionised gas seen with optical emission lines. We consider its potential origins, which include an outflowing plume formed as a result of the AGN's historical radio activity, and a plume formed following a gravitational disturbance within the cluster.

Throughout the rest of this paper we use `Abell 2390' in reference the brightest cluster galaxy of the Abell 2390 cluster, unless explicitly mentioned. With observations of stellar absorption lines from the Visible Multi-Object Spectrograph (VIMOS), the redshift of the galaxy is found to be 0.2304 0.0001 , giving a recession velocity of 69074 30 km/s Hamer2016 . The galaxy is located at RA 21:53:36.5 Dec +17:41:45.0.

sources.summary: The paper treats the cD galaxy of A2390 as the central brightest cluster galaxy: X-ray cooling centers on it, it lies 38 arcsec from the straight arc, and its mass contribution has little lensing impact.

quotes.quote: Observations with CHANDRA exhibit a very concentrated and highly peaked x-ray emission, indicating a strong cooling flow which is centered on cluster's central cD galaxy . On large scales, the x-ray morphology has been found to be strongly elliptical with an overall position angle (PA) comparable to the main cluster direction in the optical (PA =133^ ) .

The straight arc of A2390 Among several arcs and arclets, the cluster A2390 exhibits an unusual, strongly lensed straight arc (see Fig. figure0 ) which is located approximately 38'' ( 140 kpc) from the central cD galaxy . This particular arc is unusual in the sense that, as it is both located in the outer core region and adjacent to a lens galaxy lying in between arc and cD galaxy, it would be expected to appear curved with respect to the massive cluster center or the closest galaxy.

A way of compensating for this is to consider an additional contribution due to the central cD galaxy. Following the lines of Ref. , one can estimate a total galaxy mass of approximately M=1.8 10^ 12 M_ . As the central region of A2390 is neither spherically symmetric nor in equilibrium , it is important to note that such an approach has no real physical meaning, but rather offers a convenient way to tweak our cluster model. What does the above mean for our lensing analysis? Modeling the cD galaxy as a point mass, a straightforward calculation shows that its impact on the TeVeS lensing maps can be safely neglected.

sources.summary: The paper identifies A2390 H5 as a lensed z~4 LBG with nebular emission lines, unusually strong high-ionization emission, likely star-forming line widths, and weak absorption.

quotes.quote: A2390 H3 and H5 represent two distinct highly-elongated pairs of lensed images that were spectroscopically confirmed to be at different redshifts by, respectively, frye1998 and pello1999 . The tangential arc system in J1621+0607 was spectroscopically confirmed by bayliss2011 . The gravitational magnification factor is 10 in each case pello1999 .

This is straightforward when nebular emission lines are visible, such as is the case in both J1621+0607 (O iii ] 1661,6) and A2390 H5 (O iii ] 1661,6, He ii 1640, C iv 1548,51). The strong emission from highly ionized species such as He ii and C iv seen in A2390 H5 is uncommon but has been observed in some high redshift starburst galaxies and signifies an extremely young, metal-poor, and hot stellar population (e.g. fosbury2003,erb2010 ). Alternatively they may signify the presence of an active galactic nucleus, but the narrow line widths ( 50-185 FWHM, corrected for instrumental resolution) suggest an origin in star-forming H ii regions.

A2390 H5 reveals weak absorption extending to 600 , seen also in higher-ionization Si iv and C iv lines, although it is only marginally detected at <-300 . The maximum outflow velocity in J1621 is 300 .

sources.summary: The Abell 2390 BCG has a large, asymmetric molecular gas structure and unusually strong, broad absorption suggesting dense gas near the nucleus or outflow.

quotes.quote: Abell 2390 contains a significant molecular mass of 2.2 0.6 10^ 10 M _ , though due to its relatively high redshift the emission is weak and poorly resolved. Nevertheless, the molecular gas is significantly extended across a strongly asymmetric filamentary structure approximately 20 kpc long and 5 kpc wide. Evidence of velocity structure to the emission is observed, with higher velocities being found at larger radii (Fig. fig:velocity_and_dispersion_maps ), perhaps indicating a molecular outflow.

At 15000 M _ /pc ^ 2 , the absorption in Abell 2390 is 92 times stronger than the emission would predict. The absorption also has a dispersion of 52 km/s FWHM = 120 km/s, Rose2019b - extremely wide considering it is produced by just 15000 M _ /pc ^ 2 of gas. That implies the absorbing gas is in a region with a very high velocity gradient.

sources.summary: The paper identifies A2390 18 as an extremely red type 2 quasar with strong X-ray absorption, high intrinsic luminosity, and possible Fe K emission.

quotes.quote: One clear type 2 quasar was presented in f00 and elaborated upon in C02 (source A2390 18 with N_ H =2 10^ 23 cm ^ 2 and intrinsic L_ 2-10 10^ 45 , assuming the standard non-zero Lambda cosmology; see Fig 12 of C02 for the \ ACIS spectrum). This is also the reddest source in our sample, with R-K>7 (Fig fig:rk_z ).

With =2 fixed, the absorption implied is 2.2 [ 1.1, 4.0 ] 10^ 23 cm ^ 2 . In addition, there is an excess of counts close to the energy where a redshifted 6.4 keV (rest-frame) neutral Fe K line is expected to lie. The line is significant at more than 99 per cent according to an f-test, but the preferred rest-energy is 6.48 [ 6.3, 6.6 ] keV. A simple narrow redshifted gaussian line fit to this excess implies a rest-frame equivalent-width 1 keV.

sources.summary: The paper treats the A2390 BCG as an infrared-bright intermediate case with a far-infrared SED peak, modest inferred star formation, and a strong radio AGN.
sources.paper_coordinate_text: A2390 & 21 53 36.7 & + 17 41 45; These coordinates indicate the positions of the BCGs.

quotes.quote: The exceptions, however, are the BCGs in the three X-ray-brightest clusters in the sample, A1835, Z3146, and A2390. These BCGs have a prominent far-infrared peak in their spectral energy distributions (SEDs), and two of them (those in A1835 and Z3146) can be classified as luminous infrared galaxies (LIRGs: L_ IR >10^ 11 L _ ). Although radio AGNs are found to be prevalent among the BCGs, the infrared luminosities of these three BCGs, judged from the infrared SED signatures, are likely to be powered by star formation. Considering the overall trend that clusters with shorter radiative gas cooling times harbor more infrared-luminous BCGs, the enhanced star formation may be caused by the cooling cluster gas accreting onto the BCGs.
A2390 is an intermediate case in that its 15 m luminosity is only slightly excessive ( 5 ), but the 70 m photometry clearly shows that it has a prominent far-infrared peak in its SED.

The infrared-derived SFRs for the BCGs in A1835, Z3146, and A2390 are 125, 70, and 5 M _ yr ^ 1 , respectively, while the X-ray-derived mass deposition rates at the cluster center are <20 , 290, and 110 M _ yr ^ 1 , respectively, based on our own estimates. This indicates that the correspondence between the infrared-derived SFRs and X-ray-derived mass deposition rates is currently suggestive at best. Z3146 is the only case where the two quantities are broadly consistent (within a factor of several). A2390 shows a large difference (a factor of 20), which may be related to the strong radio AGN detected in the BCG of this cluster.

sources.summary: The paper describes A2390 15 as a strongly lensed type 2 quasar candidate with photometric redshift near 2.6-2.8 and dust-absorbed intrinsic ultraviolet luminosity.

quotes.quote: An additional type 2 quasar -- A2390 15, also strongly lensed by the cluster potential -- was published by C02. Determining a spectroscopic redshift for this source with both the Keck in the optical and SUBARU in the near-infrared has proven difficult (see also cowie01 ). Photometric-redshift determination suggests z=2.6-2.8 (but see also lemonon98 ).

C02 presented the broad-band SED for this source, covering the X-ray, optical, NIR, MIR and sub-mm regimes. Based on radiative-transfer modeling through dust using the publicly-available code DUSTY dusty , the presence of an absorbed quasar with optical:X-ray spectral index _ OX =1.3 and intrinsic, lensing-corrected luminosity of 2 10^ 45 erg s ^ 1 was inferred over the ultraviolet regime encompassing the big-blue bump assuming the standard cosmology; see also wfg .

sources.summary: The paper treats A2390 H3 as a z~4 gravitationally lensed LBG, noting its spectroscopic confirmation, magnification, missing nebular lines, and systemic redshift estimate from absorption.

quotes.quote: A2390 H3 and H5 represent two distinct highly-elongated pairs of lensed images that were spectroscopically confirmed to be at different redshifts by, respectively, frye1998 and pello1999 . The tangential arc system in J1621+0607 was spectroscopically confirmed by bayliss2011 . The gravitational magnification factor is 10 in each case pello1999 .

No appropriate features are detected in the spectrum of A2390 H3 and so we estimate the systemic redshift from low-ionization absorption lines using the method of jones2012 . This gives z = 4.043 0.002 = z_ IS + 190 , with uncertainty dominated by an rms difference 125 between redshifts derived from absorption lines and that obtained from nebular emission steidel2010 . The adopted systemic arc redshifts are listed in Table tab:arcs .

sources.summary: The paper describes the Abell 2390 BCG as a cooling-cluster central galaxy with recent and older radio activity, compact self-absorbed jets, giant radio lobes, gas plume, and broad absorption.

quotes.quote: Abell 2390 Galaxy and environment The brightest cluster galaxy in Abell 2390 lies in an X-ray luminous galaxy cluster with a cooling rate of 200-300 M _ yr ^ 1 Allen2001 . The central continuum source has a flat light curve dating back to at least 2013, but recent activity is indicated by high angular resolution observations Augusto2006,Rose2020 . These show young, compact and self-absorbed jets, with half the flux coming from synchrotron emission and half from dust at 850 m Edge1999, Augusto2006 . Significantly older radio activity is indicated by X-ray cavities in the cluster's hot atmosphere, as well as 300-600 kpc wide radio lobes Savini2019 .

gmw_00001288.name: NGC 4567/4568
gmw_00001288.overview: NGC 4567/4568 is an early-stage interacting galaxy pair located in the Virgo Cluster [arXiv:2303.17638]. The system features an overlapping-region molecular filament that traces a quiet collision front without dense gas or imminent star formation [arXiv:2303.17638].

sources.summary: The paper studies NGC 4567/4568 as an early-stage interacting pair, finding an overlapping-region molecular filament tracing a quiet collision front without dense gas or imminent star formation.

quotes.quote: The interacting galaxy pair in the Virgo Cluster NGC 4567/4568 is one of the best targets for investigating tidally-driven star formation. The galaxy pair is likely in the early stage of collision and probably experiencing its first encounter Kaneko13 , as evidenced by its weakly disturbed morphology and relatively large projected nuclear separation (5.6 kpc). Furthermore, the close proximity (16 Mpc: ) and small inclination of both constituent galaxies ( 44D for NGC 4567 and 58D for NGC 4568) enable us to study the details of the inner structure at a high linear resolution. The basic properties of the galaxy pair are summarised in table Table1 . The molecular gas fraction (the fraction of molecular hydrogen in the sum of the surface density of molecular and atomic hydrogen) in the overlapping region is 5-15 higher than that in the southern disc of NGC 4568, which has the same surface density of total (i.e., H _ 2 +H I ) gas.

We investigated the physical state of molecular gas in the overlapping region by observing CO( J = 1--0) and CO( J = 3--2) in an early-stage interacting galaxy pair NGC 4567/4568 with ALMA. The summary of this paper is as follows: Diffuse gas tracer CO( J = 1--0) and warmer and denser gas tracer CO( J = 3--2) are detected in the overlapping region of this galaxy pair. We confirm the existence of a filamentary structure of CO( J = 1--0) emission, which is likely tracing the collision front. The peak of CO( J = 3--2) emission, on the other hand, is not at the filamentary structure. We identified 114 molecular clouds using CO( J = 1--0) data.

gmw_00001289.name: J1204+0221
gmw_00001289.overview: J1204+0221 is a foreground quasar with a systemic redshift of z=2.4358 [arXiv:1510.06018]. The object has a bolometric luminosity of 10^46.2 erg/s and serves as a representative system for larger statistical samples [arXiv:1510.06018].

sources.summary: The paper treats J1204+0221 as a representative foreground quasar pair, giving its foreground redshift and bolometric luminosity and using it to motivate QPQ8 selection criteria.

quotes.quote: In terms of the spectral and photometric properties of the foreground quasar, the single sightline studied in QPQ3, J1204+0221, is unremarkable. J1204+0221FG has a systemic redshift of z=2.4358 , while the median of the QPQ8 sample is z=2.4 . J1204+0221FG has a bolometric luminosity of 10^ 46.2 erg s^ 1 . Thus, J1204+0221 is representative of the larger statistical sample, and it is fair for us to rely on QPQ3 to form the selection criteria for QPQ8.

gmw_00001290.name: NGC 4672
gmw_00001290.overview: NGC 4672 is a non-isolated galaxy that belongs to a group of galaxies [arXiv:1511.05416]. It is classified as a rare polar-bulge galaxy, featuring a morphologically decoupled bulge associated with an orthogonally rotating core [arXiv:1511.05416].

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 4672 as a rare polar-bulge galaxy whose morphologically decoupled bulge is tied to an orthogonally rotating core, and notes its group environment.

quotes.quote: The polar bulges are extremely rare. In a recent review devoted to the shape of galactic bulges, M endez-Abreu (2015) mentions only three such galaxies: NGC 4698 (Bertola et al. 1999; Corsini et al. 2012), NGC 4672 (Sarzi et al. 2000), and UGC 10043 (Matthews and de Grijs 2004). One of the reasons for the small number of known polar bulges is that they can be identified only in the case where the galactic disk is close to the edge-on orientation to the line of sight and therefore, the bulge inclination to the disk plane can be easily detected. If this is not the case, then the bulge looks like a bar and does not attract the attention of researchers. Given the small number of known objects, the systematics of properties and the origin of the polar bulges remain puzzles.

In two of the three galaxies (NGC 4698 and NGC 4672) the morphologically decoupled bulges are known to be related to the kinematically decoupled subsystems in their cores that rotate almost orthogonally to the galactic disks (Bertola et al. 1999; Sarzi et al. 2000).

UGC 10043 ( 7 in Table 1) is a member of a group, and it interacts with the galaxy MCG+04-37-035 (Aguirre et al. 2009). Two other galaxies (NGC 4672 and NGC 4698; see Introduction) are not isolated either. NGC 4672 is a member of a group of galaxies (Garcia 1993); NGC 4698 is a member of the Virgo cluster of galaxies.

gmw_00001291.name: UGC 10043
gmw_00001291.overview: UGC 10043 is an edge-on spiral galaxy [arXiv:1808.08525] that features a rare polar bulge [arXiv:1511.05416] and interacts with the neighboring galaxy MCG+04-37-035 [arXiv:1511.05416]. It exhibits widespread star formation along its disc [arXiv:1711.07844] and hosts a galactic wind powered by central star formation [arXiv:1808.08525, arXiv:1711.07844].

sources.summary: The paper describes UGC 10043 as an edge-on spiral with core star formation, a wind-related extended emission structure, and ionized-gas kinematics constraining its galactic wind.

quotes.quote: Galactic Wind in UGC 10043 UGC 10043 is an edge-on spiral galaxy. Observations in the H and [ N II ] lines carried out with the HST Matt2004 have shown signs of star formation in the galactic core, as well as an extended emission structure that is perpendicular to the disk and is the result of the galactic wind influence. presented diagnostic diagrams for the central part of the galaxy according to the CALIFA survey. Some of the points on the diagram relating to the central region of star formation turned out to be located in the region characteristic of photoionization by young stars, while others fell into the region typical of shock excitation. Within the framework of a shock excitation model, a wind velocity was constrained: no greater than 400 . Analyzing the gas velocity field in the \ line constructed with a scanning FPI allowed us to obtain a more strict limitation on the galactic wind velocity: less than 250 in accordance with the gas shock excitation model.

sources.summary: The paper identifies UGC 10043 as a rare polar-bulge galaxy, discusses the lack of a detected kinematically decoupled core, and notes its group membership and interaction.

quotes.quote: The polar bulges are extremely rare. In a recent review devoted to the shape of galactic bulges, M endez-Abreu (2015) mentions only three such galaxies: NGC 4698 (Bertola et al. 1999; Corsini et al. 2012), NGC 4672 (Sarzi et al. 2000), and UGC 10043 (Matthews and de Grijs 2004). One of the reasons for the small number of known polar bulges is that they can be identified only in the case where the galactic disk is close to the edge-on orientation to the line of sight and therefore, the bulge inclination to the disk plane can be easily detected. If this is not the case, then the bulge looks like a bar and does not attract the attention of researchers. Given the small number of known objects, the systematics of properties and the origin of the polar bulges remain puzzles.

No kinematically decoupled core was detected in UGC 10043 (Matthews and de Grijs 2004), although as the authors note, this could be hindered by the limited spatial resolution of the spectra they used and by the powerful absorption stripe that shields the core.

UGC 10043 ( 7 in Table 1) is a member of a group, and it interacts with the galaxy MCG+04-37-035 (Aguirre et al. 2009). Two other galaxies (NGC 4672 and NGC 4698; see Introduction) are not isolated either. NGC 4672 is a member of a group of galaxies (Garcia 1993); NGC 4698 is a member of the Virgo cluster of galaxies.

sources.summary: The paper uses UGC 10043 as an edge-on CALIFA example with strong disc SFc, extraplanar SFc-like emission, a nuclear bicone, and galactic-wind signatures from central star formation.

quotes.quote: The galaxy in the bottom row (CALIFA 0811, UGC 10043) differs from the others in Fig. \ fig:ExampleMapsEdgeOn in having much more SFc along its disc, as well as SFc-like extra-planar emission over the disc, and a bipolar cone of intermediate W_ values centred in the nucleus. This galaxy has been recently studied by LopezCoba.etal.2017 , who find the emission-line ratios and kinematics along the biconical structure to be consistent with a galactic wind powered by a central SF event. This combination of shock ionization and the widespread SF along its disc explains why there are so little signs of hDIG emission in this galaxy, although it is curious that W_ does drop close to hDIG values in the inner parts of the bicone.

gmw_00001292.name: J001605--234352
gmw_00001292.overview: J001605--234352 is a radio galaxy hosted by a disrupted, edge-on blue spiral galaxy [arXiv:1204.1391]. Located within the Abell 14 galaxy cluster, it exhibits a two-component neutral hydrogen absorption feature [arXiv:1204.1391].

sources.summary: The paper reports a new H I absorption detection in radio galaxy J001605--234352, describes a two-component line profile, and identifies its disrupted edge-on blue spiral host.

quotes.quote: J001605--234352: We detect a previously unknown H i absorption feature with a peak observed optical depth of 0.169 0.007 and an integrated optical depth of 16.9 0.7 km s ^ 1 . include this source in their sample of radio galaxies with compact cores, observed using the Very Large Array. However, they do not include it in their search for 21 cm H i absorption since the unresolved continuum flux density (255 mJy) did not meet their selection criterion of greater than 350 mJy.

The absorption line feature is best fit by two Gaussians, with a narrow component ( v _ FWHM = 64.5 4.4 km s ^ 1 ), located at the spectroscopic optical redshift, and a single broad component ( v _ FWHM = 138 23 km s ^ 1 ), which is slightly redshifted by 96 46 km s ^ 1 . The host galaxy is classified as a blue spiral UK Schmidt Telescope, Automated Photographic Measuring Bright Galaxy Catalogue; Loveday:1996 located within the galaxy cluster Abell 14 Savage:1976, Dressler:1980 . Fig. figure:source_results shows the blue optical image at the position of the target source UK Schmidt Telescope, SuperCosmos Survey; Hambly:2001 , with an edge-on spiral galaxy that exhibits some disruption at the edges.

gmw_00001293.name: J205401--424238
gmw_00001293.overview: J205401--424238 is an elliptical radio galaxy hosting an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1204.1391, arXiv:1211.3452]. Observations reveal a broad, weak neutral hydrogen absorption line [arXiv:1204.1391, arXiv:1211.3452], which is redshifted and may indicate gas infalling toward the central nucleus [arXiv:1211.3452].

sources.summary: The paper reports a new broad, shallow H I absorption feature in J205401--424238 and interprets it as gas near an AGN in an elliptical radio-source host.

quotes.quote: J205401--424238: We detect a previously unknown H i absorption feature with a peak optical depth of 0.028 0.004 and an integrated optical depth of 13.4 2.0 km s ^ 1 . This is the least significant of our three detections, however the single-component Gaussian fitting returns a significant relative log-probability of 26.3 0.1 for the spectral line hypothesis, and has a position that is consistent with the spectroscopic optical redshift. We note that this feature is apparent throughout the observation, in both polarisation feeds, and does not appear in spectra that are extracted from spatial positions significantly off the source.

The single component is shallow and broad ( v _ FWHM = 520 140 km s ^ 1 ) producing a relatively low peak, but high integrated, optical depth. The radio source is signified by a bright, flat spectrum, and was observed in the Combined Radio All-Sky Targeted Eight GHz Survey CRATES; Healey:2007 . The host galaxy is classified as an elliptical by Loveday:1996 , which is consistent with the optical image from the UK Schmidt Telescope shown in Fig. figure:source_results . That we detect only a single broad, shallow component at 21 cm suggests that we might be seeing only the absorption from gas close to the AGN, and nothing from the gas-poor elliptical host.

sources.summary: The paper verifies broad, weak associated H i 21 cm absorption toward the compact radio source PMN J2054--4242, finding high column density gas redshifted toward its central active nucleus.

quotes.quote: We report conclusive verification of the detection of associated H i 21 cm absorption in the early-type host galaxy of the compact radio source PMN J2054--4242. We estimate an effective spectral line velocity width of 418 20 km s ^ 1 and observed peak optical depth of 2.5 0.2 per cent, making this one of the broadest and weakest 21 cm absorption lines yet detected. For T_ spin /f > 100 K the atomic neutral hydrogen column density is N_ HI 2 10^ 21 cm ^ 2 . The observed spectral line profile is redshifted by 187 46 km s ^ 1 , with respect to the optical spectroscopic measurement, perhaps indicating that the H i gas is infalling towards the central active galactic nucleus.

gmw_00001294.name: UGCA 307
gmw_00001294.overview: UGCA 307 is a dwarf galaxy with an apparent B magnitude of 14.6 [arXiv:2008.13152]. Distance measurements indicate that the galaxy is a satellite companion of the Sombrero galaxy [arXiv:2008.13152].

sources.summary: The paper measures a TRGB distance for dwarf galaxy UGCA 307, gives its coordinates, magnitude and velocity, and finds it consistent with being a Sombrero satellite.

quotes.quote: In this work we present measurements of TRGB distances for two more dwarf galaxies, UGCA 307 (or DDO 153) and KKSG 30 (or LEDA 3097708) situated close to Sombrero. The distances of both the galaxies agree well with their belonging to the family of Sombrero satellites. The new distance measurements together with other less reliable distance estimates give us a possibility to make more precise value of the virial (orbital) mass of Sombrero. The dwarf galaxies UGCA 307 ( 12^h53^m56 8 - 12 06 21 ) and KKSG 30 ( 12^h37^m35 9 - 08 52 01 ) have apparent B magnitudes of 14 6 and 16 3 , respectively, and projected separations of 3 with respect to Sombrero.

Their radial velocities in the Local Group rest frame, 731 km s ^ 1 (UGCA 307) and 918 km s ^ 1 (KKSG 30), are close to the radial velocity of Sombrero, 892 km s ^ 1 . The galaxies were observed with the Advanced Camera for Surveys (ACS) aboard the Hubble Space Telescope (HST) on December 5, 2019, and March 13, 2020, as a part of the SNAP project 15922 (PI R.B. Tully). Two exposures for each object were made in a single orbit with the filters F606W (750 s) and F814W (750 s).

We found F814W (TRGB) to be 25.76^ +0.19 _ 0.11 for UGCA 307 and 25.96^ +0.08 _ 0.07 for KKSG 30. Following the zero-point calibration of the absolute magnitude of the TRGB developed by Rizzi et al. (2007), we obtained M(TRGB) values of --4.09 (UGCA 307) and --4.08 (KKSG 30).

gmw_00001295.name: KKSG 30
gmw_00001295.overview: KKSG 30 is a dwarf galaxy with an apparent B magnitude of 16.3 [arXiv:2008.13152]. Its radial velocity and distance measurements indicate that it is a satellite companion of the Sombrero galaxy [arXiv:2008.13152].

sources.summary: The paper measures a TRGB distance for dwarf galaxy KKSG 30, gives its coordinates, magnitude and velocity, and finds it consistent with being a Sombrero satellite.

quotes.quote: In this work we present measurements of TRGB distances for two more dwarf galaxies, UGCA 307 (or DDO 153) and KKSG 30 (or LEDA 3097708) situated close to Sombrero. The distances of both the galaxies agree well with their belonging to the family of Sombrero satellites. The new distance measurements together with other less reliable distance estimates give us a possibility to make more precise value of the virial (orbital) mass of Sombrero. The dwarf galaxies UGCA 307 ( 12^h53^m56 8 - 12 06 21 ) and KKSG 30 ( 12^h37^m35 9 - 08 52 01 ) have apparent B magnitudes of 14 6 and 16 3 , respectively, and projected separations of 3 with respect to Sombrero.

Their radial velocities in the Local Group rest frame, 731 km s ^ 1 (UGCA 307) and 918 km s ^ 1 (KKSG 30), are close to the radial velocity of Sombrero, 892 km s ^ 1 . The galaxies were observed with the Advanced Camera for Surveys (ACS) aboard the Hubble Space Telescope (HST) on December 5, 2019, and March 13, 2020, as a part of the SNAP project 15922 (PI R.B. Tully). Two exposures for each object were made in a single orbit with the filters F606W (750 s) and F814W (750 s).

We found F814W (TRGB) to be 25.76^ +0.19 _ 0.11 for UGCA 307 and 25.96^ +0.08 _ 0.07 for KKSG 30. Following the zero-point calibration of the absolute magnitude of the TRGB developed by Rizzi et al. (2007), we obtained M(TRGB) values of --4.09 (UGCA 307) and --4.08 (KKSG 30).

gmw_00001296.name: KKSG 29
gmw_00001296.overview: KKSG 29 is a dwarf companion galaxy of the Sombrero galaxy [arXiv:2008.13152]. Its distance of 9.82 Mpc was accurately determined using the tip of the red giant branch method [arXiv:2008.13152].

sources.summary: The paper uses KKSG 29 as an accurately measured Sombrero satellite whose distance helps constrain which neighboring galaxies belong to the Sombrero system.

quotes.quote: At present there is only one galaxy, KKSG 29, in the Sombrero vicinity with an accurately measured distance (9.82 0.32 Mpc; Karachentsev et al. 2018), determined via the tip of the red giant branch (TRGB) method. This distance places the dwarf galaxy KKSG 29 as a physical satellite of the Sombrero galaxy. In this work we present measurements of TRGB distances for two more dwarf galaxies, UGCA 307 (or DDO 153) and KKSG 30 (or LEDA 3097708) situated close to Sombrero.

Using radial velocities and projected separations of UGCA 307, KKSG 30, and a third galaxy with an accurate distance (KKSG 29), as well as 12 other assumed companions with less accurate distances, the total mass of M 104 is estimated to be (1.55 0.49) 10^ 13 M_ .

gmw_00001297.name: UM 664
gmw_00001297.overview: UM 664 is a high-redshift quasar discovered at a redshift of 2.55 [arXiv:1307.5864]. Its sightline features a damped Lyman-alpha system at a redshift of 2.402 [arXiv:1307.5864, arXiv:0801.3682], which represents the lowest-metallicity system with detected molecular hydrogen [arXiv:0801.3682].

sources.summary: The paper treats UM 664 as a high-redshift quasar sightline, using VLT/UVES observations of its DLA H2 absorption to constrain possible variation in the proton-to-electron mass ratio.

quotes.quote: Here we report a detailed analysis of 2 absorption in z =2.4018 DLA towards \ Noterdaeme07lf using the Ultraviolet and Visual Echelle Spectrograph mounted on the Very Large Telescope (VLT/UVES) spectra taken as part of the UVES large programme for testing the fundamental physics Molaro13 . \ (UM 664) with a redshift z_ em = 2.55 and an -band magnitude of 18.05 was discovered by MacAlpine82 as part of their search for high redshift quasars. The optical spectroscopic observations of \ were carried out using VLT/UVES Dekker00 Unit Telescope (UT2) 8.2-m telescope at Paranal (Chile) [ as part of ESO Large Programme 185.A-0745 `` The UVES Large Program for testing Fundamental Physics '' Molaro13 ] . All observations were performed using the standard beam splitter with the dichroic 2 (setting 390+580) that covers roughly from 330 nm to 450 nm on the BLUE CCD and from 465 nm to 578 nm and from 583 nm to 680 nm on the two RED CCDs.
The observations are comprised of 19 exposures totalling 33.3 hours of exposure time in three different observing cycles started in 2010 and finished in 2012.

sources.summary: The paper presents this DLA as the lowest-metallicity H2 detection in the sample, with a single component reaching high rotational levels and changing Doppler parameter.

quotes.quote: Q 0027 1836, =2.402 : This system has the lowest metallicity ( [ X/H ] =-1.63 ) of systems in which H _2 molecules have been detected. H _2 is detected in a single component up to rotational level J=5 and possibly 6. Thanks to the high data quality, we detected an increase in the Doppler parameter b , from low to high rotational levels, for the first time at high redshift Noterdaeme07lf .

gmw_00001298.name: NGC 4625
gmw_00001298.overview: NGC 4625 is a peculiar spiral galaxy [arXiv:1801.04328] characterized by an extended ultraviolet disk [arXiv:1206.4104, arXiv:2105.13394] and multiple ragged spiral arms [arXiv:1206.4104]. Its star formation is nearly uniformly distributed across the disk [arXiv:1206.4104], showing signatures of late-stage, inside-out disk growth [arXiv:2105.13394].

sources.summary: NGC 4625 is described as an extended-UV, H I-rich Magellanic spiral with ragged arms, nearly uniform star formation, quiescent velocity dispersion, and little star-formation impact on its neutral ISM.
sources.paper_coordinate_text: NGC 4625 in the northeast corner is located at (R.A. = 12 ^ h 41 ^ m 52 ^ s , dec = 41 ^ 16'18'' [ J2000 ] )

quotes.quote: In addition, recent GALEX observations reveal NGC 4625 to have an extended UV disk as well as multiple branching, ragged spiral arms. NGC 4625 has been noted to have one of the largest UV to optical disk (as measured by D_ 25 ) ratios of 4 (Gil de Paz et al. 2005) and an even larger H I to optical disk ratio of 9.8 (Bush Wilcots 2004). It is remarkably symmetric and Bush Wilcots (2004) noted that it seems unaffected by its apparent interaction with NGC 4618. From its morphology alone, NGC 4618 appears to be a prototypical Magellanic spiral, with an obvious stellar bar and a lopsided one-armed spiral structure.
