Both galaxies are host to on-going star formation; in NGC 4618 most of the H I regions lie near the central bar, while in NGC 4625 the star formation is nearly uniformly distributed across its disk. The goals of this work are to re-examine the issue of whether these two galaxies are indeed interacting and what effect such an interaction may be having on both NGC 4618 and NGC 4625, particularly with regard to star formation.

The H I velocity dispersion in NGC 4625 is nearly uniform across the entire disk at a value of 10 km s ^ 1 with little variation between regions with and without UV emission. Combined with the lack of noticeable H I holes, this suggests that the current and recent star formation in NGC 4625 is having little or no impact on the neutral ISM in the galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper measures a new deep CO upper limit for an XUV-disk star-forming region in NGC 4625 and uses additional outer-disk regions to test star-formation relations.

quotes.quote: We analyzed published CO measurements and upper limits for fifteen star forming regions in the XUV or outer disk of three nearby spiral galaxies and a new CO upper limit from the IRAM 30 m telescope in one star forming region at =3.4 r_ 25 in the XUV disk of NGC 4625.

We present a deep upper limit on the molecular gas content in one star forming region in the XUV disk of NGC 4625 ( D = 9.5 Mpc ; Schruba et al. \ 2011; Figure image ). We used the same naming convention as gildepaz07 and refer to this region as R2. We selected NGC 4625 because it has good ancillary datasets to trace star formation in the XUV disk and we selected R2 for the CO observations because we hypothesized that the relatively high metallicity 12+ log(O/H) = 8.5 ; gildepaz07 would enhance the likelihood of a CO detection.
Finally, we studied ten additional star forming regions in NGC 4625 (R3 - R12) that have shallow upper limits on the CO intensity from the HERACLES survey leroy09 .

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 4625 as an extended-UV disk galaxy whose outer UV, H-alpha, H I, and young-population signatures point to late-stage inside-out disk growth.

quotes.quote: NGC 4625 NGC 4625 was one of the first galaxies to be classified as having an extended UV (XUV) disc gildepaz2005,thilker2007 . The UV and H emission that persists far outside of D _ 25 combined with elevated HI surface densities (Figure fig:ngc4618_4panel ) and young t_ ySP 's (Figure fig:ngc4625_sfh ) in the same region points to late stage disc growth elmegreen_hunter2006,bush2014 . According to the color profile (Figure fig:ngc4625_fulldata ) and the radial SFHs (Figure fig:ngc4625_sfh ), the oldest stellar population in NGC4625 resides near the D _ 25 isophote. Because of the clear difference between its inner and outer regions, NGC 4625 is a prime example of a galaxy with an "inside-out" growth signature.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4625 as a peculiar blue-SED spiral with only a small modeled AGN fraction despite a previous Seyfert classification.

quotes.quote: NGC 4625 : NGC 4625 is a peculiar spiral with a blue SED. CIGALE fits f_ AGN 3 . ver10 classified it as a Seyfert galaxy (type unknown), but L13 did not define an AGN contribution for NGC 4625. Nothing in the SED requires an AGN.

gmw_00001299.name: NGC 6958
gmw_00001299.overview: NGC 6958 is an isolated, massive early-type galaxy [arXiv:1911.11491] that exhibits extended mid-infrared emission following a de Vaucouleurs profile [arXiv:astro-ph/0006328]. The galaxy hosts a regularly rotating nuclear molecular gas disk [arXiv:1911.11491] and displays a low ratio of far-infrared to blue luminosity [arXiv:astro-ph/0006328].

sources.summary: The paper reports extended mid-infrared emission in NGC 6958, a de Vaucouleurs profile, [CII] detection, low far-infrared-to-blue luminosity, and old-population UV heating consistent within uncertainties.

quotes.quote: With the exception of the 15 image of NGC 1052, the mid-IR emission from NGC 1052, NGC 1155, NGC 5866 and NGC 6958 at 4.5, 7 and 15 show extended emission. Mid-IR emission from NGC 1052, NGC 1155, and NGC 6958 follows a de Vaucouleurs profile. The ratio of 15/7 flux decreases with radius in these galaxies, approaching the values empirically observed for purely stellar systems.

All the galaxies are detected in the [ CII ] (158 ) line, and the S0s NGC 1155 and NGC 5866 are detected in the [ OI ] (63 ) line as well. Previous detections of neutral ISM are sparse: only NGC 1052 had been detected in \ and NGC 5866 in CO. The ISO-LWS observations of the [ CII ] line are more sensitive measures of cool, neutral ISM than \ and CO by about a factor of 10-100. Comparison of [ CII ] with H shows that [ CII ] does not arise from HII regions and therefore must arise in the neutral medium. Three of four early type galaxies, namely NGC 1052, NGC 6958 and NGC 5866, have low ratios of far-infrared to blue luminosity and show a lower , which is explained by postulating a softer radiation field from old stellar populations in early type galaxies, compared to spirals and irregulars, where young stars are present.

sources.summary: The paper targets the early-type galaxy NGC 6958 to compare stellar and molecular-gas dynamical black-hole mass estimates, finding consistent values and a slightly over-massive black hole.

quotes.quote: We present our ongoing work of using two independent tracers to estimate the supermassive black hole mass in the nearby early-type galaxy NGC 6958; namely integrated stellar and molecular gas kinematics. We used data from the Atacama Large Millimeter/submillimeter Array (ALMA), and the adaptive-optics assisted Multi-Unit Spectroscopic Explorer (MUSE) and constructed state-of-the-art dynamical models. The different methods provide black hole masses of (2.89 2.05) 10^8M_ from stellar kinematics and (1.35 0.09) 10^8M_ from molecular gas kinematics which are consistent within their 3 uncertainties. Compared to recent M _ BH _ e scaling relations, we derive a slightly over-massive black hole.

In this work, we have targeted the isolated ( ) massive early-type galaxy NGC 6958 from the WISDOM survey ( ). The galaxy shows clear signs of a regularly rotating nuclear molecular gas disk and regular stellar rotation within 10 '' from the center. As such, NGC 6958 is a good case to test and compare different dynamical tracers.

gmw_00001300.name: source ID 150008
gmw_00001300.overview: GLASS 150008 is a galaxy at redshift z=6.23 [arXiv:2307.00710, arXiv:2301.07126] with a young stellar population [arXiv:2301.07126] and star-formation-like ionization [arXiv:2307.00710]. It exhibits a high specific star formation rate [arXiv:2301.07126], super-solar nitrogen-to-oxygen ratios [arXiv:2307.00710, arXiv:2405.01823], and no active galactic nucleus signatures [arXiv:2307.00710].

sources.summary: The paper pinpoints GLASS 150008 as a z=6.23 high-redshift galaxy with low C/N, super-solar N/O, star-formation-like ionization, and no current AGN signatures.

quotes.quote: Among the 70 galaxies, we have pinpointed 2 galaxies, CEERS 01019 at z=8.68 and GLASS 150008 at z=6.23 , with extremely low C/N ( [ C/N ] 1 ), evidenced with ] 1907,1909, ] 1750, and ] 1483,1486, which show high N/O ratios ( [ N/O ] 0.5 ) comparable with the one of GN-z11. CEERS 01019 (GLASS 150008) has (does not have) a strong high ionization line that is explained by AGN (star-formation) photoionization models, which is (not) similar to GN-z11. Such low C/N and high N/O ratios found in CEERS 01019 and GLASS 150008 (additionally identified in GN-z11) are close to the equilibrium of the CNO cycle, suggesting that these 3 galaxies are enriched by metals processed by the CNO cycle.

We have confirmed that our derived values of C/O and 12+ ( O/H) for GLASS 150008 are consistent with those measured by Jones2023arXiv within 1 level. We find that GLASS 150008 has a super-solar N/O value of ( N/O)=-0.40^ +0.05 _ 0.07 as predicted in Figure fig:n3o3 . The derived properties of GLASS 150008 are listed in Table tab:hlt .

It should be noted that GLASS 150008 does not have very high ionization lines that require the presence of AGN. We have also checked that GLASS 150008 does not have a clear broad component in the H line profile.

sources.summary: The paper presents source ID 150008 as a z=6.23 GLASS-JWST galaxy with low C/O, young stellar population, high specific star formation rate, and candidate Lyman-break selection.

quotes.quote: We present a measurement of (C/O) = -1.01 0.12 (stat) 0.10 (sys) in a z=6.23 galaxy observed as part of the GLASS-JWST Early Release Science Program. Notably, we achieve good precision thanks to the detection of the rest-frame ultraviolet , , and \ emission lines delivered by /NIRSpec. The C/O abundance is 0.8 dex lower than the solar value and is consistent with the expected yield from core-collapse supernovae, indicating negligible carbon enrichment from intermediate-mass stars. This in turn implies rapid buildup of a young stellar population with age 100 Myr in a galaxy seen 900 million years after the Big Bang. Our chemical abundance analysis is consistent with spectral energy distribution modeling of /NIRCam photometric data, which indicates a current stellar mass M _* / = 8.4^ +0.4 _ 0.2 and specific star formation rate sSFR 20 Gyr ^ 1 .

This target (source ID 150008 in the GLASS NIRSpec target catalog and ID 2649 in the Stage 1 photometric catalog described by paris2023 ; RA, Dec = 3.6025240, 30.4192187 degrees) was originally included in the NIRSpec observations as a candidate z>5 galaxy based on photometry indicating a Lyman break. We visually inspected the GLASS-JWST spectra of known candidate z>5 sources for suitability of C/O abundance measurements and selected this as the best example based on clear detection of the necessary rest-UV lines.

sources.summary: The paper presents the reduced GLASS150008 spectrum and reports significantly high helium abundance together with large N/O values.

quotes.quote: The reduced spectrum of GLASS150008 is shown in Figure fig:spectra .
RXCJ2248-ID and GLASS150008 show the helium abundances that are significantly higher than those of the local galaxies.
GLASS150008 and RXCJ2248-ID, which show the helium overabundance in Figure fig:HeH-OH , also have large N/O values.

gmw_00001301.name: UGC 10858
gmw_00001301.overview: UGC 10858 is an inclined spiral galaxy with a redshift of z=0.01815 [arXiv:2112.14819]. Its spectrum reveals signatures of active star formation, and its disk hosted the sibling supernovae SN 2018big and SN 2019nvm [arXiv:2112.14819].

sources.summary: The paper identifies UGC 10858 as the inclined spiral host of two sibling supernovae, notes star-formation signatures and redshift from its SDSS spectrum, and compares supernova-derived distances.

quotes.quote: The parent galaxy of Type Ia SN 2018big and Type II P SN 2019nvm is UGC 10858, a spiral oriented at high inclination, in which both SN 2018big and 2019nvm appear to be embedded in the disk with a moderate offset from the host center (Figure fig:stamps ). The SDSS spectrum for UGC 10858 reveals signatures of star formation, and is the source of the redshift of z=0.01815 2020ApJS..249....3A . Type Ia and IIP SNe come from older and younger progenitor stars, respectively, and since both stellar populations are present in the disks of spiral galaxies it is not surprising to find both types of SNe in the same host.

Finally, we can make a direct comparison of the two SN-derived distances for the parent galaxy: _ Ia = 34.824 0.16 mag and _ IIP = 34.95 0.26 mag. These two values are discrepant by only 0.126 mag ( < 1 ), and their combined errors are 0.30 mag, so these particular distances have very good agreement within 0.4 .

gmw_00001302.name: IC 4397
gmw_00001302.overview: IC 4397 is a nearly face-on spiral galaxy located in the Coma Supercluster [arXiv:2112.14819]. It is classified as a Seyfert 2 active galactic nucleus with star-forming H II regions in its central spectrum [arXiv:2112.14819].

sources.summary: The paper presents IC 4397 as a nearly face-on spiral and Seyfert 2 host of sibling supernovae, with redshift, brightness, active nucleus, star-formation lines, and differing supernova locations.

quotes.quote: The parent galaxy of Type Ib/ SN 2018dbg and Type IIP SN 2019hyk, IC 4397, is a nearly face-on spiral galaxy (Figure fig:host_IC4397 , and member of the Coma Supercluster 2010A&A...518A..10V . IC 4397 has an observed redshift of z=0.014737 2002LEDA.........0P , an apparent brightness of 13.04 mag in the SDSS band 2005AJ....129.1755A , and is classified as an AGN with an active H II nucleus by 2010A&A...518A..10V . Specifically, IC 4397 is a Seyfert 2 galaxy, based on the infrared properties of its nucleus 1987ApJ...321..233E,2001ApJ...557...39P,2007AJ....134.2006R .

The SDSS spectrum reveals narrow H , [ N II ] and [ S II ] , the classic signatures of star formation SDSS DR14, 2018ApJS..235...42A . As discussed in Section ssec:sibs_19ehk20oi , the central regions of spiral galaxies have the younger stellar populations and/or higher stellar densities which could be more efficient at forming the high mass binary stars which are the progenitors of SNe Ib/. As expected, SN Ib/ 2018dbg is much closer to the core of the host than SN IIP 2019hyk, which is in the outskirts of the host.

gmw_00001303.name: GRB 100117A host galaxy
gmw_00001303.overview: The GRB 100117A host galaxy is a compact early-type galaxy located at a redshift of z = 0.915 [arXiv:1012.4009]. It features an old stellar population with a low star formation rate of less than 0.1 solar masses per year [arXiv:1012.4009].

sources.summary: The host is described as a compact early-type galaxy at z=0.915 with an old stellar population, low star formation, and sub-L-star luminosity.

quotes.quote: Follow-up optical and near-IR observations uncover a coincident compact red galaxy, identified as an early-type galaxy at a photometric redshift of z 0.6-0.9 ( 2 ) with a mass of 3 10^ 10 M _ , an age of 1 Gyr, and a luminosity of L _ B 0.5 L _ * . Spectroscopic observations of the host reveal a notable break corresponding to the Balmer/4000 \ break at z 0.9 , and stellar population spectral evolution template fits indicate z 0.915 , which we adopt as the redshift of the host, with stellar population ages of 1-3 Gyr. From a possible weak detection of [ O 2 ] 3727 emission at z=0.915 we infer an upper bound on the star formation rate of 0.1 M _ yr ^ 1 , leading to a specific star formation rate of 0.004 Gyr ^ 1 .

gmw_00001304.name: S5 0836+710
gmw_00001304.overview: S5 0836+710 is a luminous, high-redshift flat-spectrum radio quasar [arXiv:1307.0529, arXiv:1908.06644] powered by a supermassive black hole [arXiv:1307.0529, arXiv:astro-ph/0612722] and an exceptionally luminous active nucleus [arXiv:2003.01397]. The galaxy hosts a kiloparsec-scale radio jet [arXiv:1502.03741, arXiv:1307.0529] that exhibits helical oscillations [arXiv:1502.03741, arXiv:1308.6168] and frequent, luminous gamma-ray flaring [arXiv:1502.07227, arXiv:2401.14872].

sources.summary: The paper revisits 0836+710, finding predominantly longitudinal jet magnetic field behavior, oblique or offset polarization, and a monotonic transverse RM gradient near 5 mas.

quotes.quote: 0836+710 VLBA observations of 0836+710 at 8 frequencies near 5 and 8 GHz obtained in 2003 are presented by Asada et al. (2010), who found the projected magnetic field to be roughly parallel to the jet direction. They also detected a clear transverse RM gradient about 3--4 mas from the core. Figure fig:pmaps1 shows our 7.9 GHz polarization map for 0836+710. As observed previously, the jet EVPAs are primarily perpendicular to the jet, indicating a predominantly longitudinal magnetic field. In some places, the polarization is clearly offset from the center of the jet ridgeline; there are also some regions where the polarization sticks are oblique to the jet direction.

Our RM map shows a clear gradient transverse to the jet roughly 5 mas from the core. Figure fig:0836RM also shows a slice taken roughly perpendicular to the jet direction plotted together with its errors. The transverse RM gradient in this region is monotonic, and has a significance of about 3 .

sources.summary: The paper reviews VLBI studies of S5 0836+710, describing its powerful kiloparsec-scale radio jet, helical ridge-line structure, and possible instability-driven oscillations.

quotes.quote: The luminous quasar S5 0836+710 is at a redshift z=2.16 and hosts a powerful radio jet extending up to kiloparsec scales . At this redshift, 1 mas 8.4 pc (see, e.g., MOJAVE database). VLBI monitoring of the source showed kink structures and yielded estimates of the bulk Lorentz factor _ j =12 and the viewing angle _ j =3^ of the flow at milliarcsecond scales . The jet was observed at 1.6 and 5 GHz with VSOP (VLBI Space Observatory Program, a Japanese-led space VLBI mission), and oscillations of the ridge-line were also observed .

The jet in S5 0836+710 shows a structure compatible with a projected helix. This work showed that the observing array and epoch (through the uv coverage) of a given observation does not have any effect on the large-scale structures observed: The large-scale structures of the ridge-lines of the jet at a given epoch do coincide independently from the observing frequency and array. This result was interpreted as evidence of the physical nature revealed by ridge-lines. Small-scale oscillations may, on the contrary, be produced by observational artifacts.

sources.summary: The paper finds a continuous polarimetric jet-sheath signature in 4C 71 . 07, estimates sheath luminosity and radius, and models two 2014 orphan gamma-ray flares.

quotes.quote: Figure fig6 presents a stacked radio map of the blazar 4C 71 . 07 (0836 + 710). A very prominent and continuous polarimetric signature of a jet sheath is evident down the edges of the jet in this stacked radio image. Given the continuous nature of the jet sheath on the image, we can estimate the bolometric luminosity of the sheath. This is accomplished by first using the length of the polarized intensity profile along each of the slices to trace out the shaded gray regions shown in Figure fig9 (see also Figure fig6 ). The size of the sheath on each side of the slice is arbitrarily set by the location where the polarized flux falls below 0.85 times the peak value. An estimate of the bolometric luminosity ( L_ bol ) of the sheath is determined by summing up all of the deconvolved `sheath' flux (see Figures fig8 fig9 ) contained within the gray shaded regions of our stacked map.

We detect two orphan ray flares in the Fermi LAT light curve of 4C 71 . 07 during 2014 (Figure fig7 ), although the optical observations are sparse. The first orphan flare occurred in May followed by a second in October. Table tab2 lists the Ring of Fire model parameters used to obtain a fit to this pair of orphan flares. For this particular case, two consecutive blobs were launched through the ring of shocked sheath.

sources.summary: The paper studies 4C 71.07 as a high-redshift FSRQ blazar, modeling its luminous big blue bump, quasar core, jet emission, absorption corrections, disc luminosity, accretion rate, and polarization.

quotes.quote: The object 4C 71.07 is a high-redshift blazar whose spectral energy distribution shows a prominent big blue bump and a strong Compton dominance. We present the results of a two-year multiwavelength campaign led by the Whole Earth Blazar Telescope (WEBT) to study both the quasar core and the beamed jet of this source. The WEBT data are complemented by ultraviolet and X-ray data from Swift , and by ray data by Fermi . The big blue bump is modelled by using optical and near-infrared mean spectra obtained during the campaign, together with optical and ultraviolet quasar templates. We give prescriptions to correct the source photometry in the various bands for the thermal contribution, in order to derive the non-thermal jet flux.

We verified that 4C 71.07 is characterised by extreme nuclear and jet properties. Integration of the thermal continuum traced by our big blue bump template allowed us to estimate the disc bolometric luminosity, L_ disc =2.45 10^ 47 erg s^ 1 , and to derive the mass accretion rate, M 18 M_ yr^ 1 , and black hole mass, M_ BH 1.6 10^9 M_ , from it in the case of a Shakura Sunyaev disc. As a consequence, the Eddington ratio is as high as 0.66.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes 4C 71.07's spectra, deriving z_sys=2.2130, finding no NLR contribution, detecting broad-line outflows above 3000 km/s, and identifying an exceptionally luminous quasar core.

quotes.quote: In this paper we have investigated the unbeamed nuclear properties of the FSRQ 4C 71.07 through a detailed analysis of its optical and near-infrared spectra. This blazar is known for the extreme properties of its beamed radiation from the jet, including high radio, X-ray and ray luminosity, strong flux and polarization variability, and high Compton dominance e.g. raiteri2019 . Our goal here was to unveil the nature of its quasar core. As expected from high-redshift sources viewed face-on, we have found no evidence for NLR contribution in the spectra. Therefore, we have estimated the systemic redshift from the Balmer lines H and H , obtaining z_ sys =2.2130 0.0004 .
All the other primary broad emission lines appear blueshifted, which is an indication of outflow. The estimated outflow velocities can reach more than 3000 km s^ 1 .
This identifies the quasar core of 4C 71.07 as one of the most luminous among the blazar nuclei ghisellini2011,sbarrato2014 .

sources.summary: The paper presents 0836+710 as a high-redshift blazar with a powerful radio jet, multiwavelength variability, persistent gamma-ray activity, and a 2011 flare bright enough for detailed LAT study.

quotes.quote: The luminous high-redshift z =2.218; Stickel1993 quasar of the blazar sub-class 0836+710 (also known as S5 0836+71 or 4C +71.07) is characterized by a flat radio spectrum = 0.33; Kuhr81 . It hosts a powerful one-sided radio jet emerging from the core and extending up to kiloparsec scales Hummel92 . Very Long Baseline Interferometry (VLBI) images of the source show a complex motion pattern, with jet components moving from apparent subluminal to superluminal velocities Otterbein1998 .

Blazar 0836+710 has been the subject of several X-ray studies and multiwavelength modeling to understand how X-ray emission is produced and the relationship between X-ray emission and other bands . These studies conclude that 0836+710 is a typical blazar, ultimately powered by a black hole with mass 3 10 ^ 9 M _ , requiring an external source of seed photons for the high-energy Compton part of the spectrum. In the 1990's this blazar was detected and shown to be variable at > 100 MeV ray energies by EGRET on board the Compton Gamma Ray Observatory (CGRO) , with the name 3EG J0845+7049 in the Third EGRET Catalog .

Although its high redshift and steep energy spectrum might suggest that 0836+710 would produce too few rays to enable detailed variability studies, its 2011 episode () flaring activity was as bright as that of many lower-redshift blazars.

sources.summary: The paper models the S5 0836 + 710 radio jet as Kelvin-Helmholtz instability modes, deriving Lorentz factor, density ratio, Mach number, and shear-layer constraints.

quotes.quote: The radio source S5 0836 + 710 (4C + 71.07; J0841 + 7053) is a powerful low-polarization quasar (LPQ) located at a redshift of 2.17 osmer+1994 , which corresponds to the luminosity distance of 16.9 Gpc and a linear scale of 8.4 pc/mas, assuming the standard CDM cosmology ( H_0 = 73 km s ^ 1 Mpc ^ 1 , _ m = 0.27, _ = 0.73) planck2015 . It has a long and one-sided jet at parsec and kiloparsec scales. Large-scale radio emission was revealed by the VLA Karl G. Jansky Very Large Array of the National Radio Astronomy Observatory, Socorro, NM, USA and MERLIN Multi-Element Radio Linked Interferometer Network of the Jodrell Bank Observatory, UK at distances larger than 1 arcsecond hummel+1992,perucho+2012b .

We have used the results from previous studies of the S5 0836+710 jet structure that have modelled its ridgeline at 1.6 GHz, which covers up to 130 mas , by fitting transverse brightness profiles with a single Gaussian component. The ridgeline shows the presence of several oscillatory terms, which have been reported in several different works lobanov+1998,lobanov+2006,perucho+2012a . All those previous works give similar values for the wavelengths of the most prominent oscillations, using different arrays and epochs of observation. We assume that these oscillations correspond to KH instability modes developing in the flow, and propose a method to derive the physical parameters of the jet.

sources.summary: The paper reports reverberation-mapping results for S5 0836+71, measuring a tentative C IV-continuum lag, a very massive central black hole, and near-Eddington accretion.

quotes.quote: However, the one current exception to this state of affairs is S5 0836+71; although the data for this quasar are still not ideal in terms of the criteria above, they do allow a preliminary measurement of the emission-line to continuum lag. The continuum light curve for this object displays a general rise until June 2002, followed by a sharp drop in flux. This light curve has largest variation among all our monitored quasars, and the light curves of both C 4 and C 3 ] display the largest variations among all the emission line light curves.

We find the time lag between the C 4 line and the continuum of S5 0836+71 to be 595^ +86 _ 110 days, or 188^ +27 _ 37 days in the quasar rest frame. The mean FWHM of the C 4 line measured from the mean spectrum of S5 0836+71 is about 9700 . Using Eq. 5 of Kaspi et al. (2000) and the time lag of 188 days, we estimate the central mass of S5 0836+71 at about 2.6 10^ 9 M _ .
Thus, this object is accreting close to its Eddington luminosity, with L_ bol /L_ Edd 0.9 .

sources.summary: The review reports preliminary reverberation mapping of S5 0836+71, measuring a C IV lag, line width, and black-hole mass higher than the previous 3C 273 benchmark.

quotes.quote: The one current exception is S5 0836+71; although the data for this quasar are still not ideal for reverberation mapping they do allow a preliminary measurement of the emission-line to continuum lag. Figure 2 shows the continuum and C iv emission line light curves for this object. These light curves has largest variation among all our monitored quasars.

The tentative time lag between the C iv line and the continuum of S5 0836+71 is found to be 595^ +86 _ 110 days, or 188^ +27 _ 37 days in the quasar rest frame. The mean FWHM of the C iv line measured from the mean spectrum of S5 0836+71 is about 9700 km s ^ 1 . Using Eq. 5 of Kaspi et al. (2000) and the time lag of 188 days, the central mass of S5 0836+71 is estimated to be 2.6 10^ 9 M _ .

sources.summary: The paper models multiwavelength activity of high-redshift blazar S5 0836+71, finding strong X-ray and gamma-ray flares, disk-dominated optical-UV emission, and a leptonic jet scenario.

quotes.quote: A broadband study of the high redshift blazar S5 0836+71 ( z = 2.172) is presented. Multi-frequency light curves show multiple episodes of X-ray and ray flares, while optical-UV fluxes show little variations. During the GeV outburst, the highest ray flux measured is (5.22 1.10) 10 ^ 6 in the range of 0.1 300 GeV, which corresponds to an isotropic ray luminosity of (1.62 0.44) 10 ^ 50 , thereby making this as one of the most luminous ray flare ever observed from any blazar.

The various activity states of S5 0836+71 are reproduced under the assumption of single zone leptonic emission model. In all the states, the emission region is located inside the broad line region, and the optical-UV radiation is dominated by the accretion disk emission. The modeling parameters suggests the enhancement in bulk Lorentz factor as a primary cause of the ray flare.

sources.summary: The paper models the quasar 0836+710 jet as a transversely stratified relativistic flow whose observed oscillations arise from Kelvin-Helmholtz instability modes.

quotes.quote: VSOP observations of the jet in 0836+710 at 1.6 and 5 GHz have enabled tracing of the radial structure of the flow on scales from 2 mas to 200 mas along the jet and determination of the wavelengths of individual oscillatory modes responsible for the formation of the structure observed. We apply linear stability analysis to identify the oscillatory modes with modes of Kelvin-Helmholtz instability that match the wavelengths of the structures observed. We find that the jet structure in 0836+710 can be reproduced by the helical surface mode and a combination of the helical and elliptic body modes of Kelvin-Helmholtz instability. Our results indicate that the jet is substantially stratified and different modes of the instability grow inside the jet at different distances to the jet axis. The helical surface mode can be driven externally, and we discuss the implications of the driving frequency on the physics of the active nucleus in 0836+710 .

sources.summary: The paper describes 4C 71.07 as a high-redshift FSRQ with major gamma-ray flaring, previous X-ray studies, and no significant XMM-Newton IDV in 2001.

quotes.quote: 4C 71.07 S5 0836+710 (4C 71.07) is a high redshift FSRQ. With Swift observations, X-ray variability was found on a time scale of one month in the energy range 15-150 keV (Sambruna et al. 2007). The source has shown the most luminous gamma-ray flare from any blazar till date (during Oct-Dec in 2011) in 0.1--300 GeV during its outburst state (Paliya 2015), where IDV is also reported. Multiple X-ray and gamma-ray flares are reported by the author and it was noticed that the optical-UV flux do not show any significant variation since these bands are dominated by accretion disk emission. Different satellite's (including NuSTAR, SWIFT) observations of the source reported that IDV in X-ray band is absent but significant STV in different X-ray energy ranges (Ghisellini et al. 2010; Akyuz et al. 2013; Tagliaferri et al. 2015).

sources.summary: 4C+71.07 at z=2.218 has multiple gamma-ray flaring periods above its time-averaged flux, reaches peak flux on MJD 57335.21, and exceeds 1e50 erg/s eleven times.

quotes.quote: During multiple flaring periods of 4C+71.07 (Fig. strong_gray panel d), which is at a redshift of z=2.218 , the \ emission notably exceeded its time-averaged flux of (1.06 0.01) 10^ 7 photon cm ^ 2 s ^ 1 . Specifically, in three distinct flaring periods—MJD 55862.01-55932.97, MJD 57227-57245.79, and MJD 57296.92-57338.47—the flux above 138.16 MeV exceeded 10^ 6 photon cm ^ 2 s ^ 1 . The peak flux during these flaring events was at (8.02 1.40) 10^ 6 photon cm ^ 2 s ^ 1 , and was observed on MJD 57335.21.

For 4C+71.07, during the flaring periods in MJD 57227-57245.79 and MJD 57296.92-57338.47, the source luminosity exceeded 10^ 50 erg s^ 1 11 times. The highest luminosity of (2.03 0.36) 10^ 50 erg s^ 1 was observed on MJD 57335.21.

sources.summary: The paper reports first XMM-Newton results for high-redshift flat-spectrum quasar S5 0836+710, finding high background and an absorbed power-law model consistent with previous ASCA, BeppoSAX, and Chandra measurements.

quotes.quote: This flat-spectrum radio quasar has one of the highest redshifts among the objects in the present sample. The XMM-Newton data, published here for the first time, show high background. Past analyses of ROSAT and ASCA data by Cappi et al. (1997) and BeppoSAX data by Tavecchio et al. (2000) have shown a spectrum with a hard photon index ( 1.3 ), with acceptable fit also with a broken power law, but some inconsistencies in the value of the absorption. The present data are best fitted with a simple power law model absorbed by the Galactic column plus an additional absorber at the redshift of the quasar with a value (1.4 0.3) 10^ 21 cm ^ 2 , consistent with the ASCA and BeppoSAX values. This is also consistent with Chandra observations by Fang et al. (2001), who found =1.388 0.012 ( 0.5-8 keV), but a slightly lower absorption N_ H =(7.0 1.2) 10^ 20 cm ^ 2 (not redshifted).

sources.summary: The paper treats 0836+710 as a z>2 radio-loud blazar detected by Swift/BAT, with hard X-ray spectra and a factor-four 15--150 keV flux increase.

quotes.quote: We report on \ observations of four z>2 radio-loud quasars (0212+735, 0537--286, 0836+710, and 2149--307), classified as blazars. The sources, well-known emitters at soft-medium X-rays, were detected at >5 with the BAT experiment in 15--150 keV. No flux variability was detected within the XRT and BAT exposures, with the exception of 0836+710 which shows an increase of a factor 4 of the 15--150 keV flux on a timescale of one month. The 0.3--10 keV spectra are well fitted by power law models, with rather hard continua (photon indices _ XRT 1.3-1.5 ); similarly, the 15--150 keV spectra are described by power laws with _ BAT 1.3-1.8 .

sources.summary: The paper treats S5 0836+710 as a quasar whose jet ridge-line emission traces a physical wave, while noting that magnetic and hydrodynamical interpretations remain unresolved.

quotes.quote: Helical structures can also be identified in radio-jets. The ridge-line of emission of the jet of S5 0836+710 has been identified as a physical structure corresponding to a wave developing in the jet flow. I review here the evidence that has allowed to reach this conclusion, along with an associated caveat. Current data do not allow to distinguish between magnetic or hydrodynamical instabilities.

sources.summary: The paper treats S5 0836+710 as a studied AGN/powerful blazar; submitted RadioAstron observations support a Kelvin-Helmholtz jet-instability analysis over 0.02-200 mas with helical modes.

quotes.quote: The first images from these programs reach angular resolution of a few tens of microarcseconds and reveal unprecedented details about the jet collimation profile, magnetic field configuration, and Kelvin-Helmholtz instabilities along the flow in some of the most studied AGN (3C 84, BL Lac, 3C 273, S5 0836+710).

Powerful AGN KSP First results from this programme have been submitted at the time of writing, presenting L, C, and K-band observations of S5 0836+710 (Vega-Garc ia et al. \ subm.). A detailed study of the Kelvin-Helmholtz instabilities along the jet, on angular scales between 0.02 and 200 mas, was performed, resulting in an indication of helical instability modes. All observations were carried out during AO-1 and AO-2, including also 3C 273, 3C 345, and 4C+69.21.

sources.summary: The paper reports a serendipitous INTEGRAL detection of high-redshift FSRQ S5 0836+710, finding a flat spectrum consistent with earlier high-state observations but lower flux.

quotes.quote: In the same field of our primary target we also detected the high redshift blazar S5 0836+710, belonging to the FSRQ sub-class ( ), and two bright Seyfert galaxies, Mkn 3 and Mkn 6, all with brighter fluxes than S5 0716+714 (Table 1). The spectral index of S5 0836+710 is consistent with that determined through CGRO BATSE ( ) and BeppoSAX PDS ( ) observations during higher emission states (our measured flux is a factor of 3 lower than found by both BATSE and BeppoSAX ). The flat spectral slope favours the interpretation of the high energy spectrum as Compton scattering of relativistic electrons off external radiation, as opposed to synchrotron-self Compton, in FSRQ ( ).
S5 0836+710 is the second highest redshift AGN detected by , after PKS 1830-21 at z = 2.507 ( ), which indicates that \ can play a role also in exploring the high redshift universe.

sources.summary: The paper describes 0836+710 as an ultra-luminous high-redshift quasar with correlated broadband variability and VLBI jet changes; during this monitoring it showed weak variability and a hard near-IR/optical spectrum dominated by a blue bump.

quotes.quote: 0836+710 This ultra-luminous quasar at z=2.17 is showing a well correlated broad-band variability that also seems to correlate with the structural changes seen in the VLBI jet (e.g. \ 1998A&A...334..489O ). The source showed only weak variability (Figs. fig:lc fig:olc ). The hard near-IR--optical spectrum implies that the blue bump component, likely due to thermal emission from the accretion disc, is still dominant in the near-IR band, and consequently it is not possible to distinguish the high-energy part of the synchrotron component. This is actually expected, because of the high redshift of the source.

sources.summary: The paper treats S5 0836+710 as a high-redshift flat-spectrum radio quasar serendipitously detected by INTEGRAL, with a lower flux than earlier BeppoSAX observations but a consistent photon index.

quotes.quote: S5 0836+710 (z=2.172) The observation of this high-redshift flat-spectrum radio quasar was a ``gift'' from the large field of view (FOV =29^ 29^ at zero response) of the IBIS imager onboard INTEGRAL . Indeed, S5 0836+710 is 6.5^ far from S5 0716+714 , which was the target of the two above mentioned campaigns, and therefore it was detected as well. Comparison with previous observations with BeppoSAX , indicate that the flux was about a factor 3 lower, but the photon index remained constant within the measurement errors .

sources.summary: The paper describes B0836 + 710/4C +71.07 as a luminous quasar with a kiloparsec-scale radio jet, broadband variability, and detailed VLBI jet structure.

quotes.quote: B0836 + 710 4C +71.07 is a luminous quasar which hosts a radio jet extending up to kiloparsec scales. Broadband variability was observed in this source otterbein98 , and space VLBI observations have revealed detailed jet structures lobanov98b, perucho07 . Here we included the Swift data of B0836 +710 observed in February 2009 with an integration time of 9 ks (obsid 00036376005).

sources.summary: The paper studies S5 0836+710 as a luminous z=2.16 quasar whose radio jet shows helical instability, missing hot-spot emission, and likely collimation loss.

quotes.quote: For example, it was recently shown that the observed helical structures in the jet in S5 0836+710 could be associated with helical pressure waves generated by Kelvin-Helmholtz instability.
The luminous quasar S5 0836+710 at a redshift z=2.16 hosts a powerful radio jet extending up to kiloparsec scales hu92 .
In the case of S5 0836+710 , the images of the jet at the largest scales do not show any signs of enhanced emission that could be identified as the hot-spot or any clear lobe-like structure.

gmw_00001305.name: HE0435
gmw_00001305.overview: HE0435 is a quadruply lensed active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1503.02074, arXiv:1610.08504] at a redshift of 1.689 [arXiv:1503.02074, arXiv:1802.02620]. Its foreground deflector is an S0 lenticular galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0510465, arXiv:astro-ph/0511026] at a redshift of 0.454 [arXiv:astro-ph/0511026], which produces four lensed images that exhibit both intrinsic quasar variability and stellar microlensing [arXiv:1101.3664, arXiv:2107.06227].

sources.summary: The paper models HE0435 as a four-image gravitational lens, finding smooth models fail on flux ratios and that substructure near image A is strongly favored.

quotes.quote: In this paper we investigate the properties of the four image gravitational lens HE 0435 1223 (hereafter HE0435), selected for its relatively bright ( F160W<18.1 ) and well separated ( 2.4'' ) images (see Fig. fig:diagram ). Since its discovery wisotzki02a , HE0435 has been extensively studied using ground- and space-based observations. From the ground, optical spectroscopy provided early evidence for stellar microlensing and against significant differential dust extinction in the lens wisotzki03a .

As a first step we examine how well a smooth mass distribution (without substructure) can fit the observed image positions and flux ratios for HE0435. Following kochanek06a , we adopt a ``minimal'' lens model in which the main lens galaxy (G1) has an ellipsoidal mass distribution with a softened power law density profile,

Motivated by the anomaly, we hypothesise that HE0435 contains substructure and examine how well we can test that hypothesis and constrain the properties of the substructure. In this section we consider whether it is possible to explain the data with just one or a few clumps that (presumably) lie near the lensed images. In Section sec:pop we study full populations of substructure.

sources.summary: The paper monitors HE 0435--1223's four lensed quasar images in VRi, finding 2008-2009 flux decreases, color increases, intrinsic quasar variability, and likely microlensing of image A.

quotes.quote: We present VRi photometric observations of the quadruply imaged quasar , carried out with the Danish 1.54 telescope at the La Silla Observatory. Our aim was to monitor and study the magnitudes and colors of each lensed component as a function of time. We monitored the object during two seasons (2008 and 2009) in the VRi spectral bands, and reduced the data with two independent techniques: difference imaging and PSF (Point Spread Function) fitting. Between these two seasons, our results show an evident decrease in flux by 0.2--0.4 magnitudes of the four lensed components in the three filters. We also found a significant increase ( 0.05--0.015) in their V-R and R-i color indices.

These flux and color variations are very likely caused by intrinsic variations of the quasar between the observed epochs. Microlensing effects probably also affect the brightest ``A'' lensed component.

sources.summary: The authors analyze independent multi-season HE 0435 1223 light curves, keep the datasets separate, derive weighted-average time delays, and report agreement between the two measurements.

quotes.quote: - HE 0435 1223: We made use of the light curves presented in Courbin2011 spanning seven seasons using data from Euler, Mercator, Maidanak, and SMARTS and the light curves presented in Blackburne2014 spanning eight seasons using data from SMARTS. The SMARTS data used by Courbin2011 is the same as the first two seasons of data presented in Blackburne2014 . Hence we excluded the SMARTS data points from the light curves of Courbin2011 to make it independent of the light curves of Blackburne2014 . Owing to the differences in the approaches followed by these two teams of authors to derive photometry and also the photometric uncertainties, we avoided merging the two datasets. Our time delay measurements listed in Table table:time-delays are the weighted averages of the time delays measured from the two independent sets of light curves.

In Table table:HE0435 , we present our measurements of the time delays of HE 0435 1223 from the two independent sets of light curves and the resulting weighted averages. For each pair of quasar images, we see that the time delays measured from the two datasets agree to within 2 .

sources.summary: HE 0435-1223 is cleanly deconvolved despite a bright lens galaxy; its B/D chromatic increase is attributed to extinction or microlensing, with red-wing line microlensing observed.

quotes.quote: (d) HE 0435-1223: Deconvolution: Very good results are obtained with the deconvolution. The lensing galaxy is relatively bright compared to the lensed images (only 4 times fainter than the flux of the lensed images above 6500 ) and we may not exclude contamination of the latter by the lens. The symmetric location of the lensed images aside the lensing galaxy and their similar brightnesses however argue against large differential effects (i.e. if contamination takes place, the spectra of A D should be corrupted the same way by the lens).

Because we find approximately the same microlensing factor in the blue and in the red, DE ( M (blue) 1.34, M (red) 1.47, 0.81) is the most natural explanation, B being more reddened than D by the dust in the lensing galaxy. Alternatively, CML may be at work ( M 1.47, ( blue ) 0.74, ( red ) 0.82). Broad Lines: Whatever the origin of the chromatic changes, ML of the red wing of the and lines is observed.

sources.summary: The paper studies HE0435-1223 as a quadruply lensed quasar whose line-profile microlensing constrains the BLR, favoring a flattened low-ionization geometry.

quotes.quote: Using infrared spectra of the z=1.693 quadruply lensed quasar HE0435-1223 acquired in 2009 with the spectrograph SINFONI at the ESO Very Large Telescope, we have detected a clear microlensing effect in images A and D . While microlensing affects the blue and red wings of the H line profile in image D very differently, it de-magnifies the line core in image A . The combination of these different effects sets constraints on the line-emitting region; these constraints suggest that a rotating ring is at the origin of the H line.

Visible spectra obtained in 2004 and 2012 indicate that the MgII line profile is microlensed in the same way as the H line. Our results therefore favour flattened geometries for the low-ionization line-emitting region, for example, a Keplerian disk. Biconical models cannot be ruled out but require more fine-tuning.

sources.summary: The paper includes HE 0435 1223 in microlensing delay simulations, giving its four-image lens properties, redshifts, black-hole mass, disk scale, magnification maps, and delay maps.

quotes.quote: We demonstrate the effects of microlensing on gravitational lens time delays using numerical simulations for two of the lensed quasars, RXJ 1131 1231 and HE 0435 1223, used by and to constrain H_ 0 . RXJ 1131 1231 is a four image lensed quasar with z_ s = 0.658 and z_ l = 0.295 Sluse2003 . Based on the H line width from , estimated the black hole mass to be (1.3 0.3) 10 ^ 8 M_ . HE 0435 1223 is another four image lensed quasar with z_ s = 1.689, z_ l = 0.46, and an estimated black hole mass of 0.5 10 ^ 9 M_ (see Mosquera2011 and references therein). Based on Equation 5, the accretion disk sizes for RXJ 1131 1231 and HE 0435 1223 are R_0 = 7.34 10^ 14 cm (0.28 light days) and R_0 = 9.37 10^ 14 cm (0.36 light days), respectively, in the observed R band (6586 ) for an Eddington ratio of L/L_ E = 0.1 and a radiative efficiency of = 0.1.

We assume a mean microlens mass of M/M_ = 0.3 for both lenses, leading to an outer scale of 5.02 10^ 17 cm and a pixel scale of 6.12 10^ 13 cm for RXJ 1131 1231 and an outer scale of 5.89 10^ 17 cm and a pixel scale of 7.19 10^ 13 cm for HE 0435 1223. The accretion disk scale lengths R_0 are 12 times the pixel scale, while the region dominating the variability ( 5 R_ 0 ) is 60 times the pixel scale. Figure fig:map1131 and fig:map0435 show the full 8192 8192 magnification patterns for image A of each lens.

sources.summary: The paper finds HE 0435-1223 shows C III] blue-wing microlensing, contributes strongly to quadruple-system statistics, is clearly affected in C IV, and appears to be a hybrid microlensing/intrinsic-variability case.

quotes.quote: Only one system (HE 0435-1223) shows a small change (0.11 mag) with S/N > 2 in the selected integration window for the blue wing as defined in Figure em1 . Visually inspecting the data, we find changes in the extreme red wings of SBS 0909+532 and SDSS J1206+0118. These changes could have their origin in the UV Fe II blend 2861-2917 located redward of Mg II, showing up with different intensities for different quasar spectra (see Kova2015 ). At odds with the results obtained from the limited sample presented in Paper I, we find, on the basis of more available epochs, that the CIII ] line seems to be significantly more affected by microlensing than the Mg II line, indicating that it arises from a more compact region inside the BLR. We detect microlensing at the 2 level within the defined integration windows in four systems (SDSS J0246-0825, HE 0435-1223, SDSS J1155+6346, WFI 2033-4723), whereas evidence of intrinsic variability (at the 2 level) can only be found in one system (Q0957+561). Also, the systems SDSS 1004-4112, SDSS J1155+6346, and HE 1104-1805 show microlensing differences at a smaller S/N ( 1.5). Notice the impact on the statistics of the quadruple systems HE 0435-1223 and SDSS J1004+4112, which have been observed in four epochs.

After consistently separating microlensing from intrinsic variability according to the criteria explained in Section sec2.2 (see also Tables table2 and table3 ), we find that nine objects are clearly affected by microlensing at the 2 level (HE 0435-1223, HS 0818-1227, FBQS J0951+2635, QSO 0957+561, SDSS J1004+4112, SDSS J1138+0314, SDSS J1206+4332, SDSS J1339+1310, HE 2149-2745) and two systems show evidence of intrinsic variability at the 2 level (HE 1104-1805, HE 2149-2745).

We derive robust estimates of both, intrinsic variability and amplitude of microlensing between the lensed images, and consistently separated a group of five objects dominated by microlensing (SBS 0909+532, FBQS J0951+2635, SDSS J1004+4112, SDSS J1206+4332, and SDSS J1339+1310) and another group of three systems in which intrinsic variability prevails (Q 0142-100, HE 1104-1805, and WFI 2033-4723). Two systems (HE 0435-1223 and HE 2149-2745) seem to be hybrid cases with both microlensing and intrinsic variability present. Finally, the case of QSO 0957+561 may be explained by intrinsic variability combined with the large time-delay between the images plus a possible contribution from microlensing.

sources.summary: The paper describes HE0435-1223 as a galaxy-scale strong lens, giving its discovery context, lens or source properties, image configuration, and relevant environmental or model information for the survey.

quotes.quote: HE0435-1223 (herein HE0435) was discovered as part of the Hamburg/ESO (HES) survey of bright quasars wisotzki1 as a z=1.689 QSO. A follow-up high resolution image with the 6.5 m Baade/Magellan I telescope revealed the four lensed images of the quasar wisotzki2 in a configuration resembling the Einstein Cross lens Q2237+0305 huchra . The image separations are 2.3 (B-D) and 2.6 (A-C). The lens has a spectrum and a spatial profile characteristic of an early type galaxy. morgan measure the lens redshift z_l=0.4546 0.0002 .

sources.summary: The paper models HE0435 with a higher-redshift nearby galaxy whose physical location is lens-corrected, and finds its posterior favors low subhalo normalizations or a turnover.

quotes.quote: In the cases of HE0435 and WFI 2033, the nearby galaxy lies at a higher redshift than the main lens plane. The light from the galaxy is therefore subject to lensing by the main deflector, and its true physical location differs from its observed position. We estimate the true physical locations of these objects by sampling the macromodel parameter space using the image positions as constraints, and read out the physical position of background satellite given its observed (lensed) position. We then place the satellite at this derived physical location in the forward model sampling with uncertainties of 0.05 arcseconds.

The system HE0435 generally favors models with low subhalo mass function normalizations (low _ sub ), or a turnover the mass function with higher _ sub . The system WFI 2033 is the opposite, with a posterior favoring CDM-like mass functions with many lens plane subhalos.

sources.summary: The paper treats HE0435+1223 as a quad lens with measured delays, cosmographic analyses, a modeled background galaxy, and narrow-line substructure tests.

quotes.quote: HE0435+1223 This quad was discovered by Wisotzki++02 , with lens/source redshifts of z = 0.45 and z_s = 1.69 , respectively Morgan++05 . The time delays were measured at 5.3 precision Bonvin++17 , with a maximum delay of 14 days. This system was analyzed for cosmography by Bonvin++17,Wong++17,Chen++19 . We explicitly model the galaxy located behind the main deflector at z = 0.78 as an SIS profile, placing it at an angular position corrected by 0.65 \ arcsec due to foreground lensing effects from the main deflector For additional discussion, see Section 5.8 in Gilman++20a . Nierenberg++17 measured narrow-line flux ratios for this system see also Nierenberg++19 , and used them to look for substructure near the images.

sources.summary: The paper includes HE0435-1223 as a TDCOSMO lens with discovery, HST and Keck AO image modeling, time-delay measurement, and two consistent velocity-dispersion measurements.

quotes.quote: HE0435-1223: The discovery is presented by . The image modeling is presented by (for HST ) and (for Keck Adaptive Optics data). The time-delay measurement is presented by . The velocity dispersion measurement of 222 15 km/s presented by is based on Keck-LRIS spectroscopy and includes systematic uncertainties. An independent measurement of 222 34 km/s by using VLT is in excellent agreement.

sources.summary: The review uses HE0435 1223 as a lensed-quasar example, highlighting microlensing in its image light curves and its use in time-delay cosmography modeling.

quotes.quote: This probably happened, for example, in 2007 for image A of HE0435 1223. In Figure fig:H0435_lcs , we show the light curves of HE0435 1223 as well as the differential curves between the images, highlighting the microlensing signal.

An example of this phenomenon is image B of HE0435 1223, which slowly raised by 0.5 mag between 2013 and 2018. It typically happens when the stellar density is high and the quasar is located in regions where the microcaustics overlap. The net effect is a smooth variation of the microlensing magnification as the quasar moves through these crowded regions.

sources.summary: The paper describes HE0435 1223 as a quadruply lensed quasar and confirms a z=0.4546 lens redshift, with the galaxy spectrum matching an S0 template.

quotes.quote: HE0435 1223 : Discovered by Wisotzki et al. (2002), with a quasar redshift of 1.689 . This is a quadruply lensed system with a maximum image separation of 2 6 . The lens redshift was recently measured at z=0.4546 (Morgan et al. 2005). We confirm this result and find that the galaxy spectrum, presented at Fig. HE0435 , is well matched by an S0 template.

sources.summary: The paper finds most HE 0435 1223 flux ratios consistent between K and L, but the B/C ratio differs significantly, motivating a microlensing interpretation.

quotes.quote: HE 0435 1223. We consider flux ratios with respect to image C. For the A/C and D/C flux ratios, the K and L' values are consistent within the errorbars. For the B/C flux ratio, however, there is a significant difference with F_B/F_C=1.271 0.073 in K and 0.991 0.074 in L' . This discrepancy is significant at >99 confidence.

sources.summary: The paper uses HE 0435-1223 as a quadruply lensed quasar example whose microlensed light curves test time-delay estimators, spline fitting, and extrinsic-variability corrections.

quotes.quote: This is crucial since it can well be that, for example, strong extrinsic variability in quasar image A prevents us from measuring the delays t_ AB and t_ AC , while the delay t_ BC can be well determined, as observed in the case of HE 0435-1223 Courbin:2011bl .
For example, to represent the microlensing in their high-quality light curves of the lensed quasar HE 0435-1223, Kochanek:2006fp use independent quadratic polynomials for each season.

In Fig. fig_spline1 , we show three seasons of a spline fit obtained for the light curves of the quadruply lensed quasar HE 0435-1223 Courbin:2011bl . Variants of the intrinsic spline are drawn on top of the shifted data points, while the red, green, and purple splines represent the extrinsic variability for which the data points have been corrected (see caption).

sources.summary: The paper presents HE 0435--1223 as a near-textbook gravitational lens whose microlensing of image A can increase the cross relation.

quotes.quote: HE 0435--1223 is an almost textbook example for gravitational lensing, with its four nearly identical components arranged symmetrically around a luminous early-type galaxy wis02 . The cross relations are shown in blue colour in Fig. 11(). Micro-lensing was detected in a subsequent monitoring campaign wis03,koc06 which probably affects the minimum image A most strongly ric11,cou11,bra14 . Since the minimum image has positive sign of magnification, the magnified A component by micro-lensing effect also equals to increasing the cross relation.

sources.summary: HE0435 1223 is used as the detailed lensed AGN example; its four lensed images and lower host-to-AGN contrast reduce host-galaxy reconstruction bias relative to HE1104.

quotes.quote: In the remainder of this section we describe these steps in detail, referring to the specific case of HE0435 1223 as an example, when necessary. More details about this system are available in five papers detailing the cosmographic analysis H0licow1,H0licow2,H0licow3,H0licow4,H0licow5 .

HE0435 The results for HE0435 are shown in Fig. fig:bias_HE0435 , using the same format as in the previous section. We note that the bias is reduced relative to HE1104, for two reasons: i) the system has four lensed images and there is thus more information available to reconstruct the host galaxy surface brightness; ii) the contrast between host galaxy magnitude and AGN magnitude is reduced to 1-2 magnitudes.

sources.summary: The paper applies its polynomial time-delay method to HE 0435-1223, describing it as a quadruply lensed quasar whose slow microlensing and monitoring data enable comparison with prior delay estimates.

quotes.quote: We applied our method to the excellent data of the 7-years monitoring of the quadruple gravitation lens HE 0435-1223 (the photometry is available from the CDS archive, http://cdsarc.u-strasbg.fr/), which have been used by Courbin et al. (2011) to determine the time delays. The lens HE 0435-1223 is an example of the systems where microlensing events can be considered as "slow" ones. The object with a redshift of z=1.689 was identified as a quasar lensed by a z=0.4546 galaxy by Wisotski et al. (2002).
The second and third lines in Table 3 contain the time delays of HE 0435-1223 obtained by Courbin et al. (2011) from the results of the detailed seven-years monitoring.

sources.summary: The paper models HE0435 host light with a surface-brightness profile, measuring host flux, host-to-total ratio, effective radius, and Sersic index.

quotes.quote: HE0435 We follow the approach described in Section  sec:Modelling . We select 5 isolated stars in this field as initial PSFs to input to the fitting, for a total of 60 fits. Based on the top-eight choices, we perform the weighting algorithm and measure the host flux, host-to-total flux ratio, effective radius and \ index. The inference results are shown in Table  tab:host_measure , (2) (6) columns.

sources.summary: The lens in HE 0435 1223 has a confirmed redshift of 0.454 and a higher signal-to-noise spectrum that matches an S0 galaxy template.

quotes.quote: We confirm four previously measured redshifts: HE 0047 1756 ( =0.407 ), HE 0230 2130 ( =0.523 ), HE 0435 1223 ( =0.454 ) and WFI J2033 4723 ( =0.661 ).

HE 0435 1223 : This quadruply imaged quasar, discovered by Wisotzki et al. ( ), has a redshift of z=1.689 and has a maximum image separation of 2.56 , hence with little contamination of the lens spectrum by the quasar images. The redshift of the lensing galaxy has already been measured (Morgan et al. , Ofek et al. ) at =0.455 . We confirm this result, with =0.454 0.001 , and present a much higher signal-to-noise spectrum in Fig. HE0435_lens . A S0 galaxy template matches well the spectrum of the lens.

gmw_00001306.name: SDSS1206
gmw_00001306.overview: SDSS1206 is a strongly lensed active galactic nucleus at a redshift of z=1.789 [arXiv:1908.02892, arXiv:1809.01274]. While the central quasar is doubly imaged, its host galaxy crosses the inner lensing caustic to form a nearly complete, quadruply lensed Einstein ring [arXiv:1908.02892, arXiv:1809.01274].

sources.summary: The paper uses SDSS 1206+4332 as a high-quality doubly lensed quasar system with an Einstein-ring host, deriving the source angular-diameter distance from time-delay and lens-distance measurements.

quotes.quote: We apply our method to the system SDSS 1206+4332 which was discovered by Oguri2005 . It is one of high-quality lensing systesms in the catalog of H0LiCOW and has been modeled by Birrer2019 within the program. This system consists of a doubly lensed quasar with the host galaxy forming a nearly-complete Einstein ring. The image separation is 3.03'' and the time-delay measured from the light curve pair is 111.3 3 days. The redshifts of lens and source are z_L=0.745 and z_S=1.789 , respectively.

The H0LiCOW team took a blind time-delay strong lensing cosmographic analysis of the system. They combined the time-delay measurement between the two AGN images, Hubble Space Telescope imaging, spectroscopic data of the lens galaxy and the line-of-sight field measurements to give measurements of both D_L and D_ t with systematic errors under control.

sources.summary: The paper treats SDSS J1206+4332 as a doubly lensed z=1.789 quasar, monitored for seven seasons, and derives a final image time delay of 111.3 days.

quotes.quote: In this paper, we present time delays for two doubly lensed quasars: SDSS J1206+4332 ( 12^ h 06^ m 29^ s .65, +43 32 17 60; J2000.0 ) and HS 2209+1914 ( 22^ h 11^ m 30^ s .30, +19 29 12 00; J2000.0 ). SDSS J1206+4332 was discovered by Oguri2005 . They found a z=1.789 quasar whose lensed images are separated by 2 90 . The redshift of the main lensing galaxy is estimated at z=0.748 . One or two other nearby galaxies might contribute to the lensing potential.

Given the low spread in time-delay values between the methods, and given the possibility that the error bars from the dispersion methods, the regression difference technique, and the spline fit have been overestimated, while those for the NMF might be underestimated, we think that a final time delay for SDSS J1206+4332 of t_ AB = 111.3 3 is a reasonable and comprehensive value, which can be applied in lens modelling.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes SDSS 1206+4332 as a doubly imaged, strongly lensed quasar whose host forms an extended ring, using time delays, imaging, lens kinematics, and field data for cosmography.

quotes.quote: The system is of a special kind: although the quasar is only doubly imaged, parts of the host galaxy cross the inner lensing caustic and get quadruply lensed in a fold configuration forming an extended ring. This configuration allows for a very similar analysis as recently applied for quadruply lensed quasars Suyu:2010rc, Suyu:2014aq, Birrer:2016zy, Wong:2017 .

The quasar image separation is 3 03 , and its high variability allowed a precise measurement of a relative time-delay of 111.3 3 days Eulaers:2013 . The redshift of the lens was initially reported as z_ d = 0.748 and the quasar source redshift as z_ s = 1.789 Oguri:2005 . used Keck-DEIMOS Faber:2003 spectroscopy to correct the redshift of the lens to z_ d = 0.745 and measured the projected integrated stellar velocity dispersion of the lensing galaxy to be = 290 30 km s ^ 1 .

As a baseline illustration of the cosmographic content of our measurement, we present in Section  subsec:H0_j1206 the predicted value of the Hubble constant in a flat CDM universe with free \ obtained for SDSS 1206+4332, completely independent of any prior work, including the direct and inverse distance ladder methods, and prior time-delay cosmography work.

sources.summary: SDSS1206 is treated as a distinctive lensed AGN whose host is mostly quadruply imaged; the model includes neighboring perturbers and adopts a single host profile.

quotes.quote: SDSS1206 SDSS1206 is a unique system -- the AGN is doubly imaged by the deflector while most of the host falls inside the inner caustic and ends up being quadruply imaged. Following  Birrer2019 , we consider the galaxy triplet group at the north-west and use a single SIS model to denote their overall mass perturbation. Moreover, as noted by  Birrer2019 , a sub-clump is located in the north which is hardly visible see Figure 1 in Birrer2019 .

We find visible residuals at the fitted lensed arcs region using a single \ model as host. However, a double \ model does not significantly improve the goodness of fit. Thus, we adopt the single \ model in our final inference.

sources.summary: The paper finds SDSS J1206+4332 has C IV wing microlensing and outstanding asymmetry, with epoch comparisons showing image B's blue wing demagnified while its red wing enlarges.

quotes.quote: We detect microlensing in either the blue or the red wing (or in both wings with different amplitudes) of C IV in more than 50 of the systems and find outstanding asymmetries in the wings of QSO 0957+561, SDSS J1004+4112, SDSS J1206+4332, and SDSS J1339+1310. This observation indicates that the BLR is, in general, not spherically symmetric and supports the existence of two regions in the BLR, one insensitive to microlensing and another that only shows up when it is magnified by microlensing.

After consistently separating microlensing from intrinsic variability according to the criteria explained in Section sec2.2 (see also Tables table2 and table3 ), we find that nine objects are clearly affected by microlensing at the 2 level (HE 0435-1223, HS 0818-1227, FBQS J0951+2635, QSO 0957+561, SDSS J1004+4112, SDSS J1138+0314, SDSS J1206+4332, SDSS J1339+1310, HE 2149-2745) and two systems show evidence of intrinsic variability at the 2 level (HE 1104-1805, HE 2149-2745).

As discussed in Fian2018 , the distance traveled in the source plane is too small ( 0.6 light-days) for SDSS J1339+1310 to see variability in both wings. However, for SDSS J1206+4332 and SDSS J1004+4112 we obtained more interesting results, owing to larger displacements of the source ( 8 light-days for SDSS J1206+4332 and 12 light-days for SDSS J1004+4112). If we compare the four epochs in SDSS J1004+4112, we can see that the strongly magnified blue wing fades while the red wing enhances. In SDSS J1206+4332 we detect, when comparing epoch I and II, a slight demagnification of image B in the blue wing while the red wing enlarges. These observations confirm that the separation between the approaching and recending parts of the microlensed region of the BLR is about a few light-days in size.

sources.summary: The paper includes SDSS1206+4332 as a TDCOSMO lens with discovery, image modeling, time-delay work, a 290 30 km/s Keck-DEIMOS velocity dispersion, and line-of-sight analysis.

quotes.quote: SDSS1206+4332: The discovery is presented by . The image modeling is presented by . The time-delay measurement is presented by with an update by . The velocity dispersion measurement of 290 30 km/s presented by is based on Keck-DEIMOS spectroscopy and includes systematic uncertainties. The line-of-sight analysis is presented by .

sources.summary: SDSS1206 is highlighted as a lensed host where most host light is quadruply imaged while the AGN is doubly imaged, yielding small recovery bias.

quotes.quote: SDSS1206 SDSS1206 is a particularly interesting system for the study of the lensed host. While most of the host galaxy is quadruply imaged, the AGN falls just outside the inner caustic and is thus only doubly imaged Agn++16 . As expected, the accuracy of the inferred magnitudes and effective radius are similar to that obtained for quadruply imaged AGNs and the bias is smaller than our target accuracy. The results are summarized in Fig. fig:bias_SDSS1206 .

sources.summary: The paper uses SDSS J1206+4332/J1206 as a gravitational-lens time-delay distance probe, taking its time-delay and lens angular-diameter distance samples from H0LiCOW, estimating H0 from it alone, and constructing its likelihood by binning chain samples.

quotes.quote: For J1206, the time-delay distance and angular diameter distance of the lens D_ l are both provided, however, as samples of distributions available from the H0LiCOW website https://shsuyu.github.io/H0LiCOW/site/ https://shsuyu.github.io/H0LiCOW/site/ , for which a kernel density estimator based likelihood is implemented In the flat CDM model, our best fitting value of H_0 using J1206 alone is 69.94 5.58 67.86 6.1 depending on the flat prior of _m and H_0 , which is consistent with . .

For the J1206, we first piece-wise divide the chain samples of D_ t and D_d into small bins. Then we calculate the discrete PLF in every 2-dimension bin, the following procedure being the same as for the other data.

gmw_00001307.name: SDSS0246
gmw_00001307.overview: SDSS0246 is a double-image lensed quasar [arXiv:astro-ph/0612419, arXiv:2011.01971] that exhibits multi-epoch optical and near-infrared flux variations [arXiv:1101.1917]. These variations may be caused by chromatic microlensing [arXiv:1101.1917, arXiv:1206.0731], and the system displays redshifted iron K line emission in its brighter lensed image [arXiv:1306.0008].

sources.summary: SDSS0246 has a much fainter arc and limited modelling region; the host image is recoverable for brighter source magnitudes, but faint configurations are unreliable.

quotes.quote: SDSS0246 The system SDSS0246 was imaged with WFC3-UVIS, and it has a much fainter arc compared with other systems. Even though the pixels are smaller than with WFC3-IR and the resolution is higher, the available region for modelling the arc in the mask is quite limited in angular size. In spite of this serious challenge, \ successfully reconstructed the host image within our target accuracy, at least for the brighter range of source magnitudes considered here. The final results are summarized in Fig. fig:bias_SDSS0246 . Not surprisingly, the bias is larger than for other systems.
Our conclusion is that if the host galaxy is inferred to be as faint or fainter than 22.5 in the final analysis, the inference cannot be considered reliable given the current data quality.

sources.summary: The paper reports significant K/L flux-ratio differences in SDSS 0246 0825 and notes multi-epoch optical and near-infrared variation that could reflect microlensing or dark substructure.

quotes.quote: SDSS 0246 0825. The flux ratios are F_B/F_A=0.258 0.015 and 0.331 0.016 in the K and L' bands, respectively. The difference is inconsistent with measurement noise at >99.9 confidence.

SDSS 0246 0825. Previous observations in various filters and epochs point to significant variations with both time and wavelength. For instance, SDSS i and HST F814W measurements taken 20 months apart yielded F_B/F_A=0.340 0.016 and 0.247 0.017 , respectively ( 2005AJ....130.1967I ; CASTLES). At the same two epochs, SDSS g and HST F555W measurements gave F_B/F_A=0.310 0.010 and 0.483 0.010 . In the NIR, we find F_B/F_A=0.258 0.015 and 0.331 0.016 in K and L' . It is unclear whether the NIR discrepancy is produced by the same variations that affect the shorter wavelengths, or whether millilensing by dark matter substructure might be at play.

sources.summary: SDSS J0246-0825 has very good deconvolution, a B/A chromatic trend compatible with extinction or chromatic microlensing, and microlensing of the broad component.

quotes.quote: () SDSS J0246-0825: Deconvolution: The deconvolution is very good. The small systematic residual detectable in the vicinity of the brightest lensed image A contributes to less than 0.05 of the flux of A. Chromaticity: The observed chromatic trend in B/A is compatible with DE ( M (blue) = 0.32, M (red) = 0.34, = 0.76) or with CML (M = 0.34, (blue) = 0.73, (red) = 0.76). Contamination of image A by the host galaxy, unveiled as a ring feature close to A in is plausible but is probably very low due to the slit clipping and to the relative faintness of this feature. Broad Lines: Whatever the origin of the chromatic changes, the broad component of  is microlensed but ML of is more tentative.

sources.summary: The paper discusses SDSS J0246 0825 as a double-image lensed quasar whose lensing galaxy redshift is directly measured and whose spectrum matches an elliptical galaxy.

quotes.quote: SDSS J0246 0825 : This double-image quasar was discovered in the course of the Sloan Digital Sky Survey (SDSS) by Inada et al. ( ). The quasar has a redshift z =1.68 and an image separation of 1.04 . From the Mg II and Mg I absorption lines observed in the spectra of the two quasar components, Inada et al. ( ) estimate the redshift of the lensing galaxy to be =0.724 . Our direct redshift measurement =0.723 0.002 is in very good agreement with this value. The spectrum matches well that of an elliptical galaxy.

sources.summary: For SDSS0246, the authors note high-resolution WFC3-UVIS imaging but faint arcs, yet they still successfully reconstruct the host inference.

quotes.quote: SDSS0246 Having been imaged with WFC3-UVIS/F814W, the resolution of the data for this system (together with the remaining two systems) is much higher than for those imaged in the IR, with a drizzled pixel scale of 0 03 . However, the arcs are much fainter compared with those of other systems imaged in the IR band. As a result, fewer pixels with signal are available for the fit than for other systems. Nevertheless, the host inference is successfully reconstructed as shown in Figure  fig:image_inference (f) and Table  tab:host_measure (3 initial PSF guesses were adopted).

sources.summary: The paper models SDSS J0246 0825 as a double quasar lens, finding no second lensing galaxy in the new data and reconstructing host-galaxy ring intensity near the caustic.

quotes.quote: Lens model of the SDSS J0246 0825 system from Inada05 constrained only by the image positions and the flux ratio suggested that there might be a small faint lensing object near the primary lensing galaxy. This model closely traced the lensed arc, but no attempt was made to model its intensity. In contrast, no second lensing galaxy is seen in the data presented in this paper, but our approach models the ring intensity and as a result, reconstructs the host galaxy. As illustrated in Figure fig:0246_source , the quasar is quite close to the inner diamond-shaped caustic, and small changes to our model would result in a four image system rather than two, with two new images appearing at the brightest spot on the lensed arc.

sources.summary: The paper discusses SDSS 0246 0825 as a two-image lens with acceptable absorbed power-law fits, redshifted iron K emission, and extinction estimated from optical flux ratios.

quotes.quote: Power-law emission modified by foreground absorption gives acceptable fits for Q 1355 2257, HE 0047 1756, SDSS 0246 0825 (reduced ^2_ =1.09, 1.20, 1.15 for d.o.f.= 65 , 88, and 61, degrees of freedom, respectively), but not for PG 1115 + 080 (a reduced ^2_ =1.31 with d.o.f. = 363 ).

We detected redshifted iron K line emission (rest frame energy 6.4 keV) in the brighter image A of the two image lens HE 0047 1756 and SDSS 0246 0825, and in images A1, A2, and C of the quadruple lens PG 1115 + 080.

The differential extinctions of QJ 0158 4325, Q 1355 2257, SDSS 1004 + 4112, and HE 0435 1223 are estimated using the optical/near-IR data from CASTLES (three filters: F160W, F555W, and F814W; Kochanek et al. \ 2007), while those for SDSS 0246 0825 and HE 0047 1756 are based on the flux ratios from Inada et al. \ (2005) and Wisotzki et al. \ (2004), respectively (both have five filters).

gmw_00001308.name: HS2209
gmw_00001308.overview: HS2209 is a doubly imaged quasar system located at a redshift of 1.07 [arXiv:1706.01506, arXiv:2404.09954]. The system features two bright point-source images separated by 1.04 arcseconds, an Einstein ring, and a lensing galaxy [arXiv:1706.01506].

sources.summary: The paper confirms SDSS J2211+1929 as a lensed quasar system with two bright images, a lens galaxy and Einstein ring, spectroscopic redshift, and SIE lens parameters.

quotes.quote: The images show two bright point sources separated by 1.04 ^ . Despite the complex structure in the PSF, a slight excess can be seen which we associate with the lens galaxy (Figure fig:2211_psfsubtracted , left). A simple pairwise PSF subtraction, in which Image A is subtracted from Image B and vice versa, reveals the lens galaxy as well as an Einstein ring (Figure fig:2211_psfsubtracted , right). The extracted 1D spectra are consistent with coming from the same object with emission lines C III ] , C II, Mg II, H-gamma, and H-beta at redshift z_s = 1.07 .

The SIE fit with fluxes infers an ellipticity e = 0.186_ 0.009 ^ +0.001 and position angle _e = -29.7_ 0.7 ^ +0.3 . As with J0941+0518, the fit without flux observations does not reliably constrain the ellipticity position angle, but including fluxes with 20 uncertainties significantly improves the constraints. The inferred mass centroid from the SIE fit again agrees with the light centroid from the psf-subtracted images.

sources.summary: HS2209 has two HST visits modeled separately; the authors recover consistent host magnitudes and adopt the better-residual visit as the final estimate.

quotes.quote: HS2209 HS2209 was imaged by \ during two visits ( vis05 and vis06 ) at different orientations. We modelled the two visits separately and recovered mutually consistent host galaxy magnitudes. However, we found that the data from vis06 can be modelled with smaller residuals; thus the inference based on vis06 was adopted as our best estimate (using 7 initial PSF stars), as listed in Table  tab:host_measure . The inference for this system is summarized in Figure  fig:image_inference (g).

sources.summary: The paper describes B2209+1904 as a z=1.07 doubly imaged quasar whose two X-band images have a near-unity radio flux ratio and very low random-alignment probability.

quotes.quote: B2209+1904 (aka J2211+1929), a doubly-imaged quasar at z=1.07 , was catalogued, along with its lens galaxy, in the Hamburg Quasar Survey hamburg . Our X-band observations detected both quasar images as point sources with a flux ratio of A/B = 1.1 0.2 . This is slightly, but not significantly, lower than the optical flux ratio observed by Gaia in the g band of 1.5 . The chance of two random radio sources being in these positions is 7 10^ 12 .

sources.summary: HS2209 is the most challenging system in the sample by AGN brightness, yet its simulated host-galaxy recovery usually remains within the target bias level.

quotes.quote: HS2209 It is worth noting that HS2209 is the most challenging system in our sample with the AGN brightness approaching 15 mag, in the image plane. The results of HS2209 is summarized in Fig. fig:bias_HS2209 with generally larger bias than SDSS0246. Nevertheless, in most cases, the bias levels are within our target.

gmw_00001309.name: HE0047
gmw_00001309.overview: HE0047 is a doubly imaged lensed quasar [arXiv:1610.03732, arXiv:1007.3142] that exhibits a faint Einstein ring [arXiv:1007.3142] and varies in brightness [arXiv:1610.03732, arXiv:2306.03472]. Its gravitational lens is a massive elliptical galaxy [arXiv:2009.14232] located at a redshift of approximately 0.407 [arXiv:1007.3142, arXiv:astro-ph/0511026].

sources.summary: The paper monitors the lensed quasar HE 0047-1756, finding 0.2-0.3 mag component variability, a 7.6 day time delay, secular microlensing, and a bluer-when-brighter trend.

quotes.quote: The two lensed components of quasar HE 0047-1756 varied by 0.2-0.3 mag within five years. We provide, for the first time, an estimate of the time delay of component B with respect to A of t= (7.6 1.8) days for this object. We also find evidence for a secular evolution of the magnitude difference between components A and B in both filters, which we explain as due to a long-duration microlensing event.
