Finally we find that both quasars WFI 2033-4723 and HE 0047-1756 become bluer when brighter, which is consistent with previous studies.

The quasar HE 0047-1756, see Fig. fig:he0047_stamps , was discovered in the ESO Hamburg quasar survey ( , , ). found that the quasar is in fact a lensed system with two observable images that are separated by =1.44'' and these authors estimated the quasar redshift at z_Q=1.67 .

sources.summary: The paper uses lensed QSO HE 0047 1756 to probe a z=0.408 massive elliptical lens galaxy, detecting H2 absorption whose kinematics are consistent with gas bound to the galaxy.

quotes.quote: The observations use the brighter image of doubly lensed QSO HE 0047 1756 to probe the ISM of the massive ( M_ star 10^ 11 M_ ) elliptical lens galaxy at z_ gal =0.408 . Using Hubble Space Telescope 's Cosmic Origins Spectrograph (COS), we obtain a medium-resolution FUV spectrum of the lensed QSO and identify numerous absorption features from H_2 in the lens ISM at projected distance d=4.6 kpc. The H_2 column density is N( H_2 )/ =17.8^ +0.1 _ 0.3 with a molecular gas fraction of f_ H_2 =2-5 , roughly consistent with some local quiescent galaxies.

A visual inspection of our HST /COS spectrum of HE 0047 1756 A reveals the presence of numerous absorption features consistent with the H_2 Lyman and Werner bands at redshift z 0.406 , or approximately 430 \ from the systemic redshift of the lens galaxy (see Figure 2). While the velocity offset of the H_2 absorption features may seem large for ISM gas, it is partly explained by the uncertainty on the lens redshift ( 200 ; Z16). Furthermore, the projected escape velocity at =5 kpc from the lens galaxy is 400-500 \ given the estimated mass of its host dark-matter halo (Z16), so the observed H_2 kinematics is consistent with ISM gas that is bound to the galaxy.

sources.summary: HE0047 1756 is included among lensed quasars with previous Mg II masses, new Balmer-line comparisons, variability folded into photometry, and lower luminosity among the new estimates.

quotes.quote: For instance, 2017Giannini demonstrated that HE0047 1756 varied by 0.2-0.3 over a five-year period, and WFI2033 4723 varies by 0.5 mag in four years. The system HE0435 1223 varied 0.4 mag ( 2011Ricci ) and more recently, 2017bonvin presented 13-year light curves, with a variability ambplitude of 0.7 mag.

The systems HE0047 1756, HE0435 1223, SDSS0924+0219, SDSS1226 0006, LBQS1333+0113, Q1355 2257 and WFI2033 4723 have previous estimates of M _ BH using the Mg II emission lines ( 2006Peng,2012Sluse, 2017DingI ) which we compare to our Balmer lines estimates in Figure fig:halphavsmgii . Lensed quasars that have one or both M _ BH estimates presented in this work are shown in color. In general, our estimates are well correlated after we apply the offset of 2016Mejiarestrepo (0.16 dex for M _ BH measured with H , and 0.25 dex using Mg II ).
In particular, we were able to obtain estimates for the lower luminosity systems QJ0158 4325, SDSS0924+0219, HE0512 3329 and HE0047 1756 (from 10 ^ 44 to 10 ^ 46.5 ).

sources.summary: HE0047-1756 has slightly imperfect deconvolution, a monotonic B/A color trend, and microlensing of the blue wing, with possible tentative microlensing in another line.

quotes.quote: (a) HE0047-1756: Deconvolution: The deconvolution is slightly less good than for other systems. The total flux left in the residual under the QSO images never exceeds 0.1 of the flux of the QSO image. Although this is a very small amount of flux, this appears as a systematic feature suggesting that the PSF is less representative of the QSO images than in other systems.

Broad Lines: Whatever the origin of the chromatic changes, the blue wing of is microlensed. Microlensing of the blue wing of is also tentatively observed but this is more uncertain owing to the proximity of this line from the red edge of the spectrum.

sources.summary: HE0047 is the lowest-SNR lensed-arc system in the sample, producing host magnitudes with relatively large uncertainty and weighted modeling choices.

quotes.quote: HE0047 HE0047 is the most challenging system in our sample with the lowest SNR of the lensed arcs. The results, based on 3 initial PSFs, are summarized in Figure  fig:image_inference (f) and Table  tab:host_measure . The host magnitude of the HE0047 system has a relatively large uncertainty as reflected in the error bars.

We considered a set of modelling choices to perform the fitting and the final inference is based on a weighting of top-ranked choices. In particular, we treat the two different modelling approaches, i.e., noise boost and PSF iteration equally, to derive the averaged results.

sources.summary: The paper treats HE 0047-1756 as a doubly imaged quasar with an elliptical lens at z=0.407, and reports a faint Einstein ring from deconvolution.

quotes.quote: HE 0047-1756 (a) This object (RA = 00^ h 50^ m 27^ s .82 and DEC = 17^ 40'08 79 ) was discovered by Wisotzki2000 in the framework of the Hamburg/ESO Survey (HES) for bright quasars, covering the Southern sky. It was later identified by Wisotzki2004 as a doubly imaged quasar at a redshift of z_ s =1.68 . The lens is an elliptical galaxy with a spectroscopic redshift of z_ l =0.407 0.001 Cosmograil3, Ofek2006 .
HE 0047-1756 (a) A faint Einstein ring, stretched image of the quasar host galaxy, is revealed by the deconvolution.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes HE 0047 1756 as a gravitational lens, finds acceptable absorbed power-law X-ray fits, and estimates differential extinction from Wisotzki et al. flux ratios.

quotes.quote: In this paper, we analyze \ observations of 10 gravitational lenses, HE 0047 1756 (Wisotzki et al. \ 2004; Ofek et al. \ 2006), QJ 0158 4325 (Maza et al. \ 1995; Morgan et al. \ 1999; Faure et al. \ 2009), SDSS 0246 0805 (Inada et al. \ 2005; Eigenbrod et al. \ 2006), HE 0435 1223 (Wisotzki et al. \ 2002; Ofek et al. \ 2006), SDSS 0924+0219 (Inada et al. \ 2003a; Ofek et al. \ 2006), SDSS 1004+4112 (Inada et al. \ 2003b), PG 1115+080 (Weymann et al. \ 1980; Kundi et al. \ 1997), Q 1355 2257 (Morgan et al. \ 2003; Eigenbrod et al. \ 2006), HE 1104 1805 (Wisotzki 1993; Lidman et al. \ 2000), and Q 2237+0305 (Huchra et al. \ 1985), to measure the differential X-ray absorption between the lensed images, the metallicity, and the dust-to-gas ratio of lens galaxies.

In this paper, we only discuss the fits to Q 1355 2257, SDSS 0246 0805, HE 0047 1756, and PG 1115+080 in detail (Figure fig:spectra ). Power-law emission modified by foreground absorption gives acceptable fits for Q 1355 2257, HE 0047 1756, SDSS 0246 0825 (reduced ^2_ =1.09, 1.20, 1.15 for d.o.f.= 65 , 88, and 61, degrees of freedom, respectively), but not for PG 1115 + 080 (a reduced ^2_ =1.31 with d.o.f. = 363 ).

The differential extinctions of QJ 0158 4325, Q 1355 2257, SDSS 1004 + 4112, and HE 0435 1223 are estimated using the optical/near-IR data from CASTLES (three filters: F160W, F555W, and F814W; Kochanek et al. \ 2007), while those for SDSS 0246 0825 and HE 0047 1756 are based on the flux ratios from Inada et al. \ (2005) and Wisotzki et al. \ (2004), respectively (both have five filters).

sources.summary: The lens in HE 0047 1756 is confirmed at redshift 0.407, with a higher signal-to-noise spectrum well matched by an elliptical galaxy template.

quotes.quote: We confirm four previously measured redshifts: HE 0047 1756 ( =0.407 ), HE 0230 2130 ( =0.523 ), HE 0435 1223 ( =0.454 ) and WFI J2033 4723 ( =0.661 ).

HE 0047 1756 : A doubly imaged quasar discovered by Wisotzki et al. ( ). It has a redshift of z=1.67 and a maximum image separation of 1.44 . The redshift of the lensing galaxy has recently been measured by Ofek et al. ( ) at =0.408 . We confirm this result, with =0.407 0.001 , and present a much higher signal-to-noise spectrum in Fig. HE0047_lens . An elliptical galaxy template matches well the spectrum of the lens.

sources.summary: HE0047 behaves like the other double-image systems, with larger model-parameter offsets than quads because quads provide more information and greater magnification.

quotes.quote: HE0047 The results for HE0047 are summarized in Fig. fig:bias_HE0047 . They are similar to those obtained for the other doubles SDSS0246 and HS2209, and show a larger offset between input and output model parameters than is seen in the quads. This is easily understood because the quads have more information and in general larger magnifications.

sources.summary: The paper identifies HE0047 1756 as a doubly imaged lensed quasar whose lens galaxy spectrum matches a blue Sb+Sc template at z=0.408 without template emission lines.

quotes.quote: HE0047 1756 : A doubly imaged lensed quasar discovered by Wisotzki et al. (2004), with an image separation of 1 44 . The continuum and absorption features of the lens galaxy spectrum, shown in Fig. HE0047 , are best matched by an Sb + Sc template (with a mix ratio of 9:1 ), at redshift z=0.408 . We note, however, that the emission lines in the template are absent from the lens galaxy spectrum.

gmw_00001310.name: NGC 1317
gmw_00001310.overview: NGC 1317 is a double-barred spiral galaxy [arXiv:1406.2868] with an evolved bar [arXiv:astro-ph/0504646] and a central star-forming ring [arXiv:1406.2868, arXiv:2006.11828]. Likely a member of the NGC 1316 group [arXiv:1907.08265], it is gas-deficient [arXiv:1907.08265, arXiv:2101.10347] with its neutral hydrogen disk sharply truncated at the stellar disk boundary [arXiv:2101.10347].

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 1317 as a likely group member near NGC 1316, with an H I disk confined to the stellar disk, gas deficiency, no tidal asymmetry, and uncertain 3D distance.

quotes.quote: Besides NGC 1316, the observed field includes three relatively large, rich galaxies: NGC 1317, NGC 1310 and PGC 12706 (in order of increasing projected distance from NGC 1316; see Fig. fig:overlay ). These have a +200 , 0 and 350 \ offset relative to the recessional velocity of NGC 1316, respectively. The first two are likely part of the NGC 1316 group. NGC 1310 is slightly in the foreground fomalont1989,anderson2018 , while the exact 3D location of NGC 1317 is more uncertain.

Our data do not reveal any striking new \ features in NGC 1317 compared with the data of . Despite our slightly more sensitive data, we confirm their conclusion that the \ disc is confined to within the stellar disc and that the galaxy is deficient, indicating that some gas has likely been removed from its outskirts possibly following the interaction with the intra-group medium. We also confirm the lack of any asymmetries or tidal features in the \ disc. It is therefore likely that the 3D distance between NGC 1317 and the 10 times more massive NGC 1316 is too large for NGC 1317 to be tidally perturbed.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 1317 as an ancient infaller in advanced pre-processing, with low recent star formation, settled disc, red color, gas deficiency, and outside-in quenching signatures.

quotes.quote: NGC 1317 NGC 1317 is an ancient infaller into the group Raj2020 in an advanced stage of pre-processing Kleiner2021 . The AD implies recent histories that are not too different from secular ageing, with some measurable very recent star formation, although among the lowest in the sample (like all three the advanced-stage galaxies). The disc has settled, which suggests that the galaxy has not been affected by any recent (< 1 Gyr) environmental interactions. The galaxy has a red colour Raj2020 , and is deficient (with clouds nearby), as discussed in Kleiner2021 .

Raj2020 found no star formation beyond the inner 0.5 disc. This agrees with the results from phase-space analysis that suggest that the galaxy has passed through the pericentre and lost its gas through interactions with the IGrM (see also Serra2019 ). This galaxy can be transitioning from a spiral to a lenticular galaxy, similar to NGC 1436 Loni2023 .

sources.summary: The appendix describes NGC 1317 as a double-barred spiral with star formation in a ring-like area, using color and HST imaging to highlight dust structure and bar-driven ellipticity.

quotes.quote: Here we briefly add a few morphological remarks on NGC 1317 on the basis of a colour map, as has been done for NGC 1316 in Paper I, and HST-images, which to our knowledge have not yet been shown in the literature. NGC 1317 is a double-barred spiral galaxy with star formation occurring within a ring-like area. In Fig. fig:1317colour , the upper panel is a C-image, showing better the dust structures, while the lower panel is a C-R colour image (compare the colour image of NGC 1316 in Paper I). It is striking that the ellipticity of these two images is so different. We attribute this to the outer secondary bar laurikainen06 , which produces the ellipticity, but is not distinct in colour from the overall population.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 1317 as a clear evolved-bar case, using radial velocity-dispersion profiles and elevated bar-disk dispersion differences as evidence for vertical bar growth.

quotes.quote: Clear instances of evolved bars are the ones in NGC 1302, NGC 1317 and NGC 5850. Their _z radial profiles resemble Fig(s). 5(b) and 6(d). These galaxies have _ z, bar equal to 100, 145 and 60 Km/s. Although the latter value is clearly much lower, this is a result from the fact that NGC 5850 is of a later morphological class than the former galaxies. In fact, _z 30 Km/s in the three cases, an evidence that these bars have vertically grown up from their parent disks.

sources.summary: FCC022/NGC 1317 is described as an early-type spiral near NGC 1316, with H I confined to the stellar disc, a central star-forming ring, and gas loss after pericentre passage.

quotes.quote: FCC022 Also known as NGC 1317, is a spiral galaxy (SA pec) with morphological type T=1 , located at closest projected distance from NGC 1316, that is, D_ core = 0.025 deg from NGC 1316. The H i disc of the galaxy is confined to its stellar disc and has H i mass M_ hi = 26.4 0.6 10^7 M_ horell01, serra19 , while in the central part of this galaxy, that is, at R 0.5 ^ , a star-forming ring is visible. It appears to be an early-type spiral, with almost no star-forming regions beyond R 0.5 ^ , which can be seen in its SB image (Appendix FCC022 ). This can be justified with the results from phase-space analysis that FCC022 has passed through the pericentre and lost its neutral gas through interactions with the intra-group medium see also serra19 .

sources.summary: NGC 1317 is H I deficient with gas sharply truncated at the stellar disk boundary, consistent with removal of its outer disk by ram pressure or gentle tides.

quotes.quote: The in NGC 1317 is sharply truncated at the boundary of the stellar disc. Given its stellar mass and morphology, NGC 1317 is deficient by at least an order of magnitude (discussed in detail in section sec:cool_gas ).

Both these galaxies have no break (Type-I) in their stellar radial profile Raj2020 , showing no sign of disruption to their stellar body and their confined to the stellar disc, implying that the outer disc has been removed. Ram pressure or gentle tidal interactions are likely to be responsible for removing the outer disc of these galaxies.

sources.summary: The paper classifies NGC 1317 as a pseudobulge galaxy, citing The bulge has a prominent nuclear bar and spiral; n_b=1.4 , and the bulges is actively forming stars with M_ 3.6 M_ 8.0 =0.4 mag.

quotes.quote: NGC 1317 .SXR1../Sa Pseudobulge. The bulge has a prominent nuclear bar and spiral. The decomposition yields n_b=1.4 , and the bulges is actively forming stars with M_ 3.6 M_ 8.0 =0.4 mag.

gmw_00001311.name: NGC 1310
gmw_00001311.overview: NGC 1310 is a late-type barred spiral galaxy [arXiv:1907.08265, arXiv:2006.11828] featuring star-forming spiral arms [arXiv:2006.11828] and a younger central stellar population [arXiv:2311.15624]. Its extended neutral hydrogen disk contains a central depression [arXiv:1907.08265] and is surrounded by asymmetric gas extensions and anomalous-velocity clouds [arXiv:2101.10347].

sources.summary: The paper reports more sensitive H I data for NGC 1310, finding a symmetric column-density distribution, no sign of interaction with NGC 1316, a central H I depression, and an extended H I disk.

quotes.quote: Our data for NGC 1310 are significantly more sensitive than those of because of our larger primary beam (the galaxy is 20 \ from the pointing centre). Unlike in that previous study, we find the \ column density to be symmetric across the disc. Therefore, this galaxy, too, shows no signs of interaction with NGC 1316. At the resolution of our data there appears to be an \ depression in the galaxy centre. Furthermore, the \ disc is more extended than the stellar disc, which is common for galaxies of this late morphological type.

sources.summary: FCC013/NGC 1310 is described as a barred spiral with an extended H I disc, star-forming arms, a 7.8 kpc Type III disc break, and a redder outer disc.

quotes.quote: FCC013 Also known as NGC 1310, is a barred-spiral galaxy (SBcII) with morphological type T=5 , located at a projected distance D_ core = 0.25 deg from NGC 1316. The H i disc of this galaxy is more extended than its stellar disc, with M _ hi = 48.0 0.9 10^7 M_ serra19 . The galaxy has an effective radius R_ e, = 27.6 ^ and star-forming spiral arms. We detect a Type III disc-break in its SB profile and, hence, the break radius is 7.8 kpc. The outer disc appears redder than the inner disc with average (g-i)_ h_ out h_ in = 0.44 mag.

sources.summary: NGC 1310 is a massive late-type galaxy with asymmetric H I extensions and anomalous-velocity clouds that may reflect external gas accretion or cooling.

quotes.quote: Finally, the massive late-type galaxy NGC 1310 is surrounded by extensions and clouds of different velocities, which is unusual, because it is a relatively (compared to NGC 1317) isolated galaxy, with an undisturbed optical spiral morphology and a uniformly rotating disc. Despite the coarse velocity resolution, we can determine from our observations that the majority of the extensions and clouds (except for the extended component of the disc to the south) are not rotating with the disc (Fig. fig:mom1 ) and cover a broad range ( 1450 -- 1950 ) in velocity, suggesting that it may be anomalous gas from an external origin. Future data from the MFS Serra2016 with better velocity resolution will clarify this point.

Given the presence of the filaments in the vicinity of NGC 1310, the remaining clouds may be a result of hot gas, cooling in the IGM (and hot halo of NGC 1316) and being captured or accreted onto this galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper classifies NGC 1310 as an intermediate infaller in ongoing pre-processing, with extended recent star formation, disturbed asymmetric outer features, and a younger central population.

quotes.quote: NGC 1310 NGC 1310 is an intermediate infaller in the group Raj2020 , and is in an ongoing pre-processing stage Kleiner2021 . The EW(H ) profile has slight variations but hovers around log EW(H ) 1 out to 1.0 R_ e , indicating a spatially-extended high recent sSFR, which is still consistent with the secular ageing sequence of the AD. The galaxy has extended features that are disturbed and asymmetric, and the outer disc appears redder than the inner disc with average (g - i)_ h_ out h_ in = 0.44 mag Raj2020 on a scale of more than 3.0 R_ e . Our gradient from the mass-weighted SSP-equivalent ages also shows a younger population at the centre.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 1310 as an observed case where intervening-galaxy magnetic structure depolarizes background QSOs through spiral-arm and interarm screen differences.

quotes.quote: For NGC 1310 this effect was observed by and . One possibility is that the source covers both the spiral arms and interarm regions.
In this way the area covered by the spiral arms would be depolarized and the interarms would produce a coherent screen.

gmw_00001312.name: ESO 301-IG 11
gmw_00001312.overview: ESO 301-IG 11 is a late-type spiral and collisional ring galaxy [arXiv:2006.11828, arXiv:2101.10347] that is undergoing pre-processing [arXiv:2311.15624, arXiv:2101.10347]. Its distorted ring contains star-forming regions [arXiv:2006.11828, arXiv:1907.08265], and its extended neutral hydrogen disk is likely tidally disturbed [arXiv:2006.11828, arXiv:2101.10347].

sources.summary: FCC028 is presented as a late-type spiral/ring galaxy whose ring contains star-forming regions likely from a collision, with an extended H I disc and a 3.9 kpc Type III break.

quotes.quote: FCC028 FCC028 is a spiral galaxy (SmIII) with morphological type T=9 , located at projected distance D_ core = 0.28 deg from NGC 1316. From literature, the galaxy has been associated to a ring galaxy jane86, corwin85, lau82 . The ring can be seen as star forming regions in the SB image FCC028 , which has likely originated from a collision Maddox2019 . The H i disc of the galaxy is more extended than its stellar disc and has H i mass M_ hi = 13.7 0.6 10^7 M_ serra19 . We detect a Type III disc-break in its SB profile and its break radius is at 3.9 kpc.

sources.summary: The paper describes ESO 301-IG11 as an ongoing pre-processing galaxy with a flat inner EW(H) profile, distributed very young stars, blue color, and a flat age gradient.

quotes.quote: ESO 301-IG11 This galaxy is in an ongoing stage of pre-processing Kleiner2021 . The slit was placed along the major axis, and we could extract EW(H ) up to 1.5 R_ e . The integrated value is consistent with the secular ageing sequence of the AD. We measure a flat EW(H ) profile within 1.0 R_ e , showing that the population of very young stars (<10 Myr) is distributed throughout the galaxy, with a much smaller fraction of very young stars outside of 1.0 R_ e . The galaxy is blue in colour, although the outer stellar disc is redder than the inner disc Raj2020 .

sources.summary: The paper reports the first H I detection in PGC 12706, describing a bright unresolved gas source and a peculiar optical morphology with a distorted star-forming ring likely caused by a collision.

quotes.quote: Finally, we detect for the first time \ in PGC 12706. This galaxy has a peculiar optical morphology characterised by a bright, distorted ring of star formation, likely originating from a collision madore2009 . The \ is unresolved in our data and, therefore, gives us no clue about the origin of the ring. The \ is very bright, though, and future observations with higher angular and velocity resolution will be useful to understand the nature of this object.

sources.summary: ESO 301-IG 11 is described as a collisional ring galaxy with a slightly extended, likely tidally disturbed H I disk and ongoing pre-processing signatures.

quotes.quote: The collisional ring galaxy ESO 301-IG 11 has a slightly extended disc, where the extension is in the south-east direction (away from the group centre). As suggested by its classification, the is likely to have been tidally disturbed in the collision that formed the ring.

The galaxies that show current signatures of pre-processing (i.e. the ongoing category) are FCC 35, ESO 301-IG 11, NGC 1310, NGC 1316, and NGC 1326. In general, these galaxies have tails or asymmetric extended emission, typical and H _ 2 ratios (for the galaxies with H _ 2 observations) that follow the median xGASS trends in Fig. fig:scaling_rels .

gmw_00001313.name: FCC 35
gmw_00001313.overview: FCC 35 is a dwarf late-type spiral galaxy [arXiv:2006.11828, arXiv:2101.10347] that exhibits extremely strong, narrow emission lines characteristic of a blue compact dwarf or active starburst [arXiv:2311.15624]. It features a long, asymmetric neutral hydrogen tail [arXiv:2101.10347] and is currently undergoing interactions that have enhanced its recent star formation [arXiv:2311.15624].

sources.summary: The paper identifies FCC 35 as an ongoing pre-processing dwarf with very high recent sSFR, strong narrow emission lines, asymmetric star formation, and recent interactions.

quotes.quote: FCC 35 This galaxy is an intermediate infaller in the group Raj2020 , and is in an ongoing stage of pre-processing Kleiner2021 . It has the highest EW(H ) of the group sample (that is, very recent high sSFR). Its EW(H ) profile decreases from the centre outward. The spectra show extremely strong and narrow emission lines indicative of a blue compact dwarf or an active starburst galaxy.

We also note that the recent sSFR of FCC 35 is very enhanced but asymmetric (i.e. more so on the side of the galaxy in the direction of the long tail). Neither tidal nor hydrodynamical forces can be ruled out from the analysis, but it is clearly currently / recently undergoing some interaction(s) Kleiner2021 .

sources.summary: FCC035 is described as the faintest sample spiral, with a 2.8 kpc Type III break, a bluer outer disc, and H emission concentrated in a spiral-arm star-forming region.

quotes.quote: FCC035 FCC 035 is a spiral galaxy (SmIV) with morphological type T=9 , and is located at a projected distance D_ core = 0.44 deg from NGC 1316. It is the faintest galaxy in our sample M_i= -17.39 and M_*= 0.017 10^ 10 M_ . We detect a Type III disc break in its SB profile with break radius = 2.8 kpc. The outer disc is bluer than its inner disc with (g-i)_ h_ out h_ in = -0.36 mag. The H (Raj et al. in prep) distribution in the galaxy is concentrated in a star-forming region located in its spiral arms, which explains the blue ( g-i ) colours.

sources.summary: FCC 35 is a blue dwarf late-type galaxy with a long, irregular H I tail; the paper cannot distinguish tidal from hydrodynamical origins.

quotes.quote: The dwarf late-type galaxy FCC 35 has a long, asymmetric (kinematically irregular) tail pointing away from the group centre. The two closest galaxies (spatially with confirmed redshifts) are NGC 1316C and FCC 46, a dwarf late type and dwarf early type. These two galaxies have unresolved and are more deficient than the majority of the group galaxies. Neither a dynamical interaction between these galaxies nor a hydrodynamical mechanism (such as ram pressure) can be ruled out as the cause for the long, tail of FCC 35.

gmw_00001314.name: NGC 1326
gmw_00001314.overview: NGC 1326 is a barred galaxy in the Fornax cluster [arXiv:1410.5120] that has been classified as both a lenticular [arXiv:1811.00984] and a spiral galaxy [arXiv:2006.11828, arXiv:2101.10347]. It features a recently formed central bar [arXiv:astro-ph/0504646] surrounded by multiple resonance rings [arXiv:2304.00515, arXiv:astro-ph/0603124] and hosts a circumnuclear star-forming region [arXiv:1410.5120, arXiv:1811.00984].

sources.summary: The paper models NGC 1326 from HST light and TripleSpec line-width data, finding a normal stellar M/L and evidence that a 10^7 solar-mass black hole improves the fit.

quotes.quote: NGC 1326 is a ring barred S0 galaxy in the Fornax cluster with circumnuclear star formation (Buta et al 2000).

NGC 1326 has been imaged by the Hubble Space Telescope (Figure 1) and its ultraviolet light distribution is displayed in the radial profile from the IRAF STSDAS surface photometry task ellipse in Figure 2. The Jeans equation allows us to predict the velocity dispersion profile () corresponding to this light distribution, assuming spherically distributed stars on isotropic orbits. To do this, we need the logarithmic derivatives with respect to radius of the density and velocity dispersion profiles. The former is obtained numerically using an Abell transform, the latter by calculating the (small) slope of the velocity dispersion data. The visual mass to light ratio is a free parameter in this model and we fit it to the data at > 80 pc, finding M/L = 6.5 in solar units, a normal value for a stellar population not dominated by dark matter.
The addition of a 1 10 ^7 M_ black hole modifies the mass distribution and (). It is a better fit to the data than the solid line in the lower part of Figure 2.

sources.summary: For NGC 1326, the paper models ALMA torus velocity-dispersion maps, adopting an 8 pc torus radius, a 1.5e7 solar-mass SMBH, and central maxima interpreted as a torus throat.

quotes.quote: NGC 1326 and NGC 1672 velocity dispersion maps Here we apply our simulations to other AGNs for which the observational data of a torus are available. As examples, we choose NGC 1326 and NGC 1672, since the central regions are identified as the obscuring tori. Their velocity dispersion maps are presented in fig. fig:alma bottom from Combes2019 .

The major radii of the tori were taken from the ALMA observational maps: R=10 pc for NGC 1672 and R=8 pc for NGC 1326. The best combinations of the parameters are the following; for NGC 1672, the SMBH mass M_ smbh = 1 10^8 M_ ; the inclination angle of the torus = 60^ , with the relative cloud radius _ cl =0.01 or = 45^ with _ cl =0.025 ; for NGC 1326, M_ smbh = 1.5 10^7 M_ ; = 60^ with _ cl =0.01 or = 45^ with _ cl =0.025 . For all cases, the initial half-opening angle of the wind is = 45^ . Fig. fig:alma shows that our model velocity dispersion maps and the ALMA observational maps of the central regions of NGC 1672 and NGC 1326 are in qualitative agreement (as well as the NGC 1068 maps in Imanishi2018 discussed above). Velocity dispersion maps of NGC 1672 and NGC 1326 show central maxima that we identify as a torus throat, while the maximum in the left part of NGC 1326 torus can also be associated with external accretion.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 1326 as a barred lenticular with a prominent ILR ring, weak 350 GHz continuum at the CO peak, perpendicular gas structure, and dusty asymmetric star-forming ring.

quotes.quote: NGC 1326 is a lenticular barred galaxy with a contrasted ring at ILR, of radius 5.7 = 410 pc. detected radio continuum in the ring at 20, 6, and with the VLA at 3'' resolution, and also a molecular mass of 2.7 10 ^8 M _ from CO emission with the SEST-15m telescope. There is no evidence of a strong central continuum point source at centimeter wavelengths. At 350 GHz, we detect a weak continuum source, coinciding with the maximum of the CO(3-2) emission (Fig. fig:613-1326 ). About 83 of the H emission of the galaxy is coming from this nuclear ring Crocker1996 .

sources.summary: FCC029/NGC 1326 is described as the sample’s most luminous, massive double-barred spiral, with a 5.4 kpc Type III disc break and a bluer outer disc.

quotes.quote: FCC029 Also known as NGC 1326, is a barred-spiral galaxy (SBa()) with morphological type T=1 is located at a projected distance D_ core = 0.68 deg from NGC 1316. peter04 describe it as a double barred galaxy. It is the most luminous, massive galaxy in our sample of LTGs with M_i= -21.53 and M_*= 2.94 10^ 10 M_ . We detect a Type III disc-break with a break radius = 5.4 kpc. The outer disc is bluer than its inner disc with (g-i)_ h_ out h_ in =-0.24 mag.

sources.summary: NGC 1326 is a barred spiral with a ring and asymmetric southern H I emission; the feature may be tidal but instrumental effects remain possible.

quotes.quote: NGC 1326 is a barred spiral galaxy with a ring and has clumpy, extended, and asymmetric emission in the south, pointing towards the group centre. The one-sided emission could be indicative of a tidal interaction. However, this could also be an instrumental effect, as the galaxy is located very far from the pointing centre and is subjected to a variable sensitivity response. The southern side (where the tail is) is sensitive down to 6.1 10 ^ 19 atoms cm ^ 2 , while the northern side has a lower sensitivity of 2.3 10 ^ 20 atoms cm ^ 2 .
As the tails are diffuse ( < 1 10 ^ 20 atoms cm ^ 2 ), more sensitive observations are needed to determine if NGC 1326 has extended emission on the northern side.

sources.summary: The paper finds NGC 1326 has bar-linked inner and outer rings, a prominent HI ring at the outer optical ring, and enhanced SFR at resonance radii.

quotes.quote: NGC 1326: The centre left panels in Fig. fig:mosaic1 show the optical image and velocity map of the galaxy NGC 1326. This galaxy is observed to posses a bar, an inner ring and and outer ring, which is evident from the optical image. In terms of the 1 distribution, we find a very prominent 1 ring of gas almost coinciding with the outer ring seen in the optical. In addition, we observe an extended 1 feature north of the galaxy, which indicate some form external perturbation. The velocity profile is indicative of a rotating ring/disk. We derive all four resonances and find that the location of the iUHR corresponds to an inner stellar ring and at the location of the OLR, we find the outer stellar ring. Furthermore, we find that at the location of the OLR radius there is a ring of 1 gas.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 1326 as a recent infaller in ongoing pre-processing, measured only in its central core and noted as the most massive late-type sample galaxy.

quotes.quote: NGC 1326 This galaxy is a recent infaller into the group Raj2020 , and is in an ongoing stage of pre-processing Kleiner2021 . We could only measure the flux from within the central core of the galaxy (out to 0.3 R_ e ) and not from the extended diffuse stellar halo. We therefore did not include this galaxy in the AD along with the integrated values from the other galaxies. NGC 1326 is the most massive late-type galaxy in the sample, and shows plenty of star-forming regions Raj2020 .

sources.summary: The paper classifies NGC 1326 as having a recently formed, vertically thin bar, with low bar velocity dispersion and radial profiles matching expected young-bar behavior.

quotes.quote: One can say with a certain degree of confidence that NGC 1326, NGC 4394 and NGC 5383 harbor recently formed bars. Their results resemble what we would expect based on Fig(s). 5(a) and 6(a) (for NGC 1326 and NGC 5383) and 6(b) (for NGC 4394). Moreover, _ z, bar < 40 Km/s and _z < 10 Km/s (although we do not have this parameter for NGC 5383), that in fact point to these bars as being still a vertically thin structure, belonging to the plane of the disk.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 1326 as a barred ringed galaxy with an inner ring aligned with the bar and an R_1 outer ring elongated perpendicular to it.

quotes.quote: NGC 1326 is a well studied example of an (R_1)SB()0/a galaxy (Buta ). Its bar is surrounded by an inner ring which is almost exactly aligned with the bar and has roughly the same diameter. Its outer ring is clearly R_1 and is elongated perpendicular to the bar (Buta et al. , see Fig. fig:ngc1326 ).

gmw_00001315.name: FCC 46
gmw_00001315.overview: FCC 46 is a nucleated dwarf elliptical galaxy [arXiv:2101.10347, arXiv:1105.4809] that exhibits properties of both early- and late-type galaxies [arXiv:1810.00550]. It features kinematically decoupled gas [arXiv:2311.15624, arXiv:2101.10347] and a quenched outer region [arXiv:2311.15624], alongside central star formation likely triggered by a recent minor merger [arXiv:2311.15624, arXiv:2101.10347].

sources.summary: The paper describes FCC 46 as a small early-type dwarf in advanced pre-processing, quenched in its outer regions, with decoupled gas and evidence for a recent star-forming event.

quotes.quote: FCC 46 This early-type dwarf is in an advanced stage of pre-processing Kleiner2021 . It is a very small galaxy, and has very little (unresolved) that appears kinematically decoupled from the stellar body. The AD implies a recent history significantly different from secular ageing and little to no recent star formation in the outer regions. The galaxy is quenched according to the AD. The EW(H ) profile decreases sharply between 0.6 and 1.0 R_ e .

The gradient from the mass-weighted, SSP-equivalent ages shows older stars in the central region, although both the central and outer regions have ages older than 10 Gyr. analysis infer that it has recently experienced a star-forming event, such as a minor merger with a late-type dwarf where it accreted that is now in a polar ring (kinematically decoupled from the stellar body) rotating around the optical minor axis Kleiner2021 .

sources.summary: FCC 46 is a deficient dwarf elliptical with kinematically decoupled gas, consistent with external accretion and possibly a minor merger.

quotes.quote: Out of all the galaxies with , FCC 46 is the most deficient given its stellar mass. It is a dwarf elliptical with a recent star formation event and was first detected as a polar ring orbiting around the optical minor axis by . As the is kinematically decoupled from stellar body, the gas was likely accreted from an external source deRijcke2013 .

Our measured mass (Table tab:HI_prop ) is consistent with that from deRijcke2013 , although, as a result of our sensitivity at that position, we do not detect the diffuse component that shows the minor axis rotation. A minor merger event (e.g. with a dwarf late type) is consistent with the morphology and 10 ^ 7 of found in FCC 46.

sources.summary: The paper flags FDS22DWARF244/FCC46 as a dwarf with mixed early- and late-type traits, including a reddish outer spheroid, bluish embedded disk, and central star formation.

quotes.quote: The two galaxies with differing classifications (FDS22DWARF244/FCC46 and FDS16DWARF000/FCC148) are both dominated by reddish spheroid component in their outer parts, but have embedded bluish disks. Their colors within the effective radii are also clearly bluer than the ones of the red sequence galaxies. These galaxies clearly have properties of both late-type and early-type galaxies. Since they have signs of recent or ongoing star formation in their central parts we classify them as late types, consistently with BCDs.

sources.summary: The paper describes FCC046 as a nucleated, HI-detected dwarf with H and O emission and four resolved stellar populations.

quotes.quote: FCC046 This galaxy has emission in H _ and [ O iii ] .
It is nucleated and is detected in HI.
We could distinguish 4 populations in both the 1 arcsec and 1 extractions.

sources.summary: FCC 046 is described as a nucleated dS0/dE4 transition-type dwarf with H i and H-alpha detection and a suspected central metallicity drop.

quotes.quote: FCC 046 This is a dS0(dE4) galaxy. It is nucleated and detected in H i and H _ . This TTD (according to our classification) is suspected in a central metallicity drop paperII .

gmw_00001316.name: FCC 19
gmw_00001316.overview: FCC 19 is a dwarf lenticular galaxy with a stellar mass of $3.4 \times 10^8$ solar masses [arXiv:2101.10347]. Although its color is similar to star-forming galaxies, it has no detected neutral hydrogen, likely due to stripping by the tidal field and hot halo of nearby NGC 1316 [arXiv:2101.10347].

sources.summary: FCC 19 is a dwarf lenticular with no detected H I despite sensitive coverage, likely stripped by the NGC 1316 tidal field and hot halo.

quotes.quote: FCC 19 is a dwarf lenticular galaxy (Fig. fig:cutouts ) with a stellar mass of 3.4 10 ^ 8 Liu2019 . It has a g - colour of 0.58 Iodice2017 , which is similar to the colour of NGC 1310, NGC 1326, NGC 1326A, and ESO 301-IG 11 (Table tab:HI_prop ), which have regular fractions and are likely forming stars. However, no is detected in FCC 19 and we measure a 3 upper limit of -2.3 (Fig. fig:scaling_rels ) assuming a 100 line width.

FCC 19 is situated in the most sensitive part of the image, meaning that the galaxy truly does not contain . Being so close (70 kpc in projection) to NGC 1316, the tidal field and hot halo of NGC 1316 are likely to have played significant roles in removing the from FCC 19. The has likely been stripped from the galaxy and lost to the IGM.

gmw_00001317.name: NGC 5529
gmw_00001317.overview: NGC 5529 is an edge-on Sc spiral galaxy [arXiv:1804.09071, arXiv:1305.3130] featuring a box- or peanut-shaped central bulge [arXiv:1804.09071, arXiv:1305.3130] and a warped stellar and gas disk [arXiv:1507.03095, arXiv:2210.12255]. It belongs to a rich galaxy group [arXiv:1804.09071, arXiv:1507.03095] and is connected to nearby satellite companions via atomic hydrogen bridges [arXiv:1804.09071, arXiv:1209.1513].

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 5529 as an Sc edge-on group galaxy with optical warp, central B/PS structure, H I bridges to satellites, gas inflow, and underestimated MIR-FIR emission.

quotes.quote: NGC 5529 NGC 5529 is an Sc edge-on galaxy with slightly lower than 90^ inclination ( 87.9^ ). A prominent optical warp and B/PS structure in the centre are the characteristic features of this galaxy. It is part of a rich galaxy group with at least 16 other members 2007A&A...474..461I . 2004MNRAS.352..768K already studied the atomic gas content of this galaxy and discovered that NGC 5529 is connected to two of its satellites through H i bridges. They also found a bright H i ridge in the major axis position-velocity diagram, which could indicate the presence of a strong spiral arm.

2015A&A...582A..18A also found a moderate warp along the line of sight and detected a radial inflow of gas at about 15 km/s in the outer half of the disc. The latter is probably linked to the accretion of gas from the satellites. 2015A&A...582A..18A measured a total atomic hydrogen mass of 2.69 10^ 10 M_ .

sources.summary: NGC 5529 is treated as an Sc galaxy with a warp, box-shaped bulge, prominent dust lane, complex FIR profiles, and secondary peaks suggestive of spiral structure.

quotes.quote: NGC 5529 With a distance of 49.5 Mpc, NGC 5529 has a D_ 25 diameter of 6 35 and is classified as an Sc type galaxy 1999A&A...344..868X . The system shows a clear warp in POSS Blue and Red 1990MNRAS.246..458S and a box-shaped bulge 1987A&AS...70..465D . The fitted inclination is 86 94 2007A&A...471..765B , exposing a very prominent dust lane 1996A&AS..117...19D along the major axis as well as in the vertical direction 1996A&AS..117...19D . The dust distribution was previously modelled in 1999A&A...344..868X and 2007A&A...471..765B based on a global fit to optical extinction data, both determining a dust scalelength of 50 , corresponding to 11.7 kpc 2007A&A...471..765B or 11.9 kpc 1999A&A...344..868X , which is about 35 2007A&A...471..765B , respectively 50 1999A&A...344..868X , larger than their fitted stellar disc scalelength. All PACS and the SPIRE 250 m \ horizontal profiles have a clear central peak, along with two secondary peaks on the SE side at 0 8 and 1 5 from the centre and another secondary peak around 0 6 on the other side, the latter having a small plateau at PACS wavelengths.

A single dust disc with an exponential distribution does not suffice to describe the FIR emission for this galaxy, as can be concluded from the horizontal profiles, mostly due to the central regions where the profiles are relatively flat. The mentioned secondary peaks can be clearly distinguished in the images up to 250 m, but given their differing positions on either side of the central peak, one would tend to conclude it is more likely that it concerns a spiral structure rather than a few ring-like structures.

sources.summary: NGC 5529 is an Sc galaxy with bar evidence, group membership, a warped stellar and H I disk, H I bridges to companions, and extraplanar gas from interaction.

quotes.quote: NGC 5529 NGC 5529 is an Sc type galaxy located at a distance of 49.5 Mpc. Like IC 2531, NGC 5529 was also part of the sample of galaxies analysed by . In the region of the peanut-shaped bulge, find a typical figure-of-eight pattern in their optical emission line position-velocity diagram, indicating the presence of a bar. In the outer regions of the galaxy, the optical emission line profiles are again double peaked.

NGC 5529 is part of a rich galaxy group with at least 16 other members irwin07 . Its stellar disk shows a clear warp and extends out to larger (projected) radii on the NW side. In their study of the atomic gas in this galaxy, discovered that NGC 5529 is connected to two of its companions, MGC +06-31-085a and NGC 5529 B, through H i bridges.

sources.summary: The paper flags NGC 5529 as a Main-sample galaxy with vertical-profile bumps suggesting an additional extended dust component, arguing the extraplanar FIR excess is real rather than PSF or inclination artefact.

quotes.quote: Four galaxies from the Main sample (NGC 4244, ESO 373-008, NGC 5529, and NGC 5746) show bumps in their cumulative vertical profiles above the model profile which can point to the existence of another vertically extended component.

We note that this extraplanar FIR emission, which typically starts to dominate at heights 1--2 kpc above the mid-plane, cannot be explained by the scattered light of the PSF since we accounted for this effect while modelling. Also, variations in the galaxy inclination angle as described in Sect. sec:method cannot explain the observed excess of dust emission (the red spread along the model profile in Fig. fig:profiles shows a one- spread of the model profiles for varied inclination angles, as described in Sect. sec:3Ddisc ). This indicates that the observed extraplanar emission is real and produced by the dust at high distances above the mid-plane.

sources.summary: The paper finds NGC 5529 has a clearly warped disc producing strong quadrupolar signal across central and outer disc regions in the mid-plane asymmetry map.

quotes.quote: For NGC 3628, NGC 4710, NGC 5073, and NGC 5529 the mid-plane asymmetry maps are strongly affected by a quadrupolar signal (which affects at least half the extent of the galaxy itself). This signal implies that we were unable to correctly identify the disc PA, despite our careful analysis. Visual inspection of the S ^4 G images of these galaxies and corresponding unsharp masked images (Fig.  fig:sample , middle panels) identifies strong and intrinsically asymmetric features in the discs (such as warps and/or lopsided discs) which may have affected the measurement of the disc PA.

Finally, NGC 5529 also hosts a clearly warped disc, which produces a strong quadrupolar signal along both the central and the outer regions of the disc, with an opposite sign in the mid-plane asymmetry map.

sources.summary: NGC 5529 can be fit despite a slight outer warp; its disk agrees with prior V-band analysis and only weak clumpy K-band extinction is seen.

quotes.quote: NGC 5529: apart from a slight warp in the external part of the disk, a fit can be achieved, with residuals smaller than 30 in 70 of the data points. The fit of the disk is consistent with the analysis of a V-band image in XilourisSub1998 . Only tenuous, clumpy extinction can be seen in the K' image.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 5529 as having a genuine thick-disc antitruncation, a significant warp, and several nearby companions, with a bridge to MCG +06-31-085a suggesting an interaction-driven origin.

quotes.quote: That in NGC 5529 seems to be a genuine antitruncation, with the host galaxy having a significant warp. Additionally, NGC 5529 has at least four companion galaxies (Irwin et al. 2007) within 10 arcmin or 150 kpc in projection. The brighter of these satellite galaxies, MCG +06-31-085a, is around four magnitudes fainter than the main galaxy and is connected to it by an \ bridge (Kregel et al. 2004), suggesting that the unusual thick disc antitruncation could be the consequence of an interaction.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 5529 as an edge-on spiral detected as extended 30 GHz emission, with NVSS-consistent morphology and a synchrotron-like spectral index.

quotes.quote: One such object, identified as the edge-on spiral galaxy NGC 5529, was detected in the \ field. The fitted source parameters are in good agreement with the NVSS survey nvssSurvey , which detects a source 2 in extent, at a position angle of 64 , with an unresolved minor axis. Comparison of the integrated \ flux for this object ( 2.55 0.22 mJy) with the integrated flux from the NVSS catalog yields a spectral index _ 1.4 GHz/ =0.64 0.04 , consistent with synchrotron emission.

gmw_00001318.name: NGC 4038
gmw_00001318.overview: NGC 4038 is the northern component of the interacting Antennae galaxies [arXiv:0909.5186, arXiv:0807.3955], featuring a rotating disk [arXiv:astro-ph/0702341] and an elongated, ring-like nuclear structure [arXiv:astro-ph/0309377]. The galaxy exhibits intense circumnuclear star formation [arXiv:0909.5186] and a flat stellar metallicity gradient likely caused by gas redistribution during the merger [arXiv:1509.04937].

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4038 as the northern Antennae galaxy, highlighting old clusters, western-loop UV emission, intense circumnuclear activity, and a nuclear SED unlike NGC 4039.

quotes.quote: The regions (16 - 22) in the northern galaxy NGC 4038 have the lowest 24 /8 ratios and reddest UB colors as compared with other regions, which is consistent with the fact that large number of rather old (100 Myr 500 Myr) star clusters are found there ( Whitmore1999 ; Mengel2005 ) In the UV wavebands, on the contrary, the Western-loop regions (22-32) have the strongest UV emissions, largest UV excesses, and relatively strong 8 (PAH) emissions, yet low 24 /8 ratios. These are in contrast with those in the overlap regions and indicate their relatively late star formation stage, which is also evidenced by the large bubbles found there Whitmore1999 . brandl2009 also presents the IRS spectra of the UV brightest region 26, which has significantly weaker strength of the radiation field, yet stronger PAHs emission features, than do the overlap regions, in agreement with our broadband analysis.

The circumnuclear star-forming region of NGC 4038, namely region 33, has 24 /8 ratio close to that of the overlap regions, suggesting its intense current star formation activity. The prominent, yet redder, UV emission reveals that this region may also hold large post-starburst ( > 10 Myr) stellar populations. For the two nuclei regions, NGC 4039 has both weaker 8 PAH emission and 24 dust emission indicating its lower star formation activity, whereas NGC 4038 has almost the same broadband SEDs as that of the local H _ II galaxy NGC 2798 in the plotted wavelength range.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 4038/9 as the merging Antennae system, finding an offset relation caused by merger-induced recent star formation and lower [N II] emission per SFR.

quotes.quote: Our selection criteria include five objects: the edge-on spiral NGC 891, face-on spirals M51 and M83, and the interacting Antennae galaxies NGC 4038 and 4039 (4038/9 hereafter). Just as NGC 891 is the member of the sample that typifies an edge-on spiral, M51 and M83 are representative of face-on spiral galaxies and thus the most logical choices for a comparison with NGC 891. We also include the merging galaxies NGC 4038/9 to draw comparisons with more intense star-forming regions.

Due to the model degeneracy between t_ d and (see herreracamus2014 for details), M83 and NGC 4038/9 exhibit a stellar population with either a star formation duration of t_ d 20 Myr and 1 or more recent star formation episodes of t_ d =2 Myr and between 1 and 3 . This suggests these two galaxies also possess regions with higher SFRs from recently-triggered star formation, likely due to the merger (NGC 4038/9) or central starburst (M83), as we discuss above.

Alternatively, dust obscuration may become more effective in more compact star forming regions, meaning more ionizing photons go towards dust heating instead of gas heating. We used a simple model based on Starburst99 to predict the _ SFR - F_ [ NII ] 205 relation, finding that regions with more recent star formation in the merging Antennae galaxies NGC 4038/9 and the central starburst in M83 likely cause the offsets in \ emission per unit SFR surface density found between these galaxies and the normal star-forming discs.

sources.summary: HST images show NGC 4038 has chaotic dust, interface star formation, an uncertain nuclear knot, an elongated ring-like structure, and X-ray evidence for a mild starburst/hot wind.

quotes.quote: NGC 4038 and NGC 4039 (The Antennae) Perhaps the best-known system in the sequence is the Antennae. We use the spectacular HST V, I , and H images of this system from whit99 ( whit99 ; see also whit95 and Figure f:mosaic ). These images show the chaotic dust lanes and prevalent star formation in the interface between the two galaxies.

The nuclear region of the northern component of the Antennae, NGC 4038, is bracketed by star clusters in the V and I images. The position of the nucleus is most likely near the knot "J" in the classification scheme of whit95 , and close to the nuclear position identified in the radio continuum by zhang01 ( zhang01 ; using the radio continuum data of neff00 neff00 ) and in the near-infrared by meng01 . In our I-band image, this is located near the center of the frame displayed in Fig. f:n4038 . There we see an elongated ring-like structure, with an axis ratio of about 0.3 and a diameter of about 2 5 (260 pc), pointing north-northeast.

sources.summary: The paper treats The Antennae as a nearby major merger, reassesses disputed distance estimates, and recommends D = 22 +/- 3 Mpc as the best current distance.

quotes.quote: The Antennae ( 4038/39) are the nearest example of a major merger involving two gas-rich disk galaxies of comparable mass. They allow us to study processes of dissipational galaxy assembly from close up, thus providing a valuable glimpse of what must have been more frequent events in the early universe. The Antennae have been observed extensively at all wavelengths (e.g., X-rays: fabb04 ; UV: hibb05 ; optical: whit99 ; IR: gilb07 ; and 21-cm line: hibb01 ) and have also been repeatedly modeled via N-body and hydrodynamical simulations e.g., tt72,barn88,miho93,hibb03 .

Traditionally, the distance to 4038/39 has been derived from the systemic recession velocity, yielding about 20 Mpc for H_0 = 72 . Recently, this widely adopted distance has been challenged based on photometry of the presumed tip of the red giant branch (TRGB), which seems to yield a shorter distance of 13.3 1.0 Mpc and, with it, nearly 1 mag lower luminosities and smaller radii for objects in this prototypical merger.
Finally, assessing our three distance estimates we recommend using a conservative, rounded value of D = 22 3 Mpc as the best currently available distance to The Antennae.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4038 as the less disturbed Antennae component, with a nearly Gaussian central CO profile, counterclockwise disk rotation, and a distinct western arc likely produced by interaction.

quotes.quote: The CO (2--1) spectra of Fig. 4 show that the galactic disk gas of NGC 4038 is much less disturbed than that of the IAR and NGC 4039. The shape of the central CO (2--1) spectrum of NGC 4038 deviates only slightly from a Gaussian. It does not allow us to distinguish different velocity components any more than does the series of channel maps (Fig. 3).

The line shape (centred at 1640 km s ^ 1 ) and FWHM line width of 90 km s ^ 1 are in accordance with the nuclear region not being heavily disrupted by anomalous motions due to the encounter. The small velocity gradient in the disk from 1570 in the southeast of NGC 4038 to 1650 in the west agrees with counterclockwise rotation (Hibbard et al. 2001) and an almost face-on view. The western arc shows a different velocity field rising from 1520 in its northern part to 1600 in the west and to 1700 in the southwest.

sources.summary: The paper describes the Antennae as the merging NGC 4038/NGC4039 pair, emphasizing tidal tails, merger-induced star formation, old tail populations, and an uncertain 13.3-20 Mpc distance.

quotes.quote: The Antennae galaxy (NGC 4038 and NGC4039) are the most well known pair of merging galaxies, showing extended tidal tails 1972ApJ...178..623T and clear evidence for merger induced star formation and the formation of young star clusters 1995AJ....109..960W . 2005ApJ...619L..87H analyse GALEX data and conclude that in the tidal tails there is evidence for recent star formation with an age around the dynamical age of the tail ( 300 Myr), although most of the stars in the tails date from before that period. These populations of both merging galaxies are consistent with a star formation history that peaked very early 15 Gyr ago in the models of 2003AJ....126.1276K . The distance to the galaxy pair is estimated around 20 Mpc from its velocity of about 1650 km/s, which can be quite unreliable for such small distances due to anisotropies in the velocities. 2004AJ....127..660S determine a distance to the galaxies of 13.8 Mpc, based on scaling of the red giant branch tip in the old population observed at the very end of the southern tail using WFPC2 data and recently 2008arXiv0802.1045S confirm their finding, with ACS data revising the distance estimate to 13.3 Mpc.

sources.summary: The paper describes the Antenn galaxy as a colliding system whose HST-observed cluster complexes contain young massive clusters likely to merge into multi-metallic globular-cluster-like objects.

quotes.quote: Hubble Space Telescope observations of globular clusters (GCs) in the Antenn galaxy show clusters of clusters, or regions in the galaxy that span hundreds of parsecs, where many of the GCs are doomed to collide, and eventually merge.

In particular, in the Antenn galaxy (NGC 4038/NGC 4039), gravitationally bound clusters appear to exist with a sizeable difference in ages, hence possibly also metallicity. These objects will eventually merge -- and, if they survive long enough, will eventually lead to GC-like objects with bimodal, and possibly multi-modal distributions e.g. Kroupa98,PeacockEtAl2013 .

Observations of colliding galaxies such as the Antenn galaxy show bound systems of young, massive clusters. In this system, Hubble Space Telescope (HST) observations exhibit relatively small regions spanning a few hundreds of pc embracing hundreds or even thousands of young clusters, i.e. clusters of clusters or ``cluster complexes'' CC from now onwards, see e.g. and references therein Kroupa98,WhitmoreEtAl10 .

sources.summary: The paper maps dense and shocked molecular gas in NGC 4038, identifying nuclear molecular tracers, offsets between star-forming and gas-rich regions, and likely inflow-driven shocks.

quotes.quote: We present 1 ( < 100 pc) resolution maps of millimeter emission from five molecules--CN, HCN, HCO ^ + , CH _ 3 OH, and HNCO--obtained towards NGC 4038, which is the northern galaxy of the mid-stage merger, Antennae galaxies, with the Atacama Large Millimeter/submillimeter Array. Three molecules (CN, CH _ 3 OH, and HNCO) were detected for the first time in the nuclear region of NGC 4038. High-resolution mapping reveals a systematic difference in distributions of different molecular species and continuum emission.

In this study, we focus on the nuclear region of NGC 4038, which is the northern galaxy of the Antennae galaxies, and investigate the distributions of the dense and shocked gas at a 100 pc scale. The purposes of this study are to reveal dusty star forming regions using dense gas tracers and continuum emission, and search for the presence of gas inflows using potential molecular line tracers of shocks (SiO, CH _ 3 OH, HNCO).

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 4038's northern nucleus with X-ray source 25, notes nearby hard sources in a molecular-gas-rich region, and finds the nucleus has modest X-ray luminosity.

quotes.quote: The northern nucleus: NGC 4038 Based on the radio coordinates of the Northern nucleus we identify it as our source 25. The offsets of the centroid of this source (0.3-10.0 keV band) from the 4 cm and 6 cm radio sources are 1.1'' and 1.87'' , respectively. In this region there are three more sources (sources 24, 22, 26), of which the last two are only detected above 2.0 keV. This is not surprising, given the large amounts of molecular gas present in this region (Wilson 2000). The luminosity of the nucleus is modest ( 10^ 39 ) just slightly higher than the luminosity of two of the neighboring sources.

sources.summary: The paper reports a CS(5-4) detection toward the Antennae/NGC 4038 nucleus, notes its interacting star-forming context, and estimates its source-averaged CS column density.

quotes.quote: In this letter, we report in the center of M 82 the first detections of the CS(5-4) and CS(7-6) transitions and the detection of the CS(5-4) line towards the Antennae galaxies (NGC 4038).
High resolution ^ 12 CO(1-0) maps indicate that the nearby (D = 13.8Mpc, see Savi04 ) Antennae interacting galaxies are likely sites of rapid high mass star formation Wils00 .

For the NGC 4038 nucleus, we were not able to perform a similar study as no other transitions of CS were observed towards this source. However, we make a rough estimate the source-averaged total CS column density from the CS(5-4) line in NGC 4038 to be N(CS) = 1.8 10^ 13 cm ^ 2 .

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 4038 as an interacting-galaxy TRGB case, favoring Schweizer et al.'s fainter tip measurement over a brighter value from a young-population field.

quotes.quote: An additional illustration is provided by the interacting galaxy NGC 4038. Saviane:etal:08 , considering an ACS HST field containing clear tracers of a young population, found in the quadrant most distant of the star-forming tidal tail two discontinuities along the red-giant branch, the one at m^ * _ I =26.65 and the other one at 27.5 . The authors identified the brighter one with the TRGB. However, Schweizer:etal:08 applied a Sobel edge-detection kernel to the whole ACS field and convincingly showed the TRGB to lie at m^ * _ I =27.46 0.12 ; it is the faintest TRGB so far measured.

sources.summary: The paper determines NGC 4038 stellar metallicity from three super star clusters, finding near-solar average metallicity and a flat gradient attributed to gas redistribution after strong interaction.

quotes.quote: We present a direct determination of the stellar metallicity in the close pair galaxy NGC 4038 (D = 20 Mpc) based on the quantitative analysis of moderate resolution KMOS/VLT spectra of three super star clusters (SSCs). The method adopted in our analysis has been developed and optimised to measure accurate metallicities from atomic lines in the J band of single red supergiant (RSG) or RSG-dominated star clusters. Hence, our metallicity measurements are not affected by the biases and poorly understood systematics inherent to strong line H 2 methods which are routinely applied to massive data sets of galaxies. We find [ Z ] = +0.07 0.03 and compare our measurements to H 2 strong line calibrations. Our abundances and literature data suggest the presence of a flat metallicity gradient, which can be explained as redistribution of metal-rich gas following the strong interaction.
Here we analyse KMOS/VLT spectra of three SSCs to measure the central metallicity and the metallicity gradient across the disk of NGC 4038.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4038 as an Antennae component with high sSFR, a central SFR peak, and efficient star formation at the southern tidal-arm branching point.

quotes.quote: NGC 4038 and NGC 4039, both of which are a part of the Antennae Galaxies, have sSFR higher than 1.0 10 ^ 9 yr ^ 1 .
A peak of SFR in NGC 4038 (top) is at its centre, while a peak of NGC 4039 (bottom) is off the centre.
The second most efficient star formation is seen at a branching point of the southern tidal arm of NGC 4038.

gmw_00001319.name: DES J0408-5354
gmw_00001319.overview: DES J0408-5354 is an early-type lensing galaxy located at a redshift of z = 0.597 [arXiv:1702.00072]. It acts as a gravitational lens for a background quasar at z = 2.375, producing multiple lensed images [arXiv:1702.00072, arXiv:2007.01308].

sources.summary: The paper reports DES J0408-5354 as the first DES quad-like lensed quasar system, with three quasar images at z=2.375 and an early-type lens at z=0.597.

quotes.quote: We report the discovery and spectroscopic confirmation of the quad-like lensed quasar system \ found in the Dark Energy Survey (DES) Year 1 (Y1) data. This system was discovered during a search for DES Y1 strong lensing systems using a method that identified candidates as red galaxies with multiple blue neighbors. \ consists of a central red galaxy surrounded by three bright ( i < 20 ) blue objects and a fourth red object. Subsequent spectroscopic observations using the Gemini South telescope confirmed that the three blue objects are indeed the lensed images of a quasar with redshift z = 2.375 , and that the central red object is an early-type lensing galaxy with redshift z = 0.597 . \ is the first quad lensed quasar system to be found in DES and begins to demonstrate the potential of DES to discover and dramatically increase the sample size of these very rare objects.

sources.summary: The paper highlights DESJ0408-5354 as a lens system with two strongly lensed sources, a quadruply imaged quasar, a satellite, and line-of-sight galaxies.

quotes.quote: DESJ0408-5354 This lens systems stands out from the rest due to the presence of two strongly-lensed sources, in addition to the quadruply-imaged quasar at z = 2.355 , discovered by . The time delays for this system were measured by Courbin++18b at a precision of 1 with the longest delay 150 days. Shajib++19b modeled this system for cosmography, and exploited the additional information provided by the multiple lensed sources to measure H_0 at 3.8 precision (not including substructure). The main deflector is accompanied by a satellite galaxy that splits one of the lensed images, and several other galaxies along the line of sight. Shajib++19b modeled six of these line-of-sight galaxies, but in our mock lens models we only include the nearest two (G3 and G4, using the naming convention from Shajib++19b ).

sources.summary: The paper includes DES0408-5354 as a TDCOSMO lens with discovery, imaging modeling, an independent blind STRIDES/TDCOSMO analysis, time-delay measurement, and velocity-dispersion measurements.

quotes.quote: DES0408-5354: The discovery is presented by . The imaging modeling is presented by . A second team within STRIDES and TDCOSMO is performing an independent and blind analysis using a different modeling code (Yildirim et al in prep). The time-delay measurement is presented by . The velocity dispersion measurements are presented by .

sources.summary: The paper uses DES J0408-5354 quasar light curves alongside TDC1 simulations, splits observations into two 93-day periods, and calculates image time delays summarized in its appendix table.

quotes.quote: The data used are the simulated light curves used in , produced in the context of the blind Time Delay measurement competition named Time Delay Challenge 1 (TDC1) , and from the quasar DES J0408-5354 . We split the total time of observations in two equal time periods. Each period consists of 658 days for the trial curves, and of 93 days for DES J0408-5354. The Time Delay between each image is then calculated for each period. The Time Delay estimates are shown in App. AppendixD and summarized in Table tabletimedelay .

gmw_00001320.name: IRAS F10398+1455
gmw_00001320.overview: The infrared galaxy IRAS F10398+1455 exhibits a steep, red 5–8 micron emission continuum and strong polycyclic aromatic hydrocarbon emission bands that indicate starburst activity [arXiv:1605.06999, arXiv:1407.0914]. Simultaneously, the galaxy displays silicate emission features, which suggest heating from an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1605.06999, arXiv:1507.03280].

sources.summary: The paper identifies IRAS F10398+1455 as one of three spectroscopically anomalous galaxies whose Spitzer spectra combine steep 5-8 micron continua, strong PAH bands, and silicate emission linked to starbursts and AGN heating.

quotes.quote: We investigate the dust properties in three spectroscopically anomalous galaxies (IRAS F10398+1455, IRAS F21013-0739 and SDSS J0808+3948). Their Spitzer /IRS spectra are characterized by a steep 5--8 emission continuum, strong emission bands from polycyclic aromatic hydrocarbon (PAH) molecules, and prominent 10 silicate emission. The steep 5--8 continuum and strong PAH emission features suggest the presence of starbursts, while the silicate emission is indicative of significant heating from AGNs. The simultaneous detection of these two observational properties has rarely been reported on galactic scale.

We have modeled the Spitzer /IRS spectra of three spectroscopically anomalous galaxies (IRAS F10398+1455, IRAS F21013-0739 and SDSS J0808+3948) by decomposing them via , subtracting the starlight, PAH and atom-line components, and then modeling the residual dust component with the \ torus model. The IR spectral characteristics of these galaxies are unique in the sense that they show silicate emission which is characteristic of AGNs, while they also show a steep 5--8 continuum and strong PAH emission features typical for starburst galaxies. In contrast, AGNs exhibit a flat emission continuum at 5--8 and lack the PAH emission features. The 9.7 and 18 silicate features seen in emission in these three galaxies are seen in absorption in starbursts.

sources.summary: The paper reports anomalous Spitzer/IRS dust properties in IRAS F10398+1455, combining red mid-infrared continuum, silicate emission, and strong PAH bands in a young AGN/starburst system.

quotes.quote: Here we report the detection of anomalous dust properties by Spitzer /Infrared Spectrograph in three galaxies (IRAS F10398+1455, IRAS F21013-0739 and SDSS J0808+3948) which are characterized by the simultaneous detection of a red 5--8 emission continuum, the 9.7 and 18 silicate emission features as well as strong PAH emission bands. These apparently contradictory dust IR emission properties are discussed in terms of iron-poor silicate composition, carbon dust deficit, small grain size and low dust temperature in the young AGN phase of these three galaxies.
Here we report the simultaneous detection of a low, red 5--8 emission continuum and strong silicate emission features in three galaxies, IRAS F10398+1455, IRAS F21013-0739 and SDSS J0808+3948.

The 10.51 [ S IV ] line is prominent in IRAS F10398+1455 and IRAS F21013-0739, but not seen in SDSS J0808+3948. No obvious high ionization-potential lines like [ Ne V ] 14.32 and [ O IV ] 25.89 (which are typical in AGNs) are detected in our three galaxies. Finally, the 9.7 and 18 amorphous silicate emission features are clearly seen in all three galaxies. In Figure 1a we also compare the mid-IR spectra of these three galaxies with the averaged spectrum of starburst galaxies (Brandl 2006) and the average spectrum of quasars (Hao 2007). It is seen that our three sources have a comparable silicate emission strength as quasars, but they show a much steeper or ``redder'' 5--8 continuum than typical AGNs or quasars. The slope of the 5--8 continuum, defined as d F_ /d (where F_ is the observed flux and is wavelength), is 4.1--4.6 for the three sources, while the 5--8 continuum is flat or ``gray'' for AGNs (with a slope of 0.8). The ``red'' 5--8 emission continuum and the strong PAH emission features seen in these galaxies are also seen in starburst galaxies. However, the 9.7 and 18 emission features seen in these galaxies are never seen in starburst galaxies. Type 1 AGNs (and some type 2 AGNs; see Sturm et al. \ 2006, Mason et al. \ 2009, Nikutta et al. \ 2009, Shi et al. \ 2010) exhibit the silicate emission features seen in these galaxies, but their 5--8 emission continuum is often much flatter and do not show the PAH emission features which are prominent in these three galaxies. The exact profiles and strengths of the derived silicate emission features are sensitive to the assumed underlying continuum (e.g., see Sirocky et al. \ 2008, Baum et al. \ 2010, Gallimore et al. \ 2010). To subtract the continuum, we take two approaches: (1) we select five points at 5--7 , 14.5--15.0 and 29--30 to define a underlying continuum which is calculated with a spline function (see Figure 2); (2) we modify the PAHFIT software of Smith et al. \ (2007) to fit the observed IRS spectrum of each source with a combination of PAH features, amorphous silicate features, modified blackbodies, and starlight, with the sum of the modified blackbodies and starlight representing the continuum underneath the PAH and silicate features (see Figure 3). Not surprisingly, the silicate emission features derived from these two approaches differ substantially: the 9.7 feature derived from the spline method is considerably weaker than that derived from the PAHFIT method, while it is in the opposite for the 18 feature. Nevertheless, it is clear that both the 9.7 and 18 amorphous silicate emission features are present in these three sources. Crystalline forsterite silicates are also present in these galaxies as revealed by the sharp features at 19, 23, and 27.5 (see Figures 2, 3). The detection of crystalline silicate dust in extragalactic sources has been reported for some ULIRGs (Spoon et al. \ 2006), for PG 2112+059, a broad absorption line quasar (Kemper et al. \ 2007), for a distant absorber at redshift z_ abs 0.886 along the line of sight toward the gravitationally lensed quasar PKS 1830-211 (Aller et al. \ 2012), and for a z_ abs 0.685 absorber toward the gravitationally lensed blazar TXS 0218+357 (Aller et al. \ 2014). In the Galactic ISM the silicate dust is predominantly amorphous (Li Draine 2001, Kemper et al. \ 2004, Li et al. \ 2007). As shown in Figures 2, 3, the silicate features of these three galaxies are broad and smooth with sharp crystalline silicate features superimposed, suggesting that the silicate dust in these sources is mainly amorphous. These three galaxies are found when cross-matching the SDSS and Spitzer /IRS low resolution spectra (L. Hao 2014, in preparation). Xie (2014a) have studied the multi-wavelength properties of these three galaxies and demonstrated that they may harbor a young AGN at the center. Among these galaxies, IRAS F21013-0739 and SDSS J0808+3948 are Lyman Break Analogs (LBA; Heckman 2005, Hoopes 2007). LBAs are rare in the local universe. They are selected based on their far-UV properties and they show similar properties as that of high-redshift Lyman Break Galaxies (Steidel et al. \ 1999). Among the 30 LBA galaxies, six are found to contain a young, very compact ( 10 ^2 ), highly massive (several 10 ^9 ) Dominant Central Object (DCO, Overzier 2009). Interestingly, both F21013-0739 and SDSS J0808+3948 are found to have DCOs. It appears that the occurrence of the anomalous mid-IR spectral character is associated with the galaxies with a DCO. IRAS F10398+1455 is not a LBA.

sources.summary: The paper models IRAS F10398+1455 as one of three anomalous infrared galaxies whose spectra combine starburst-like PAH/continuum emission with AGN-like silicate emission and comparatively cool dust.

quotes.quote: The Spitzer /Infrared Spectrograph spectra of three spectroscopically anomalous galaxies (IRAS F10398+1455, IRAS F21013-0739 and SDSS J0808+3948) are modeled in terms of a mixture of warm and cold silicate dust, and warm and cold carbon dust. Their unique infrared (IR) emission spectra are characterized by a steep 5--8 emission continuum, strong emission bands from polycyclic aromatic hydrocarbon (PAH) molecules, and prominent silicate emission. The steep 5--8 emission continuum and strong PAH emission features suggest the dominance of starbursts, while the silicate emission is indicative of significant heating from active galactic nuclei (AGNs).

We have modeled the Spitzer /IRS spectra of three spectroscopically anomalous galaxies (IRAS F10398+1455, IRAS F21013-0739 and SDSS J0808+3948) in terms of a simple model consisting of a mixture of warm and cold silicate dust, and warm and cold carbon dust. The IR spectral characteristics of these galaxies are unique in the sense that they show silicate emission which is characteristic of AGN while they also show a steep 5--8 emission continuum and strong PAH emission features which are characteristic of starburst galaxies.
